title: Operations Manager (Credit Repair, Marketing & Real Estate Operations)
description:
WHO WE ARE

We are a dual-pillar business operating at the intersection of real estate acquisition and credit-driven business funding. On the real estate side, we specialize in creative financing — subject-to deals, cold outreach on vacant, pre-foreclosure, and tax-lien properties. On the funding side, we help small business owners clean up their credit profiles and unlock $50K–$150K+ in business capital to scale through hiring, advertising, and infrastructure.
Both businesses are growing fast. We are not looking for someone to maintain the status quo. We are building the infrastructure to scale, and we need the right operator at the center of it.

THE ROLE
This is not a coordinator position. You are the operational backbone of two businesses. You will manage talent, oversee execution, own systems, and ensure both pillars perform at a high level — simultaneously. You will be the primary point of contact between leadership and the team. Decisions that used to wait will now have a home with you.
If you are someone who needs to be told what to do every step of the way, this is not the role for you. If you thrive in ambiguity, move fast, build structure where there is none, and hold people accountable to outcomes — read on.

WHAT YOU WILL OWN

CREDIT REPAIR OPERATIONS
    •    Oversee day-to-day credit repair operations and client fulfillment from onboarding through resolutionEnsure effective use of compliant credit repair methods and workflows across all active client casesManage CRM systems for client onboarding, dispute tracking, and client communicationMonitor KPIs including turnaround times, client progress, and retention ratesImprove and document SOPs to ensure consistency and scalability as the client base grows

MARKETING OPERATIONS
    •    Coordinate and oversee marketing execution across all social media platformsEnsure production of high-quality, attention-grabbing short-form content — Reels, clips, viral editsOversee workflows for content batching, editing standards, transitions, hooks, captions, and CTAsAlign all marketing output with lead generation goals, brand standards, and compliance requirementsServe as the operational bridge between marketing content and sales funnels

REAL ESTATE OPERATIONS & VA MANAGEMENT
    •    Manage and communicate with real estate virtual assistants to ensure daily output and accountabilityEnsure accurate property research and market targeting for subject-to and creative finance opportunitiesOversee CRM workflows for real estate lead intake, tracking, and follow-upCoordinate use of tools including Go High Level and Lead Shepherd to manage pipeline activityAct as liaison between leadership and VA teams to maintain performance standards

TALENT SOURCING & TEAM BUILDING
    •    You are the eyes and ears of this organization when it comes to hiring — across every pillar, every role, every verticalActively source, vet, and recruit candidates for open roles across the credit repair arm, real estate arm, and marketing and media arm — without waiting to be directedOwn the full hiring pipeline: job post creation, application review, candidate screening, interviews, and final recommendations to leadershipSource talent through OnlineJobs.ph, LinkedIn, and any other platform necessary to find the right people at the right timeIdentify gaps in the team before they become problems — if a role needs to be filled, you are expected to have candidates in the pipeline proactivelyOnboard all new hires with proper documentation, SOPs, access, and expectations from day oneMonitor new hire performance through a structured probation period and make retention or replacement recommendations accordinglyMaintain a running roster of vetted talent that can be activated when a role opens — the business does not stop for vacancies

SYSTEMS & AUTOMATION
    •    Identify opportunities to automate repetitive tasks and streamline workflows using AI and CRM toolsImplement and optimize CRM automations, pipelines, tagging systems, and reporting dashboardsContinuously improve internal systems and document SOPs as the business scalesServe as the primary point of contact between the founder and the entire team

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

CREDIT REPAIR — EXTENSIVE KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED
    •    Deep, hands-on knowledge of credit repair processes — dispute methods, Metro 2 compliance, goodwill letters, rapid rescore strategies, and credit bureau protocolsAbility to audit a client's credit profile and identify the correct course of action without guidanceExperience managing active credit repair caseloads and ensuring clients move through the pipeline efficientlyIf you cannot perform the work yourself, you must be capable of identifying, hiring, and managing the talent who can — no exceptions

MEDIA & MARKETING — EXTENSIVE KNOWLEDGE REQUIRED
    •    Extensive experience with short-form content creation and strategy — Reels, TikTok-style edits, viral clips,  ---------- s, and carousel postsProficiency with CapCut Pro for video editing — transitions, hooks, captions, sound design, and platform-optimized outputStrong understanding of content batching, scheduling, and maintaining consistent brand voice across platformsKnowledge of what drives engagement, reach, and conversion in the credit repair and real estate nichesAbility to oversee and quality-control a content creator or media VA — and step in to execute if that role is vacantExperience with Meta Ads Manager or paid social campaigns is a strong plus

REAL ESTATE — VAST EXPERIENCE REQUIRED
    •    Extensive real estate operations experience — creative financing, subject-to acquisitions, seller finance, and distressed property targetingHands-on experience with multiple real estate platforms and lead generation portals (see tools list below)Ability to manage and direct VA cold callers, skip tracers, and lead managers toward daily performance targetsStrong understanding of how to identify, qualify, and track motivated seller leads through the full pipelineIf a tool or strategy can improve team performance and pipeline output, you are expected to know it or find it

OPERATIONS & LEADERSHIP
    •    Proven ability to hire, vet, onboard, and manage remote talent across multiple departmentsIf a role is unfilled, the responsibility does not disappear — it falls on you until the right person is in placeStrong SOP development skills — you document everything so the business is never dependent on one personHigh-level proficiency with CRM and automation platforms — Go High Level strongly preferredA-player standard: you own every outcome under your oversight, you hold your team accountable, and you deliver results — not reasons

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE
    •    Credit repair clients move smoothly through the system with clear tracking and minimal bottlenecksMarketing content is consistent, high-quality, and aligned with growth goals across both brandsReal estate VAs operate with clear direction, accurate data, and strong accountability to daily targetsEvery open role has qualified candidates in the pipeline before leadership has to askNew hires are onboarded cleanly, perform to standard, and integrate without disrupting operationsSystems are documented, automated, and scalable — not dependent on any single personLeadership is freed from daily operational oversight and focused entirely on growth

PLATFORM & TOOL PROFICIENCY
Extensive experience across multiple platforms is required. You must be proficient in at least 2–3 per category. The more you bring, the stronger your application.

CRM & AUTOMATION
Go High Level*
Podio
HubSpot
ClickUp / Asana / Trello
Zapier / Make

REAL ESTATE PORTALS & LEAD GEN
Lead Shepherd*
PropStream*
Smarter Contact / SmrtPhone*
REI Reply
Batch Leads / Batch Dialer
DealMachine
Skip Genie

CONTENT & MEDIA
CapCut Pro*
Meta Ads Manager
Canva Pro
Later / Buffer / Metricool
Loom / Zoom
GENERAL OPERATIONS
Google Workspace
Slack
Notion
ChatGPT / AI Tools
Asterisk = considered essential. All others strengthen your application.

COMPENSATION
$100 – $130 USD / Month · Part-Time or Full-Time · Remote
This role is flexible — part-time or full-time arrangements are both on the table depending on your availability and the needs of the business. What matters most is that the team gets built right and results are delivered consistently. The right candidate who demonstrates value will have room to grow with us long-term.

HOW TO APPLY
Do not send a generic application. We will not respond to them.
In your application, tell us: (1) which of the two business verticals you have the deepest experience in, (2) the tools you are proficient in and a specific result you drove using them, and (3) describe a time you managed a team remotely and how you held them accountable. Applications that skip these will be disqualified.

We move fast. If you are right for this role, you will know within days.

title: ReadyMode Dialer Setup Specialist (One-Time Project)
description:
**Budget:** $150-$200 fixed price
**Timeline:** Complete within 3-5 days

**About the project:**
We have a fully built GHL automation system for a real estate wholesaling business. The ReadyMode predictive dialer account is already active and the GHL integration is configured and tested. We need an experienced ReadyMode specialist to set up the dialer properly so the VA can start making outbound calls immediately.

**What needs to be done:**

1. Create 6 separate outbound campaigns:
   - Tax Delinquent
   - Open Liens
   - Fair/Poor Condition
   - Vacant Properties
   - Long-Term Owners
   - General Distressed

2. Set up 12 call dispositions:
   - No Answer, Voicemail Left, Wrong Number, Do Not Call, Not Interested, Follow Up, Call Back Later, Warm Lead, Hot Lead, Appointment Set, Offer Requested, Dead Lead

3. Configure recycling rules:
   - No Answer ? auto redial
   - Voicemail Left ? auto redial
   - Follow Up ? scheduled callback
   - Do Not Call ? permanently remove
   - Wrong Number ? permanently remove

4. Configure local presence caller IDs for Washington State

5. Upload and map lead list CSV to the correct campaigns (we provide the files)

6. Assign agent to campaign playlists

7. Configure dialer speed settings

8. Test the full flow -- dial a test lead, submit a call result, confirm it syncs to GHL automatically

9. Brief the client on daily dialer operation

**What we provide:**
- Full ReadyMode admin access
- Lead list CSV files ready to upload
- GHL integration already live and tested
- VA SOP document for reference

**Requirements:**
- Must have hands-on experience setting up ReadyMode or XenCALL for real estate outbound campaigns
- Must be available for a short video call to walk the client through the setup
- No GHL or coding experience required

**To apply:**
Briefly describe your ReadyMode/XenCALL experience and how many campaigns you have set up previously.

title: Lead Generation and Business Development Specialist
description:
US Panels — Job Description
Lead Generation & Business Development Specialist

Type: Full-Time · Remote
Experience: 3–5 Years
Industry: Commercial Construction / Building Materials

Web Scraping   ·   Email Campaigns   ·   Cold Outreach   ·   CRM Management   ·   Pipeline Reporting

ROLE OVERVIEW

US Panels manufactures insulated metal panel (IMP) systems used in cold storage, warehouses, food processing plants, data centers, and industrial buildings. Our customers are the contractors, developers, engineers, and facility owners who design and build these projects across North America.
The Lead Generation & Business Development Specialist is the engine behind our outbound pipeline. This is a high-activity, data-driven role built for someone who is equal parts researcher, communicator, and organizer. You will identify new industry verticals, build targeted prospect lists through web scraping and research, execute multi-channel outbound campaigns, and ensure every qualified lead is captured and tracked in the CRM. You work closely with the content team and sales team to keep messaging sharp and pipeline moving.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Web Scraping & List Building
• Identify and research new industry verticals and geographic markets for outbound prospecting (cold storage developers, food processing contractors, industrial GCs, data center builders, warehouse owners, etc.).
• Use web scraping tools and research platforms to compile accurate, targeted contact lists — names, titles, company, email, phone, and LinkedIn.
• Leverage tools such as LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo, ZoomInfo, Hunter.io, or similar platforms to source and verify contact data.
• Continuously expand and refresh prospect databases as new verticals are identified or existing lists age out.
• Segment lists by industry vertical, company size, geography, and decision-maker role to enable precise targeting.

Email Campaign Execution
• Build and launch outbound email sequences targeting segmented prospect lists — cold outreach, follow-ups, and re-engagement campaigns.
• Write or customize campaign copy in coordination with the content team to ensure messaging is relevant, personable, and aligned with current marketing initiatives.
• A/B test subject lines, email copy, and calls to action to continuously improve open rates and reply rates.
• Monitor deliverability, bounce rates, unsubscribes, and replies; maintain list hygiene and CAN-SPAM compliance.
• Coordinate send timing, frequency, and segmentation to avoid audience fatigue and maximize response.
B2B Outreach — Cold Email, Cold Call & LinkedIn
• Execute high-volume, personalized outbound outreach across three channels: email, phone, and LinkedIn.
• Make introductory calls to email-engaged prospects to qualify interest and move them toward a sales conversation.
• Send personalized LinkedIn connection requests and follow-up messages to targeted contacts at priority accounts.
• Maintain a consistent daily outreach cadence and log all activity in the CRM without exception.
• Represent US Panels professionally and build genuine rapport — you are the first human impression for most prospects.
Lead Capture & CRM Entry
• Enter all new contacts, outreach activity, responses, and lead status updates into the CRM accurately and promptly.
• Qualify inbound leads from the website and digital campaigns before passing to the sales team.
• Document lead context thoroughly at the time of handoff: company background, project type, timeline, decision-maker, and any expressed needs.
• Ensure zero leads fall through the cracks — own follow-up cadences until a lead is disqualified or handed off.
Cross-Team Collaboration
• Work closely with the content team to align outbound messaging with active campaigns, blog content, and downloadable assets.
• Coordinate with the sales team on target account priorities, territory coverage, and feedback on lead quality.
• Share insights on which verticals, messages, and contact types are responding so both teams can adapt quickly.
• Participate in regular pipeline and campaign review meetings.
Reporting & Analytics
• Report weekly on outreach volume, open rates, reply rates, calls made, meetings booked, and leads entered into the CRM.
• Track pipeline value generated from BDR-sourced leads and report on conversion at each stage.
• Identify what is working across channels, verticals, and messaging — and what is not — and bring recommendations to the team.
• Maintain a clean, organized view of campaign performance in the CRM and reporting dashboards.

REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

Lead Generation & Sales
• 3–5 years of experience in a BDR, SDR, lead generation, or outbound sales role.
• Proven track record building prospect lists from scratch and converting cold outreach into qualified pipeline.
• Experience selling into or prospecting construction, building materials, manufacturing, or industrial B2B markets is strongly preferred.
Data Scraping & Research
• Hands-on experience using web scraping tools, data enrichment platforms, or research workflows to build contact lists.
• Proficiency with Apollo, ZoomInfo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Hunter.io, or comparable prospecting tools.
• Ability to identify the right contacts within target companies and verify data accuracy before outreach.

Email Marketing & Outreach
• Experience writing and executing outbound email campaigns using HubSpot, Mailchimp, Apollo, Outreach, or similar platforms.
• Comfortable crafting personalized, concise outreach that does not read like a mass email.
• Familiarity with email deliverability, list hygiene, and CAN-SPAM compliance basics.
CRM & Data Entry
• Proficient in HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce, or a comparable CRM platform.
• Meticulous about data accuracy — contact records, activity logs, and pipeline stages are always current and correct.
• Highly organized; able to manage large lists, multi-touch cadences, and concurrent campaigns without losing track.
Communication & Collaboration
• Strong written communication — outreach emails are clear, personable, and compelling.
• Comfortable on the phone; able to build rapport quickly with busy construction professionals.
• Collaborative team player who communicates proactively with content and sales counterparts.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Experience prospecting into cold storage, food processing, data center, or warehouse construction markets.
• Familiarity with commercial construction project timelines and procurement decision-making.
• Exposure to trade shows or industry associations (AGC, GCCA, IARW, BOMA).
• Experience with LinkedIn outreach automation tools (Lemlist, Dripify, or similar).

TOOLS USED IN THIS ROLE

Apollo / ZoomInfo / Hunter.io   ·   LinkedIn Sales Navigator   ·   HubSpot or Pipedrive CRM

Outreach / Lemlist or similar   ·   Mailchimp / HubSpot Email   ·   Google Sheets / Workspace

PERFORMANCE METRICS

• Qualified prospects added to CRM per week and month.
• Outbound email open rates, reply rates, and positive response rates.
• Cold calls made and conversion to booked conversations.
• Meetings booked and handed off to the sales team.
• Pipeline value generated from outbound-sourced leads.

HOW TO APPLY

Please submit the following:
1. Resume or CV
2. Brief description of the industries you have prospected into and the tools you used.
3. An example of an outbound email or campaign sequence you wrote (anonymized if needed).

Applicants with demonstrated success building pipeline from cold outreach in technical or industrial B2B markets will be strongly preferred.

title: Full-Stack Engineer (Node + React) - Long- Term, Full-Time, Real Estate Tech Startup
description:
ABOUT US
We are building a new kind of real estate platform in the United States. It is called The Buyer Listing Service.
Today, real estate websites only show homes that are for sale. We are building the buy side of the market for real estate agents. On our platform, real estate agents post buyer want-listings, not for sale listings. Our system then uses AI to match both buyer agents and seller agents together. We work directly with the official real estate databases (called MLSs) that agents use across the US. More of these databases are signing up with us every month and we're growing fast. 

We are a small and fast-moving team. When you write code here, it ships quickly and real people use it. This is a rare chance to help build a brand-new product from a growth stage, take real ownership, and grow with the company.

THE KIND OF PERSON WE'RE LOOKING FOR
We are looking for an experienced engineer who can work independently. Over time, you will own and run large parts of this platform. We are relying on your expertise. This is not a junior role and we are not looking for someone we train from the basics. If you need a senior developer checking all of your work, this is not the right fit. If you want real ownership and the chance to be the technical expert on a growing product, it is a great fit.

WHAT YOU WILL DO
• Build and improve features across our website and our backend API
• Review and merge code changes (we use a clean pull request workflow on GitHub)
• Connect and clean real estate data from different sources so it fits our system
• Use AI tools (like Claude) to help map and clean messy data
• Work closely with the founder and one other developer, and increasingly own your areas

YOU MUST HAVE
• 4+ years of professional full-stack experience, and the proven ability to work on your own
• Strong fundamentals, not just frameworks. You can explain how JavaScript works (for example, the event loop), how the web works, and how to model and query a SQL database.
• Strong skills in Node.js and Express
• Strong skills in React
• Comfortable writing raw SQL (we use MySQL, no ORM)
• Real experience integrating and cleaning messy data and APIs from other systems
• A habit of learning on your own. You have personal projects or things you built to teach yourself.
• Clean Git habits and small, focused pull requests
• Good written English (most of our work is async and in writing)
• Able to follow written rules and instructions exactly

HOW WE THINK ABOUT AI
We use AI tools like Claude every day and we want people who use them well. But we are hiring an experienced engineer, not a person who copies and pastes from a chatbot. You should be able to explain why your code works, and fix it when the AI gives a wrong answer. In our process we will always ask you to explain your decisions, so deep experience matters more than fast answers.

NICE TO HAVE
• Experience with AWS
• Experience using AI or LLMs (Claude, OpenAI, etc.) in real projects • Experience with data pipelines or ETL

HOW WE WORK
• We write small, focused changes. We do not refactor code that is not part of the task.
• We follow a written set of rules for the codebase. Following instructions matters more here than working fast.
• We welcome AI tools. We want people who use them well to do more.

HOURS
Full-time, 40 hours per week. We would like you to work US East Coast hours if you can. This helps you work at the same time as the rest of the team.

HOW TO APPLY
1. Start your message with the word BLUEPRINT so we know you read this post.
2. Send a link to one project you built and are proud of. Tell us in 2 or 3 sentences what it does, what part you built, and why you built it that way. Be ready to explain your choices on a call.
3. Tell us your experience with Node, React, and SQL, and the largest project you have owned or led.
4. Tell us if you have used AI or LLM tools in your work, and how.
Our process is a short call, then a deeper technical interview, then a small paid task using fake data, then a short call to walk through your code together. We will reply to strong applicants.

title: AI-Powered Full-Stack Developer (Next.js / React / Supabase) — Work Directly With Founder
description:
Project-Based to start ? Long-Term / Full-Time potential for the right person
Compensation: Per project (fixed price or hourly, depending on scope) — fair rates, paid on time

I'm a solo founder running multiple products and client projects: an iOS app, a SaaS platform, e-commerce stores, and custom web applications. I ship fast, I use AI tools heavily (Claude Code, Cursor), and I'm looking for a developer who works the same way.
How this starts: We begin with small, clearly scoped projects. You deliver well, communicate well, and we keep going — bigger projects, more responsibility, and for the right person a stable long-term engagement. Think of the first projects as a paid trial that goes both ways.
This is NOT a job where you sit in meetings and wait for tickets. You get a problem, you solve it, you ship it.
What you'll work on:

Building and maintaining full-stack web apps (Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind)
Backend work with Supabase and PostgreSQL (auth, RLS, edge functions, migrations)
Integrating third-party APIs (payments, email, AI APIs)
Shopify theme customizations and fixes for client stores
Deployments via Docker/Dokploy on VPS (Hetzner) — I'll show you my setup
Using AI agents (Claude Code) as part of the daily workflow — prompt-driven development is the standard here, not the exception

How we'll work:

Async-first via Slack/Telegram, short Loom videos instead of meetings
You get clear specs and full context, then you own the task end-to-end
Direct communication with me — no project managers, no bureaucracy
I review code, but I don't micromanage


Requirements

2+ years building production web apps with React/Next.js
Solid with TypeScript, Tailwind, Node.js, PostgreSQL (Supabase a big plus)
You already use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Copilot) daily in your workflow — and can tell me HOW
Git workflow is second nature (branches, PRs, clean commits)
You can read a vague requirement, ask the right questions ONCE, and then deliver
English good enough for clear written communication

Bonus points:

Experience with Claude Code or other agentic coding tools in headless/CLI mode
Shopify (Liquid) experience
React Native / Expo experience
DevOps basics: Docker, Nginx, CI/CD, VPS management


What you get

Paid, clearly scoped starter projects — no unpaid "test tasks"
A founder who pays on time, every time
Modern stack, no legacy PHP nightmares
Real ownership: your features go live and get used
A clear path to a stable long-term role with performance-based raises — if you make me faster, you earn more


How to Apply
Applications are filtered automatically. To prove you read this:

Start your application with the word "SHIPIT"
Send 1–2 links to projects you built (live URL or GitHub)
Answer in 2–3 sentences: Describe one concrete way you used AI to ship something faster. What was the task, what tool, what was the result?
Tell me your hourly rate (or how you prefer to price small projects) and your current availability

Generic copy-paste applications without the keyword will be ignored.
I don't need someone who needs hand-holding. I need someone who solves problems. If that's you, apply now.

????????????????????
APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

speed or careful communication?

title: Engineer  - Adobe - AI Retouching & Workflow Engineer (Portrait / Fashion-Level Studio)
description:
Job Overview
We are a NYC-based premium portrait studio specializing in maternity, newborn, and beauty work. We are rebuilding our entire retouching pipeline using AI tools and are looking for a technically strong, detail-obsessed teammate to join us long-term.

This role combines:

High-end portrait retouching (Vogue-level quality)

AI-assisted workflow design and optimization ( ---------- , Imagen, Photoshop, Lightroom)

Process documentation so the system can be used and taught to others

If you are an engineer / technical creative who loves problem-solving, software, and visual quality, this is for you.

Responsibilities
Retouching & Visual Quality

Retouch high-resolution RAW images for maternity, newborn, and beauty portraiture

Perform advanced skin work (dodge & burn, texture preservation, natural results – no plastic skin)

Refine tones, contrast, and color grading suitable for large-format fine art prints

Clean details: hair, background, wardrobe, and small distractions

Maintain consistency of look across full sets and across multiple shoots

Learn and match a Vogue-level editorial standard

Reference quality: Lola Melanie, Annie Leibovitz

AI & Workflow / Engineering

Help design and refine an AI-assisted retouching pipeline using:

 ----------  plugins

Imagen AI

Photoshop (latest)

Lightroom (latest)

Map the current process from RAW to final print and identify bottlenecks

Create and refine Photoshop actions, batch processes, and smart presets to standardize the flow

Experiment with and evaluate new tools/features for speed and consistency

Record your screen and document your steps to build internal training material and SOPs

Proactively propose improvements to speed, quality, and reliability

Ideal Background
You do not have to be a traditional career retoucher, but you must bring a strong mix of technical ability + visual sensitivity.

You might be a great fit if you:

Have a background in engineering, computer science, 3D, or other technical fields

Are very comfortable learning new software, figuring things out from tutorials and documentation

Have solid Photoshop and Lightroom experience (retouching, layers, masks, color work)

Enjoy building and optimizing systems, not just “doing tasks”

Care deeply about detail and visual quality, even if you’re still growing toward top fashion-level retouching

Bonus points if you:

Have experience with 3D modeling, CAD, or technical graphics

Have used AI or automation tools (plugins, scripts, actions, or no-code workflow tools)

Have any portfolio in photography, retouching, 3D, or design

Have worked on beauty, fashion, or portrait images before

Requirements
Strong technical mindset and problem-solving skills

Experience with Photoshop and Lightroom (or strong evidence you can ramp up very quickly)

Willingness to practice and accept critique to reach a Vogue-level, natural, refined look

Stable internet and a reliable computer capable of handling high-resolution files

Ability to work graveyard shift (first month), then flexible schedule with some overlap

Good written English for communication and process documentation

Looking for stable, long-term remote work (Philippines-based preferred)

Trial Task
Shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a small paid test, which includes:

Retouching a small set of images (around 3–5) using our tools

Providing a screen recording of your retouching process with light explanation:

How you structure your workflow and layers

How and where you use AI vs manual work

Any ideas you see for improving speed and consistency

This test helps us evaluate both your eye for quality and your engineering mindset.

How to Apply
Please send PDF including:

Cover letter

Resume

Portfolio (before/after examples strongly preferred)

Links to any social profiles and website/portfolio

Subject line: AI Retouching & Workflow Engineer – [Your Name]

title: Creative Internship: Learn and Earn
description:
We Don't Just Hire VAs. We Build Them.
Elorae works with companies such as Leaders Academy, a speech and debate coaching company based in Seoul, Korea. We work with clients across Asia and we run fast. Our team is small, our standards are high, and everything we produce is seen by real students, real parents, and real partners.
We are opening internship slots for college students who want to do real work, build a real portfolio, and get paid while doing it.
This is not a coffee-fetching internship. You will be assigned to an active workstream from week one.

How It Works
You start at 5 hours in week one. By month three, you can be at 20 hours. The pace depends on you. Every intern earns 100 PHP per hour from day one. No trial period without pay. No unpaid probation.
If your school requires official documentation for academic credit, we coordinate directly with your institution.
If you perform, you are first in line when a paid VA position opens. We hire from within.

What We Are Looking For
We organize our internship positions into three tiers.
???? Green — Actively recruiting each term:
Graphic Designer — You work in Canva daily and have a portfolio that shows it. You design social media graphics, event banners, marketing assets, and YouTube thumbnails for real programs with real audiences. If your work is strong, it goes live immediately.
Copyeditor — You catch what others miss. You edit student essays, program materials, and organizational communications with precision and consistency. Chicago Manual of Style is not a stranger to you. Strong English is non-negotiable.
Social Media Manager — You understand platforms, timing, and what makes content land. You manage scheduling, captions, and posting across channels. You are not just a user of social media. You think about it strategically.
Media Editor — You edit video with real software, not just phone apps. You produce polished short-form content, event highlights, and branded video assets. A portfolio with actual edited footage is required.

???? Yellow — Limited placement for exceptional candidates, irregular availability:
Web Developer — You have built or significantly maintained a real website and can show it.
Personal Assistant — You are exceptionally organized, proactive, and comfortable supporting a fast-moving executive directly.
Events Coordinator — You have coordinated a real event from planning through execution and can walk us through what you did.
Tutor — You have subject mastery in English, writing, or speech and debate and genuine teaching instinct.

???? Red — VA services exist in these roles. We do not host interns here:
Accounts Technician
Data Analyst
Administrative Assistant
Software Developer

What You Need
You are currently enrolled in a college or university in the Philippines. You are at least a second-year student. You have a stable internet connection: minimum 30 Mbps download, 5 Mbps upload, under 50 ms ping. You have a dedicated workspace and a working laptop or desktop. Sessions and check-ins are live on camera. A bad connection wastes everyone's time.

What You Get
This is paid work. You earn 100 PHP per hour from day one. You build a real portfolio on real programs. You receive a certificate of completion from Leaders Academy. If your institution requires official internship documentation for academic credit, we coordinate directly with your school. And if you perform, you are first in line when a VA position opens. We hire from within.

How to Apply
Complete our application form:  ---------- 
In the form, indicate which tier and role you are applying for. Green tier applicants must include a portfolio link.

Attentiveness Check
Write "Twenty" in your subject line. This tells us you read the full posting. Applications without it will not be reviewed.

One Last Thing
If you are looking for easy hours without doing real work, this is not the right fit. The internship structure is designed to show us what you can do and help you grow. We will know within the first two weeks.

title: Senior Amazon PPC Account Coordinator - Long-Term Career Role
description:
We're a boutique Amazon advertising agency serving US and European clients, and an Amazon Ads Verified Partner. We've built the agency around long client tenures and a small core team that has worked together for years.

About the Role:
You'll be the daily point of contact for our US clients. You'll work directly with the founder and the campaign team, owning client communication, weekly reporting, and account context. Your job is to make sure every client feels heard, informed, and well-served.

This is a long-term role. We're looking for someone who wants to build a career with one company, not someone looking for the next gig in 6 months. If that's you, keep reading.

What you'll do:

Monitor and respond to client messages on Slack and Microsoft Teams, with live coverage during the daily US overlap window and 8 to 10 hour response time outside of it
Use our SOPs and account context to respond to routine account questions independently
Coordinate with our internal campaign team to surface client requests and deliver answers
Prepare weekly performance summaries for clients. We have templates and a reporting framework
Maintain detailed account notes so the team always has full context
Escalate strategic decisions and complex issues directly to the founder

What you bring:

2 to 4 years working with US Amazon clients at an agency or in-house at an Amazon brand
You've independently owned client accounts, not just assisted on them
Excellent written English. Non-negotiable, since you'll be the voice clients see
Strong knowledge of Amazon Seller Central and Amazon Advertising console
You understand the difference between ACoS and TACoS and can explain it clearly to a client
Comfortable using Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Workspace daily
Reliable home internet and a quiet workspace for occasional client calls
Long-term mindset. You want stability, growth, and a team that values you

What we offer:

$7 to $10/hr starting rate based on experience, with formal review at 90 days
Clear growth path: part-time to full-time, coordinator to senior coordinator to account manager
Direct working relationship with the founder, not buried under layers of management
Small team where your work is seen and your voice matters
Paid Philippine public holidays

How to apply:
Send us a Loom video (3 to 4 minutes max) where you answer these three questions in order:

Tell us about yourself and the kind of role you're looking for next? 
Imagine your client is a smart business owner who doesn't know Amazon Ads well. In about 60 seconds, explain the difference between ACoS and TACoS, and why both matter.
A client messages you on Slack saying "Our ACoS jumped this week, what's going on?". You haven't looked at the data yet. What's your first reply to them, and why?

Attach your resume along with the Loom link. Applications without a Loom will not be reviewed. We'll respond to every serious submission within 5 business days. Trial task will be paid.

title: Website & AI Marketing Specialist
description:

title: GoHighLevel CRM, Funnel & Automation Specialist — Organic Lead Generation & Sales System
description:
# GoHighLevel CRM, Funnel & Automation Specialist Needed — Organic Lead Generation + Sales System

We are looking for an experienced **GoHighLevel CRM Specialist** who can help us build, manage, and improve our lead generation and sales system.

This is **not a basic VA position**.

We need someone who already understands GoHighLevel at a high level and can take ownership of our CRM, funnels, automations, pipelines, forms, email/SMS follow-up, and lead tracking with training focusing on our company, not Go High Level. 

Our company is building an organic lead generation system, meaning we are **not running paid ads right now**. We need someone who understands how to use GoHighLevel to turn organic traffic from social media, DMs, content, partnerships, webinars, and landing pages into real leads and sales.

## Main Role

Your job will be to help us use GoHighLevel as the backend sales machine for our business.

This includes:

* Building and managing GHL funnels
* Creating landing pages and opt-in pages
* Creating forms, surveys, and lead capture systems
* Setting up CRM pipelines
* Building email and SMS automation workflows
* Setting up follow-up sequences for leads
* Tracking where each lead is in the sales process
* Creating tags, triggers, and smart lists
* Helping improve conversion rates
* Creating sales pages and payment links
* Organizing leads from organic marketing channels
* Helping us turn social media attention into leads and customers

## Important: We Need a GHL Expert, Not Just a Task Taker

We are looking for someone who knows GoHighLevel better than we do.

You should be able to recommend the best way to set things up instead of waiting for us to explain every detail.

We want someone who can say:

“This is the best way to build the funnel.”

“This workflow should be improved.”

“This automation can help increase sales.”

“This new GHL feature would help the business.”

“This pipeline needs to be cleaned up.”

“This lead source is converting better than the others.”

You must be someone who thinks strategically and understands how CRM, funnels, automation, and sales all work together.

## Responsibilities

* Build and maintain GoHighLevel funnels
* Create landing pages for different offers
* Create lead magnets, opt-in forms, and surveys
* Set up CRM pipelines for lead tracking
* Build automation workflows using email and SMS
* Set up follow-up sequences for cold, warm, and hot leads
* Track funnel performance and lead movement
* Help organize leads from social media and organic traffic
* Create simple reports showing leads, conversions, and sales activity
* Test and improve pages, forms, and automations
* Stay updated on new GoHighLevel features
* Recommend and roll out new GHL tools/features that can help the company grow
* Help create a repeatable system that turns organic content into customers

## Required Skills

You must have real experience with:

* GoHighLevel CRM
* GHL funnels and websites
* GHL workflows and automations
* Email and SMS campaigns
* Forms and surveys
* Tags, triggers, and smart lists
* Pipelines and opportunities
* Lead nurturing
* Sales follow-up systems
* Basic funnel strategy
* Basic copywriting for landing pages and follow-up messages
* Understanding how organic traffic can become leads and sales

## Preferred Experience

Experience with any of the following is a plus:

* Business credit, finance, coaching, digital products, or service-based businesses
* Organic social media lead generation
* Webinar funnels
* Course or digital product funnels
* Stripe/payment setup inside GHL
* Appointment or consultation funnels
* Affiliate or referral tracking
* AI tools inside GoHighLevel
* Reporting and conversion tracking

## What We Are Building

We are building a system where organic marketing creates attention, and GoHighLevel captures and converts that attention into leads and sales.

The process will look like this:

Organic content ? funnel/landing page ? lead form/survey ? automated follow-up ? CRM pipeline ? sales page/payment link ? customer follow-up

Your role is to help build, manage, and improve this system.

## Work Style We Are Looking For

We are looking for someone who is:

* Proactive
* Strategic
* Organized
* Experienced with GoHighLevel
* Detail-oriented
* Good at solving problems
* Comfortable making recommendations
* Able to work with minimal training
* Willing to learn new GHL features
* Focused on results, not just completing tasks

We do not want someone who only waits for instructions. We want someone who can help us make the system better.

## Starting Pay

Starting pay is **$6 USD per hour**.

This is the starting rate while we confirm your skill level, reliability, and ability to generate results.

Once expert-level ability is shown, the system is running properly, leads are being captured, and sales are being generated, we are open to paying much more.

We are looking for someone who can grow with the company long-term.

## Schedule

Part-time to start, with the opportunity to grow into more hours based on performance.

Please include your availability and preferred working hours in your application.

## How to Apply

Please answer the following questions when applying:

1. How long have you been using GoHighLevel?
2. What parts of GHL are you strongest in?
3. Have you built funnels inside GHL before? Please describe one.
4. Have you built email/SMS automation workflows before? Please describe one.
5. Have you worked with organic lead generation before?
6. What GHL feature do you think most businesses underuse?
7. If we had social media traffic but no paid ads, how would you use GHL to turn that traffic into leads and sales?
8. Please send examples, screenshots, Loom videos, or a short explanation of GHL work you have done.
9. What is your desired hourly rate after proving expert-level results?

## Final Note

We are looking for a GoHighLevel specialist who can help us build a real sales system.

If you are only a general VA with basic admin experience, this may not be the right role.

If you are confident with GHL, funnels, CRM, automations, and organic lead conversion, we would love to hear from you.

title: FULL-TIME META & GOOGLE ADS SPECIALIST
description:
FULL-TIME META & GOOGLE ADS SPECIALIST

Long-Term Role | Stable Pay | Growth Opportunity

Steady Auto Growth is hiring a full-time Meta & Google Ads Specialist to join our growing team.

We are looking for someone who genuinely understands how to launch, manage, optimize, and scale advertising campaigns across both Meta and Google.

This is not an entry-level position.

We want someone who can think strategically, analyze data, identify problems, communicate with creative teams, and continuously improve campaign performance through testing and optimization.

You will be responsible for overseeing multiple client accounts and helping businesses generate leads, appointments, and revenue through paid advertising.

If you enjoy analyzing performance, running split tests, improving creative, optimizing campaigns, and scaling accounts, this may be the perfect role for you.

You will work directly with our copywriting team, video editors, graphic designers, website team, and leadership team to improve campaign performance and create winning advertising systems.

This role requires both analytical thinking and strong communication skills.

Responsibilities

• Launch and manage Google Ads campaigns

• Monitor daily campaign performance

• Analyze key performance metrics

• Identify opportunities for improvement

• Create and oversee split tests

• Optimize campaigns for lead generation and conversion performance

• Work directly with copywriters to improve messaging

• Work directly with video editors and designers to improve creatives

• Work with website and funnel teams to improve conversion rates

• Provide clear feedback and direction to creative teams

• Scale winning campaigns

• Troubleshoot underperforming campaigns

Requirements

• Minimum 2 years of experience managing Meta Ads

• Minimum 2 years of experience managing Google Ads

• Experience running lead generation campaigns

• Strong understanding of campaign structure and optimization

• Experience running split tests and interpreting results

• Strong understanding of conversion tracking

• Familiarity with Google Tag Manager, GA4, and Meta Pixel

• Ability to analyze data and make strategic decisions

• Experience working with creative teams

• Strong communication skills

• Highly organized and detail-oriented

• Comfortable receiving direct feedback

• Looking for a long-term role

Bonus Skills (Not Required)

• GoHighLevel experience

• Funnel building experience

• Landing page optimization

• CRM experience

• Call tracking experience

• Service-based business advertising experience

• Local lead generation experience

Compensation

• $350 USD per month plus bonuses

• Full-time position

• Long-term growth opportunity

• Stable employment

• Direct collaboration with leadership

• Opportunity to advance into a Senior Media Buyer role

Work Schedule

• Full-Time (40 hours per week)

• Flexible schedule

• Performance and results matter more than hours worked

About Steady Auto Growth

Steady Auto Growth helps service-based businesses scale through paid advertising, websites, CRM systems, automation, and sales infrastructure.

We focus heavily on lead generation systems that help businesses generate measurable growth through both Meta and Google advertising.

Your role will directly impact client revenue, lead generation, and campaign performance.

How To Apply (Read Carefully)

To apply, message Ted directly on WhatsApp:

WhatsApp: +1 (339) ---------- Your application MUST include ALL of the following:

1. A short introduction about yourself

2. A video introduction (2-5 minutes)

3. A voice note introducing yourself

4. How many years of Meta Ads experience you have

5. How many years of Google Ads experience you have

6. Screenshots of campaigns you currently manage or have managed

7. Screenshots of reporting dashboards or campaign performance metrics

   • Campaign objective
   • Testing strategy
   • Optimization decisions
   • Results achieved

9. Your favorite campaign you've ever run and why it performed well

10. A brief explanation of your split-testing process

11. Industries you have experience advertising for

12. Experience with GA4, Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and conversion tracking

13. Why you believe you would be a strong fit for this role

14. Your skill level (1-5)

Skill Scale

5 = Expert

4 = Advanced

3 = Independent & Confident

2 = Intermediate

1 = Beginner

Applications missing any of the required items above will not be reviewed.

Ready to Manage Real Ad Spend?

If you understand Meta and Google advertising, enjoy optimizing campaigns, communicating with creative teams, and helping businesses grow, message Ted on WhatsApp today.

We are looking for someone who doesn't just launch ads—we are looking for someone who can analyze data, run meaningful tests, improve creative performance, optimize campaigns, and help build scalable advertising systems.

title: FULL-TIME GOHIGHLEVEL (GHL) AUTOMATION SPECIALIST
description:
FULL-TIME GOHIGHLEVEL (GHL) AUTOMATION SPECIALIST

Long-Term Role | Stable Pay | Growth Opportunity

Steady Auto Growth is hiring a full-time GoHighLevel Automation Specialist to join our growing team.

We are looking for someone who genuinely understands GoHighLevel and can build, troubleshoot, optimize, and improve automation systems without constant supervision.

This is not an entry-level position.

We want someone who can confidently build workflows, automations, pipelines, CRM systems, lead nurture sequences, appointment booking systems, and internal operational processes.

If you enjoy building systems that make businesses run more efficiently, this may be the perfect opportunity.

Job Overview

As our GoHighLevel Automation Specialist, you will be responsible for building and managing the backend systems that power both our agency and our clients.

title: Senior Airbnb Virtual Assistant (2+ Years STR Experience Required)
description:
Looking for a Long-Term Career in the Short-Term Rental Industry?

Hostier is a fast-growing UK-based Airbnb and property management company. We manage a portfolio of short-term rental properties and are looking for an experienced Virtual Assistant to join our team.

This is not a temporary position or a side hustle. We are looking for someone who wants stability, responsibility, and the opportunity to grow with a company that is expanding year after year.

If you have experience in the Short-Term Rental industry and enjoy solving problems, helping guests, and keeping operations running smoothly, we’d love to hear from you.

Why Join Hostier?

* Long-term, stable position
* Work with an established and growing UK company
* Clear systems and processes
* Supportive team environment
* Opportunity for increased responsibility and career progression
* Work directly within the fast-growing Airbnb and STR industry

Essential Requirements

Please only apply if you meet all of the following:

* Minimum 2 years of Short-Term Rental (STR) experience
* Excellent written and spoken English
* Strong customer service skills
* Ability to work independently and solve problems proactively
* Experience communicating with guests via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Reliable internet connection and suitable home office setup

Highly Desirable Experience

* Previous experience working directly for Airbnb
* Experience using Airbnb,  ---------- , VRBO, Hostaway, Enso Connect, Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, or similar platforms
* Experience coordinating cleaners and maintenance teams
* Experience managing guest communications and reservations
* Experience using Notion, Airtable, Slack, and Google Workspace

Key Responsibilities

* Managing guest communications via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Handling inbound and outbound calls
* Managing reservations and guest journeys
* Scheduling and coordinating cleaners
* Managing maintenance requests
* Handling guest issues and escalations
* Monitoring guest reviews and feedback
* Email management and administration
* Reporting and operational support
* Team communication via Slack

Working Hours

* 48.5 hours per week
* Rotating shifts between 09:00–22:00 GMT
* Weekend and public holiday coverage required

Who We’re Looking For

We value:

* Reliability
* Accountability
* Professionalism
* Attention to detail
* Strong communication skills
* A proactive attitude
* A desire to build a long-term career

Application Instructions

To confirm you have read this advert, please include the phrase:

HSTR

Please also provide:

1. Your CV/resume
2. A summary of your STR experience
3. The companies you have worked for and the length of time with each
4. The systems you have used (Hostaway, Enso Connect, Airbnb,  ---------- , Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, etc.)

Applications that do not include the phrase HSTR will not be considered.

We look forward to hearing from ambitious candidates who are excited to contribute, grow, and build a long-term career with Hostier.

title: Guest Experience Specialist, Third Coast Elevated Stays
description:
Third Coast Elevated Stays is a locally rooted, family-owned property management company serving the Texas coast markets of Galveston, Corpus Christi, Rockport, Port Aransas, and South Padre Island. We are focused on providing elevated stays for our guests and elevated stewardship for our homeowners. Our goal is to grow in a way that celebrates what makes the Texas coast special - its people, coastal culture, beach communities, and local character - while delivering elevated experiences for guests and thoughtful, reliable stewardship for homeowners.

Tech stack (daily tools): Guesty (PMS + guest inbox), Breezeway (tasks/workorders + cleaning), RingCentral (calls/SMS).

Our Elevated Promises
Everything we do is guided by our Elevated Promises. They are our operating standard and the way we earn five-star stays and trusted, long-term homeowner partnerships.

1. Elevated Guest Engagement: Proactive, elevated hospitality from booking to five-star review. 
We believe vacations are special—something people save for, plan for, and look forward to. Our job is to make every stay feel seamless and memorable. We anticipate needs, communicate early, check in during the stay, and resolve issues quickly with warmth and care. Our guest journey is intentionally crafted to deliver the kind of responsiveness, thoughtfulness, and attention to detail that turns great trips into truly elevated experiences.

2. Elevated Home Stewardship: We care for every home as if it were our own.
As homeowners in these markets, we know what true home stewardship looks like. We operate with a proactive mindset of quality, consistency, and pride. Weekly home checks, rigorous cleaning standards, quality control, seasonal readiness, and preventive maintenance are all part of how we ensure every property is clean, protected, and presentation ready, for guests and for you. We treat every home with the same respect and diligence we expect for our own.

3. Elevated Homeowner Partnerships: We treat each home like our own investment.
Every homeowner has different goals – maximizing revenue, balancing personal use, focusing on long-term appreciation, or simply enjoying peace of mind. Our role is to be a true partner in those goals, not simply a property manager. As owners and operators in this region ourselves, our guidance comes from real-world results, not theory. Through regular business reviews, thoughtful design and amenity recommendations, revenue and pricing insights, and brand-building support, we help each property reach its highest potential.

Our Guest Experience team is one of the biggest ways we deliver these promises. This is hospitality work. You are a core part of our team, not an outsourced call-center function.?

Job Description: Guest Experience Specialist - Texas Coast
As a Guest Experience Specialist, you own guest communication and coordination. You are the voice of Third Coast, a core part of our team, and a key driver of five-star stays. You will manage guest messaging and calls, coordinate workorders and cleaning schedules with our local teams and vendors, and bring elevated hospitality and experiences to every stay.

You will own:
• Proactive outreach and warm guest engagement that drives five-star stays
• Guest messaging and phone communication
• Workorder coordination and task follow-through
• Cleaning scheduling and day-to-day coordination with our local team and vendors

Working Hours
We provide live coverage from 7:00am to 11:00pm Central Time (CDT). You will be assigned a consistent shift. On-call after-hour coverage (11:00pm-7:00am CDT) is handled by a separate rotation.

Central Daylight Time (CDT) ? Philippines Time (PHT):
• 7am–3pm CDT ? 8pm–4am PHT
• 11am–7pm CDT ? 12am–8am PHT
• 3pm–11pm CDT ? 4am–12pm PHT
• 11pm–7am CDT ? 12pm–8pm PHT

Compensation and Incentives
• Base pay: $800 USD per month (full-time, based on experience)
• 13th month pay: offered in December (prorated for partial years)
• Monthly 5-Star Bonus: $50 if the monthly average rating is 4.85+ and the review rate is 60%+.
• PTO: Accrues at 1 paid days per month (usable after 90 days; no annual rollover)

Location
These roles are fully remote within the Philippines. 

Role and Responsibilities

Elevated Guest Engagement (Guesty + text/email)
• Answer guest messages (text, email, OTA inbox via Guesty) with warmth, clarity, and genuine care
• Proactively share helpful guidance (arrival, access, weather/roads, home-specific tips) and check in during the stay
• Keep conversations organized and ensure no guest is left waiting or confused
• Ensure every guest experience feels special and elevated

Welcome outreach (required for every reservation)
• Place a welcome phone call to every guest prior to arrival day and send a personalized welcome text
• Confirm access and comfort, offer help, and document any concerns quickly
• Identify opportunities to make the guest’s stay an elevated and memorable experience

Phone support (RingCentral)
• Answer calls quickly and calmly and return missed calls promptly
• Troubleshoot common issues (access, Wi-Fi, amenities, heating/hot tub basics) and escalate when needed

Workorders and coordination (Breezeway)
• Create workorders fast when issues arise and confirm vendor/team acknowledgment
• Track progress, follow up, and close the loop with guests and the local team
• Keep notes and task status clean so handoffs are seamless

Cleaning scheduling and turnovers (Breezeway)
• Coordinate turn cleaning schedules and special requests
• Ensure all turns are scheduled a minimum of one week prior to booking and confirm scheduled cleaner by 6:00pm CDT the night prior to a scheduled cleaning and communicate key notes to cleaners

Documentation and handoffs
• Maintain accurate property notes and updates
• Close out open items before shift end and hand off active issues with clear context

Monthly SLA Standards (required for bonus eligibility)
• Guest messages answered within 15 minutes during your shift (text, email, Guesty inbox)
• Phone calls answered within 30 seconds during your shift
• No open guest messages left at shift end; clean handoffs with clear notes
• Workorders created promptly and vendor/team acknowledgment confirmed quickly

Property Listing Management & OTA Updates
• Maintain accurate, up-to-date listings across Guesty, Airbnb, Vrbo, and other OTAs.
• Update photos, amenities, room configurations, access notes, and seasonal details as needed.
• Ensure descriptions reflect the true guest experience with clear, warm, and helpful language.
• Flag inconsistencies, outdated information, or missing details to the Manager for correction.
• Support seasonal updates (spring break, summer beach season, hurricane season, local events, and holiday periods) to elevate guest expectations and reduce pre-arrival questions.
• Ensure every listing reflects our Elevated Promises — clarity, care, and a welcoming tone.

Local expertise
• Become an expert on Galveston, Corpus Christi, Rockport, Port Aransas, and South Padre Island, including seasonal rhythms, beach conditions, weather impacts, and local norms.
• Learn the best restaurants, cafes, shops, beach activities, family experiences, fishing and boating options, and local attractions in each market so you can offer personalized, concierge-level recommendations that elevate each stay.
• Know our homes in detail: complete Matterport and photo walkthroughs; learn layouts, bedding, amenities, quirks, parking, access instructions, and any “good to know” details for guests.
• Stay current on local events and updates by checking event calendars, tourism sites, and community pages (including relevant local Facebook groups).
• Use your local and property knowledge proactively to help guests plan better stays, avoid common issues, and feel confidently looked after from booking to checkout.

What we look for
• Excellent written and spoken English and a warm, helpful tone
• Hospitality mindset (you enjoy caring for people, not just closing tickets)
• Strong ownership and follow-through, especially on weekends and busy days
• Detail-oriented and eager to learn homes, markets, and local context
• Comfortable learning and working inside Guesty, Breezeway, and RingCentral
• Reliable internet and a quiet, professional work environment

How to apply

Please submit the following:
• Resume/CV
• 2-minute voice recording introducing yourself and your experience (English)
• Speedtest.net result (screenshot)

Short written samples: How would you respond to the following guest messages?
• “The hot tub isn’t heating. Can you help?”
• “Can we request a late checkout tomorrow?”
• “We’re staying in Port Aransas — any recommendations for a great breakfast spot for our family?”

Short written samples: These three guests message you at the same time. In what order would you respond and why?
• “We can’t get into the home, the code isn’t working.”
• “The Wi-Fi is slow and we need it for work.”
• “Can we check out one hour later?”

Suggested email subject line: "Texas Coast Guest Experience VA - [Your Name]"

title: Revenue Manager, Sierra Sol Elevated Stays
description:
Sierra Sol Elevated Stays | Revenue Manager
Philippines – Direct Hire (Remote), aligned to U.S. hours

Sierra Sol Elevated Stays is a locally rooted, family-owned property management company serving New Mexico and Texas with 1,000+ vacation rental homes across multiple markets. Our love for these regions is what led us to build Sierra Sol Elevated Stays, focused on providing elevated stays for our guests and elevated stewardship for our homeowners. Our goal is to grow in a way that celebrates what makes our communities special, while delivering elevated experiences for guests and thoughtful, reliable stewardship for homeowners.

Our Elevated Promises
Everything we do is guided by our Elevated Promises. They are our operating standard and the way we earn five-star stays and trusted, long-term homeowner partnerships.

Elevated Guest Engagement: Proactive, elevated hospitality from booking to five-star review.
We believe vacations are special, something people save for, plan for, and look forward to. Our job is to make every stay feel seamless and memorable. We anticipate needs, communicate early, check in during the stay, and resolve issues quickly with warmth and care. Our guest journey is intentionally crafted to deliver the kind of responsiveness, thoughtfulness, and attention to detail that turns great trips into truly elevated experiences.

Elevated Home Stewardship: We care for every home as if it were our own.
As homeowners in our markets, we know what true home stewardship looks like. We operate with a proactive mindset of quality, consistency, and pride. Weekly home checks, rigorous cleaning standards, quality control, seasonal readiness, and preventive maintenance are all part of how we ensure every property is clean, protected, and presentation ready, for guests and for you. We treat every home with the same respect and diligence we expect for our own.

Elevated Homeowner Partnerships: We treat each home like our own investment.
Every homeowner has different goals, maximizing revenue, balancing personal use, focusing on long-term appreciation, or simply enjoying peace of mind. Our role is to be a true partner in those goals, not simply a property manager. As owners and operators in these regions ourselves, our guidance comes from real-world results, not theory. Through regular business reviews, thoughtful design and amenity recommendations, revenue and pricing insights, and brand-building support, we help each property reach its highest potential.

Revenue Management is one of the biggest ways we deliver these promises. This is business partnership work. You are a core part of our team, not an outsourced analyst function.

Job Description - Revenue Manager

Our Revenue Manager owns pricing, fee strategy, and performance analytics across 1,000+ vacation rental homes in New Mexico and Texas. You will maximize homeowner revenue and company profitability through disciplined pricing optimization, thoughtful fee architecture, and clear, data-driven storytelling to homeowners and internal leaders. You will build scalable reporting systems, lead owner business review preparation, and partner closely with Finance (reporting to our CFO), General Managers, Marketing, and Business Development to translate insights into action.

The Revenue Manager will own:
• Pricing strategy across all channels to maximize occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, and Net RevPAR
• Fee strategy to optimize company profitability while protecting guest conversion and homeowner satisfaction
• Owner quarterly and annual business reviews: templates, reporting, insights, and preparation support
• Quarterly market insights reporting by market, including context (weather, events, market shifts) and company and unit performance vs market benchmarks
• Rental Potential Analysis process and templates for prospective homeowners
• Amenity and upgrade ROI recommendations for current homeowners (incremental revenue projections and payback analysis)
• Growth analytics and new homeowner identification using tools like AirDNA, Wheelhouse, Audiences, and our CRM, including lead scoring and data enrichment

Working Hours
This role is full-time and will primarily align to U.S. business hours, expect 4-6 hours overlap with U.S. Central Time zone.
• Core hours: Monday to Friday during U.S. hours with flexibility based on business needs
• Weekly revenue standups and market reviews (standing cadence)
• Light weekend monitoring during peak periods as needed for pacing shifts, promotions, and exception management

Compensation and Incentives
• Base pay: $1,800–$2,300 USD per month (full-time, based on experience, STR revenue expertise, and ability to lead multi-market strategy)
• 13th month pay: Provided in December (prorated for partial years)
• Performance Bonus: Quarterly performance bonus target of 15–20% of base pay (annualized), tied to a scorecard
• PTO: Accrues at 1.25 paid days per month (usable after 90 days; no annual rollover)

Location
This role is fully remote within the Philippines.

Role and Responsibilities

Champion the Elevated Promises:
• Lead with an owner-partner mindset, ensuring our recommendations balance homeowner goals, guest conversion, and long-term performance
• Communicate insights with clarity and warmth, especially when market conditions are challenging
• Hold a high bar for data accuracy and defensible assumptions in all owner-facing reporting

Revenue Management and Pricing Strategy
• Own portfolio pricing strategy across channels, including rate plans, minimum stay rules, last-minute strategy, and length-of-stay controls
• Maintain pricing guardrails by market, season, and property type (floors, ceilings, event premiums, and exception rules)
• Monitor pacing daily and weekly, identify anomalies early, and recommend corrective actions
• Partner with GMs and Guest Experience leadership to align pricing with operational realities and guest experience considerations

Fee Strategy and Profitability Optimization
• Design and refine guest-facing and owner-facing fee structures with clear guardrails and internal governance
• Assess fee elasticity and conversion impact, and recommend adjustments based on performance data
• Collaborate with Finance to ensure fee models and reporting definitions are consistent across markets and statements

Owner Business Reviews (QBR/ABR) and Reporting
• Own Quarterly & Annual Business Review templates and associated regular updates
• Segment owners (top performers, at-risk, new homes, premium homes) and tailor insights accordingly
• Prepare performance narratives: what happened, why it happened, what we did, and what we will do next quarter
• Build owner retention risk indicators and trigger internal workflows when performance and sentiment signals indicate risk

Market Insights and Benchmarking
• Produce quarterly market insights reports by market, including seasonality, demand drivers, weather impacts, and competitive dynamics
• Benchmark performance against market data and comp sets where available, and translate into clear, owner-friendly insights
• Maintain a market events calendar and seasonal assumptions library to support consistent forecasting and storytelling

Growth Enablement
• Develop and maintain Rental Potential Analysis tools and assumptions (conservative, base, and upside cases)
• Partner with Business Development and Marketing to create compelling, data-driven sales enablement content
• Lead new homeowner identification by utilizing tools to identify new homeowner leads, building target lists, scoring leads, and loading actionable opportunities into the CRM
• Optionally support initial outreach sequences (email or text) if structured as part of the role

Amenity and Upgrade ROI Recommendations
• Build an amenity uplift and ROI library by market and property type (e.g., hot tubs, EV chargers, game rooms)
• Provide clear owner recommendations with projected revenue lift, payback period, and considerations for ongoing maintenance and guest experience
• Create upgrade packages and playbooks to help owners d ----------  faster and align improvements to guest expectations

Tools, Systems, and Operating Rhythm
• Become an expert user and functional owner of tools like Wheelhouse, AirDNA, Audiences, and our CRM
• Build dashboards and weekly reporting for portfolio health (occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, Net RevPAR, margin, lead time, cancellations)
• Establish a structured experimentation approach for fee changes, discount ladders, minimum stay rules, and channel strategies
• Run a weekly cadence with Finance and operations leaders to review pacing, underperformers, and action plans

Revenue Manager Success Measures
• Portfolio and market performance improves across occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, and Net RevPAR relative to baseline
• Fee strategy increases company profitability without meaningfully reducing conversion or owner satisfaction
• QBRs and ABRs are delivered on time with clear insights and consistent data accuracy
• Underperforming homes are identified quickly and translated into actionable plans with internal owners
• Market insights reporting builds trust and reduces owner friction during challenging demand periods
• Rental Potential Analyses support growth conversion and help fill the top of the homeowner funnel

Qualifications
• 3+ years experience in revenue management for short-term rentals
• Experience managing revenue for 200+ units and/or multi-market portfolios
• Experience running experimentation (discount ladders, minimum stay changes, fee tests) with documented outcomes
• Experience using pricing and market data tools (Wheelhouse, PriceLabs, Beyond, AirDNA, etc.)
• Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills (modeling, scenario analysis, clean reporting)
• Strong written communication and ability to translate data into clear owner-friendly insights

How to apply
Please submit the following:
• Resume/CV
• Short written note: Why Sierra Sol Elevated Stays, and why this role
• One work sample (or short write-up): A market or portfolio performance insight you have delivered and the actions you recommended
• Provide a 1–2 page write-up (or slides) addressing the following:
Market: Angel Fire, New Mexico
Problem: pacing is down YoY due to lack of snowfall and spring break is coming up in March
Actions: 3 specific actions you would take this week and why, what you would do next to evaluate those changes, and what your expected goals or outcomes would be
Suggested email subject line: "Revenue Manager, Sierra Sol - [Your Name]"

title: Part-Time IT & Security Engineer (Tier 2/3)
description:
Part-Time IT & Security Engineer (Tier 2/3)
Weekend Coverage + Optional Weekday Evenings | Remote, Philippines

Sentry is a US-based managed service provider (MSP) and physical security integrator serving small and mid-sized businesses across the New York metro area. On the IT side, we manage our clients' networks, servers, Microsoft 365 environments, endpoints, and phone systems. On the security side, we design, install, and support their video surveillance and access control systems.

We do things differently from most MSPs: we don't run a Tier 1 help desk. When a client reaches us, they get a skilled engineer who takes ownership immediately, not a scripted first-tier queue or an automated phone tree. That first-contact competence is a big part of why our clients enjoy working with us, and it's why we only hire technicians at Tier 2 and above.

We're hiring part-time to cover our weekends, Saturday and Sunday, and we have additional weekday evening shifts available for the right person. Weekends and evenings are much quieter than our weekday daytime operation, so this isn't a high-pressure call queue. You'll handle the calls, emails, and tickets that do come in, owning each from first contact through resolution, and spend the rest of your time moving project work forward: deployments, configurations, maintenance windows, documentation, and backlog cleanup. Weekends and evenings are also when we can safely take systems offline for upgrades that would disrupt clients during the day, so a meaningful part of this role is planned project execution, not just reactive support.

The weekend coverage is what we're most eager to fill. If you're interested, you can also pick up one or more weekday evening shifts on top of it; the full schedule is below.

Because you'll often be our main presence outside core business hours, independence and sound judgment matter here. You won't be alone: you'll have escalation paths to senior engineers and the owner, and we take on the hardest problems as a team, but you should be comfortable owning a client environment and knowing when to escalate. The role is fully remote, and you'll be a full member of our team, not an isolated, outsourced contractor.

What You'll Do

- Handle client requests by phone, email, and ticket during your shifts, and own them from first contact through resolution
- Deliver a confident, professional experience on every interaction; clients should feel they're in good hands from the first minute
- Execute project work handed off from the weekday team: deployments, migrations, network and system configuration, and backlog items that need uninterrupted time to complete
- Run maintenance windows: patching, firmware upgrades, server maintenance, and other changes best done while clients are offline
- Administer Microsoft 365 / Entra ID (Azure AD): user and license provisioning, Exchange Online, mail flow, and email security
- Monitor and respond to RMM alerts (Atera) and manage endpoint security tools (Huntress, Bitdefender)
- Troubleshoot networks: firewalls (FortiGate), switches and access points (UniFi), VLANs, VPNs, and DNS
- Administer Windows Server, Active Directory, and Group Policy
- Manage and verify backups (Datto)
- Support VoIP systems and IP phones
- Configure, support, and troubleshoot video surveillance systems (IP cameras, NVRs, and video management software such as Hikvision, Digital Watchdog, and Speco), including secure remote access and viewing
- Administer access control systems (users, credentials, and door schedules) and troubleshoot controllers and readers
- Remotely support our field technicians during security-system installs and service calls
- Keep clear, accurate documentation for every client environment, including writing up the project work you complete
- Work alongside our AI tooling, which we use heavily across daily operations

What We're Looking For

- 3+ years of hands-on IT support experience, ideally at an MSP
- Excellent spoken and written English, with a strong customer-first attitude; you'll be the voice of our company on every call
- The confidence to own client interactions and project work independently, along with the judgment to know when to involve the team
- Self-direction: with lighter supervision outside business hours, you should be able to pick up a project, see it through, and document it without hand-holding
- Solid knowledge of Microsoft 365/Entra, Windows Server, Active Directory, and networking fundamentals
- Hands-on experience with firewalls and managed switches (FortiGate and UniFi a strong plus)
- Experience with IP video surveillance and/or access control systems, or the aptitude and willingness to develop this expertise quickly (it's a core part of what we do)
- Reliable high-speed internet with a backup connection
- A quiet, dedicated home workspace

Bonus Points

- Deep familiarity with AI tools, especially Claude, which we use extensively day to day
- Spanish-speaking ability (written and/or spoken)
- Hands-on experience with VMS platforms (e.g., DW Spectrum), access control systems, or remote video monitoring services (e.g., Immix)
- Certifications (CompTIA A+/Network+/Security+, Microsoft, Fortinet NSE, Ubiquiti, Cisco CCNA)
- PowerShell or other scripting

Work Setup

- 100% remote, based in the Philippines
- Part-time, permanent employee position, not contract work
- All hours are in US Eastern Time, which maps to an overnight or early-morning shift in the Philippines (about an hour later when the US is on standard time)

Weekend coverage, the core of this role (both days):
- Saturday, 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM ET (roughly 10:00 PM Sat to 10:00 AM Sun PHT)
- Sunday, 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM ET (roughly 10:00 PM Sun to 10:00 AM Mon PHT)
- About 24 hours per week

Optional weekday evening shifts, pick any one, two, or all three:
- Tuesday, 5:00 PM to 10:00 PM ET (roughly 5:00 AM to 10:00 AM Wed PHT)
- Thursday, 5:00 PM to 10:00 PM ET (roughly 5:00 AM to 10:00 AM Fri PHT)
- Friday, 5:00 PM to 10:00 PM ET (roughly 5:00 AM to 10:00 AM Sat PHT)
- 5 hours per shift

What We Offer

- Competitive USD pay, scaled to the shifts you take (same hourly-equivalent rate across all of them):
  - Weekend coverage (Saturday and Sunday): $1,200 to $1,500 per month, based on experience
  - Each weekday evening shift you add: approximately $250 to $315 per month per shift
  - For reference, taking all five shifts works out to roughly a full-time load at about $1,950 to $2,450 per month
- Paid time off, pro-rated for a part-time schedule
- A stable, long-term role with real room to grow, including the potential to expand into more hours
- A supportive, AI-forward team that invests in its people

How to Apply

To apply, send your resume and a short note about your MSP and IT experience, including any hands-on work with security systems and AI tools, and let us know which shifts you're available for: weekend coverage, weekday evenings, or both.
