title: Automation Specialist Needed to Build Credit Repair Client Onboarding System
description:
We are looking for an experienced automation specialist to help build out a complete automated onboarding system for our credit repair business.

This system will support clients after they purchase our low-ticket credit repair offer and join our paid Skool community. The goal is to create a smooth backend process where new clients are automatically onboarded, educated, tagged, added to the CRM, and placed into follow-up sequences with as little manual work as possible.

What We Need Built

We need someone who can help connect and automate the following platforms:

* Skool
*  ---------- 
* Zapier
* GoHighLevel / GHL
* Replit or a simple FAQ/resource page
* Text/email automation sequences

Project Overview

When a new client joins our paid Skool group, they should receive an automatic DM with clear onboarding instructions.

The message should tell them to:

* Introduce themselves
* Complete the onboarding form
* Save/text the support number
* Understand support hours and next steps

From there, the client should complete a  ----------  onboarding form.

 ----------  Onboarding Form

The form needs to collect client information and document uploads, including:

* First name
* Middle name
* Last name
* Email
* Cell phone
* Home address
* Date of birth
* Full SSN
* Mother’s maiden name
* Driver’s license upload
* Social Security card upload
* Recent utility bill upload
* MyFreeScoreNow login
* SmartCredit login

The form should also include embedded Loom/video instructions so clients understand exactly how to complete it without needing extra support.

Automation Flow Needed

Once the client submits the  ----------  form, we need:

1. A Zapier notification sent to a designated onboarding channel
2. Client information pushed into GoHighLevel
3. Client tagged as a credit repair client
4. Client added to the correct GHL opportunity pipeline/stage
5. Client automatically added to a 5-day text and email onboarding sequence
6. A next-steps video/page sent to the client after onboarding
7. A simple FAQ guide/resource page created to answer common credit repair questions and reduce support volume

5-Day Text/Email Sequence

We need a basic 5-day onboarding sequence built inside GHL that explains the most important things clients need to know after enrolling.

The sequence should help reduce confusion, set expectations, and tell clients what to do next.

Examples of topics:

* What happens after onboarding
* How long credit repair rounds take
* What documents are needed
* How to check progress
* When and how to contact support

Ideal Candidate

You should be experienced with:

* Zapier automations
*  ----------  forms and boards
* GoHighLevel workflows, tags, opportunities, pipelines, and SMS/email campaigns
* Skool community setup
* Basic client onboarding systems
* Building simple FAQ/resource pages
* Clean workflow documentation

Bonus if you have experience with:

* Credit repair businesses
* Funding businesses
* Client onboarding automations
* Low-ticket subscription offers
* CRM pipeline automation

Deliverables

By the end of the project, we need:

* Skool auto-DM onboarding message set up
*  ----------  onboarding form built
*  ----------  board organized for new client submissions
* Zapier automation connecting  ----------  to GHL
* Zapier notification to onboarding channel
* GHL tags, pipeline, and opportunity stage created
* 5-day SMS/email sequence built in GHL
* Next-steps video/page added into the onboarding flow
* FAQ guide/resource page created
* Full testing of the complete client journey
* Simple Loom walkthrough showing how the system works

Goal

The goal is to create a fully automated backend system where a client can purchase, join the community, complete onboarding, get added to the CRM, receive next steps, and start receiving automated education without us having to manually manage every step.

We want the process to feel clean, professional, and simple for the client.

To Apply

Please send:

1. Examples of similar automation systems you’ve built
2. Which platforms you have the most experience with
3. Your estimated timeline for this project
4. Your fixed price or hourly rate
5. Any questions you have about the workflow

We are looking for someone who can move fast, communicate clearly, and build this system correctly the first time.

title: AI Outbound Campaign Manager
description:
Are you the person who can run a system and make it better?

Do you like using AI tools to clean up messy work, catch tiny errors, and make a process run smoother the next time?

Formwork is hiring an AI Outbound Campaign Manager to run and improve our outbound campaign workflow using ChatGPT/Codex, Claude, Google Sheets, campaign tools, and our internal /run-campaign system.

This is not a basic assistant role. It is also not a strategy-only role. You will be the person operating the outbound machine every day: launching campaigns, checking data, spotting problems, and making the workflow better as you go.

If that sounds satisfying, keep reading.

About Formwork

Formwork helps commercial construction companies build stronger sales and business development systems.

A major part of that work is outbound: cold email, LinkedIn, phone lists, campaign setup, lead quality checks, and campaign QA.

We have built internal AI-assisted workflows that handle more of the heavy lifting now. What we need is a careful operator who can sit inside those workflows, run them consistently, and keep improving the system behind them.

What the work looks like

You will help run outbound campaigns from setup to launch while improving the workflow behind the scenes.

For each campaign, you would:

- Run the /run-campaign workflow inside Claude/Codex that does the following below
- Prepare and clean campaign lead lists
- Check names, companies, emails, LinkedIn URLs, titles, and dynamic fields
- Dedupe contacts and apply suppression/exclusion lists
- Upload leads into campaign tools
- Run preflight checks before a campaign goes live
- Monitor campaign health and flag issues quickly
- Document what broke, what worked, and what should be improved
- Turn repeated fixes into better prompts, checklists, SOPs, or small workflow improvements
- The work is systems-driven: you will run the process, notice friction, and help make the process sharper each week.

You'll crush this role if you...

- Are actively using ChatGPT, Codex, Claude, Cursor, Replit, or similar tools to build tools, apps, automations, or workflows you can name and explain
- Like checklists, clean data, workflow optimization, and careful execution
- Are comfortable working with CSVs and Google Sheets
- Have experience with lead generation, CRM work, cold email, sales ops, marketing ops, BPO ops, or data-heavy admin work
- Can learn new tools quickly and follow detailed workflows without hand-holding
- Notice small errors other people miss
- Communicate clearly in written English
- Are curious about AI workflows and want to get better at improving them
- Can record short Loom videos to explain what you did
- You do not need to know every tool before starting. You do need to be sharp, careful, and excited to learn.

This role is not for you if...

- You want only creative strategy work
- You only want to execute tasks and have no interest in improving workflows
- You rush through details
- You use AI tools without checking the output
- You wait silently when something breaks
- You need someone checking on you every hour

Role details

- Part-time role, 10-20 hours/week
- $800-$1,300 USD/month depending on experience
- 3-4 hours of overlap with US Eastern Time
- Start as soon as possible
- 90-day probationary period with structured check-ins

How to apply

Do not only apply through OnlineJobs.ph.

To be considered, complete this application form:

 ---------- 

The form takes about 10-12 minutes and includes a short personalized video. Applications without the video will not be reviewed.

We care more about how you think, how carefully you follow instructions, and how clearly you communicate than whether your resume is perfect.

Formwork. Building Beyond Today.

title: Airbnb Virtual Assistant / Customer Service Representative (Full-Time)
description:
Join a Growing Airbnb & Property Management Company

Hostier is a rapidly growing short-term rental (STR) and property management company. We manage a portfolio of Airbnb and vacation rental properties, delivering exceptional guest experiences while continuing to expand our operations.

We are looking for a dedicated Virtual Assistant who is seeking a long-term career opportunity and has proven experience within the short-term rental industry.

Essential Requirements

Please only apply if you meet the following criteria:

* Minimum 2 years of Short-Term Rental (STR) experience
* Excellent written and spoken English
* Experience communicating with guests via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Strong organisational and problem-solving skills
* Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks
* Reliable internet connection and suitable home office setup

Preferred Qualifications

The following would be highly advantageous:

* Previous experience working directly for Airbnb
* Experience using Airbnb,  ---------- , VRBO, Hostaway, Enso Connect, Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, or similar STR platforms
* Experience with Notion, Airtable, Slack, and Google Workspace
* Previous Virtual Assistant experience

Responsibilities

Your day-to-day duties may include:

* Managing guest communications via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Handling inbound and outbound calls
* Managing reservations and guest journeys
* Coordinating cleaning schedules and maintenance requests
* Managing guest issues and escalations
* Email management (maintaining a zero-inbox approach)
* Monitoring and responding to guest reviews and feedback
* Administrative support, reporting, and data management
* Communicating effectively with tea ---------- mbers via Slack

Working Hours

* 48.5 hours per week
* Rotating schedule within our operating hours of 09:00–22:00 GMT
* Weekend and public holiday coverage required

Who We’re Looking For

We are seeking someone who wants to build a long-term career with our company. We value reliability, professionalism, accountability, attention to detail, and a proactive attitude.

Application Instructions

Please also provide:

1. Your current CV/resume

3. The companies you have worked for and the length of time you worked with each company
4. The systems and software you have used (e.g. Airbnb, Hostaway, Enso Connect, Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, etc.)

Applications that do not include the phrase “HSTR” will not be considered.

We look forward to hearing from motivated candidates who are excited to contribute, grow, and build a long-term career with Hostier.

title: Online Business Project Coordinator
description:
For Health Coaching / Online Education Brand
Paid Mini Test ? Long-Term Part-Time Role

I’m looking for a highly organized online business project coordinator to help me organize the online coaching / online education side of my business.

This is a project-management-heavy role with marketing and funnel awareness. I am not looking for someone to only schedule posts or complete random admin tasks. I need someone who can help organize moving pieces, track projects, coordinate with my copywriter and clinic coordinator, manage documents, map funnels, and help me turn scattered ideas into clear, executable steps.

I run an established in-person acupuncture / Chinese medicine clinic, and I’m slowly building the online coaching / online education side of my business.

I’m also a full-time clinician, busy mom to a 2 year old, creative business owner/online content creator, and I have ADHD, so I often have a lot of ideas moving at once. At the same time, I’m building this in a realistic season of life. I am not looking for someone who pushes me to move faster just for the sake of moving fast. I need someone who can help me capture my ideas, organize them, prioritize what matters, and turn them into clear next steps without making everything feel urgent or overwhelming and I can take better action. My focus these days is everywhere, so keep me in check.

This role will start small because the online side of the business is still in the startup / buildout phase. I’m being thoughtful with budget and capacity, but I am looking for someone long-term. If the fit is strong and the online business grows, this role can grow in hours, responsibility, and pay over time.

About the Role

I’m one online creator / founder building the online education and coaching side of my business while still running my in-person clinic.

This is not a big corporate team or a fully built-out company with perfect systems. It’s me, my ideas, my content, my offers, and a small support team around me.

You’ll be working closely with me side by side, helping me organize the moving pieces, capture ideas, track next steps, and make sure things don’t fall through the cracks.

I need someone who is comfortable over-communicating in a helpful way. I would rather you ask, clarify, confirm, and update me than quietly disappear or assume.

A big part of this role is helping me take what’s in my head and turn it into something visible, organized, and actionable.

This could look like:

Listening to a Loom or voice note and turning it into a clean plan
Organizing scattered Google Docs across different accounts
Finding duplicate files and recommending the master version
Creating project trackers
Coordinating with my copywriter
Touching base with my clinic coordinator when needed
Checking what still needs my decision
Reminding me what’s pending
Helping me prioritize what actually matters next
About the Stage of the Business

This role is for someone who is comfortable joining during the startup / buildout phase of the online side of my business.

My in-person clinic is established, but the online coaching / online education side is still being built. That means the systems are not perfect yet. Some documents live in different Google Drives. Some projects are still in idea form. Some funnels are still being mapped. My signature system and coaching container are still being organized.

That is part of why I’m hiring.

I need someone who is not intimidated by a business that is still taking shape. I need someone who can help bring structure, clarity, and follow-through while things are being built.

This is not a polished corporate role where everything is already systemized. This is a chance to help build the operating system of a growing online health coaching / education brand from the ground up.

I already have support from a copywriter and clinic coordinator, and this role will involve helping coordinate between people, projects, tools, documents, and ideas.

The role will start lean, around 5–10 hours per week, because I am being thoughtful with budget and capacity. But I am looking for someone long-term. If the fit is strong and the online business grows, there is room for this role to grow in hours, responsibility, and pay over time.

Who I’m Looking For

You are not just a task-taker.

You are a systems thinker, strong communicator, and organized follow-through person. You can take a messy brain dump and turn it into a clean plan, project tracker, funnel map, folder system, or task list.

You are proactive. You notice what’s missing. You follow up without being asked. You over-communicate in a helpful way. You can manage up and keep me updated without me having to chase you.

I need someone who can help organize the chaos without killing the creativity.

Personality-wise, I’m looking for someone warm, steady, intuitive, organized, and marketing-minded. Someone who can bring calm to the chaos, help me see the big picture, and keep things moving without adding pressure. Tell me when things are time sensitive and what I need to get ahead of. I want to be able to turn off from work at times to be a present mom because you are handling it. 

Experience with online health coaches, online educators, wellness brands, course creators, or service-based online businesses is strongly preferred.

This Role Is For Someone Who Can Help With
Project Management & Online Operations

You will help organize the online side of the business so projects do not live only in my brain.

This may include:

Turning messy ideas, Loom videos, voice notes, and brain dumps into organized plans
Creating and updating project trackers
Mapping out what exists, what is missing, and what needs attention
Keeping projects moving across the team
Tracking deadlines, owners, links, assets, and next steps
Following up with me and other tea ---------- mbers when something is pending
Creating simple SOPs and repeatable workflows
Helping me see the big picture so I can execute
Giving me simple updates on what is done, what is missing, and what needs my decision
Google Drive / Document Organization

A big part of this role will be helping me organize documents across multiple Google Drives and email accounts.

Some of my materials currently live in my personal email, some in my social media / online business email, and some in my clinic email. I also have duplicate documents because I’m building this business while in a mentorship and while running my clinic.

I need someone who can help audit where everything lives, identify duplicates, recommend the correct “source of truth,” organize files into clean folders, and create a system so future documents are easier to find.

This may include:

Auditing Google Drive folders across multiple accounts
Identifying duplicate documents
Creating a master document inventory
Recommending which version should become the master file
Creating a clean folder structure for the online coaching / education business
Separating clinic, social media, and coaching offer materials
Creating archive folders instead of deleting files
Linking important documents into a master tracker
Coordinating with my copywriter to make sure copy docs, emails, landing pages, and launch assets are saved and tracked correctly
Helping organize my signature system / coaching framework so I can see the big picture and finish building it

Important: I do not want files deleted without approval. I prefer archiving and reviewing before anything is removed.

Signature System / Coaching Container Organization

I’m still building out my signature system, coaching container, and step-by-step process for the online side of the business.

I need help organizing my ideas into a visible structure.

This may include helping me organize:

Core promise
Ideal client
Signature method / framework
Step-by-step process
Client journey
Program modules
Worksheets and homework
Client resources

Email and sales copy
Questions that need my decision

You do not need to create my intellectual property for me. I need someone who can help me organize my thoughts, documents, and ideas so I can actually finish building the offer.

Marketing & Funnel Systems

You should understand online health coaching, online education, funnels, lead magnets, emails, and content flow.

You will help with:

Mapping funnels and customer journeys
Checking that social media CTAs connect to the right ManyChat flow, lead magnet, email sequence, and offer
Reviewing email sequences to make sure everything is complete and ready to go
Making sure emails are uploaded correctly in Kit / ConvertKit
Eventually helping with GoHighLevel / GHL setup and organization once I move into that platform
Checking subject lines, preview text, links, buttons, tags, triggers, automations, and delivery flows
Flagging missing links, broken steps, unclear CTAs, or gaps in the customer journey
Updating me with what is done, what is missing, what needs my decision, and what has already been followed up on

My copywriter may create the emails and landing page copy, but you will help make sure everything is complete, organized, uploaded, connected, tested, and ready to go.

I need someone who can tell me:

“Here’s what’s done. Here’s what’s missing. Here’s what needs your decision. Here’s what I already followed up on.”

Social Media Operations

You will not be responsible for being the entire creative strategist, but you may help organize and manage the backend of social content.

This may include:

Organizing content ideas, captions, hooks, video files, B-roll, thumbnails, and assets
Keeping the content calendar updated
Tracking what has been filmed, edited, posted, scheduled, or repurposed
Making sure social captions and CTAs match the correct link, keyword, lead magnet, or funnel
Coordinating with my copywriter, video editor, designer, or other tea ---------- mbers
Helping ensure content connects to email list growth, lead magnets, and offers
Helping organize social media workflows so content does not get lost
Future Client Delivery Systems

I am building a future online coaching offer, so I also need help organizing the backend for future coaching clients.

This may include:

Creating organized Google Drive / Dropbox folder structures for future coaching clients
Building a repeatable client folder template
Creating systems for intake forms, assessments, session notes, action plans, client resources, and progress tracking
Organizing client-facing vs. internal folders
Creating file naming conventions
Creating a master client tracker
Helping document the client onboarding and delivery process

The goal is to make sure future coaching clients have a clean, professional backend experience and that nothing gets lost.

Team Coordination

You will coordinate with:

My copywriter, who is very organized and owns much of the copy / messaging flow
My clinic coordinator, who supports the in-person clinic side
Other contractors such as editors, designers, or VAs as needed

You should be comfortable checking in, clarifying next steps, documenting decisions, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks.

Must-Have Experience

Please only apply if you have experience with:

ManyChat
Kit / ConvertKit
GoHighLevel / GHL experience, even though I do not currently have GHL yet
Online health coaching, online education, course creators, wellness brands, or service-based online businesses
Google Drive / Google Docs organization
Project management or operations coordination

You do not need to be a world-class expert in every tool, but you must understand how funnels, email systems, automations, tags, links, lead magnets, and customer journeys connect.

Helpful Experience

Experience with any of the following is a plus:

Zapier
 ---------- 
Payhip
Kajabi, ThriveCart, or membership platforms
Squarespace or WordPress
Notion, ClickUp, Trello, Asana, or Google Sheets
Google Drive / Dropbox
Canva
Metricool or social scheduling tools
Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, YouTube Shorts
Loom
AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, or similar
You’re a Great Fit If
You are hyper-detail-oriented
You are proactive, not reactive
You communicate clearly and consistently
You can manage up and follow up with me when needed
You are comfortable with a creative, ADHD business owner
You can take messy information and organize it
You notice missing pieces before I do
You understand funnels, lead generation, and online education
You are comfortable documenting systems
You can work independently after receiving context
You are looking for a long-term role that can grow over time
You can help create calm and clarity, not more pressure
You enjoy helping founders organize the messy middle
This Is Not a Fit If
You need constant direction to stay on task
You disappear when things get quiet
You say yes to everything but do not follow through
You wait for every tiny instruction before taking action
You are uncomfortable working with imperfect information
You struggle to communicate clearly
You only want simple admin tasks
You have no experience with ManyChat, Kit / ConvertKit, or GHL
You are not comfortable coordinating with other tea ---------- mbers
You need everything to already be perfectly organized before you can help
You only want to join a business where all systems are already fully built and documented
Paid Mini Test

This role starts with a small paid mini test.

Pay: $20 fixed
Deadline: 24 hours after receiving materials
Format: Google Doc preferred
Logins: No logins required for the test

I will send you a short Loom video and/or short business overview where I explain the current state of my online business, what I’m building, and what feels messy.

Please watch the Loom and create one simple Google Doc with:

A clean summary of what you understand
The main projects / moving pieces you heard
A simple project tracker or priority list
Anything that sounds missing, unclear, duplicated, disconnected, or at risk of being dropped
The questions you would ask me next
A sample weekly update you would send me if you were managing this ongoing

I do not need a complicated system. I want to see how you think, organize, communicate, and bring clarity to messy information.

Part of this test is seeing how well you can work with imperfect information, organize the chaos, and tell me what needs to happen next.

Ongoing Role & Pay

If the paid mini test goes well, this can grow into an ongoing part-time role.

Starting structure:

5–10 hours/week to start
$10–$12/hr depending on experience
Potential to grow to 10–20 hours/week
Potential rate increase as ownership grows

This role will start small because I am still figuring out exactly what support I need. But if the person is a strong fit, organized, proactive, and truly helps me move forward with less mental overwhelm, I would love for this to become a long-term role.

To Apply

Put the word FLOWER in your subject line. If it’s missing, your application will not be reviewed.

Please send a 2–3 minute talking head video using Loom or a similar tool. Include your Loom link directly in your application message. 

In your video, please cover as much as you can:

Who you are and something personal about you
Your experience with ManyChat, Kit / ConvertKit, and GHL
Your experience with online health coaching, online educators, course creators, or wellness brands
Your experience organizing Google Drive / Google Docs, project trackers, or messy business systems
How you use AI in your work and where you draw the line
Why this role interests you
An example of a time you took messy information and turned it into an organized system, tracker, funnel map, folder structure, or plan
What you love to do for fun!
Your hourly rate and weekly availability

Applications without a video will not be considered.

title: Client Project Coordinator
description:
We are seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented individual to join our growing team as a Client Project Coordinator.

Responsibilities include:

• Assisting with the coordination of custom software, CRM, automation, and website projects for our clients.
• Attending client and internal meetings and documenting requirements, decisions, action items, open questions, and next steps.
• Converting client requests, meeting discussions, and emails into organized project documentation and Jira tasks.
• Tracking project progress and following up with tea ---------- mbers regarding open items, blockers, and deadlines.
• Preparing project status updates and summaries for management.
• Helping maintain project documentation, specifications, implementation plans, and project priorities.
• Supporting communication and coordination between Operations, Engineering, and client-facing staff.

This role is not focused on building software, websites, CRM systems, or automations directly. Instead, the Client Project Coordinator will work closely with the company owner to help organize and coordinate a growing portfolio of client projects. Over time, the ideal candidate will develop a strong understanding of each project’s goals, priorities, and requirements so they can help identify missing information, connect related discussions, and ensure projects continue moving forward.

The ideal candidate will be highly organized, proactive, detail-oriented, and comfortable managing many moving pieces simultaneously. Strong written and spoken English communication skills are required. Experience coordinating projects is preferred. Experience working in the software, website, CRM, automation, consulting, or technology industries is also preferred, as it will help the candidate understand client requests and communicate effectively with technical tea ---------- mbers.

This is a full-time position. Because this role works closely with our clients and tea ---------- mbers in the United States, candidates must be willing and able to work primarily during United States Eastern Time business hours.

Immediately upon hiring, we honor most Filipino holidays except for All Saints and All Souls Day. After your 6-month probationary period, you will also qualify for vacation leaves, sick leaves, and 13th month.

Founded in 2006, Organize My People is a technology implementation and consulting firm focused on helping organizations utilize CRM systems, automation tools, custom software, and websites to improve their operations. Our clients include nonprofit organizations, educational institutions, event organizations, faith-based institutions, property management companies, healthcare offices, and other businesses throughout the United States, Canada, and Australia.

title: Growth Marketer — Acquisition (B2B / Manufacturing) - Content + Strategy Driven B2B Growth
description:
Read this first:

This post is for one specific person: a content-first growth marketer with genuine marketing intelligence, real B2B experience, and a serious work ethic. We are hiring one person. If that sounds like you, keep reading. 

Who we are:

Feight AI builds custom AI solutions for manufacturing companies in Central Europe doing over €1M in annual revenue. We design and build automation specifically for how each client actually runs their operations.

Our vision is simple. We want to be one of the top names companies in our region think of when they need serious AI work done for their business.

Where we are right now: the technical side is solid, the founder closes deals one to one, and the product works. What's missing is marketing. It's at zero. No content, no funnel, no paid presence.

That's the job. A blank canvas. The person we hire gets to define what good marketing at Feight AI looks like from scratch.

We're looking for the person who runs our entire acquisition function on the ground. What we need now is someone to take over content, channels and paid ads. Not as an assistant. As the person who owns this side of the business day to day.

What matters most:

Two things will d ----------  whether this works.

The first is content creation. Our organic growth on Meta, LinkedIn, YouTube and the blog depends on it, and our paid ads only work if the creative is strong. So we need a real creator. Someone who writes, edits, scripts, and ships content consistently and well.

The second is marketing intelligence. By that we mean the ability to look at the data and figure out what's actually going on. If a campaign is dying, you should be able to tell whether it's an audience problem, a message problem, an offer problem, or a channel problem, and what to try next. We give you the direction. You make it real on the ground and adjust as you go.

If both of these aren't your strong suit, this won't work.

What you'll be doing

Day to day you'll handle:

Posting and managing content across Meta (FB + IG), LinkedIn, YouTube and our blog.
Building funnels: landing pages, lead magnets, email sequences.
Running paid ads (Meta/LinekdIn/Google): creative, audiences, budgets, testing, scaling.
Using AI tools to move faster than people who do this work manually.

You don't have to be a world expert at every one of these. Competent and resourceful is fine.

What we are not asking for:

No cold outreach. No warm outreach. We are not interested in that channel at the  ---------- nt. The focus is content, funnels and ads.

Who we are hiring:

We are looking for someone who:

Creates content as their main skill. Not someone who "also writes" or manages other writers. You make the stuff yourself.
Can look at a week of data and tell us in five minutes what's working, what isn't, and what they'd change.
Is organized. You know your numbers, you report on a regular rhythm without being chased, and you come to us with proposals, not just observations.
Has actually driven B2B pipeline for international clients before. Real numbers, real results. Not just "campaigns you were part of."
Has B2B acquisition experience specifically. Not general agency work, not e-commerce, not social media management.
Has a strong work ethic. If you say you'll ship something, it ships. On time. At the quality you promised.
Actually likes this work. Hooks, funnels, ads, content. This is the kind of work you like to do.
Wants to earn more through results, not through hours. The base is steady. The bonus is where it gets interesting.
Communicates well, professionally, without us having to chase replies.
Doesn't need micromanaging. We give direction and context. You give back a working engine.
Can start moving fast. You don't need three months to figure out the basics.

This probably isn't for you if:

Content creation isn't your strongest skill.
You can execute a plan but you can't tell why something isn't working when the numbers go sideways.
You've never personally owned B2B acquisition outcomes.
You need long onboarding to figure out the basics.
Your background is general agency, e-commerce or social media management.
You're mainly motivated by hourly pay, not by performance.

What we offer

$1,500/month base, paid reliably and on time.
A performance bonus tied to qualified leads and closed revenue. If your work drives sales, the bonus has no upper limit. This role exists for someone who wants the upside.
Room to grow. As Feight scales, this seat scales with it. If things go well, today's Growth Lead becomes tomorrow's Head of Growth with a team underneath.
Fair, direct treatment. Clear expectations, no politics, predictable payments. We treat people running important parts of our business like partners.
Remote work with autonomy. Work on your own rhythm as long as the work ships and the numbers move.

To apply:

Start your message with the word PLAYBOOK on the first line, so we know you read the post. Anything without it gets skipped.

Then send us:

A short video, 1 to 2 minutes, where you introduce yourself. Who you are, what you've done, and why this role caught your attention.
Three samples of content you've personally created. At least one of them should be video.
Two or three B2B results you've personally driven. Numbers, links, before and after. The harder the proof, the better.
Your full AI stack for marketing work. What tools you actually use for content, funnels, design, copy, ads, research, anything else. We want to see the whole setup, not just one tool.

Generic copy-paste applications get skipped. We would rather wait than fill the seat with the wrong person.

Next steps:

Here's what happens after you send your application:

We'll get back to you within 5 business days.
If your application makes it through the first filter, we'll send you a short trial task. It should take less than an hour.
The top candidates from that round move on to an interview with the founder.

All the best,

FEIGHT AI Team.

title: CRO Expert / Conversion Rate Optimization Specialist
description:
We are a U.S.-based performance growth partner working with home service companies. We scale our clients through paid acquisition, sales optimization, and full-funnel growth systems. We bring e-commerce-level marketing rigor into an industry that has been operating a decade behind.

We're hiring a CRO Expert to own conversion rate optimization across our client portfolio. Your job is to find every drop-off point in the funnel — from ad click to booked appointment — and systematically test, optimize, and lift conversion at every stage.

You'll be working on landing pages, lead forms, quiz funnels, booking flows, and the entire user journey from cold traffic to scheduled in-home consultation. Every percentage point you lift in conversion translates directly to client revenue, which is exactly the kind of impact we measure and reward.

**WHAT YOU'LL DO:**

- Audit landing pages, lead forms, quiz funnels, and booking flows across all client accounts
- Identify drop-off points using heatmaps, session recordings, and analytics (Hotjar, Microsoft Clarity, GA4, etc.)
- Build a structured CRO testing roadmap with clear hypotheses, success metrics, and prioritization
- Design and launch A/B tests on headlines, hooks, offers, form layouts, button placement, social proof, and page structure
- Optimize lead forms — field count, qualification questions, multi-step vs. single-step, mobile vs. desktop
- Improve quiz funnels and lead magnets for higher opt-in and qualification rates
- Optimize Thank You pages, confirmation flows, and post-booking experience
- Work directly with copywriters, designers, and developers to ship test variants quickly
- Track and report on conversion rates at every stage: ad click ? landing page ? lead ? booked appointment ? confirmed appointment
- Maintain a CRO testing log — every test documented with hypothesis, result, and learning
- Recommend funnel architecture improvements based on user behavior data

**WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:**

- 3+ years of hands-on CRO experience for lead-gen or e-commerce funnels
- Proven track record lifting conversion rates — you can point to specific tests, before/after numbers, and revenue impact
- Deep knowledge of landing page builders: Unbounce, Instapage, Leadpages, GoHighLevel, Funnelytics, Webflow, or similar
- Fluent in CRO analysis tools: Hotjar, Microsoft Clarity, GA4, Google Tag Manager, Mixpanel
- Strong understanding of A/B testing methodology, statistical significance, and test prioritization frameworks (ICE, PIE, etc.)
- Sharp eye for UX, copy, and design — you understand why pages convert, not just what to test
- Comfortable working with copywriters and designers to ship variants — you give clear, decisive creative feedback
- Strong written and spoken English — you'll be communicating directly with U.S. tea ---------- mbers
- Analytical and methodical — you think in conversion rates, statistical confidence, and incremental lift
- Self-starter who can operate with full autonomy
- Reliable internet, quiet workspace, and ability to overlap with U.S. business hours
- No ego. Humble, hungry, and willing to dig into the messy work of testing and iteration

**BONUS POINTS:**

- Experience with home services, local services, or lead-gen funnels specifically
- Experience optimizing quiz funnels ( ---------- , Outgrow, Typeform,  ---------- )
- Familiarity with GoHighLevel funnel building and automation
- Basic HTML, CSS, or no-code dev skills for rapid landing page edits
- Experience working with paid media teams (Meta, Google, TikTok) on creative-to-landing-page alignment
- Background in DTC or e-commerce CRO (we bring e-com principles into home services)
- Experience with multivariate testing, not just basic A/B
- Knowledge of mobile-first design and conversion best practices

**CULTURE:**

This is a relentless execution culture. We move fast, we test fast, we ship fast. We measure conversion lifts in basis points and revenue, not opinions. If you want a chill role where you write strategy docs that never get implemented, this isn't for you. If you want to ship tests every week and watch the numbers move, apply.

**HOW TO APPLY:**

Send your application with:

1. A 60-second video introducing yourself in English — pitch us on why we should hire you
2. Two specific CRO case studies — landing page or funnel, what you tested, before/after conversion rates, revenue impact
3. Screenshots of CRO tools you've used (Hotjar dashboards, A/B test results, GA4 reports — blur sensitive info if needed)
4. Links to landing pages or funnels you've personally built or optimized
5. Why you want this specific role

Applications without a video and case studies will not be considered.

---

**Wage/Salary:**
$1,200 – $2,500 USD/month + performance bonuses tied to conversion lift and client results

title: Shopify Store Operator — AI-Driven Store (Systems-Minded, Full-Time)
description:
About us
We're a USA-based premium contemporary home décor brand — sculptural furniture, lighting, mirrors, and wall art. Our product pages read like a design gallery, not a discount store. We're looking for one sharp, detail-obsessed person to help us run and grow the store. You'll work US-friendly hours; we'll confirm the exact window.

What you'll do
• Operate our AI-driven store (the core). We run a custom dashboard, an AI agent system, and automation that does a lot of the work. Your job is to understand how it all fits together and run the machine — the right tool, the right step, in the right order.
• Run the AI listing pipeline. Our AI generates the product listings; you operate the workflow, push products live, and keep them flowing as new products arrive. You operate it — you don't write the copy.
• Quality-check for accuracy. Catch the factual errors that matter — wrong dimensions, wrong price, wrong product — before anything goes live.
• Own the store day to day. Handle whatever the Shopify store and storefront need; you're the go-to operator for "the store."
• Troubleshoot. When something looks off (wrong stock on the site, a product not showing, automation acting up), figure out where the problem is and drive the fix or flag it clearly.

This is NOT a copywriting job (the AI writes), NOT customer support, and NOT a basic listings job. It's a run-the-system role for someone who likes understanding how things work.

Who you are
• You're systems-minded and tech-comfortable. You enjoy learning how a complex setup works and can reason about how the pieces connect. You don't need to code — you need to "get" how things run and operate inside them. This is the most important thing.
• You use AI tools (ChatGPT / Claude) daily, know how to drive them to a good result, and can tell when the output is wrong.
• You're comfortable in Shopify (products, variants, collections, the draft-to-active flow).
• You're a troubleshooter — when something breaks, you trace it instead of guessing.
• You're adaptable — happy owning "the whole store," not just ticking off a fixed task list.
• You're meticulous about accuracy. A wrong dimension or price drives you crazy.
• Your written English is strong enough to communicate clearly. (You won't be writing product copy — the AI does that — so polished marketing writing is not required.)

What we offer
• Competitive pay with real raises as you grow.
• Full-time, long-term, stable work with one company (not juggling clients).
• 13th-month pay / year-end bonus plus performance bonuses.
• A founder who values good work and gives clear direction.

HOW TO APPLY (please read — applications that skip a step won't be reviewed)
1. Start your message with the word ATELIER so we know you actually read this post.
2. Tell us: in 2–3 sentences, describe a system, tool, or automated workflow you learned and operated — and how you used an AI tool (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) in your work.
3. Quick reasoning question. Imagine a store set up like this: a dashboard pushes inventory to Shopify; an AI tool drafts the product listings; nothing goes live until a person approves it. A customer reports that a product shows the wrong stock number on the website. In a few sentences: how would you figure out where the problem is? (We're not looking for a "correct" answer — we want to see how you think through a system.)

We read every application that follows these three steps. We're excited to meet you.

title: ExperienExperienced GoHighLevel Tech VA (Funnel & Automation Specialist)
description:
This role is 15 hours/week ($360–$600/month) Hourly rate: $6–$10/hr depending on experience.

We have a virtual event launching imminently, and we need an experienced Tech Virtual Assistant to handle all the behind-the-scenes tech smoothly. 
If you’re a GoHighLevel wizard who loves building funnels and automations, keep reading! 

Key Responsibilities: (First 6-10 weeks focus)
- Build and launch a complete Summit Funnel in GoHighLevel: Setup multiple pages (registration, upsell checkout, thank-you) on our custom domain with mobile-responsive design. Embed videos that autoplay with subtitles and sound on click. Integrate our payment gateway for sales.
- Email Marketing & Automation: Create automated email sequences for registrants, upsell buyers, and general broadcasts. Segment the audience with tags for targeted follow-ups. Ensure SMTP and domains are correctly configured for optimal deliverability.
- Speaker Referral Tracking: Duplicate the main funnel pages for 21-25 guest speakers with unique URLs. Use Canva to personalize promo graphics for each speaker, and build a simple Google Sites “promo center” where speakers download their assets and links.
- Launch Monitoring: During the 10-day promo period (beginning of September), track daily opt-in stats per speaker (clicks vs sign-ups) and report these in a Google Sheet update. Coordinate with us if any speaker’s numbers are low, etc.
- Live Event Support: Schedule and send reminder emails/texts for our live sessions (using GoHighLevel workflows). 

- Set up replay pages as needed.

Must-Have Skills & Experience:
- GoHighLevel Expertise: Minimum 1-2 years hands-on experience in GoHighLevel. You have built marketing funnels and automation workflows end-to-end. Please be prepared to discuss specific funnels or campaigns you’ve built.
- Funnel & Marketing Tech: Proficient in connecting domains, building landing pages, integrating checkout forms/payments, setting up tagging & segmentation, and troubleshooting funnel metrics (opt-in rates, etc.).
- Email Marketing: Experience with crafting and scheduling email campaigns, setting up SMTP providers, and managing email lists. Know how to avoid spam folders (DKIM/SPF settings, etc.).
- Video Editing Basics: Ability to use tools like Descript, or similar to add captions, trim videos, and export for web upload. (Expert video editing not required, but you should be able to do quick basic edits for our content.)
- Graphic Design: Comfortable with Canva for simple graphic tasks (e.g., editing speaker promo images). Bonus if you have a good eye for layout when assembling pages.
- Tech Troubleshooting: You are the kind of person who googles error messages and figures things out. Whether it’s a DNS setting or a Zapier integration, you don’t give up easily when a tech issue arises.

Communication & Work Habits: 
- Fluent English (written and spoken). You must be reliable in regular check-ins we expect a brief report each workday on progress or roadblocks. 
The ideal candidate is proactive, asks questions when unsure, and takes ownership of her/his role. We do not micromanage, so we need someone who can manage their tasks and deliver on deadlines.

Compensation: 
- Starting at $6-10 per hour, depending on experience. We pay via bi-weekly. 
- This role starts at 10-15hours/week, but will require maybe more hours during launch weeks (September 12-October, for example). 

To Apply: 
- Please send your resume and a brief cover letter to  ----------  your subject line with “GHL TechVA Application – [Your Name]”. 
- In your cover letter, confirm you can work according to Central European Time zone stated and send us a link to your most complex GoHighLevel (or funnel) project – and what the outcome was. If you have any work samples (screenshots or links to funnels, emailsequences, etc.), please include them! 

We need someone to start as soon as possible, so please apply only if you are ready to begin immediately.

title: Venue Analytics SaaS Platform Development
description:
About the Product

We are building a venue analytics SaaS platform for venue managers. It allows managers to upload floor performance data and receive formatted analytics reports. The product has a highly specific visual identity built around a dark theme, serif/monospace typography pairing, and a precise colour and data hierarchy system.

What You'll Be Building

This is a focused, design-led data product. The core work involves:

Building and refining a multi-tab React dashboard with a custom design system (no off-the-shelf UI kits)
File upload and data ingestion pipelines
Backend APIs in Python/FastAPI with PostgreSQL for data storage and processing
Report generation and formatting output for venue managers
Auth flows, user settings, and account management

What We're Looking For

Frontend craft is the priority. You should be able to look at a detailed design spec and implement it with precision, including pixel-accurate layouts, custom typography, and a data visualisation layer that actually looks considered. Functional but ugly is not good enough for this product.

Backend competence is required but does not need to be your primary strength. You should be comfortable with APIs, data modelling, and file processing without needing to be a systems engineer.
Required

React (strong, with evidence of polished dashboard or data product work)
TypeScript
Python / FastAPI
PostgreSQL
Custom CSS / Tailwind with the ability to deviate from defaults intentionally
Git / GitHub

Helpful

Experience with file upload and parsing pipelines (CSV, Excel)
Chart or data visualisation libraries (Recharts, D3, or similar)
Report generation (PDF output)
Stripe billing
Render or similar hosting

Who This Suits

A developer who takes visual quality seriously alongside technical correctness. You should be able to show at least one dashboard or data product you have built that you are genuinely proud of aesthetically, not just one that worked.
Working Arrangement

Remote, Philippines-based preferred
Full-time
Must overlap some hours with Australia/AEST
Long-term engagement
Please include your expected monthly or hourly rate

How to Apply
Send through your CV or profile, GitHub or code examples, and at least one example of a dashboard or data-heavy frontend you have built. A short note on which parts of this role you are strongest in is appreciated.

title: Back-End Developer - Infrastructure & Serverless (Cloudflare, Docker, Serverless)
description:
About the Role
We are a small founder-led team building an AI-powered business platform that brings CRM, content generation, lead generation, automation, and messaging into one connected system. We are hiring a back-end developer who lives in the infrastructure layer.

Our stack runs on platforms like Cloudflare (Workers, Wrangler, KV, R2, Durable Objects), Docker, DigitalOcean, and PostgreSQL. You will own how the platform runs on that stack — the serverless workers, the containers, the deployment pipeline, and the monitoring that keeps everything healthy at scale. You will also play a central role in migrating us off our current platform onto this target stack.

This is not a feature-building role. We want someone who genuinely enjoys deployments, monitoring, and the work that keeps a production system clean and fast. You will work directly under our lead engineer, alongside a small back-end team that respects deep work and ships often.

Core Skills
Strong TypeScript or Node.js
Real production experience with Cloudflare Workers (or comparable serverless: AWS Lambda, Vercel Edge, etc.)
Comfortable with Linux, Docker, and Docker Compose
Logging, monitoring, and on-call response habits
PostgreSQL working knowledge
Solid Git and code review habits
Uses AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) to ship faster

Bonus Points - share with us any experience you have with: 
Cloudflare Wrangler, KV, R2, Durable Objects experience specifically
Real production migration experience between platforms
Go (or willingness to learn)
Portainer or Kubernetes
SaaS or multi-tenant background

How to Apply
Send us the following:
1. Your full name and location
2. Years of back-end development experience
3. Your top 3-5 strongest tools or platforms (be specific — Cloudflare Workers, Docker, AWS Lambda, PostgreSQL, etc.)
4. Link to your GitHub, portfolio, or any production work you can show
5. Hours per week you can commit, and your timezone
6. Your desired hourly rate
7. In 2-3 sentences: the most challenging production deployment, migration, or infrastructure problem you have solved. What broke, what you did, and what the outcome was.
8. In 1-2 sentences: which excites you more — designing a clean deployment pipeline from scratch, or hunting down why a production system is slow? Why?

Applications missing the final two answers will not be reviewed.

Thanks - we are building and growing fast and looking for real workers to help join an fun, integrity driven team to help us scale!

title: Freight forwarding
description:
Ocean Export Booking Coordinator (Freight Forwarding / NVOCC Operations)

Company: US Global Shipping

Location: Remote (Philippines)

Job Type: Full-Time

Working Hours: U.S. Eastern Time (North Carolina, USA)

About Us

US Global Shipping is a U.S.-based international freight forwarding and NVOCC company specializing in ocean exports, vehicle exports, containerized cargo, auto parts, hazardous cargo, and logistics services worldwide.

We are looking for an experienced Ocean Export Booking Coordinator to join our team and take ownership of carrier bookings, shipment coordination, and communication with shipping lines, warehouses, trucking companies, customers, and our documentation team.

Minimum Requirements

* Minimum 3 years of experience in Freight Forwarding or NVOCC Operations
* Experience handling Ocean Export Shipments
* Strong knowledge of container shipping operations

title: Real Estate VA - Marketing, Lead Generation, & Appointment Setting
description:
RAAH Real Estate is seeking a proactive, results-driven Virtual Assistant to support our marketing, lead generation, database management, and client engagement efforts. This position is ideal for someone who understands the real estate sales process and enjoys creating systems that generate appointments, nurture relationships, and support business growth.
The ideal candidate is highly organized, tech-savvy, self-motivated, and focused on measurable outcomes. This role is not simply administrative—it is a revenue-support position directly contributing to lead generation, conversion, and client experience.

Key Responsibilities
Lead Generation & Nurturing
Manage and nurture incoming leads through CRM systems.
Conduct follow-up via phone, text, email, and social media messaging.
Schedule appointments for agents with qualified buyers, sellers, and investors.
Reactivate dormant leads within the database.
Maintain accurate lead records and tracking.
Marketing & Content Management
Create and schedule social media content.
Design marketing materials using Canva or similar platforms.
Assist with email marketing campaigns and newsletters.
Support listing marketing efforts.
Monitor and report on marketing campaign performance.
CRM & Database Management
Maintain data integrity within CRM systems.
Create and manage drip campaigns, workflows, and automations.
Segment contacts and manage tagging systems.
Generate reports on lead activity and conversion metrics.
Administrative Support
Assist with transaction-related communication when needed.
Maintain project timelines and task management systems.
Coordinate marketing calendars and campaign schedules.
Identify opportunities to improve efficiency and workflow processes.

Skills (What You Can Do)
Required
Strong written and verbal English communication.
CRM management experience.
Social media marketing experience.
Canva or graphic design experience.
Lead follow-up and appointment-setting experience.

Email marketing proficiency.
Strong organizational and time-management skills.
Ability to work independently and prioritize tasks.

Preferred
Real estate industry experience.
Experience with Follow Up Boss, kvCORE, Lofty, BoomTown, or similar CRM systems.

Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and Youtube advertising experience.
Basic video editing skills.
Experience with Google Workspace and Microsoft Office.

Knowledge (What You Know)
Required
Understanding of lead generation and customer relationship management.
Knowledge of social media platforms and best practices.
Understanding of marketing funnels and lead nurturing processes.
Familiarity with business communication standards.
Preferred
Understanding of residential real estate transactions.
Knowledge of real estate marketing strategies.
Familiarity with MLS systems and real estate terminology.
Understanding of buyer and seller lead conversion processes.

Abilities (How You Perform)
The successful candidate must demonstrate the ability to:
Work independently with minimal supervision.
Communicate professionally with clients and prospects.
Manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Meet deadlines consistently.
Learn new software and systems quickly.
Analyze data and identify opportunities for improvement.
Maintain confidentiality and professionalism.
Take initiative and proactively solve problems.
Consistently follow established processes while improving them when appropriate.
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)
Success in this role will be measured by:
Leads contacted
Response rates
Appointments scheduled
Database growth and cleanliness

Email campaign performance
Social media engagement
Lead conversion metrics
Task completion and timeliness

Compensation
Compensation will be based on experience and qualifications and may include performance-based incentives tied to appointments set, qualified leads generated, and business growth metrics.

About RAAH Real Estate
At RAAH Real Estate, we believe real estate is about serving people well. "Raah" means to tend, guide, and care for others. We are committed to delivering exceptional client experiences through integrity, expertise, innovation, and genuine relationships. We are seeking team members who share these values and are passionate about helping our clients and business thrive.

title: Real Estate Media Photo QC, Delivery, and Client Outreach
description:
Are you someone who takes pride in doing detail-oriented work and enjoys having a clear routine with room to handle new things as they come up? Do you like knowing that your work has a direct impact on a real business and its clients? If so, we'd love to have you on our team!

About Us: Avionne Photography is a real estate photography and media business based in Northern New Jersey. We shoot properties for real estate agents and deliver high-quality photos, drone aerials, videos, floor plans, and 3D tours. We run a tight, efficient operation and we're looking for a Virtual Assistant who can help us keep deliveries fast, clients happy, and our pipeline growing.

What You'll Be Doing:
• Photo QC & Delivery: Review completed photos from our editors each morning, do light cleanup in Lightroom, build the property website in our delivery platform, and send finished media to agents on time.
• Retouch Requests: Handle photo revision requests from agents throughout the day, routing and resolving them quickly so nothing sits.
• Client Outreach: During downtime between editing jobs, use our Instagra ---------- thod to send outbound messages and help us connect with potential new clients.
• Booking & Scheduling: Use our booking system to add new agents, schedule shoots, and keep client records accurate.

What We're Looking For:
• Willingness to Work in Eastern Time (9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET): Our clients are based in the New York metro area, so we need someone who's available and responsive during those hours.
• A Sharp Eye for Detail: Photo quality control is the core of this role. You need to be someone who notices things and takes the time to get it right.
• Reliability & Follow-Through: Our agents count on fast turnaround. We need someone who treats every delivery like it matters — because it does.
• Flexibility & Adaptability: The morning routine is consistent, but the rest of the day can shift. We need someone who can prioritize on the fly and handle what comes up.
• Your Own Adobe Lightroom & Photoshop Licenses: This role requires active subscriptions to both applications.

Why You'll Love Working with Us:
• Clear Systems:We use one on one training and documented SOPs for everything, so you'll never be guessing what to do or how to do it.
• Supportive Team: We’re here to help each other out, and we’ll make sure you have everything you need to succeed.
• Steady Work: Real estate photography keeps a consistent pace, so you'll have reliable work rather than feast-or-famine scheduling.
• Room to Grow: As our business expands, so does the role. We'd love to find someone who wants to grow with us long-term.

If you are interested: Send your resume, a screenshot of an internet speed test, and a video introducing yourself to   ----------  and we will reach out with next steps!

title: Vibe Coding / Dev Expert Needed To Build Apps
description:
Looking for a vibe coding expert that also has real developer experience

Need someone help create simple, complete, sellable apps built with modern AI tooling

We ship real products. Some go to our community, some become licensed architectures that other businesses run on. Either way, "production ready" is the standard, not "looks good in a demo."

You will work directly with the founder to build and harden apps. The job has two sides that feed each other.
The first is building. You will spin up websites and apps for our community using Lovable, then extend them with real code when the visual builder runs out of road. Claude Code and Codex are your daily drivers for the deeper work.
The second is QA. You will take apps that mostly work and make them genuinely production ready. That means hunting edge cases, locking down security, and catching the problems before a user or a buyer ever sees them

We're building them on Lovable to let the members easily remix them

You will be building community websites and apps in Lovable, then customize them with hand-written code where needed
Use Claude Code and Codex for features, refactors, debugging, and anything beyond what a visual builder handles cleanly
Set up and maintain Supabase backends: schema, auth, row-level security policies, and edge functions

Integrate APIs and third-party tools (Claude API, Stripe, GoHighLevel, and similar)

QA apps end to end across user flows, error states, edge cases, and mobile responsiveness
Run a security review on every app, with real attention to RLS policies, auth, and data exposure
Document bugs clearly, fix them, and confirm the fix holds

Take apps from "working" to "ready to sell or deploy"

What you bring (required)

Real development experience. You can read, write, and debug actual code (React, TypeScript, SQL), not just work inside drag-and-drop builders

Hands-on experience with Claude Code

Hands-on experience with Codex

Working familiarity with Lovable

A solid grasp of full-stack web development across frontend, backend, and databases

Strong QA instincts. You find the edge case the rest of us missed, and you think like both a user and an attacker

Bonus points

Deep Supabase experience, especially RLS and edge functions
A track record shipping production SaaS that real people pay for
Familiarity with GoHighLevel, n8n, or Stripe

Who thrives here

You take a rough idea and turn it into a shipped product without needing a spec for every line
You move fast, and you slow down exactly where it matters, like security and data
You are comfortable working in an AI-native, agent-driven workflow

Who this isn't for

No-code-only builders who freeze the  ---------- nt they have to open the code
Anyone who needs a detailed ticket before they can start
People who think "it works on my screen" means the job is done

Looking forward to working together!

title: Construction Operations Manager — Systems Builder
description:
We are a small Canadian real-estate developer in Nova Scotia. We design and build multi-unit residential projects. Our design side runs on systems that work. Our construction side doesn't yet — and we're going to be honest with you about that.

The mess, plainly: multiple active builds. Subcontractor sourcing that stalls for weeks because we lack options. Procurement scattered across spreadsheets. Supplier outreach so uncoordinated that suppliers have asked who actually speaks for us. Site records living in chat threads and people's memory.

This job is not to follow our process. There isn't one. The job is to build it, then run it with the team.

What you'll own (outcomes, not tasks):

A subcontractor & supplier pipeline — sourcing ? bids ? decision ? contract, visible at every stage
Procurement that doesn't stall: one owner (you), clear status, automated follow-ups
Site execution rhythm: daily logs, punch lists, schedules people can actually trust
The tooling is your call — ClickUp, Sheets, Airtable, Quickbase, GoHighLevel, n8n, anything. We don't require coding. We require systems that keep running when you're not pushing them.

How we work: Remote, full-time, Atlantic Time overlap. You report directly to the CEO, with real authority from day one. We are AI-native — Claude sits inside every workflow here and we'll expect it in yours. We pay for proof, fast: salary reviews follow demonstrated ownership, not anniversaries — ask us to show you how that has worked here. People who build core systems at Helio participate in the upside.

Who tends to fit: estimators, site engineers, QS, construction PMs, and ops leads who got so frustrated with manual chaos that they built trackers, databases, SOPs, or automations nobody asked for. If you have never voluntarily systematized something, this role will frustrate you.

To apply — no cover letter. Answer two questions (bullet points fine; specifics win; please don't polish it with AI):

Show us something you built that nobody asked you to build — a tracker, database, process, automation, or small business. A link beats a description. What broke first, and what did you change?

If you took over construction operations for a company you'd never seen, what would you need to learn in week one — and how would you learn it?

Put FOUNDATION as the first word of your application.

title: Lead Generation on LinkedIn
description:

We are looking for a thoughtful, organized, detail-oriented LinkedIn Lead Generation Assistant to help us identify and connect with right-fit prospects for our coaching, workshop, and keynote speaking business.

Our company, Your Mindset Coaching & Consulting, helps high-achieving leaders, teams, educators, and organizations pursue extraordinary results without sacrificing their health, relationships, or self-worth. Our work focuses on healthy high performance, leadership, team culture, self-worth, resilience, and sustainable excellence.

We are looking for support with LinkedIn research, lead generation, outreach tracking, and booking introductory conversations with qualified prospects.

This is not a spam outreach role. We are looking for someone who understands quality over quantity, thoughtful personalization, respectful follow-up, and professional communication.

Responsibilities:

* Research and identify right-fit prospects on LinkedIn and related websites
* Build targeted lead lists of potential coaching, workshop, keynote, and organizational clients
* Find relevant decision-makers such as HR leaders, People & Culture leaders, Learning & Development leaders, school leaders, district leaders, executive directors, CEOs, founders, conference organizers, and professional development contacts
* Track leads in a spreadsheet or CRM with accurate notes and next steps
* Draft personalized LinkedIn connection requests and follow-up messages using our templates and voice
* Monitor replies and flag warm leads for us to respond to personally
* Help manage a simple follow-up system so good opportunities do not fall through the cracks
* Provide a weekly report on leads researched, messages sent, replies received, and calls booked

Required skills:

* Excellent written English
* Experience with LinkedIn lead generation and prospect research
* Experience with LinkedIn Sales Navigator
* Experience supporting coaches, consultants, speakers, trainers, or B2B service providers
* Strong attention to detail
* Experience helping book discovery calls or introductory meetings
* Ability to qualify leads, not just collect names
* Comfortable using Google Sheets or a simple CRM
* Able to follow clear systems and update records accurately
* Professional, respectful communication style
* Reliable, proactive, and organized
* Comfortable working independently and asking good questions when needed

Nice to have:

What success looks like:

* Success in this role means helping us build a steady pipeline of right-fit prospects and warm conversations. We care about quality leads, thoughtful outreach, and booked meetings with people who may genuinely benefit from our work.

To apply—please include:

* A brief summary of your LinkedIn lead generation experience
* Examples of the types of clients or industries you have supported
* The tools you have used for lead generation and tracking
* Your availability and preferred hourly rate
* Please include a short 30–60 second video introducing yourself and sharing why you think you would be a good fit for this role. It does not need to be professionally produced — a simple phone or webcam video is fine.
* Finally, please find 5 potential LinkedIn prospects who may be a good fit for our coaching, workshop, or keynote services. For each one, include their name, title, organization, LinkedIn profile, and one sentence explaining why you chose them.

title: Digital Marketing Specialist
description:
We are looking for a creative, results-driven **Marketing Manager / Marketing Specialist** to help grow our brand, sell tickets, attract new customers, and improve our overall marketing strategy.

The ideal candidate should have proven experience creating marketing campaigns, managing digital platforms, analyzing performance, and developing clear strategies that lead to measurable results.

The selected candidate will be responsible for:

Applicants must be prepared to:

**Qualifications**

title: Reservationist — Premier International Transportation (PIT Drives)  Washington, DC (Remote)
description:
About Us
Premier International Transportation (PIT Drives) has been delivering elite luxury ground transportation across the Washington DC metro area since 2010. From executive airport transfers at DCA, IAD, and BWI to corporate roadshows, wedding limousines, and private aviation logistics, we serve a discerning clientele who expect nothing short of excellence. We're growing, and we're looking for a polished, detail-oriented Reservationist to be join our team.

The Role
As a Reservationist, you are often the first point of contact for our clients. You'll handle inbound calls, respond to booking inquiries, and process reservations through our online platform ( ---------- ). Your professionalism and warmth set the tone for the white-glove experience our clients expect from the moment they reach out to us.

Key Responsibilities
- Answer inbound phone calls and respond to emails/chat inquiries promptly and professionally
- Process new reservations, modifications, and cancellations using the mylimobiz platform
- Collect accurate trip details: pickup/drop-off locations, passenger count, vehicle preference, flight information, and special requests
- Confirm bookings and communicate trip summaries to clients and dispatch
- Coordinate with the dispatch team to ensure seamless handoffs and real-time updates
- Handle customer questions about services, fleet options, pricing, and policies
- Proactively follow up with clients before and after trips to ensure satisfaction
- Maintain accurate records and uphold client confidentiality at all times

Qualifications
- 1–3 years of experience in customer service, reservations, hospitality, or a related field
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, Outlook) and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Gmail, Calendar)
- Prior experience in luxury transportation, hospitality, or travel services
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills — clear, confident, and courteous
- Comfortable working with online booking software (training on mylimobiz provided)
- Strong attention to detail; accuracy in data entry is essential
- Ability to multitask and stay calm under pressure during busy periods
- Professional phone etiquette with a warm, client-first demeanor
- English speaking and writing fluency

What We Offer
- Competitive hourly pay / salary (commensurate with experience)
Flexible scheduling options
- Supportive, team-oriented work environment
- Opportunity to grow within a well-established luxury transportation company
- Be part of a brand trusted by executives, diplomats, and discerning travelers

To Apply
Send your resume and a brief introduction to  ----------  with the subject line: Reservationist Application – [Your Name]

title: Senior Full-Stack AI Developer - Whitelabel SaaS Platform
description:
Location: Remote
Employment Type: Full-time Contract/Employee  
Salary Range: Negotiable   
Experience Level: Senior (8-12+ years)

About This Opportunity - before I start, we're building MANY applications, tools and automations, we have over 200,000 business owners who've come to us to scale with AI. Our brand is AI For Business

We're seeking an exceptional Senior Full-Stack AI Developer to single-handedly architect and build a comprehensive AI-powered SaaS platform similar to  ----------  (but way fewer models) with whitelabel capabilities.

Looking for a developer who thrives on complex technical challenges and wants to create the next generation of AI-powered applications from the ground up

What You'll Build

You'll be responsible for creating a complete AI-powered web application featuring:
- Multi-AI Model Integration: ChatGPT 4o, Claude Sonnet 4, Perplexity Pro, and free models via OpenRouter
- Advanced Image Generation: GPT 4o image, Ideogram, Flux via Fal.ai API
- Video Generation: SORA, VEO 3, Minimax, Kling integration via Fal.ai API
