title: eCommerce Virtual Assistant
description:
We are expanding our remote team and looking for a reliable, disciplined, and ambitious eCommerce Virtual Assistant who wants long-term growth.

Our local agency is based in the Philippines and manages multiple eCommerce brands for international clients. This role is for someone who takes work seriously, learns fast, and wants to build a real career in digital work, not just a short-term VA job.

This is a busy and fast-paced role. Since we work with multiple clients, there will be days with several tasks, urgent revisions, and deadlines. We need someone who can stay organized, work under pressure, follow instructions properly, and remain reliable even when the workload gets heavy.

If you are organized, proactive, and can work independently, this is a strong opportunity.

About the Role

Start Date
Immediate

Schedule
Graveyard shift Philippine time
Monday to Friday
Weekends off

Please note that occasional overtime may be required when there are urgent client tasks, important deadlines, or time-sensitive updates. This does not happen every day, but we need someone who understands that eCommerce work can sometimes require flexibility.

Responsibilities

You will support daily operations across multiple eCommerce brands. Tasks include:

• Managing and updating Shopify, WooCommerce, and Amazon stores
• Writing, editing, and optimizing product listings
• Conducting product, competitor, and market research
• Handling basic customer service and order processing
• Completing website updates and admin tasks
• Assisting with marketing and content-related tasks
• Creating simple graphics and visuals using Canva
• Helping with basic website layout and design improvements
• Using ChatGPT and AI tools to improve speed and accuracy
• Following systems, checklists, and instructions precisely
• Handling multiple tasks across different client accounts
• Responding quickly to task updates during working hours
• Staying organized even when the workload is busy

As you improve, you will manage client accounts more independently and take on higher-value tasks.

Requirements

• Fast learner with strong problem-solving skills
• Organized, detail-oriented, and accountable
• Able to follow instructions and meet deadlines
• Strong communication and reliability
• Proactive mindset and good work ethic
• Comfortable working in a busy and fast-paced environment
• Able to handle pressure and urgent tasks professionally
• Own laptop or desktop
• Stable, high-speed internet connection
• Fully available during assigned working hours
• No other job, client, or commitment that will affect your focus, schedule, or performance

Preferred but Not Required

• Experience with Shopify
• Basic graphic design skills or Canva experience
• Understanding of layout, spacing, and clean design
• Familiarity with ChatGPT or other AI tools
• Basic video editing skills using CapCut
• Previous VA, eCommerce, customer service, or admin experience

We prioritize attitude, discipline, consistency, and reliability over years of experience. Skills can be trained, but work ethic, honesty, and commitment are required from the start.

Compensation and Growth

• Monthly Range: ?25,000 to ?30,000
• Performance Bonus: ?5,000 to ?8,000 per month
• Clear path for raises, promotions, and leadership roles
• Long-term stability for consistent top performers

Why Join Us

• Stable long-term work with a structured eCommerce agency
• Clear systems, feedback, and guidance
• Learn Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, and TikTok Shop operations
• Work with automation and AI tools daily
• Gain real experience handling international eCommerce brands
• Real opportunity to grow into senior or leadership roles

Important

This is not a passive VA role where you only wait for instructions. We need someone who can move fast, think clearly, stay focused, and take responsibility for their work.

We are looking for someone who wants to grow with the company long term. If you are only looking for a side job, temporary work, or something easy, this may not be the right fit.

Application Instructions

Please submit the following:

Resume
Internet speed test result
Laptop or PC specifications, including model, processor, RAM, and storage
Short cover letter explaining who you are, what you’ve done, and why you are a strong fit

NOTE:
This is a long-term position with strong potential for career growth. We are looking for someone fully committed to the role. You must not have other jobs, clients, or commitments that may interfere with your schedule, focus, availability, or performance.

title: Customer Service Representative (Remote)
description:
IMPORTANT: Apply via our website - NOT through OnlineJobs.ph

Go to  ---------- /customer-service-vacancy and submit your application there.

We are a growing digital company based in the Netherlands and we are looking for a reliable and empathetic Customer Service Representative to join our team. You will be the first point of contact for our customers - handling questions, solving problems, and making sure every customer leaves the conversation satisfied.

You are NOT doing cold outreach. All conversations are with existing customers or people who have already shown interest in our products. Your job is to help, reassure, and resolve.

IMPORTANT: You will be communicating in written English with customers from the UK, USA, Australia, and other English-speaking markets. Excellent written English is a must - professional, warm, and clear.

What You'll Do:
- Respond to customer emails, live chat, and support tickets in a timely manner
- Handle refund requests, complaints, and product questions with empathy and professionalism
- Answer pre-sale questions and help guide potential customers to a purchase decision
- Troubleshoot common issues and provide clear step-by-step solutions
- Escalate complex issues to the right tea ---------- mber when needed
- Keep track of conversations and outcomes in our CRM

Requirements:
- Excellent written English - professional, warm, and natural (not robotic)
- Experience in customer service, support, or a client-facing role
- Ability to stay calm and empathetic under pressure, especially with frustrated customers
- Strong problem-solving skills and a proactive attitude
- Reliable internet connection and consistent availability
- Comfortable working independently and managing your own queue

Nice to Have:
- Experience with helpdesk tools like Gorgias, Freshdesk, Zendesk, or similar
- Background in e-commerce or digital product support
- Experience handling refunds, complaints, or escalations
- Previous remote work experience

Compensation:
- $4 - $6 per hour (depending on experience)
- Paid weekly or bi-weekly

Why Work With Us:
- Long-term position with growth opportunities
- Clear processes and templates - we set you up for success
- Supportive team - you are never alone with difficult cases
- Flexible schedule with consistent hours

Please include in your application:
1. Describe a time you handled a difficult or frustrated customer. What did you do and what was the outcome?
2. How would you respond to a customer demanding a refund in an aggressive way?
3. What customer service tools or platforms have you used before?
4. What is your availability per week and which time zones can you cover?

We review applications quickly and will respond within 48 hours.

title: Salesforce Administrator
description:
We are a driven group of people who believe we can have a significant impact on the small business finance industry. LLama Loan was founded on the premise that honest finance partners can help small businesses get through challenging times and resolve their cash flow problems. Our risk infrastructure is iterated constantly, and we are quick enough to make decisions. Our company benefits from market turbulence. We think outside the box.

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented Salesforce Administrator to manage, maintain, and enhance our Salesforce platform. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in Salesforce administration, security, automation, data management, reporting, and user support. This role will work closely with business stakeholders to ensure Salesforce solutions align with organizational needs and business processes.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: 
Salesforce Administration
• Configure and maintain Salesforce objects, fields, page layouts, record types, validation rules, and custom settings.
• Manage Salesforce users, roles, profiles, permission sets, and access controls.
• Implement and maintain Salesforce security model, including sharing rules, role hierarchy, and field-level security.
• Support sandbox management, refresh activities, and environment governance.

Automation & Process Optimization
• Design, build, and maintain Salesforce Flows to automate business processes.
• Analyze existing processes and recommend improvements to increase efficiency and scalability.
• Troubleshoot and resolve automation-related issues.

Reporting & Analytics
• Create and maintain custom report types, reports, and dashboards.
• Develop meaningful business insights through Salesforce reporting and analytics.
• Support stakeholders with ad hoc reporting requirements.

Data Management
• Perform data imports, exports, cleansing, deduplication, and mass updates using Salesforce tools and Excel.
• Manage data integrity, quality, and governance processes.
• Utilize Data Loader and other Salesforce data management tools for bulk operations.

Deployment & Testing
• Participate in deployment planning and execution across Salesforce environments.
• Perform system testing, user acceptance testing (UAT), and validation of new features and enhancements.
• Maintain deployment documentation and change management processes.

Integrations & System Support
• Support Salesforce integrations with third-party applications and internal systems.
• Assist in troubleshooting integration issues and coordinate with development teams when necessary.
• Monitor system performance and ensure platform reliability.

User Support & Training
• Provide day-to-day Salesforce support to users.
• Create and maintain documentation, training materials, and best practices.
• Conduct user training sessions and onboarding for new tea ---------- mbers.

Required Skills & Qualifications
• Strong knowledge of Salesforce Security Model (Profiles, Permission Sets, Roles, Sharing Rules, Field-Level Security).
• Experience with Page Layouts, Record Types, Custom Fields, Validation Rules, and Formula Fields.
• Hands-on experience with Salesforce Flow automation.
• Strong reporting and dashboard development skills.
• Experience creating Custom Report Types and advanced reports.
• Knowledge of Salesforce deployment processes and sandbox management.
• Experience with Salesforce data management tools, including Data Loader.
• Strong Excel skills for data analysis, cleansing, and bulk updates.
• Understanding of Salesforce integrations and APIs.
• Experience with testing, troubleshooting, and system maintenance.
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

Preferred Qualifications
• Salesforce Administrator Certification.
• Experience with Sales Cloud and Service Cloud.
• Knowledge of change sets, DevOps, and release management practices.
• Basic understanding of Apex, SOQL, and Lightning Components.
• Experience working in Agile environments.

Success Factors
• Ability to understand business requirements and translate them into Salesforce solutions.
• Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality.
• Proactive approach to identifying process improvements and platform enhancements.
• Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.



For interested applicants, PLEASE SEND AN ACCESSIBLE LINK TO YOUR UPDATED CV/RESUME.

title: VA - Affiliate Marketing Technical Integration Manager
description:
I run a marketing agency that I started last year with a $4,000 loan and scaled to seven figures in annual revenue within 18 months. I'm hiring one person to work alongside me long-term and gradually take over key parts of what I do day-to-day.
This is a hands-on role on a fast-moving team, and you'll learn the exact systems behind that growth. (You can see how we operate on my YouTube channel.)   ---------- 
I'm not looking for a long résumé or 10 years of experience — I'm looking for a caffeine addicted addicted kller that will stop at nothing to make his dreams come true, and eventually move to like a 1st world country.

Compensation & benefits

$1000/month base salary
Performance bonus/commission on top of base for strong work
Direct mentorship and access to the playbook and SOPs that took the business to seven figures in one year
(People pay me 500/hour for consulting)
Real room to grow into a managerial position worth 5k/mo as the business grows

What you'll be doing

Taking over repetitive editing tasks I currently handle myself, following existing video tutorials and SOPs 
Producing static image creatives, including AI-generated statics (e.g., with Nano Banana)
Strong technical integration background needed — connecting tools using APIs and webhooks, connect anythign amrketing related (Pixels, CAPI, GTM, Webhook, Zapier)
Working inside our marketing stack: lead-distribution software (e.g., LeadProsper, Leadshook) and funnel builders


This is intermediate integration work — not advanced heavy custom development. 

Extreme ambition — you want to grow alongside a fast-scaling business
Strong sense of urgency — we move fast, and you're willing to put in extra hours when it matters (time is tracked, and extra work is compensated)
Eager to learn — for example, editing on CapCut can be picked up in a few days, and our internal videos/SOPs cover the rest
Reliable, self-directed, and comfortable owning tasks end-to-end

Skills
Required

Willingness and ability to learn editing and AI tools like Veo and Kling quickly (basic to no prior editing experience needed)
Technical integration skills: APIs, webhooks, CAPI, GTM, Pixels, and connecting marketing tools, Google spreadsheet (SUPER Important)
Comfort using AI tools to handle technical and coding tasks

BIG BONUS: You have experience in affiliate marketing understand this site -  ----------  CPC, CPA, CPL (not TikTok influencer stuff)

Nice to have
Intermediate or advanced video/image editing skills
Kling 3.0, Seedance AI Skills

How to apply (please read)

A Loom video introducing yourself is mandatory. I review a large volume of applicants and will not consider applications 1k plus )without one. In the video, tell me anything you think would be useful to someone in my position, scaling a marketing agency fast.
Watch a few videos on my channel first, so you understand exactly what we do. Thank you kindly and have an amazing day.

title: Sales & CRM High Level  (GoHighLevel Experience Preferred)
description:

title: Funnel Builder and Meta ads Specialist
description:
We are a fast-growing marketing agency in high-growth businesses in coaching and consulting. With consistent $100,000 monthly revenue and a Q3 2025 goal of $250,000, we build scalable marketing machines that prioritize performance and measurable outcomes. Our lean, outcome-obsessed team is seeking a hybrid GHL Funnel Builder and Meta Media Buyer to architect funnels and drive high-impact ad campaigns that fuel our clients’ growth.

This role combines expertise in GoHighLevel (GHL) funnel building with advanced Meta advertising skills to drive lead generation and conversions for our $99/month community and $15,000 upsell offer. The ideal candidate is a detail-oriented, high-output operator who thrives in a high-trust, performance-driven environment, with a knack for building funnels and scaling Meta ad campaigns.

Key Responsibilities:

GHL Funnel Building (50% of Time)

Construct and customize GHL funnels for lead magnets, community sign-ups, and upsell paths using pre-designed templates.
Set up funnel components, including landing pages, opt-in forms, and automations (e.g., lead tagging, email triggers), adhering to the agency’s launch checklist.

Customize funnel designs (e.g., text, images, videos) based on client needs, ensuring mobile-friendly layouts and seamless user experiences.

Test funnels for functionality (e.g., form submissions, page loading) before launch to ensure quality and performance.

Document funnel setup processes to support team training and maintain consistency.

Meta Media Buying (40% of Time)

Build, manage, and scale performance-driven Meta campaigns across multiple brands (real estate, digital products, services, SaaS).

Develop ad copy, creative briefs, and thumb-stopping hooks that drive high ROAS, CTR, CPL, and LTV.

Test ad angles, lead magnets, videos, UGC, and static creatives at speed, optimizing based on data-driven insights.

Manage tracking, retargeting, and attribution within Meta Ads Manager to ensure accurate performance reporting.



Create growth loops integrating Meta ads with GHL funnels, websites, email, and AI outreach.

Collaboration and Reporting (10% of Time)

Collaborate with content teams, funnel builders, and leadership to align on strategy and vision.

Build and maintain reporting dashboards to track funnel performance and ad metrics, enabling smart decision-making.

Provide weekly progress updates to Agata on funnel setups, ad performance, and any challenges encountered.

Offer feedback on templates and ad strategies to improve efficiency and results.

Required Skills and Experience

GHL Funnel Building:

1+ year of experience with GoHighLevel, including building landing pages, opt-in forms, and basic automations.

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow established processes (e.g., templates, checklists).

Proficiency in managing multiple funnel projects and meeting deadlines.

Meta Media Buying:
- 3+ years managing $20,000+/month in Meta ad spend profitably.
- Deep mastery of Meta Business Suite, Ads Manager, audiences, and tracking.
- Strong knowledge of creative strategy (scripts, ad structures, testing methodologies).
- Expertise in A/B testing, scaling principles, and performance funnels.

General:
- Excellent organizational skills and proficiency in tools like Google Sheets, Google Docs, or similar for task tracking and documentation.
- Reliable internet connection and availability to work remotely (Philippines preferred).

Preferred Skills
- Experience supporting marketing campaigns for coaches/consultants.
- Familiarity with Google Ads or email marketing platforms (e.g., Active Campaign).
- Prior work in fast-paced, remote team environments.

Why Join Us?
- Work directly with founders and senior operators in a dynamic 8-person agency.
- Own ad accounts and funnels with full trust to perform, no micromanaging.
- Drive real business growth across multiple verticals, contributing to our $250,000 Q3 revenue goal.
- Grow your skills in a supportive, high-performance team with opportunities for bonuses and real upside.
- Work remotely from the Philippines with a proven team delivering exceptional results for coaches and consultants.

To Apply

Submit a 2–3 minute video addressing:
1. Your best-performing Meta campaign (what made it work?).
2. Your unique approach to building and scaling GHL funnels and Meta ads. 
3. Why you’re the hybrid GHL and Meta expert we’ve been looking for.

title: SEO AEO AI SEO GEO Specialist
description:
We are seeking a seasoned SEO AEO GEO AI SEO Specialist to join our team. This role demands demonstrated experience, a data-driven approach, and the ability to leverage AI tools to optimize both traditional and emerging search experiences.

About the Role
Position**: SEO AEO AI SEO Specialist
Department**: Marketing / Growth
Location**: Remote or on-site (flexible)
Employment Type**: Full-time
Experience Level**: Senior/Lead with verifiable results

What You’ll Do
Strategy Development**: Create and execute comprehensive SEO and AEO (AI-Enhanced Optimization) strategies that improve organic visibility, featured snippets, and voice/search results across traditional and AI-powered channels.
  Develop roadmaps for on-page, technical, and off-page SEO with an AI-centric focus.
  Integrate AI-driven insights to anticipate user intent and optimize for voice, visual, and chat-based search experiences.
GMB/Local SEO Management**: Optimize Google My Business (GMB) profiles to improve local rankings, reviews, and engagement.
  Monitor local presence, citations, and NAP consistency.
  Align local content and events with overall SEO strategy.
AI Tools & Automation**: Implement and manage AI platforms (e.g., ChatGPT-based workflows, AI-assisted content generation, and voice-search optimization tools).
  Build and refine prompts, workflows, and dashboards to streamline SEO activities.
  Explore and pilot new AI-driven ranking signals and ranking-tracking methodologies.
Content Optimization**: Guide content teams on AI-assisted content creation, semantic optimization, and structured data.
  Ensure consistency with brand voice while leveraging AI to scale quality.
Analytics & Reporting**: Measure performance, run experiments, and report on key metrics (traffic, rankings, conversions, LTV, and ROI).
  Use AI-powered analytics to derive actionable insights and optimize campaigns.
Collaboration & Leadership**: Partner with product, engineering, and marketing teams to implement technical SEO improvements and ensure scalable AI integration.
  Mentor junior tea ---------- mbers and share best practices in AI-enhanced SEO.

What We’re Looking For
Experience-Driven**: A proven track record in delivering tangible SEO results, with measurable improvements in rankings, traffic, and conversions.
GMB/Local Expertise**: Demonstrated success optimizing Google My Business profiles and local search presence.
AI Proficiency**: Strong familiarity with AI tools and workflows (e.g., ChatGPT or equivalent) for SEO tasks, content ideation, and voice/search optimization.
Technical Skills**: Solid understanding of technical SEO, schema markup, site speed, crawlability, indexation, and tooling (GA4, Search Console, log file analysis, rank trackers).
Strategic Mindset**: Ability to balance short-term wins with long-term growth, experimentation, and data-driven decision-making.
Communication**: Clear, collaborative communicator who can translate complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders and guide cross-functional teams.

Preferred Qualifications
Certifications in SEO, local SEO, or analytics.
Experience with multi-channel SEO, including voice search, visual search, and AI-assisted content.
Familiarity with competitive analysis, backlink strategy, and content marketing alignment.

Compensation & Benefits
Salary**: Competitive, with a premium offering after a successful trial period based on demonstrated impact.
Trial Period**: A clearly defined initial trial period during which you will demonstrate practical SEO capabilities and provide tangible examples of results (including work on GMB and AI-enhanced initiatives).
Performance Review**: Premium compensation awarded upon meeting or exceeding agreed Uplift milestones.
Benefits**: Comprehensive health/dental/vision coverage, retirement benefits, paid time off, flexible work arrangements, ongoing learning budget.

What to Expect During the Hiring Process
Portfolio or case studies showing prior SEO and local optimization results.
Examples of campaigns that utilized AI tools for SEO tasks.
A practical assessment or task related to optimizing a GMB profile and a sample AI-driven content plan.
Behavioral and technical interviews to assess fit and communication skills.

How to Apply
Please submit:
  Your resume highlighting relevant SEO and local optimization achievements.
  Case studies or links to campaigns with measurable outcomes.
  A short description of how you have used AI tools to improve SEO results, including any successful experiments in voice or AI-assisted content.

If you’re a results-oriented SEO professional with a strong background in local optimization and AI-enabled strategies, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today to join a dynamic team that values experience, innovation, and measurable impact.

title: Healthcare Marketing and Flow manager
description:
About Our Clinic

His and Hers Hormone Replacement Therapy is a growing hormone health, medical weight loss, and wellness clinic with locations in Chehalis and Longview, Washington. We help patients feel heard, supported, and cared for through provider-guided hormone therapy, testosterone therapy, women’s hormone health, medical weight loss support, wellness injections, IV therapy, and related services.

We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented virtual assistant to help us use AI tools and organized workflows to improve patient communication, follow-up, education, and satisfaction.

Main Goal of the Role

The main goal of this role is to help our clinic improve the patient experience by using AI-supported systems for communication, follow-up, patient education, review requests, internal organization, and service improvement.

This person will not provide medical advice, diagnose patients, or make clinical decisions. 

Patient Satisfaction & Follow-Up
Help organize patient feedback, survey responses, reviews, and common patient concerns
Track patient satisfaction themes and report recurring issues to the clinic owner or manager
Draft friendly follow-up messages for patients after visits, injections, consultations, or treatment check-ins
Help create review request messages and patient appreciation touchpoints
Help identify patients who may need additional non-clinical follow-up from the clinic team

AI Support & Content Assistance
Use AI tools to help draft patient-friendly messages, FAQs, emails, blog outlines, social posts, and internal scripts
Turn clinic instructions into clear patient education handouts or message templates
Help organize AI-generated content into usable workflows, checklists, and templates
Review AI content for tone, clarity, and consistency before sending it to clinic leadership for approval
Make sure AI-created content sounds warm, professional, simple, and patient-friendly

Patient Communication Support
Draft patient communication templates for appointment reminders, missed appointment follow-up, treatment check-ins, and wellness education
Help organize email or text message sequences for new patients, active patients, and returning patients
Create templates for common patient questions, always using approved clinic language
Escalate clinical or sensitive patient questions to the clinic team instead of answering independently
Administrative Workflow Support
Help organize SOPs, checklists, staff scripts, and internal clinic documents
Assist with CRM, email, spreadsheet, and task management organization
Track marketing and patient experience tasks
Help maintain content calendars for emails, blogs, Google Business Profile posts, and social media
Support patient retention efforts through organized follow-up systems
Reputation & Review Support
Help monitor review platforms, organize reviews, and prepare response drafts
Draft professional review responses for approval
Help create systems to ask happy patients for reviews in a compliant and respectful way
Track review trends and common patient compliments or concerns

If i could find someone who specializes in marketing strategies and content creation that would be a bonus.

Willing to increase pay based on quality results and output.

title: Full-Time Podcast Manager
description:
Job Title: Full-Time Podcast and Social Media Manager

Company Overview:
We are a fast-growing agency that empowers entrepreneurs to achieve their goals and enhance their visibility through impactful content creation. Our mission includes producing and managing high-quality podcast content and leveraging social media strategies to engage audiences, build brand authority, and drive growth.

Position Overview:
We are seeking an experienced Podcast and Social Media Manager to oversee and manage multiple podcasts while driving social media marketing efforts. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in podcast production, social media management, guest coordination, and content promotion. This is a full-time role that requires excellent organizational skills, creativity, and the ability to handle the unique needs of various clients.

Key Responsibilities:

Podcast Management:
• Manage all aspects of podcast production for multiple shows, ensuring timely and high-quality delivery.
• Coordinate guest interviews, including outreach, scheduling, and preparing guests for their appearances.
• Develop and manage production schedules, ensuring deadlines are consistently met.
• Create and oversee workflows to streamline podcast operations and optimize efficiency.
• Collaborate with editors and content creators to produce engaging audio and video content.
• Write and edit show notes, titles, and descriptions for each episode.
• Distribute episodes across podcast platforms (e.g., Spotify, Apple Podcasts) and track performance metrics.
• Monitor analytics and provide clients with regular reports on podcast performance.
• Stay updated on podcasting trends, tools, and industry best practices to ensure competitive positioning.

Social Media Management:
• Develop and execute social media strategies to promote podcast episodes and other related content.
• Create and schedule posts on platforms like Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, TikTok, and Twitter.
• Edit and repurpose podcast content into engaging social media clips and graphics.
• Use tools like CapCut, Canva, or Descript to optimize and streamline social media content creation.
• Engage with followers, respond to comments, and foster online communities around podcast brands.
• Monitor social media analytics to measure campaign performance and optimize strategies for better engagement and reach.

Additional Responsibilities:
• Coordinate with marketing teams to align podcast and social media strategies with broader client goals.
• Assist in advertising and sponsorship outreach for podcast monetization opportunities.
• Conduct market and competition analysis to identify trends and new opportunities in podcasting and social media.

Qualifications:
• Proven experience managing multiple podcasts or similar content projects.
• Strong understanding of podcast platforms, workflows, and best practices.
• Solid experience in social media management and marketing, including analytics and campaign optimization.
• Familiarity with social media platforms and tools such as Hootsuite, Buffer, or Sprout Social.
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills for guest coordination and audience engagement.
• Proficiency in podcast editing software (e.g., Audacity, Adobe Audition) and social media content creation tools.
• Ability to multitask and work in a fast-paced environment while maintaining high attention to detail.

Preferred Qualifications:
• Experience with video podcasting and SEO optimization for podcast episodes and promotional materials.
• Knowledge of automation tools like Zapier and CRM platforms.
• Familiarity with paid social media advertising strategies.

What We Offer:
• Flexible remote working environment.
• Opportunities for professional growth and development.
• Collaborative and supportive team culture.
• Competitive compensation based on experience

Application Requirements:
To apply for this position, candidates MUST complete all three steps below:
1. Submit a resume outlining your experience.
2. Provide a portfolio showcasing podcasts and social media projects you’ve managed.
3. Submit a link to a newly created video introduction where you:
• State the current date (to verify it’s a new video).
• Explain why you are the perfect fit for this role.
• Highlight relevant projects you’ve completed and your approach to podcast management and social media marketing.
• Showcase examples of your work or workflows if possible.

Please Note:
• Do not apply if you do not complete all three requirements as described above.
• Old video introductions or videos that do not include all required components will NOT be considered.
• Applications submitted directly on other platforms will not be considered.

How to Apply:
Submit your application via this link:  ---------- 

We are excited to review your application and see how your expertise can elevate our podcast and social media content to new heights!

title: Full-Stack SaaS Web App Developer, AI Prototype to Production
description:
Company Overview

We are seeking a skilled Full-Stack SaaS Web App Developer who can help turn AI-built prototypes into production-ready web apps, SaaS-style tools, and internal team systems. This role requires substantial availability during U.S. Eastern business hours for communication, collaboration, updates, and technical questions.

Position Overview

We are hiring a Full-Stack SaaS Web App Developer to support the development of internal tools, team portals, dashboards, and SaaS-style web applications.

Some apps are currently built or prototyped in Lovable and connected to GitHub. This role requires someone who can review existing AI-generated app code, determine what can be reused or improved, connect the backend properly, set up deployment, and help move prototypes into stable production environments.

This is not a basic website role. We need someone who understands frontend, backend, databases, authentication, APIs, deployment, and app logic. Because this work involves ongoing collaboration and technical decision-making, the person must be available for regular communication during U.S. Eastern hours.

Key Responsibilities
• Review Lovable/AI-generated apps and connected GitHub repositories
• Turn prototypes into production-ready web apps
• Build and clean up React, Next.js, and/or TypeScript applications
• Set up or improve Supabase, PostgreSQL, Firebase, or similar backend/database systems
• Build dashboards, portals, admin panels, forms, and workflow tools
• Set up authentication, user roles, and permissions
• Connect approved APIs, webhooks, and third-party tools
• Collaborate with our automation specialist on n8n or API-based workflows when needed
• Deploy apps using Vercel, Railway, Render, VPS, or similar platforms
• Set up environment variables and secure API key handling
• Identify what is functional, what is prototype-only, and what should wait
• Document setup, architecture, deployment steps, and technical decisions
• Provide regular updates, blockers, and questions

Qualifications
• Proven experience with full-stack web app development
• Experience with React, Next.js, and/or TypeScript
• Experience with GitHub and version control
• Experience with Supabase, PostgreSQL, Firebase, or similar backend/database tools
• Experience setting up authentication, user roles, and permissions
• Familiarity with API integrations and webhooks
• Experience deploying apps with Vercel, Railway, Render, VPS, or similar platforms
• Ability to review existing code and explain what should be reused, refactored, or rebuilt
• Strong communication and documentation habits
• Substantial availability during U.S. Eastern business hours

Preferred Skills
• Experience with Lovable, Bolt, Replit, or other AI/no-code/low-code app builders
• Experience taking AI-generated prototypes into production
• Familiarity with n8n, Zapier, or Make
• Experience with Stripe, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Claude, or other AI APIs
• Experience with ClickUp, Google Drive, Gmail, Google Calendar, or similar business tools
• Experience building internal tools, agency operations systems, SaaS apps, dashboards, or portals
• Understanding of secure app development practices

Compensation
Pay is negotiable based on experience and technical depth.

Application Process
To apply for this position, candidates MUST complete all of the following steps:

1. Submit a resume outlining full-stack development, SaaS, web app, or internal tool experience.
2. Upload examples or screenshots of previous apps, dashboards, portals, SaaS products, GitHub repositories, or deployment projects.
3. Submit a newly recorded video introduction where you:
    State the current date
    Briefly explain your full-stack development experience
    Describe your experience with React, Next.js, TypeScript, GitHub, Supabase, APIs, and deployment
    Explain whether you have worked with Lovable, AI-generated prototypes, or no-code/low-code app builders
    Briefly describe one web app, SaaS product, dashboard, or internal tool you helped build or deploy
    Confirm your regular availability during U.S. Eastern business hours

Important Application Instructions
• Applications must be submitted through this link:  ---------- /4130RpL
• Applications submitted directly on other platforms will not be reviewed
• All steps must be completed to be considered

title: Self- Hosted Automations Technician (Part-Time)

title: Data Extraction and Automation Specialist
description:
Company Overview

We are seeking a skilled Data Extraction and Automation Specialist who understands modern web scraping, lead data enrichment, automation workflows, and structured data management. Availability during U.S. Eastern hours is required.

Position Overview

We are hiring a Web Scraping and Lead Data Automation Specialist to support advanced list building, structured data extraction, and enrichment workflows. This role focuses on pulling targeted lead data at scale using Apify, Python, Clay, and APIs.

This is a hands on technical role for someone who understands scraping logic, selectors, pagination, anti-bot challenges, deduplication, and data normalization.

Key Responsibilities

• Build and maintain lead scraping workflows using Apify actors or Python
• Extract structured data from websites, directories, databases, and public sources
• Handle pagination, login flows, rate limits, and anti-scraping mechanisms
• Clean, dedupe, and normalize datasets
• Enrich scraped data using Clay
• Connect scraped and enriched data to CRM systems, Google Sheets, or CSV exports
• Build or integrate APIs for data transfer when needed

title: Client Operations Manager
description:
Remote · Full-time · $2,000+ CAD/month (Starting)

ABOUT THE COMPANY
Manzano Creative is a Canadian content and Meta advertising agency based in Winnipeg, Manitoba, serving service-based businesses — primarily dental clinics and home service companies. We run multiple productions per week, manage active Meta ad campaigns, and coordinate creative work across a distributed remote team.
Our systems run on Slack, Notion, Google Drive, GHL, and Meta Ads Manager. We move fast. We hold ourselves to a high standard of communication and execution. This is an agency environment, not a corporate one.

WHAT THIS ROLE IS
This is not a VA position. This is not an entry level role.
This is one of the most important hires inside Manzano Creative. You will own three things simultaneously — the client relationship, the campaigns, and the copy. You are the person clients hear from. You are the person who builds and monitors their campaigns. You are the person who writes their ads.
When a client signs, you take over. You onboard them, set up their systems, write their scripts, launch their campaigns, keep them updated, and make sure they get results. You work closely with the Production Coordinator to stay on top of every project so no client question ever goes unanswered.
If you need constant direction, hand-holding, or supervision — this is not the right fit. If you thrive on ownership, move fast, communicate clearly, and genuinely care about client results — read on.

WHAT YOU WILL OWN
Client Success & Communication

First point of contact for every client from the  ---------- nt they sign
Full client onboarding — Notion hub setup, Slack channel, Google Drive folders, GHL sub-account
Sending the welcome message and guiding clients through onboarding steps
Monday check-in messages and consistent ongoing communication
Answering client questions about campaigns, projects, and production progress
Coordinating and confirming shoot days with clients
First delivery of content to clients for review
Receiving client revision feedback and passing to Production Coordinator
Monthly performance reporting and results walkthroughs
Keeping clients informed, confident, and retained

Media Buying & Campaign Management

Setting up Meta campaigns following Manzano Creative's proven structure
Creating and updating lead forms inside Meta Ads Manager
Mapping lead forms into GHL and ensuring leads fire correctly into Google Sheets
Daily campaign monitoring across all active client accounts
Flagging performance issues and making proactive optimization suggestions
Ensuring all tracking, automations, and CRM connections are functioning correctly

Copywriting & Ad Creative

Writing ad scripts for paid ads and nurture videos
Writing primary text and headlines for Meta campaigns
Pulling angles, hooks, and pain points from Fathom transcripts and client onboarding forms
Using AI tools to generate and refine copy based on Manzano Creative formulas and winning scripts
Sending scripts to clients for review and incorporating feedback


REQUIREMENTS

Proven hands-on experience managing Meta Ads Manager campaigns — non-negotiable
Strong understanding of Meta campaign structure — campaigns, ad sets, ads, lead forms, budgets, placements
Experience with GHL or similar CRM platforms
Excellent written English — clear, confident, and professional in every interaction
Strong copywriting instincts — you understand hooks, pain points, and what makes an ad convert
Ability to write ad scripts, primary text, and headlines independently
Experience working inside Slack, Notion, and Google Drive
Comfortable communicating directly with clients in a professional and warm manner
Highly organized with strong attention to detail
Ability to work independently without constant supervision or reminders
Expected to attend and participate in weekly tea ---------- etings via video call
Reliable internet connection and consistent availability during agreed hours


NICE TO HAVE

Previous experience working inside a content or advertising agency
Familiarity with direct response copywriting frameworks
Experience using AI tools such as Claude or ChatGPT for copy refinement
Understanding of video production workflows
Experience reading and working from sales call transcripts or onboarding forms


WHO THRIVES IN THIS ROLE
The people who do best here are proactive, organized, and take genuine ownership of their work. They communicate clearly and professionally without being asked. They notice what needs to happen before being told. They understand both the creative and analytical sides of advertising. They care about client results — not just completing tasks. They are comfortable being the face of the agency to clients on a daily basis.

WHO STRUGGLES IN THIS ROLE
If you need detailed instructions for every task, send short or vague responses, wait to be told what to do next, or become overwhelmed under pressure — this role will not be a good fit. We respect your time and ask that you respect ours.

GROWTH OPPORTUNITY
This role has genuine room to grow in scope and compensation as the agency scales. We are building a team of operators and leaders — not a team of assistants. The right person will have long-term runway here and will become a core part of what Manzano Creative is building.

HOW TO APPLY
To be considered, please include all of the following in your application:

A brief introduction — who you are, your background, and why this role interests you
Specific examples of Meta ad campaigns you have managed — industries, budgets, and results if possible
A writing sample — share a piece of ad copy, a script, or primary text you have written previously
A short paragraph describing how you stay organized when managing multiple clients and priorities at once
Your current availability and time zone
A short video introduction (2–3 minutes) — record yourself explaining why you are a strong fit for Manzano Creative specifically. Tell us what you know about what we do, what draws you to this type of agency, and how your experience maps to this role. We are looking for clarity, confidence, and genuine interest — not a polished production

Applications without all of the above will not be reviewed.
We look forward to hearing from candidates who are serious about this opportunity.

title: HOA Manager
description:
The HOA Property Manager is responsible for the day?to?day operations, governance support, vendor oversight, and owner/resident experience across an assigned portfolio of homeowner associations. This role requires strong analytical ability, impeccable attention to detail, and exceptional written and verbal communication skills. The manager serves as the primary liaison among the Board of Directors, unit owners, residents, vendors, and internal teams (Maintenance and Finance), ensuring that association operations comply with governing documents, applicable laws, and company standards while meeting service-level and financial targets.

Key Outcomes (What Success Looks Like):

Accurate, timely Board packets and meeting facilitation with clear minutes and action logs
Portfolio-level adherence to budgets, assessments, and reserve funding plans in partnership with Finance HOA
Proactive maintenance planning and on-time completion of preventive and corrective work with Maintenance HOA
High owner/resident satisfaction, timely resolution of requests, and transparent communications
Clean annual audits, renewed insurance policies, and documented compliance with statutes and governing documents
Up-to-date task/project management in Asana and on-time delivery of recurring HOA cycles and filings

Key Responsibilities:
1) Board & Governance Management
Serve as the primary point of contact for Board members; prepare agendas, Board packets, and management reports for regular and special meetings.
Interpret and apply governing documents (CC&Rs, bylaws, rules) and relevant state statutes in daily operations; coordinate with counsel when required.
Facilitate annual meetings, elections, and owner notices in accordance with legal and governing document requirements.
2) Owner & Resident Relations
Maintain responsive, professional communication with trustees/Board, unit owners, and residents via email, phone, and portal.
Issue timely association-wide notices (policy changes, maintenance schedules, community events, compliance reminders).
Manage and resolve escalations, complaints, architectural review requests (ARC), violations, and hearings within established SLAs.
3) Financial Coordination & Reporting 
Support budget development, reserve study implementation, and monthly variance commentary; review financials for accuracy.
Monitor assessment billing, collections, payment plans, and delinquency follow-up in alignment with collection policies.
Review and approve invoices, code expenses to budgets, and validate vendor work completion prior to payment.
4) Maintenance, Projects & Vendor Management (with Maintenance HOA)
Translate Board priorities into annual maintenance calendars; schedule and track preventive maintenance and inspections.
Open, monitor, and close work orders; ensure scope, SLA, and quality standards are met; maintain accurate asset records.
Bid, negotiate, and manage vendor contracts (landscaping, janitorial, security, pools, roofing, elevators, etc.); perform periodic vendor reviews.
Coordinate capital improvement projects (RFPs, proposals, schedules, owner notifications, punch lists, closeout documents).
5) Information Management & Systems
Keep all HOA Asana projects/boards current with daily updates, dependencies, and follow-ups; maintain recurring tasks and automations.
Process resale and lender questionnaire packages accurately and within service timelines; maintain fee schedules.
Maintain organized digital records (governing docs, minutes, contracts, insurance, warranties, architectural files) in the association’s repository.
6) Team Leadership & Development
Train and onboard new APMs/PMs on processes, systems (Asana, accounting/portal), compliance, and customer service standards.
Promote a collaborative, service-oriented culture aligned with company values; escalate staffing/resource needs to the Unit Head.
Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):
On-time completion rate for recurring HOA tasks and compliance filings
Work-order first-response and average time-to-close (by priority)
Delinquency rate and collections effectiveness; percent of assessments collected
Budget adherence (OPEX/CapEx) and variance accuracy; invoice cycle time
Owner/resident satisfaction score; complaint resolution time
Board satisfaction score (post-meeting surveys) and action-item closure rate
Qualifications:
3+ years of HOA/condo association management or related property management experience
Demonstrated proficiency with task/project management tools (Asana) and owner portals; strong Excel/Google Sheets skills
Exceptional communication (written and verbal), meeting facilitation, and stakeholder management
Strong analytical, organizational, and time-management skills; high attention to detail
Working knowledge of governing documents, meeting procedures, collections, elections, and common HOA statutes

title: Sales Manager
description:
Sales Operations Manager / AI Sales Performance Manager
About Cynergists
Cynergists provides advanced AI Agents designed to help businesses automate workflows, increase productivity, and unlock scalable growth. We do not just fill seats. We deploy intelligent operations.
We are building the future of AI-powered business growth through agentic CRM systems, AI Workforce Agents, sales automation, lead generation, and intelligent follow-up systems. Our mission is to help small and mid-sized businesses scale like giants without needing bloated internal teams.
The Role
We are looking for a highly organized, assertive, and tech-curious Sales Operations Manager to help lead and manage our growing sales team.
This is not a traditional sales manager role where you are expected to personally close every deal. Your main responsibility is to make sure the sales team is executing every day, following the process, using the CRM correctly, improving from AI-generated feedback, and moving opportunities through the pipeline.
You will work directly inside the Cynergists CRM and with our AI Sales Trainer to monitor sales activity, review call performance, track follow-ups, identify stuck deals, and help each sales representative improve. The right person is calm under pressure, strong with details, comfortable holding people accountable, and excited about using AI to improve sales performance.
Our uploaded sales role template emphasizes diagnostic selling, pipeline management, AI sales training, CRM usage, and market feedback as core responsibilities for Cynergists sales positions . This manager role builds on that foundation but focuses on leading the team and making sure the sales system runs properly.

Key Responsibilities
Sales Team Accountability
Manage daily sales activity across the team, including calls, texts, emails, booked appointments, follow-ups, and pipeline movement.
Ensure every sales representative is following the required sales process, completing assigned outreach, updating the CRM, and staying on top of their opportunities.
Run daily or weekly check-ins with sales reps to review activity, performance, challenges, and next steps.
Hold reps accountable when activity drops, follow-ups are missed, or pipeline records are incomplete.
CRM and Pipeline Management
Monitor the Cynergists CRM to ensure leads, contacts, opportunities, notes, tasks, and deal stages are updated correctly.
Identify stale opportunities, missing follow-ups, incomplete contact records, and deals with no next step.
Make sure every active prospect has a clear next action assigned.
Track the full pipeline from first conversation to booked appointment, proposal, close, or lost deal.
AI Sales Coaching and Performance Review
Use Cynergists’ AI Sales Trainer and call scoring tools to review sales rep performance.
Monitor call scores, conversation quality, objection handling, buyer sentiment, follow-up quality, and close attempts.
Help sales reps understand their AI-generated feedback and turn it into better sales behavior.
Identify patterns across the team, including weak openers, poor discovery, missed closes, pricing objections, or lack of urgency.
Sales Reporting
Prepare weekly sales performance reports for leadership.
Track key sales metrics, including:
Calls made
 Conversations started
 Appointments booked
 Show rate
 Follow-up completion
 Pipeline created
 Deals closed
 Close rate
 Average call score
 Rep-by-rep performance trends
Flag underperforming reps and recommend corrective action.
Sales Process Improvement
Help improve scripts, sales workflows, CRM automations, objection handling, and follow-up sequences.
Listen to feedback from business owners and sales reps to identify gaps in our offers, sales materials, or AI Agent catalog.
Relay market feedback to leadership and the development team so Cynergists can improve its products and sales process.
Training and Onboarding
Assist with onboarding new sales representatives.
Teach reps how to use the CRM, follow the sales process, review AI feedback, and manage their pipeline.
Help create simple SOPs, checklists, and training materials for the sales team.
Run roleplays and objection-handling practice when needed.

What We Are Looking For
Strong Accountability
You must be comfortable holding sales reps accountable. This role is not just admin support. You need to be able to say, “This was not completed,” “This follow-up was missed,” or “Your call score dropped and we need to fix it.”
Sales or Appointment Setting Experience
You do not need to be a world-class closer, but you should understand sales activity, follow-up, objections, CRM pipelines, and appointment setting.
Experience managing or supporting outbound sales teams is a major plus.
CRM Experience
Experience with Go High Level, HubSpot, Salesforce, Close, Pipedrive, or another CRM is preferred.
You must be comfortable learning software quickly and working inside dashboards every day.
Tech-Savvy Mindset
You do not need to be a developer, but you must be excited about AI, automation, and software.
You should be comfortable using AI tools to review performance, summarize activity, identify problems, and improve sales execution.
Organized and Detail-Oriented
You must be able to manage multiple reps, tasks, follow-ups, reports, and deadlines without letting things fall through the cracks.
Clear Communication
Strong written and verbal English communication is required.
You will communicate with sales reps, leadership, and occasionally other departments, so clarity matters.
Calm Under Pressure
Sales teams move fast. Leads get missed. Reps get frustrated. Deals stall.
We need someone who stays calm, solves problems, and keeps the team moving.

Preferred Qualifications
Experience managing appointment setters, sales reps, or virtual sales teams
 Experience working remotely
 Experience with Go High Level
 Experience with AI tools or sales automation
 Experience reviewing call recordings or sales performance
 Experience creating SOPs or training materials
 Experience working with U.S.-based companies
 Strong spreadsheet and reporting skills

Performance Expectations
Success in this role will be measured by:
Sales reps completing required daily activity
 CRM records staying clean and updated
 Follow-ups being completed on time
 Pipeline stages being accurate
 Improvement in rep call scores
 Increase in appointments booked
 Increase in close rate
 Reduction in missed leads and stalled deals
 Clear weekly reporting to leadership

Why Join Cynergists?
Work in the Future of AI Sales
You will be helping build and manage an AI-powered sales organization, not an outdated manual sales team.
Real Leadership Opportunity
As Cynergists grows, this role can become a larger leadership position managing more reps, more processes, and more revenue operations.
Remote Work
This is a remote position. You can work from your own space while supporting a fast-growing U.S.-based AI company.
AI-Powered Training and Support
You will have access to our AI Sales Trainer, CRM tools, call scoring, and internal systems to help you manage the team effectively.
Growth-Oriented Culture
We are building fast. The right person will have room to grow with the company.

Schedule
Full-time preferred.
Must be available during core U.S. business hours for sales team check-ins, reporting, and leadership communication.

Compensation
Competitive monthly compensation based on experience.
Performance bonuses may be available based on team sales activity, booked appointments, closed revenue, and CRM compliance.

To Apply
Submit your resume and a short cover letter explaining:
Why you would be a strong fit to manage a sales team
Your experience with CRM systems or sales operations
 Your experience holding people accountable
 Why you are interested in AI-powered sales

title: REAL ESTATE MARKET & LAND ACQUISITION ANALYST
description:
About Us
MGVG Development is a Georgia-based multifamily development company focused on developing workforce housing communities throughout the state. We currently own and operate approximately 400 apartment units and are actively expanding our development pipeline.

We are seeking a highly analytical, detail-oriented Real Estate Market & Land Acquisition Analyst to identify underserved housing markets, locate development opportunities, and help drive our expansion strategy.

This is not a typical virtual assistant position. We are looking for someone who can think critically, conduct independent research, and help identify our next development opportunities.
Position Overview
Your primary responsibility is to identify Georgia markets that have a demonstrated need for additional housing and then locate development sites that meet our acquisition criteria.

You will effectively serve as our acquisitions research department by identifying both the market opportunity and the land opportunity.
Primary Responsibilities
Market Research & Housing Demand Analysis
• Identify underserved housing markets throughout Georgia
• Analyze population growth, employment growth, housing shortages, vacancy rates, occupancy levels, rent trends, and workforce housing demand
• Review comprehensive plans, housing studies, and economic development announcements
• Identify cities and counties actively seeking additional housing development

Development Pipeline Research
• Track multifamily developments under construction
• Monitor planning commission agendas, rezoning applications, and development permits
• Identify competing apartment developments and estimate future supply

Land Acquisition Research
• Identify development sites through Crexi, LoopNet, LandWatch,  ---------- , Zillow, county GIS systems, and local broker listings
• Verify ownership, zoning, rezoning potential, utility availability, flood status, and road frontage

Market & Site Feasibility
• Determine whether rents can support $1,150+ one-bedroom units and $1,300+ two-bedroom units
• Verify documented housing need and likelihood of multifamily support
• Evaluate market competition and site viability
Development Site Criteria
• Georgia locations only
• Population greater than 13,000
• 4-8 acre parcels
• Highway frontage or major road frontage
• Water and sewer available
• Outside flood zones
• Suitable for 72-120 unit apartment developments
• Land pricing generally between $25,000-$50,000 per acre
• Strong workforce housing demand
• Potential for rezoning to multifamily
Weekly Deliverables
Market Opportunity Report:
• Top markets ranked by housing need, rent potential, employment growth, development competition, political support, and overall opportunity score

Land Opportunity Report:
• Address
• Parcel number
• Acres
• Asking price
• Zoning
• Utility availability
• Flood zone status
• Rent comps
• Development activity nearby
• Overall acquisition score

Acquisition Recommendations:
• Top opportunities with supporting rationale
Required Skills
• Strong real estate research experience
• Excellent internet research skills
• Advanced Excel or Google Sheets skills
• Strong written English
• Ability to work independently
• High attention to detail
• Strong critical thinking skills
Preferred Experience
• Multifamily development
• Commercial real estate
• Land acquisition
• GIS mapping
• Planning and zoning
• Market feasibility analysis
• Economic development research
Compensation
Please include:
• Hourly rate
• Weekly availability
• Relevant experience
• Resume
• Examples of similar work completed
Application Test
Qualified candidates will complete a paid test assignment:

1. Identify a Georgia market with documented housing demand.
2. Identify one development site meeting our criteria.
3. Provide supporting research and recommendations.

Applicants who can demonstrate strong research, analytical ability, and independent thinking will be given priority.

title: Credentialing Specialist
description:
About Handspring Health
Handspring is a virtual mental health clinic for children, young adults, and families. We are dedicated to transforming mental healthcare for families through high-quality, accessible virtual therapy.

About the Role
The Credentialing Analyst will support the day-to-day management of provider credentialing, licensing, payer enrollment, and related operational systems. This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, comfortable working with data and trackers, and able to spot gaps, inconsistencies, and next steps across multiple systems.
This is an analytical operations role. The person in this role will not just complete tasks, but will also help maintain clean data, monitor workflow progress, identify blockers, and give the team clear visibility into credentialing status.

Key Responsibilities
Maintain accurate provider credentialing and licensing data across internal trackers, spreadsheets, shared drives, and external systems.
Review provider files for missing, outdated, or inconsistent information.
Track credentialing, licensing, payer enrollment, recredentialing, renewal, and CAQH attestation deadlines.
Update and maintain CAQH profiles, payer portals, NPI records, license documentation, malpractice insurance records, and other provider files.
Prepare, submit, and track credentialing and payer enrollment applications under the direction of the operations team.
Monitor provider status across credentialing workflows and flag delays, blockers, or missing items.
Create and update reports showing credentialing status, upcoming deadlines, outstanding documents, payer enrollment progress, and other key workflow metrics.
Reconcile information across systems to ensure provider records are accurate and consistent.
Support provider onboarding by collecting, reviewing, and organizing required credentialing documents.
Follow up with providers, internal tea ---------- mbers, and external organizations to move credentialing work forward.

What We Are Looking For
3+ years of experience in healthcare credentialing, provider enrollment, licensing, payer operations, healthcare administration, or operations analysis.
Strong spreadsheet skills and comfort maintaining detailed trackers.
Experience working with U.S. healthcare credentialing workflows.
Experience with CAQH ProView strongly preferred.
Experience with payer portals, provider enrollment, NPI records, state licenses, malpractice documentation, and credentialing files preferred.
Strong attention to detail and ability to identify inconsistencies across documents and systems.
Comfort working independently in a remote environment.
Strong written communication skills.
Ability to manage recurring workflows, deadlines, and follow-ups without repeated reminders.
Ability to handle confidential provider information with professionalism.
Behavioral health, telehealth, therapy, or multi-state provider credentialing experience is a plus.

Systems Experience Preferred
CAQH ProView
NPPES / NPI Registry
Payer portals
Google Sheets or Microsoft Excel
Google Workspace
Credentialing trackers or project management tools
Shared drives and document management systems
EHR or provider management systems

Nice to Have
Experience credentialing therapists, psychologists, social workers, or other behavioral health providers.
Experience with multi-state licensing workflows.
Experience supporting Medicaid, commercial insurance, or payer enrollment processes.
Experience building or maintaining dashboards, trackers, or workflow reports.
Experience working with offshore or distributed U.S.-based healthcare teams.
Success in This Role Looks Like
Credentialing data is accurate, complete, and consistently updated.
The team has clear visibility into provider status, outstanding items, and next steps.
Licensing, CAQH, payer enrollment, and recredentialing deadlines are tracked proactively.
Provider files are organized, audit-ready, and easy to review.
Workflow blockers are identified early and escalated appropriately.
Trackers and reports are reliable enough for the team to use in weekly operations planning.

title: Executive Assistant-Right Hand-Fast-Growing Comic Book Company
description:
Operations Manager-Execution Partner-Project Killer-Fast-Growing Comic Book Company
Own the operations backbone of a niche comic book brand, work directly with leadership, and get promoted fairly by numbers. 

This role is for that special person who wants to become truly exceptional. If that’s you, you already know this message is your signal.

What You Get If You Win Here
- High autonomy: once you’re ramped, you own your queue, schedule, and methods as long as the KPIs are hit. Lots of freedom to figure it out, great for someone that loves building with Legos
- Direct proximity to leadership: you work daily with the Ops Manager on the most important projects in the company.
- Clear advancement path: your growth is tied to visible numbers (tasks shipped, zero-miss cadence, freed founder/ops time, profit/leading metrics), not politics.
- Insane skill growth: the pace and standards here mean every 12 months with us will feel like 3 years of experience in a normal VA/ops role. You can leapfrog your friends. If you join this will change your life. You will work harder than you’ve ever worked before and grow faster than you’ve ever grown before. 
- Comic Books!: you’re not just pushing random SaaS tickets; you’re inside a real comic book store seeing rare old comic books, private deals, and the business behind the Hollywood Marvel Movies that everyone loves!
If you’re that person who enjoys working really hard to the point that it takes 2-3 people to do what you did listen up: 
Our tasks in asana for projects and ongoing operations are moving at a snail’s pace.
Ops Manager + CEO is chasing tasks, slack policing, cleaning up Asana, and re-explaining context instead of focusing on their jobs.

We’ve had “pleasant” VAs who:
Can’t drive tasks to completion independently
Mark tasks complete with no proof
Miss reports/skip cadence and require chasing. 
Drift on ownership, urgency & follow thru
Need hand-holding 
I don’t need another task hopper. I need a project killer who comes into ambiguity, creates structure, drives tasks to completion, and actually frees the Ops Manager to focus on his key projects. If this works, his deep work hours and project output go up because you’re handling everything else. We will measure that and tie your promotions/raises to it. This is not for everyone. We’re looking for winners. 

What You’ll Actually Do
You will be Ops Manager’s execution partner and force-multiplier. Concrete outputs:
Own Asana: every task has an owner, due date, Done Check, and proof link when completed
Maintain an active queue of 20+ meaningful tasks, prioritized against our key rocks (Marketing, Sales, Ongoing Operations, Cleanup, Admin)
Ship 25+ tasks/week with proof, minimal rework, and zero “ghosted” items
Proactively handle operational issues/fires in designated lanes
Maintain Reporting at Set Cadence, Follow-up loops across projects
Add leverage: plug AI into our workflows (Asana, docs, reports) to make us faster and cleaner without being asked
We do NOT need more SOPS-although you might tighten them on the fly. We need someone to do the operations. If you want to sit and write playbooks all day, this is the wrong seat-that will be an earned responsibility later. If you aren’t ready to work hard daily do not apply. 

How We Set You Up To Win (First 90 Days)
We don’t just throw you in the deep end. In your first 30 days, you’ll shadow the Ops Manager, learn our Asana + reporting systems, and get a clear scorecard so you always know what “great” looks like. By 30 days, you’ll own your own project lanes with weekly 1:1s for feedback, support, and removing blockers so you can move faster instead of guessing what we want.

Real Growth Example 
One of our Filipino teammates started with us on basic execution work at ~$800/month. Over time, he took ownership of his queue, fixed broken processes, and became the person we trusted to “just handle it.” As his scope grew, so did his comp and stability. Now he makes ~$2,500/month (and we’re working on getting him even more) because he stuck with it and actually grew. A lot of people say they want that, but their resumes show 6–12 month hops. The pattern here is simple: prove you can own outcomes over time, and we grow your responsibilities, trust, and pay accordingly.

Schedule, Hours & Time Zone (No Surprises)
This is a full time, US hours role (roughly US Eastern business hours) with a true 50–60 hour workweek during this growth season. Expect most weeks to be closer to that 60 hour mark. If you want a strict 40 hour cap, this is the wrong seat; if you want a place where extra effort is noticed, measured, and rewarded, you’ll feel at home here. This is not just a job. This is a commitment.

What Winning Looks Like
By Day 30: Ops Manager is spending ~95% of his time on projects, not Slack/Asana/admin fires, because you’re handling those and executing projects alongside him. Profit and leading metrics are moving up faster because of you

By 12 months: You are Ops Manager’s go to operator & on track to become Ops Manager. Nothing important moves in Ops without you knowing. You own the Asana board, key ops projects, and reporting cadence. You’re the person we trust to “just handle it,” and your comp reflects that increased ownership. 

If you become that go to operator, you’ll have real say in how we run Ops – you won’t just be ‘executing tickets,’ you’ll help design the system.”

Who This Is NOT For
You prefer to ‘coordinate’ and assign tasks to others instead of doing the work yourself with excellence.
You want to manage people before you’ve truly proven you can personally execute at a high level. 
You want a chill, low-pressure 9–5
You need to be told what to do all day and hate being judged by objective numbers
You mark tasks complete without proof or next steps
You change jobs every 6–18 months and blame the company/manager/market/
You get defensive or freeze when given direct feedback or pressure
You are currently juggling multiple full time clients and want to “squeeze us in” on the side.
You are mass applying to dozens of jobs and just want to see who bites first.


Who This IS For
You’ve stayed 2–3+ years in at least one role and can prove outcomes
You like pressure, highly competitive environments, being the one who “gets it done” when others stall
You’ve built systems (trackers, automations, SOPs), not just followed them
You are tech-comfortable: Asana (or similar), CRMs, zaps, AI tools, simple web/ops/marketing stacks
You’re willing to bet on one company instead of juggling lots of smaller, easier gigs.
You hate corporate BS– endless meetings, vague goals, and politics – and would rather be rewarded for what you actually create.
You’re happy to grind 50–60 hours/week no issues. We’re in a BIG growth & push season. It will be like this for the next year and then we’ll see. Everyone wants to grow a lot, but it’s actually happening here.  

Comp & Growth
(All of this is written into your contract.)
Months 1–3: Ramp / Trial
• $800/month base, guaranteed, while you learn our systems and we test fit.
• No bonus in this phase. You’re paid to prove you can hit the KPI floors.
Month 4+ – Performance Based Upside
• Base stays at $800 until you’ve hit KPI floors for 2 straight months. After that, base increases to $900/month going forward.
• On top of base, you can earn:
$400 bonus in months where you hit 3 out of 4 KPIs (with Output as one of them).
$1,000 bonus in months where you hit 4 out of 4 KPIs on the weekly scorecard.
• In practice, that means:
Solid performance months ~$1,200–$1,300/month
Killer months (4/4 KPIs)~$1,800–$1,900/month
All bonuses are 100% KPI based  (shipped outcomes, taking load off the Ops Manager, zero CEO/manager rescues, tight open loops) and 4/5 culture.

Training & Growth Investment (on top of pay)
We actively invest in making you a sharper teammate. Over time, we’ll put thousands of dollars into your development (courses, coaching, playbooks, and tools) that we choose together based on what will make you more effective in this seat.
We also protect uninterrupted deep?work blocks for you every week (no rando ---------- etings, no Slack spam). You’re paid to ship outcomes, not sit in back?to?back Zoom calls.


Important: This is an ON FIELD role. You will not have a team under you to delegate to. 
95% of your time is doing the work yourself, 5% is light coordination. If your default mode is to make plans and pass tasks to others, this will be a bad fit. 
How To Apply (24-Hour Test)
Our Hiring Process (So There Are No Surprises)

If your 24 hour answers impress us, the next steps are:
A short interview focused on your past execution.
A live 60 minute work trial inside our real tools where you’ll work directly with the Ops Manager. We do this because talk is cheap. If you don’t like being tested on real work before getting hired, this won’t be a fit.

Do NOT click quick-apply. In the next 24 hours, you must:

Reply with answers to ALL of these (short but specific; bullets are fine):
Q1: Describe the most complex project you personally drove fro ---------- ssy start to completion where you did most of the execution yourself, not just assigning tasks. Include at least one example of how you set your own deadlines and moved forward when other people were slow to respond.” 
Q2: Share 3 links or screenshots (Google Docs, Sheets, Asana boards, trackers, automations, etc.) that you built yourself which show how you organize and drive work. Briefly explain each.
Q3: You have 30 tasks due this week and realize you’ll miss 5. Walk me through, step-by-step, how you handle it with your manager and stakeholders.
Q4:  Below are 6 messy “tasks” we actually see (all dumped into one list):
– “Fix Asana, it’s a mess”
– “Seller lead texting follow up??”
– “Finance com/projections cadence d2 ”
– “Inventory backlog insane”
– “Need better reporting”
– “Update SOPs
In this role you will NOT have anyone to delegate to assume it’s just you and the Ops Manager.
In a Google Doc, please:

Rewrite these into clear tasks with: Owner (you or Ops Manager), Due Date (next 7–30 days), and a concrete Done Check + proof required. “Write your task descriptions as if you are committing: ‘I will do X by Y time. If Z doesn’t happen by [time], here’s my fallback.’ Generic ‘follow up / request access’ answers will fail you.” 
Put them in the order you’d actually do them and explain why in 3–5 bullet points.
Link the Google Doc here (view access).
Spend no more than 30–45 minutes. If you can’t figure this out on your own, this role will be too hard in real life – that’s okay, just skip applying.

Attach your PDF resume/Google Drive link.

Compensation here is performance based. Raises are earned through clearly exceeding KPIs and demonstrating core values consistently. If you want “stability” to mean “I can coast and still get raises,” this won’t feel stable. If you want stability to mean “the better I get, the more valuable and secure I become,” you’ll like it here.

title: Tech VA - 7-Figure Solo Man Marketing Company
description:
Hi hopefully you are having a wonderful day!

I started this marketing agency last year with a $4,000 loan and scaled it to seven figures in annual revenue within 18 months. I'm hiring one person to work alongside me long-term and gradually take over key parts of what I do day-to-day.

This is a hands-on role on a fast-moving team, and you'll learn the exact systems behind that growth. You can see how we operate on my YouTube channel:  ---------- 

I'm not looking for a long résumé or 10 years of experience. I'm looking for a caffeine-addicted killer who'll stop at nothing to make their dreams happen.
Compensation & benefits

$1,000/month base salary
Performance bonus/commission on top of base for strong work
Direct mentorship and access to the playbook and SOPs that took this business to seven figures in one year (people pay me $500/hour for consulting)
Real room to grow into a managerial position worth $5k/month as the business grows

What you'll be doing

Technical integrations — this is the core of the role. Connecting tools using APIs and webhooks, and wiring up anything marketing-related: Pixels, CAPI, GTM, webhooks, Zapier. 

Working inside our marketing stack: lead-distribution software (e.g., LeadProsper, LeadsHook) and funnel builders.

Building and maintaining systems in Google Sheets.

This is intermediate integration work — not advanced, heavy custom development.

Who you are

Extreme ambition — you want to grow alongside a fast-scaling business.
Strong sense of urgency — we move fast, and you're willing to put in extra hours when it matters (time is tracked, and extra work is compensated).
Eager to learn — our internal videos and SOPs cover most of what you'll need.
Reliable, self-directed, and comfortable owning tasks end-to-end.

Required skills

Technical integration skills: APIs, webhooks, CAPI, GTM, Pixels, and connecting marketing tools. Google Sheets (SUPER important).
Comfort using AI tools to handle technical and coding tasks.

Being able to follow SOPs and learn basic editing, graphic design, and spreadsheet management

Nice to have

General automation / no-code experience (Zapier, Make, n8n, GHL, etc.).
Any hands-on experience wiring marketing platforms together.

We do PAY PER LEAD - So having affiliate background is huge bonus.


Watch a few videos on my channel first, so you understand exactly what we do.
Thank you kindly, and have an amazing day.
