(All of this is written into your contract.)
Months 1–3: Ramp / Trial
• $800/month base, guaranteed, while you learn our systems and we test fit.
• No bonus in this phase. You’re paid to prove you can hit the KPI floors.
Month 4+ – Performance Based Upside
• Base stays at $800 until you’ve hit KPI floors for 2 straight months. After that, base increases to $900/month going forward.
• On top of base, you can earn:
$400 bonus in months where you hit 3 out of 4 KPIs (with Output as one of them).
$1,000 bonus in months where you hit 4 out of 4 KPIs on the weekly scorecard.
• In practice, that means:
Solid performance months ~$1,200–$1,300/month
Killer months (4/4 KPIs)~$1,800–$1,900/month
All bonuses are 100% KPI based  (shipped outcomes, taking load off the Ops Manager, zero CEO/manager rescues, tight open loops) and 4/5 culture.

Training & Growth Investment (on top of pay)
We actively invest in making you a sharper teammate. Over time, we’ll put thousands of dollars into your development (courses, coaching, playbooks, and tools) that we choose together based on what will make you more effective in this seat.
We also protect uninterrupted deep work blocks for you every week (no rando ---------- etings, no Slack spam). You’re paid to ship outcomes, not sit in back to back Zoom calls.


Important: This is an ON FIELD role. You will not have a team under you to delegate to. 
95% of your time is doing the work yourself, 5% is light coordination. If your default mode is to make plans and pass tasks to others, this will be a bad fit. 
How To Apply (24-Hour Test)
Our Hiring Process (So There Are No Surprises)

If your 24 hour answers impress us, the next steps are:
A short interview focused on your past execution.
A live 60 minute work trial inside our real tools where you’ll work directly with the Ops Manager. We do this because talk is cheap. If you don’t like being tested on real work before getting hired, this won’t be a fit.

Do NOT click quick-apply. In the next 24 hours, you must:

Reply with answers to ALL of these (short but specific; bullets are fine):
Q1: Describe the most complex project you personally drove fro ---------- ssy start to completion where you did most of the execution yourself, not just assigning tasks. Include at least one example of how you set your own deadlines and moved forward when other people were slow to respond.” 
Q2: Share 3 links or screenshots (Google Docs, Sheets, Asana boards, trackers, automations, etc.) that you built yourself which show how you organize and drive work. Briefly explain each.
Q3: You have 30 tasks due this week and realize you’ll miss 5. Walk me through, step-by-step, how you handle it with your manager and stakeholders.
Q4:  Below are 6 messy “tasks” we actually see (all dumped into one list):
– “Fix Asana, it’s a mess”
– “Seller lead texting follow up??”
– “Finance com/projections cadence d2 ”
– “Inventory backlog insane”
– “Need better reporting”
– “Update SOPs
In this role you will NOT have anyone to delegate to assume it’s just you and the Ops Manager.
In a Google Doc, please:

Rewrite these into clear tasks with: Owner (you or Ops Manager), Due Date (next 7–30 days), and a concrete Done Check + proof required. “Write your task descriptions as if you are committing: ‘I will do X by Y time. If Z doesn’t happen by [time], here’s my fallback.’ Generic ‘follow up / request access’ answers will fail you.” 
Put them in the order you’d actually do them and explain why in 3–5 bullet points.
Link the Google Doc here (view access).
Spend no more than 30–45 minutes. If you can’t figure this out on your own, this role will be too hard in real life – that’s okay, just skip applying.

Attach your PDF resume/Google Drive link.

Compensation here is performance based. Raises are earned through clearly exceeding KPIs and demonstrating core values consistently. If you want “stability” to mean “I can coast and still get raises,” this won’t feel stable. If you want stability to mean “the better I get, the more valuable and secure I become,” you’ll like it here.

title: Airbnb | Virtual Assistant | Full Time
description:
Join a Growing Airbnb & Property Management Company

Hostier is a rapidly growing short-term rental (STR) and property management company. We manage a portfolio of Airbnb and vacation rental properties, delivering exceptional guest experiences while continuing to expand our operations.

We are looking for a dedicated Virtual Assistant who is seeking a long-term career opportunity and has proven experience within the short-term rental industry.

Essential Requirements

Please only apply if you meet the following criteria:

* Minimum 2 years of Short-Term Rental (STR) experience
* Excellent written and spoken English
* Experience communicating with guests via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Strong organisational and problem-solving skills
* Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks
* Reliable internet connection and suitable home office setup

Preferred Qualifications

The following would be highly advantageous:

* Previous experience working directly for Airbnb
* Experience using Airbnb,  ---------- , VRBO, Hostaway, Enso Connect, Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, or similar STR platforms
* Experience with Notion, Airtable, Slack, and Google Workspace
* Previous Virtual Assistant experience

Responsibilities

Your day-to-day duties may include:

* Managing guest communications via phone, email, and messaging platforms
* Handling inbound and outbound calls
* Managing reservations and guest journeys
* Coordinating cleaning schedules and maintenance requests
* Managing guest issues and escalations
* Email management (maintaining a zero-inbox approach)
* Monitoring and responding to guest reviews and feedback
* Administrative support, reporting, and data management
* Communicating effectively with tea ---------- mbers via Slack

Working Hours

* 48.5 hours per week
* Rotating schedule within our operating hours of 09:00–22:00 GMT
* Weekend and public holiday coverage required

Who We’re Looking For

We are seeking someone who wants to build a long-term career with our company. We value reliability, professionalism, accountability, attention to detail, and a proactive attitude.

Application Instructions

Please also provide:

1. Your current CV/resume
2. Details of your Short-Term Rental experience
3. The companies you have worked for and the length of time you worked with each company
4. The systems and software you have used (e.g. Airbnb, Hostaway, Enso Connect, Guesty, Hospitable, Lodgify, etc.)

We look forward to hearing from motivated candidates who are excited to contribute, grow, and build a long-term career with Hostier.

title: Financial Operations Manager
description:
We’re Hiring: Financial Operations Manager

We are looking for a highly organized, proactive, and detail-oriented Financial Operations Manager to support bookkeeping, reporting, payroll coordination, cash flow tracking, tax coordination, and operational financial analytics across multiple business and personal entities.

This role is ideal for someone who is not just comfortable with numbers, but also systems-minded, AI-first, and able to help leadership stay financially organized across several moving parts. We need someone who can take ownership, think ahead, identify issues before they become problems, and use tools, systems, and AI to make financial operations cleaner and more efficient.

You will work closely with leadership and coordinate with tax consultants, contractors, affiliates, and financial service providers.

## About the Role

You will help manage financial operations across multiple entities, including personal finances, real estate-related businesses, and a growing SaaS company. This includes maintaining accurate records, preparing financial reports, tracking cash flow, coordinating tax documentation, monitoring contractor and affiliate payments, and supporting leadership with financial visibility and decision-making.

This is not a passive bookkeeping role. We are looking for someone who can manage recurring responsibilities, improve financial systems, flag risks early, and recommend better ways to organize, report, and automate finance-related work.

The role serves as the primary financial coordination point between ownership, tax professionals, contractors, affiliates, and financial service providers.

## What You’ll Do

### Bookkeeping & Financial Records

* Maintain bookkeeping and financial records across multiple business and personal entities.
* Keep transactions properly categorized and reconciled.
* Ensure financial records are accurate, organized, and up to date.
* Maintain clean documentation for expenses, income, transfers, contractor payments, affiliate payouts, and business obligations.
* Identify inconsistencies, missing records, or unusual activity and flag them proactively.

### Financial Reporting & Cash Flow

* Prepare monthly financial reports and cash flow updates.
* Monitor cash balances across accounts and flag upcoming funding needs.
* Advise ownership when transfers are needed to cover payroll, taxes, contractor payments, subscriptions, affiliate payouts, and other obligations.
* Support forecasting and financial planning through clear reporting and analysis.
* Help leadership understand what the numbers mean, not just what the numbers are.

### Tax Coordination & Compliance Support

* Coordinate with tax consultants and assist with tax preparation.
* Help organize tax documents, tax organizers, contractor records, and compliance-related files.
* Monitor contractors and affiliates for payment tracking and 1099 compliance.
* Ensure financial documentation is organized and accessible for tax professionals.
* Track recurring tax-related deadlines and follow up before they become urgent.

### Payroll & Contractor Payments

* Process or coordinate payroll for business ownership and tea ---------- mbers where applicable.
* Track contractor and affiliate payouts.
* Review payout approvals and ensure payment information is accurate.
* Maintain records of completed and pending payments.
* Help improve payment tracking systems so there is clear visibility into who has been paid, who is pending, and what requires approval.

### SaaS / Operational Analytics

* Manage Stride CRM user sign-up databases and related financial tracking.
* Monitor user analytics, affiliate performance, and payout approvals.
* Help track revenue, churn, signups, affiliate activity, and related SaaS metrics.
* Support leadership with insights that connect financial data to business performance.
* Identify trends, gaps, or anomalies in business performance data.

### Process Improvement & AI Leverage

* Use AI tools to reduce repetitive manual work and improve reporting speed.
* Use AI to help organize financial data, summarize reports, analyze spreadsheets, create checklists, draft documentation, and identify process gaps.
* Look for recurring finance tasks that can be automated, systemized, or simplified.
* Document repeatable workflows so the finance function becomes easier to manage over time.
* Help turn messy information into clean, usable trackers, reports, and decision-making tools.

### Financial Liaison & Communication

* Serve as the primary financial coordination point between ownership, tax professionals, contractors, affiliates, and financial service providers.
* Communicate clearly when information is missing, deadlines are approaching, or decisions are needed.
* Help keep leadership informed without requiring constant follow-up.
* Ask smart questions when something is unclear instead of guessing.

## What We’re Looking For

### Required

* Strong bookkeeping and financial operations experience.
* Experience preparing financial reports and cash flow updates.
* Comfortable managing financial records across multiple entities or business units.
* Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
* Clear written communication.
* Highly organized and reliable.
* Able to work independently and follow through without constant supervision.
* Comfortable handling sensitive financial information with discretion.
* Willing to document processes and improve systems over time.
* Proactive enough to flag risks, missing information, or upcoming obligations before being asked.
* Comfortable using AI tools to improve speed, organization, documentation, reporting, and analysis.

### Strongly Preferred

* Experience working with U.S.-based businesses.
* Experience coordinating with tax preparers, accountants, or CPAs.
* Familiarity with payroll, contractor payments, and 1099 tracking.
* Experience supporting entrepreneurs, real estate businesses, SaaS companies, or multi-entity owners.
* Experience with financial dashboards, spreadsheets, and operational reporting.
* Strong Google Sheets or Excel skills.
* Ability to use AI tools thoughtfully and responsibly in financial operations.

### Bonus Points

* Experience with Xero, QuickBooks, Google Sheets, Stripe, payroll tools, or affiliate tracking.
* Experience with real estate, land investing, or SaaS metrics.
* CPA, accounting degree, bookkeeping certification, or tax-related experience.
* Ability to build or improve financial dashboards.
* Experience building SOPs or documentation for finance workflows.
* Experience creating financial trackers, forecasting models, or automated reporting systems.

## Who You Are

You are someone who:

* Takes initiative instead of waiting to be chased.
* Uses AI and automation to reduce repetitive manual work.
* Thinks critically about financial systems, reporting, and process gaps.
* Notices risks early and communicates them clearly.
* Can turn messy information into organized reports, trackers, and next steps.
* Looks for better ways to complete recurring finance tasks.
* Understands that this role is not just bookkeeping, but financial operations support for leadership decision-making.
* Protects sensitive information and handles financial data with care.
* Is comfortable working in a fast-moving environment with multiple businesses and priorities.
* Can balance accuracy, speed, and good judgment.

## Success in This Role

In the first 30 days, success would look like understanding the financial structure, current reporting process, recurring obligations, payroll/payment cadence, and key entities involved. You should be able to identify what reports exist, what is missing, what needs cleanup, and what recurring workflows must be protected.

By 60–90 days, we would expect you to own the recurring finance cadence more independently, provide clear monthly reports, flag cash flow needs ahead of time, maintain organized records, support tax coordination, and help improve overall financial visibility across the businesses.

Long term, success means leadership has cleaner visibility into cash flow, reporting, taxes, contractor payments, affiliate payouts, and business performance without constantly having to chase information.

## Compensation & Details

* Remote position
* Part-time or full-time depending on experience and fit
* Long-term opportunity for the right person
* Compensation based on experience, skill level, and communication ability
* Must be available for some overlap with U.S. Eastern Time
* Reports directly to leadership

## How to Apply

We’d love to get to know the person behind the resume, not just look through generic links or a basic application.

Please apply here:  ---------- 

In the form, we’ll ask for:

1. Your resume
2. A short summary of your bookkeeping / financial operations experience
3. Your expected hourly rate
4. A short Loom video

For the Loom, please keep it around 2–4 minutes and answer:

1. Tell us about the most complex bookkeeping, financial operations, or multi-entity finance role you’ve handled.
2. How do you stay organized when managing finances across multiple entities, people, accounts, or deadlines?
3. What recurring finance tasks do you think should be automated or systemized?

We personally review applications and videos because we’re looking for someone who brings more than bookkeeping experience. We’re looking for someone thoughtful, proactive, organized, and able to bring real financial clarity to the team.

title: Lovable Developer Needed – Final Testing & Launch Support
description:
Lovable Developer Needed – Final Testing & Launch Support

We are looking for an experienced Lovable developer to review and finalize several existing Lovable projects before launch.

### Responsibilities

* Audit the entire platform and user experience
* Test all pages, forms, booking flows, and automations

title: Email Copywriter (eCommerce)
description:
We are a done-for-you email marketing agency working with eCommerce brands across haircare, skincare, wellness, food & beverage, and gifting niches. We handle everything: strategy, copy, design, and implementation. We are growing fast and looking for someone to grow with us.

We are looking for a talented Email Copywriter who can produce high-converting copy across all areas of email marketing.

What You'll Do:

Flows & Automation
- Write copy for automated email flows including welcome series, abandoned cart, browse abandonment, post-purchase, win-back, sunset, and VIP sequences
- Craft subject lines, preview text, body copy, and CTAs optimized for each flow trigger and audience segment

Campaigns
- Write 2x weekly broadcast campaign emails per client (promotions, product launches, seasonal, storytelling, educational)
- Adapt tone and messaging to match each brand's unique voice

SMS Copy
- Write short-form SMS messages for campaigns and automated flows
- Balance urgency, personality, and compliance within tight character limits

Strategy & Optimization
- Contribute to email calendar planning and content strategy
- Write A/B test variations for subject lines, CTAs, and offer framing
- Review performance data and suggest copy improvements based on open rates, CTR, and revenue per recipient

Creative Collaboration
- Brief the design team on email layout and visual direction
- Write copy that fits within designed templates and renders well across devices

Other Copy Needs
- Write popup and signup form copy to grow client email lists
- Occasionally support with ad copy, landing page copy, or product descriptions as needed

What We're Looking For:
- 3+ years of email copywriting experience, ideally in eCommerce or DTC
- Strong grasp of consumer psychology, storytelling, and direct response
- Ability to write in multiple brand voices: luxury, playful, bold, minimal
- Experience with Klaviyo or similar ESP
- Fast, reliable, and able to manage multiple client accounts simultaneously
- Portfolio of email copy or live campaigns required

Nice to Have:
- Familiarity with Shopify or eCommerce metrics
- SMS copywriting experience
- Understanding of list segmentation and deliverability basics

SKILLS: Copywriting, Email Marketing, Klaviyo, SMS Marketing, eCommerce, Direct Response, Campaign Strategy

title: Go High Level Automation Builder Part-Time / Long-Term
description:

— Tyden Solutions

title: Software Developer
description:
***PLEASE READ ON HOW TO APPLY***

Job Title: Software Developer
Location: Remote | Philippines based ONLY
Salary Range: Php 180,000 per month
A MUST: Minimum of 5 years of solid experience

We’re looking for talented Software Developers to join our remote-first team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for
building and maintaining responsive, high-performing full- stack web applications using Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and
Supabase.

Our ideal candidate writes clean, maintainable code, takes ownership of their work, and thrives in a fast-paced
startup environment where autonomy, communication, and craftsmanship are valued.

Key Responsibilities
- Design, develop, and maintain web applications using TypeScript, React, and Next.js.
- Work with Supabase (PostgreSQL) to design, query, and optimise relational database schemas.
- Integrate third-party APIs, including reading, understanding, and applying API documentation.
- Implement responsive, accessible, and performant user interfaces with Tailwind CSS.
- Utilise AI-powered tools (e.g. Cursor, Claude, and similar) to accelerate development while
maintaining high code quality and exercising sound engineering judgment over AI-generated output.
- Collaborate with other developers and/or stakeholders to refine requirements, propose technical solutions, and deliver features on agreed timelines.
- Work with background jobs and scheduled tasks using Inngest (or similar services) for cron-like and event-driven workflows.
- Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve issues across the stack, including in production environments.
- Produce clear technical documentation for features, architectural decisions, and processes.
- Communicate proactively and clearly within a remote team, including written updates and participation in online meetings across UK-Philippines time zones.

Must-Have Skills:
- Strong proficiency in TypeScript, React, and Next.js
- Experience with Supabase or relational databases (PostgreSQL)
- Ability to read and integrate third-party API documentation
- Strong understanding of Tailwind CSS and responsive design principles
- Familiarity with Git, CI/CD pipelines, and modern development workflows
- Ability to work independently and manage time effectively in a remote environment
- Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
- Comfortable using AI-assisted development tools while maintaining engineering judgment and security best practices

Nice-to-Have Skills
- Experience with React Native and mobile development tools (Expo, Xcode, Android Studio)
- Experience with Inngest or similar background job / cron orchestration tools
- Familiarity with Vercel serverless and edge functions

Tech Stack

Primary:
- TypeScript
- React
- Next.js
- Tailwind CSS
- Supabase (PostgreSQL)

Secondary / Bonus:
- React Native
- Expo
- Python

Tools & Platforms:
- Git / GitHub
- Vercel
- Supabase
- Inngest
- Standard remote collaboration tools (Slack/Teams, Jira/Linear or equivalent)

***PLEASE READ ON HOW TO APPLY***

How to Apply:
To apply for this role, please submit a link to your resume and a brief Loom video (max 2 minutes) introducing yourself and highlighting your skills relevant to the role. In your video, please address the following:
- A short introduction of yourself and why you're interested in this role.
- Your previous experience as a Software Developer and specific experience with Next JS/Supabase
- Key skills or qualifications that make you a strong fit for this position.

Please note: Applications without a video will not be reviewed. We look forward to welcoming a passionate developer who prioritizes clean code, performance optimization, and delivering exceptional user experiences.

title: Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator
description:
Company: Temp Stay USA (Tucson, AZ furnished rentals)
Remote | 20-30 hrs/week | Start ASAP (may grow into more hours as experience / tasks grow)
Hourly Rate: Commensurate with Experience and Fit
Business: Short-term and Mid-Term Rentals Furnished Rentals

Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator

TempStayUSA is seeking a highly organized, tech-savvy Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator to support lead generation, social media operations, prospect outreach, research, and business growth initiatives.

This role sits at the intersection of marketing, operations, lead generation, social media, and business development. The ideal candidate is equally comfortable building relationships, managing systems, researching opportunities, organizing workflows, and supporting growth initiatives across multiple channels.

Key Responsibilities

1 ) Lead Generation & Outreach

* Research landlords, Airbnb hosts, property managers, corporate housing providers, and strategic partners
* Conduct thoughtful outreach through OTAs, email, social media, and other channels
* Build and nurture relationships with prospects over time
* Track communication and follow-up activities across multiple platforms
* Support inbound lead management and prospect nurturing

2) Social Media Operations

* Manage social media scheduling and publishing workflows
* Monitor engagement and respond to comments, messages, and inquiries
* Track growth metrics and performance analytics
* Support hashtag research and audience growth initiatives
* Coordinate content calendars and posting schedules
* Assist with basic video editing (capcut) and content repurposing
* Engage across all channels and repost content to various "groups"

3) Revenue Operations & Systems

* Maintain lead and prospect data within HubSpot
* Support workflow management in ClickUp
* Assist with CRM hygiene and reporting
* Track outreach performance and engagement metrics
* Help improve operational processes and workflows
* Utilize AI tools to improve efficiency and productivity

4)  Research & Market Intelligence

* Research prospective partners and leads
* Identify off-platform contact information and business details
* Conduct targeted online research projects
* Track competitor activity and market trends
* Understand our market and ensure you are "in the know" 
* Support business development initiatives with research and analysis

Most Important Requirements

**Relationship Building & Prospect Nurturing**

Success comes from building authentic relationships, maintaining thoughtful follow-up, and creating trust through consistent communication. 

**Organization & Follow-Through**

You must be comfortable managing communication and tracking activities across OTAs, social media platforms, email campaigns, CRM systems, and project management tools.

**Excellent English Communication Skills**

Strong written and verbal English is required. Professional, natural communication is a critical part of this role.

** Strong Social Media Experience**

Experience managing social media operations, engagement, analytics, scheduling, and audience growth is required. You must be able to describe how you support this role and what YOUR metrics are in building audience. 

**Tech-Savvy & AI-Oriented**

You should be comfortable learning and working within modern software ecosystems and AI-assisted workflows. We use a lot of technical processes - AI Agents for many things, Zapier for many integrations. You must be comfortable learning new systems and processes and comfortable learning how to efficiently prompt AI systems. 

Experience with tools such as:

* HubSpot (Sales & Marketing)
* ClickUp
* Apollo
* Zoho Social
* Canva
*CapCut
* ChatGPT and other AI tools
* Google Workspace
* Hospitable and/or OwnerRez is highly valuable

**STR / OTA Experience Required**

Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, BDC, short-term rentals, hospitality, guest communications, or property management is strongly preferred. Understanding the industry, terminology, and customer mindset is important for success in this role.

Our Ideal Candidate

You are:

* Organized
* Detail-oriented
* Curious
* Proactive
* Relationship-focused
* Comfortable with technology
* Strong at follow-up
* Excited to work in an AI-assisted operating environment

If you enjoy building relationships, solving problems, learning new tools, and helping grow a hospitality business, we'd love to hear from you.

Must have:
Strong written English
Organized + proactive
Reliable daily execution + reporting

How to Apply (Required)
Please send ALL of the following:
  - Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself + why you’re a fit (emphasize relevant experience & achievements)
  - Your resume and/or LinkedIn profile
   - A list of tools you’ve used (CRM, PMS, social tools, etc.)
   - Your portfolio if you have one / or samples of work

Answer this question:
“What would you do in your first 7 days to generate more inquiries?”

Applications without Loom will not be reviewed.

title: eCommerce Virtual Assistant
description:
We are expanding our remote team and looking for a reliable, disciplined, and ambitious eCommerce Virtual Assistant who wants long-term growth.

Our local agency is based in the Philippines and manages multiple eCommerce brands for international clients. This role is for someone who takes work seriously, learns fast, and wants to build a real career in digital work, not just a short-term VA job.

This is a busy and fast-paced role. Since we work with multiple clients, there will be days with several tasks, urgent revisions, and deadlines. We need someone who can stay organized, work under pressure, follow instructions properly, and remain reliable even when the workload gets heavy.

If you are organized, proactive, and can work independently, this is a strong opportunity.

About the Role

Start Date
Immediate

Schedule
Graveyard shift Philippine time
Monday to Friday
Weekends off

Please note that occasional overtime may be required when there are urgent client tasks, important deadlines, or time-sensitive updates. This does not happen every day, but we need someone who understands that eCommerce work can sometimes require flexibility.

Responsibilities

You will support daily operations across multiple eCommerce brands. Tasks include:

• Managing and updating Shopify, WooCommerce, and Amazon stores
• Writing, editing, and optimizing product listings
• Conducting product, competitor, and market research
• Handling basic customer service and order processing
• Completing website updates and admin tasks
• Assisting with marketing and content-related tasks
• Creating simple graphics and visuals using Canva
• Helping with basic website layout and design improvements
• Using ChatGPT and AI tools to improve speed and accuracy
• Following systems, checklists, and instructions precisely
• Handling multiple tasks across different client accounts
• Responding quickly to task updates during working hours
• Staying organized even when the workload is busy

As you improve, you will manage client accounts more independently and take on higher-value tasks.

Requirements

• Fast learner with strong problem-solving skills
• Organized, detail-oriented, and accountable
• Able to follow instructions and meet deadlines
• Strong communication and reliability
• Proactive mindset and good work ethic
• Comfortable working in a busy and fast-paced environment
• Able to handle pressure and urgent tasks professionally
• Own laptop or desktop
• Stable, high-speed internet connection
• Fully available during assigned working hours
• No other job, client, or commitment that will affect your focus, schedule, or performance

Preferred but Not Required

• Experience with Shopify
• Basic graphic design skills or Canva experience
• Understanding of layout, spacing, and clean design
• Familiarity with ChatGPT or other AI tools
• Basic video editing skills using CapCut
• Previous VA, eCommerce, customer service, or admin experience

We prioritize attitude, discipline, consistency, and reliability over years of experience. Skills can be trained, but work ethic, honesty, and commitment are required from the start.

Compensation and Growth

• Monthly Range: ?25,000 to ?30,000
• Performance Bonus: ?5,000 to ?8,000 per month
• Clear path for raises, promotions, and leadership roles
• Long-term stability for consistent top performers

Why Join Us

• Stable long-term work with a structured eCommerce agency
• Clear systems, feedback, and guidance
• Learn Shopify, Amazon, WooCommerce, and TikTok Shop operations
• Work with automation and AI tools daily
• Gain real experience handling international eCommerce brands
• Real opportunity to grow into senior or leadership roles

Important

This is not a passive VA role where you only wait for instructions. We need someone who can move fast, think clearly, stay focused, and take responsibility for their work.

We are looking for someone who wants to grow with the company long term. If you are only looking for a side job, temporary work, or something easy, this may not be the right fit.

Application Instructions

Please submit the following:

Resume
Internet speed test result
Laptop or PC specifications, including model, processor, RAM, and storage
Short cover letter explaining who you are, what you’ve done, and why you are a strong fit

NOTE:
This is a long-term position with strong potential for career growth. We are looking for someone fully committed to the role. You must not have other jobs, clients, or commitments that may interfere with your schedule, focus, availability, or performance.

title: Go High Level Expert
description:
GoHighLevel CRM Builder & Automation Specialist (Systems Operator + Client-Facing)

We are hiring a high-level GoHighLevel builder who can architect, automate, and optimize CRM systems that directly impact revenue.

This is not a VA admin role.

This is a systems builder + client-facing position for someone who understands how leads flow, how conversions happen, and how automation drives growth—and can confidently communicate that to clients.

We run a fast-moving business with real lead volume, real customers, and real money on the line.
If systems break, we feel it immediately.

If you are someone who watches tutorials and “figures things out as you go,” this is not for you.

If you can step in, understand a business model, build clean systems, and speak confidently with clients… keep reading.

What You’ll Own

You will be responsible for building and maintaining the infrastructure that powers our sales, marketing, onboarding, and follow-up—while also supporting clients directly.

- CRM Architecture & System Design
- Build and manage GoHighLevel sub-accounts
- Design pipelines that reflect real sales processes
- Create dashboards leadership can rely on
- Automation & Workflow Execution
- Build advanced workflows, triggers, and automation logic
- Automate lead routing, follow-ups, onboarding, and re-engagement
-Ensure all automations are clean, reliable, and scalable
Funnels, Forms & Conversion Flow

- Build and optimize funnels, landing pages, forms, and surveys
- Connect lead capture directly into pipelines and automation systems
- Improve conversion by reducing friction
- Data Integrity & Segmentation
- Implement clean tagging systems and naming conventions
- Maintain accurate, deduplicated contact data
- Audit and fix messy CRM structures

Integrations & Tech Stack

Integrate GoHighLevel with Shopify and other tools
- Trigger automations from real business events
- Diagnose and fix integration or automation issues quickly
- Client Support & Communication
- Meet with clients to understand their needs and workflows
- Assist with onboarding and system walkthroughs
- Troubleshoot issues and explain solutions clearly
- Act as a bridge between technical systems and client understanding
- Documentation & SOPs
- Document systems so the team and clients can use them
- Keep workflows organized as we scale
- Optimization & Scaling
- Identify bottlenecks and inefficiencies
- Improve systems based on performance
- Continuously upgrade the CRM as the business grows

What Success Looks Like
- Leads are routed instantly and correctly
- Follow-up happens automatically without manual work
- Clients understand and trust the systems you build
- Sales team has full visibility into pipeline and performance
- Systems run clean—and when they don’t, they’re fixed fast

Must-Have Skills (No Exceptions)

- Proven experience building inside GoHighLevel (not just using it)
- Strong understanding of:
- Pipelines and opportunity stages
- Workflow logic and automation timing
- Lead lifecycle and CRM structure
- Experience with email and SMS automation inside GHL
- Experience integrating GoHighLevel with Shopify (Zapier, Make, or direct)
- Ability to troubleshoot broken workflows and integrations quickly
- Strong attention to detail
- Ability to communicate clearly with non-technical clients
- Comfortable getting on Zoom calls and leading conversations
- Reliable internet and consistent availability


EMAIL ONLY:  ----------  WITH :

1.) SUBJECT LINE : GO HIGH LEVEL SPECIALIST
2.) A VIDEO OF YOURSELF EXPLAINING YOUR EXPERIENCE
3.) YOUR RESUME
4.) C2 ENGLISH A MUST
5.) EXPERIENCE USING "UPHEX" IS A MASSIVE BONUS
6.) REQUESTED SALARY
7.) MUST BE ABLE TO WORK EST TIME ZONE


EMAIL ONLY:  ----------

title: Premium Project Manager & Client Experience Lead (100% Remote / US Hours)
description:
We are an elite, high-output boutique business consulting firm that designs and implements scalable operational engines for growing small businesses. We implement premium systems architecture—including  ---------- , ClickUp, Softr, Airtable, and  ---------- —so visionary business leaders can step back from day-to-day chaos and focus entirely on high-value growth.

We move fast, take absolute ownership, and operate at a strict New York standard of excellence. We are currently seeking an elite, heavy-hitting Project Manager & Client Experience Lead to serve as the critical bridge between our consulting strategies, our technical builders, and our high-net-worth clients.

The Mission: Management & Communication (Not Building)
Let’s be entirely clear from day one: You are not building these software platforms. You are managing, communicating, and ensuring the right things happen at the right time.

You will completely own the client experience from the initial onboarding call to final project delivery. If you are an exceptional communicator, a meticulous operational organizer, and someone who gets genuine satisfaction from watching a chaotic business transform into a flawless machine, this is your invitation to the top 1%.

Your Core Responsibilities
1. Client Management & Flawless Communication
The Source of Truth: Own the entire client relationship lifecycle from onboarding through project completion.

Live Leadership: Lead onboarding sessions, operational check-ins, and ongoing alignment calls with absolute confidence and executive poise.

Firewall Triage: Manage high-velocity, professional communications across our executive Gmail, Slack, and WhatsApp Business channels for active projects and sales pipelines.

Proactive Assurance: Ensure that every client always knows exactly what has been completed, what is happening right now, and what milestones are coming next.

2. Project, Timeline & Delivery Tracking
Portfolio Control: Simultaneously track timelines, budgets, task allocations, and roadmaps across multiple active client projects.

Freelancer Execution: Manage our team of technical builders and freelancers. You will ruthlessly hunt down action items, enforce accountability, and aggressively unblock tasks before they cause project delays.

Asset Housekeeping: Maintain, categorize, and organize our internal Loom and Scribe video libraries.

3. Software Partnership Ecosystem
Vendor Relations: Act as the primary professional representative of our firm to software partners ( ---------- , Softr, ClickUp, etc.).

Asset Tracking: Manage referral agreements, track outbound partner commissions, and ensure our corporate tier certifications across relevant platforms remain current and compliant.

4. Strategic Sales Support
Deal Coordination: Assist with sales preparation materials, coordinate discovery calls, participate in sales conversations, and help scope out project parameters.

The Ideal Candidate Profile
Experience & Professional DNA
Technical Ecosystem: Expert-level proficiency in  ----------  and Google Workspace is mandatory.

Tenure: Minimum 2+ years of direct project management or operations experience within a fast-paced agency, consulting, or B2B environment.

Client Mastery: A proven track record of directly managing high-value client relationships, leading live video calls, and resolving friction seamlessly.

The People Director: Exceptional experience managing remote freelancers, designers, or technical developers across competing project timelines.

Ecosystem Familiarity: Highly comfortable navigating the structural frameworks of  ---------- , ClickUp, Softr, Airtable, and  ---------- . You don’t need to know how to code them—you need to know them well enough to manage the people who do.

Technical & AI Fluency
Advanced AI Leverage: Daily, fluent use of ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini to draft flawless communications, summarize meeting transcripts, and optimize your personal organization.

Operational Mindset
Extreme Ownership: You require zero hand-holding. You spot operational bottlenecks weeks before they become issues and resolve them autonomously.

The Security Boundary: We enforce a strict, zero-tolerance policy against job-stacking. We are looking for an exclusive, dedicated partner who wants to plant permanent professional roots with us.

Compensation & Strategic Value
Hourly Rate: $8.00 – $13.00 / hour (Dependent entirely on the depth of your agency project management mileage and communication poise).

Hours: Consistent, guaranteed 40 hours per week operating on US-based hours.

Streamlined Payouts: Fast, automated weekly payments processed securely via the Wise platform.

How to Apply (The Science of Vetting)
We apply a strict filtering process to instantly eliminate automated application bots and average candidates. Failure to follow these exact steps will result in immediate disqualification.

Step 1: Change your email or application subject line to exactly: [Project Manager - Your Full Name]

Step 2: Submit your resume alongside a brief overview of your background managing client relations within an agency or consulting firm.

Step 3: Answer our Core Competency Trap Question with a specific, real-world example:

"We are 48 hours away from a major system launch for a high-paying, assertive consulting client. Suddenly, the client sends an urgent WhatsApp message demanding a massive, completely out-of-scope change to their  ----------  workflow. If you implement this change, it will completely break the  ----------  automation backend that our freelancer spent all week building, delaying the launch.
The client is pushing hard and wants it done immediately. You cannot build or fix the automations yourself. Explain your exact step-by-step strategy to diplomatically manage this client’s expectations, protect our builder's time, and keep the project on track without dropping the ball or letting the founder intervene."

title: Senior Airbnb Operations Manager (Part-Time, Hostaway)
description:
We are seeking an experienced Airbnb Operations Manager to support our growing short-term rental business. This position will begin part-time and has the potential to grow into a full-time role if we d ----------  it is needed..

We currently have five high-end short-term rental properties located in North Carolina and California. Our guests expect exceptional service, fast communication, and prompt issue resolution.

This is NOT an entry-level position. We strongly prefer candidates who have worked for Airbnb in Tier 2 Support, Senior Support, Trust & Safety, Escalations, Claims, Case Management, or a similar role. We are looking for someone who understands Airbnb’s systems, policies, and internal processes at an advanced level and can confidently navigate complex guest, claim, and support situations.

Because we operate hospitality businesses, some evening and weekend availability is required. Guest issues do not always occur during standard business hours, and we are looking for someone who can provide responsive support when needed.

We value organization, ownership, follow-through, urgency, and proactive communication more than speed.

Responsibilities

* Responding to guest inquiries and issues promptly, ideally within minutes whenever possible during working hours
* Guest communication before, during, and after stays
* Managing reservations in Hostaway
* Coordinating cleaners and maintenance vendors
* Managing Airbnb Resolution Center cases from initial submission through escalation, follow-up, and final resolution
* Managing Airbnb damage claims from documentation and submission through final resolution and payment
* Escalating cases with Airbnb Support and following up persistently until the desired outcome is achieved
* Preparing and submitting requests for Airbnb review removals when reviews violate Airbnb policies
* Following up on maintenance, guest, and operational issues until fully resolved
* Keeping tasks organized and updated in Asana
* Communicating proactively with tea ---------- mbers through Slack
* Identifying potential issues before they become problems
* Maintaining clear documentation of actions taken, outstanding issues, and next steps
* Independently managing issues from start to finish without constant supervision

Requirements

You must have:

* Significant Airbnb rental operations experience
* Extensive Hostaway experience
* Experience handling Airbnb Resolution Center cases
* Experience escalating cases with Airbnb Support and following them through to resolution
* Experience managing Airbnb damage claims
* Experience coordinating cleaners and maintenance vendors
* Excellent written English
* Strong organizational skills
* Experience using task management software such as Asana
* Excellent communication skills
* A habit of proactively updating tea ---------- mbers and documenting work
* Strong attention to detail and persistence when managing unresolved issues

Strong Preference

We would especially like to hear from candidates who have:

* Worked for Airbnb in Tier 2 Support, Senior Support, Trust & Safety, Escalations, Claims, Case Management, Community Support, or a similar role
* Worked for a vacation rental management company
* Managed multiple Airbnb properties simultaneously

To Apply

Please answer the following questions without the use of AI:

1. Describe your Airbnb and short-term rental operations experience. Please include number of yours, your level of experience with Hostaway.

2. What is the most complex Airbnb issue you personally handled from start to finish?
(Examples: Resolution Center case, damage claim, guest dispute, review removal, Trust & Safety issue, etc.)

3. What hours are you generally available, including evenings and weekends?

4. Do you understand if this is a part-time role, with potential to lead to a full-time role if needed?

5. If you have worked at AirBnB, what was your job role title and for how long did you work there?

Please begin your application with:

“I am an experienced Airbnb operations professional.”

Applications that do not follow the instructions above will not be considered.

title: Tech VA
description:
JL Marketing is a fast-growing Meta Badged Partner specializing in lead generation for health, wellness, and fitness businesses. We're excited to expand our remote team and are looking for a Tech VA (Tech Virtual Assistant) who is passionate about technology, eager to learn new skills, and excited to become an integral part of our team! If you're a self-starter with a great attitude and a desire to grow your tech abilities, this is the perfect opportunity for you. We are only reviewing applications submitted through our website.

Role Overview

We're searching for a dedicated Tech VA (Tech Virtual Assistant) to support our tech team. In this role, you'll get hands-on experience with various technologies and processes, including helping with our CRM platform (HubSpot), assisting with simple automations, and supporting web updates. This is an entry-level position where we'll provide full training on everything you need to know. You'll have a fantastic opportunity to learn and grow within an innovative tech environment.

Responsibilities

Support CRM Tasks: Help with managing our CRM (HubSpot), including entering data, keeping information organized, and supporting basic workflow setups.
Assist with Automations: Learn to assist in creating and managing simple automations to help our internal operations run more smoothly.
Web Asset Support: Learn to help manage and update web assets, including WordPress sites, to ensure they're user-friendly and up-to-date.
Technical Assistance: Work alongside our tech team to help identify and resolve minor technical issues.
Project Coordination: Assist in keeping track of project progress and providing updates to the team.

Qualifications

Eager to Learn: A strong interest in technology and a genuine desire to learn new software, systems, and processes.
Great Listener & Follows Directions: Excellent at understanding and following instructions carefully.
Team Player: Open to collaborating with a supportive team and contributing to a positive work environment.
Reliable Communication: Good written and verbal English communication skills, with a proactive approach to asking questions and providing updates.
Comfortable with Remote Work: Able to work effectively from home and align your working hours closely with US time zones.

What We Offer

Full Training Provided: We are committed to teaching you everything you need to succeed in this role—no prior experience required!
Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive pay for motivated individuals.
Flexible Remote Work: Enjoy the flexibility of a fully remote position, allowing you to work from anywhere.
Growth & Learning: Opportunity to work with a fun, supportive team and contribute to innovative projects in the AI automation and marketing space.
Collaborative Environment: Join a dynamic team that meets daily on Zoom, with regular huddles to align on goals and share ideas. Your contributions will be valued and make a real impact.

How to Apply

Ready to start your tech journey and join a dynamic team at JL Marketing? We are only reviewing applications submitted through our website. To demonstrate your attention to detail, please start your answer to the final question on the application form with the word BLUE. Please fill out our application here: jlmarketing.com/tech-va-hiring-application/

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Technical Operations Specialist (Tracking, Integrations & Automation)
description:
Technical Operations Specialist (Tracking, Integrations & Automation)

About Us

We operate multiple online businesses that rely heavily on accurate tracking, attribution, integrations, automation and data flows.

We are looking for a highly technical and detail-oriented Technical Operations Specialist who enjoys solving problems, debugging systems and ensuring that data moves correctly between platforms.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys figuring out why something is not working and takes pride in making systems reliable, scalable and well documented.

----------

Primary Responsibility

Your primary responsibility is ensuring that tracking, attribution, integrations and automation workflows work correctly across all systems.

- If tracking breaks, we lose visibility.
- If postbacks fail, campaigns cannot be optimized.
- If integrations fail, leads and revenue data become unreliable.

You will be responsible for identifying issues, implementing solutions and making sure systems continue to operate correctly.

----------

Core Focus (80%)

Tracking & Attribution

You will work extensively with:

* Meta Pixel
* Meta Conversions API (CAPI)
* Google Ads Conversion Tracking
* Google Tag Manager (GTM)
* UTM tracking
* gclid and fbclid troubleshooting
* Conversion validation
* Attribution debugging

Postbacks & Affiliate Tracking

A significant part of this role involves:

* Affiliate network integrations
* Postback setup and testing
* Conversion tracking validation
* Lead tracking validation
* Revenue tracking validation
* Troubleshooting missing conversions
* Investigating attribution discrepancies

Webhooks & API Integrations

You will regularly work with:

* Webhooks
* REST APIs
* JSON payloads
* Authentication methods
* CRM integrations
* Lead routing systems
* Event-based systems

----------

Secondary Focus (20%)

Automation & Data Flows

You will:

* Build and maintain automation workflows
* Debug automation failures
* Troubleshoot data flow issues
* Validate event delivery
* Monitor implementation quality

Tools may include:

* n8n
*  ---------- 
* Zapier

Technical Operations

You will also assist with:

* Maintaining technical documentation
* Managing documentation platforms
* Testing implementations before launch
* Monitoring operational systems
* Reproducing and documenting bugs
* Supporting internal technical projects
* Keeping implementation documentation up to date

Customer Implementations

You will help onboard customers by:

* Configuring integrations
* Testing tracking implementations
* Validating event flows
* Verifying attribution setups
* Creating implementation documentation

Technical Troubleshooting

You should be comfortable investigating issues such as:

* Leads not arriving in a CRM
* Conversions missing in Meta or Google Ads
* Failed postbacks
* Webhooks not firing
* API requests being rejected
* Attribution mismatches
* Data missing from reports
* Automation workflows failing
* Incorrect data mappings
* Missing events or revenue data

----------

Typical Week

A typical week may include:

* Debugging a Meta Conversions API implementation
* Testing affiliate network postbacks
* Investigating why conversions are not appearing in Google Ads
* Reviewing webhook payloads
* Validating CRM lead delivery
* Troubleshooting automation workflows
* Setting up tracking for a new customer
* Investigating attribution discrepancies
* Updating technical documentation
* Reproducing bugs and documenting findings
* Assisting with internal technical systems

----------

Requirements

Must Have

* Strong understanding of Meta Pixel
* Strong understanding of Meta Conversions API
* Strong understanding of Google Ads tracking
* Strong understanding of Google Tag Manager
* Strong understanding of affiliate tracking and postbacks
* Strong understanding of webhooks
* Experience with API integrations
* Experience with JSON payloads
* Experience with n8n,  ----------  or Zapier
* Strong troubleshooting mindset
* Excellent written and spoken English

Nice To Have

* CRM experience (HubSpot, Pipedrive, GoHighLevel or similar)
* Postman experience
* Basic SQL knowledge
* Experience troubleshooting data pipelines
* Experience troubleshooting scraping systems
* Basic frontend knowledge
* Basic GitHub experience
* Basic Next.js familiarity
* Familiarity with documentation platforms such as Fumadocs, Docusaurus or Nextra
* Experience maintaining technical documentation
* Basic understanding of deployment workflows

----------

What We Value

We care more about problem-solving ability than certifications.

We are looking for someone who:

* Enjoys debugging technical issues
* Thinks systematically
* Documents their work
* Takes ownership
* Communicates clearly
* Is proactive and detail-oriented
* Can work independently
* Wants to grow with the company long term

----------

Compensation

* Full-time position
* $800/month during the initial 2-month probation period
* Increased to $1,000/month after successful completion of probation
* Long-term opportunity with room to grow

title: High-Level Content & Visibility Operator (Kajabi • YouTube • Podcast • Growth)
description:
I’m not looking for a basic VA.
I’m looking for someone EXCEPTIONAL!

Someone who understands how to take a message, a movement, a personal brand — and turn it into visibility, authority, growth, and demand.
Someone who understands energy and execution.
Someone who gets why certain content lands… and why most content disappears into the void.

ABOUT ME
My name is Keri Blair.
I help high-performing women expand into the next level of who they were always meant to be.
The work blends:
identity transformation
nervous system work
wealth expansion
energy work
embodiment
visibility
personal power
emotional mastery
My audience includes high-level women:
CEOs, financial advisors, entrepreneurs, executives, and women who are done playing small.
This brand is luxury, depth, truth, transformation, magnetism, and expansion.
And I need someone who can help me build the media side of it at a very high level.

WHO I’M LOOKING FOR
You are not just “good at tasks.”
You understand:
branding
psychology
visibility
conversion
aesthetics
emotional resonance
audience growth
content ecosystems
You understand what makes people: stop scrolling, click, watch, feel something, trust, buy
You know how to create content that feels elevated, intentional, clean, powerful, and alive.
You know the difference between content that looks edited… and content that actually moves people.

YOU MUST BE VERY STRONG IN:
Kajabi
YouTube SEO
Podcast editing & production (Riverside)
Short-form video editing
Repurposing content strategically
Organizing content systems
Understanding growth

YOUR ROLE
You’ll help take long-form content (FB Lives, podcast episodes, trainings, videos, etc.) and turn it into a powerful visibility machine across platforms.
This may include:
Kajabi setup + organization
landing pages
automations
course uploads
podcast production
YouTube optimization
titles/descriptions/tags/thumbnails
editing reels + clips
content repurposing
visibility strategy
helping content convert
organizing workflows and systems
helping scale the brand overall

THIS IS IMPORTANT:
I move fast.
I need someone proactive, emotionally intelligent, creative, detail-oriented, responsive, and highly accountable.
I do not want someone who disappears for hours.
I do not want endless over-editing.
I do not want corporate-feeling content.
I do not want generic.
I want someone who understands:
clean. magnetic. elevated. impactful. alive.

BONUS IF YOU:
understand luxury/premium brands
understand coaching/personal brands
understand female audiences
have strong design taste
understand storytelling and hooks
know how to increase audience growth organically
understand content strategy deeply
can think independently
bring ideas proactively

TO APPLY:
Please send:
Examples of your work
YouTube channels/podcasts/social brands you’ve helped grow
Your Kajabi experience
A short Loom video introducing yourself
What you think most personal brands are currently getting wrong with content
Your hourly rate + availability during MST hours
If you are truly exceptional, there is opportunity for long-term growth and higher compensation.
I’m not looking to build average.
And I’m not looking for someone who wants to stay small.

title: VA - Exec Assistant
description: I am seeking an executive assistant to work with me as the owner of a roofing company to help me manage and grow my Go High Level CRM with automation, AI tools, landing pages and anything that can help grow without additional labor.  Also open to assisting with Appointment setting, lead aquisition, lead follow-up, facebook ad management, social media management.

title: GHL/ SOCIAL MEDIA CONCIERGE
description:
The Red Pearl Standard™

POSITION: GHL / Social Media Concierge 
Part-Time to Full-Time | Paid Trial Period | Long-Term Opportunity

ABOUT THE COMPANY

The Red Pearl Standard™ is a behavioral health systems consulting and leadership development firm founded by a healthcare executive, consultant, speaker, and educator with 17+ years of clinical and operational experience. Our mission is to elevate healthcare leadership, spotlight extraordinary nurses, and help healthcare organizations achieve operational excellence.

ABOUT THE ROLE::
We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and tech-savvy GHL / Social Media Concierge to support the Founder & CEO directly. This is not a general VA role. You will be working inside a fast-moving, high-standard executive brand and you must be able to keep up.

This role combines:
- GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM management, automation, and pipeline oversight
- Social media content scheduling, engagement, and repurposing
- Video editing and multi-platform content creation
- Lead tracking and pipeline management
- Zoom event coordination and support
- Business operations and executive support

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:
The ideal candidate:
Has hands-on GoHighLevel experience (not just awareness, actual builds)
 Manages social media for professionals or executives (LinkedIn experience strongly preferred)
 Is proficient in Canva, CapCut, BIGVU, or similar tools
 Can repurpose long-form video into multiple content assets
 Has experience coordinating virtual events (Zoom)
Is detail-oriented and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously
 Communicates proactively and meets deadlines without being chased
 Has a reliable internet connection, quiet workspace, and professional setup
 Is comfortable on camera for daily check-in meetings

Experience working with healthcare professionals or executive brands is a strong plus.

COMMITMENT & COMMUNICATION REQUIREMENTS:

This role requires consistent communication, accountability, and professionalism. The selected candidate will be required to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) due to access to confidential business, client, and operational information.

During the initial one-week paid trial period, daily Zoo ---------- etings are required for training, alignment, task review, and goal setting. You must be punctual, maintain a professional appearance, keep your camera on, and have a stable internet connection.

After the trial week, meeting frequency will be adjusted based on performance and demonstrated independence.

This position follows a structured path:
One-week paid trial period
90-day probationary period (upon successful trial completion)
Long-term position with The Red Pearl Standard™ (upon successful probation)

HOW TO APPLY:
Applications submitted without the completed screening form will NOT be considered.

STEP 1: Review this job description in full.
STEP 2: Complete the mandatory competency screening form here :
 ----------

title: Operations & Project Execution (Fast Growing)
description:
Operations Manager-Execution Partner-Project Killer-Fast-Growing Comic Book Company
Own the operations backbone of a niche comic book brand, work directly with leadership, and get promoted fairly by numbers. 

This role is for that special person who wants to become truly exceptional. If that’s you, you already know this message is your signal.

What You Get If You Win Here
- High autonomy: once you’re ramped, you own your queue, schedule, and methods as long as the KPIs are hit. Lots of freedom to figure it out, great for someone that loves building with Legos
- Direct proximity to leadership: you work daily with the Ops Manager on the most important projects in the company.
- Clear advancement path: your growth is tied to visible numbers (tasks shipped, zero-miss cadence, freed founder/ops time, profit/leading metrics), not politics.
- Insane skill growth: the pace and standards here mean every 12 months with us will feel like 3 years of experience in a normal VA/ops role. You can leapfrog your friends. If you join this will change your life. You will work harder than you’ve ever worked before and grow faster than you’ve ever grown before. 
- Comic Books!: you’re not just pushing random SaaS tickets; you’re inside a real comic book store seeing rare old comic books, private deals, and the business behind the Hollywood Marvel Movies that everyone loves!
If you’re that person who enjoys working really hard to the point that it takes 2-3 people to do what you did listen up: 
Our tasks in asana for projects and ongoing operations are moving at a snail’s pace.
Ops Manager + CEO is chasing tasks, slack policing, cleaning up Asana, and re-explaining context instead of focusing on their jobs.

We’ve had “pleasant” VAs who:
Can’t drive tasks to completion independently
Mark tasks complete with no proof
Miss reports/skip cadence and require chasing. 
Drift on ownership, urgency & follow thru
Need hand-holding 
I don’t need another task hopper. I need a project killer who comes into ambiguity, creates structure, drives tasks to completion, and actually frees the Ops Manager to focus on his key projects. If this works, his deep work hours and project output go up because you’re handling everything else. We will measure that and tie your promotions/raises to it. This is not for everyone. We’re looking for winners. 

What You’ll Actually Do
You will be Ops Manager’s execution partner and force-multiplier. Concrete outputs:
Own Asana: every task has an owner, due date, Done Check, and proof link when completed
Maintain an active queue of 20+ meaningful tasks, prioritized against our key rocks (Marketing, Sales, Ongoing Operations, Cleanup, Admin)
Ship 25+ tasks/week with proof, minimal rework, and zero “ghosted” items
Proactively handle operational issues/fires in designated lanes
Maintain Reporting at Set Cadence, Follow-up loops across projects
Add leverage: plug AI into our workflows (Asana, docs, reports) to make us faster and cleaner without being asked
We do NOT need more SOPS-although you might tighten them on the fly. We need someone to do the operations. If you want to sit and write playbooks all day, this is the wrong seat-that will be an earned responsibility later. If you aren’t ready to work hard daily do not apply. 

How We Set You Up To Win (First 90 Days)
We don’t just throw you in the deep end. In your first 30 days, you’ll shadow the Ops Manager, learn our Asana + reporting systems, and get a clear scorecard so you always know what “great” looks like. By 30 days, you’ll own your own project lanes with weekly 1:1s for feedback, support, and removing blockers so you can move faster instead of guessing what we want.

Real Growth Example 
One of our Filipino teammates started with us on basic execution work at ~$800/month. Over time, he took ownership of his queue, fixed broken processes, and became the person we trusted to “just handle it.” As his scope grew, so did his comp and stability. Now he makes ~$2,500/month (and we’re working on getting him even more) because he stuck with it and actually grew. A lot of people say they want that, but their resumes show 6–12 month hops. The pattern here is simple: prove you can own outcomes over time, and we grow your responsibilities, trust, and pay accordingly.

Schedule, Hours & Time Zone (No Surprises)
This is a full time, US hours role (roughly US Eastern business hours) with a true 50–60 hour workweek during this growth season. Expect most weeks to be closer to that 60 hour mark. If you want a strict 40 hour cap, this is the wrong seat; if you want a place where extra effort is noticed, measured, and rewarded, you’ll feel at home here. This is not just a job. This is a commitment.

What Winning Looks Like
By Day 30: Ops Manager is spending ~95% of his time on projects, not Slack/Asana/admin fires, because you’re handling those and executing projects alongside him. Profit and leading metrics are moving up faster because of you

By 12 months: You are Ops Manager’s go to operator & on track to become Ops Manager. Nothing important moves in Ops without you knowing. You own the Asana board, key ops projects, and reporting cadence. You’re the person we trust to “just handle it,” and your comp reflects that increased ownership. 

If you become that go to operator, you’ll have real say in how we run Ops – you won’t just be ‘executing tickets,’ you’ll help design the system.”

Who This Is NOT For
You prefer to ‘coordinate’ and assign tasks to others instead of doing the work yourself with excellence.
You want to manage people before you’ve truly proven you can personally execute at a high level. 
You want a chill, low-pressure 9–5
You need to be told what to do all day and hate being judged by objective numbers
You mark tasks complete without proof or next steps
You change jobs every 6–18 months and blame the company/manager/market/
You get defensive or freeze when given direct feedback or pressure
You are currently juggling multiple full time clients and want to “squeeze us in” on the side.
You are mass applying to dozens of jobs and just want to see who bites first.


Who This IS For
You’ve stayed 2–3+ years in at least one role and can prove outcomes
You like pressure, highly competitive environments, being the one who “gets it done” when others stall
You’ve built systems (trackers, automations, SOPs), not just followed them
You are tech-comfortable: Asana (or similar), CRMs, zaps, AI tools, simple web/ops/marketing stacks
You’re willing to bet on one company instead of juggling lots of smaller, easier gigs.
You hate corporate BS– endless meetings, vague goals, and politics – and would rather be rewarded for what you actually create.
You’re happy to grind 50–60 hours/week no issues. We’re in a BIG growth & push season. It will be like this for the next year and then we’ll see. Everyone wants to grow a lot, but it’s actually happening here.  

Comp & Growth
(All of this is written into your contract.)
Months 1–3: Ramp / Trial
• $800/month base, guaranteed, while you learn our systems and we test fit.
• No bonus in this phase. You’re paid to prove you can hit the KPI floors.
Month 4+ – Performance Based Upside
• Base stays at $800 until you’ve hit KPI floors for 2 straight months. After that, base increases to $900/month going forward.
• On top of base, you can earn:
$400 bonus in months where you hit 3 out of 4 KPIs (with Output as one of them).
$1,000 bonus in months where you hit 4 out of 4 KPIs on the weekly scorecard.
• In practice, that means:
Solid performance months ~$1,200–$1,300/month
Killer months (4/4 KPIs)~$1,800–$1,900/month
All bonuses are 100% KPI based  (shipped outcomes, taking load off the Ops Manager, zero CEO/manager rescues, tight open loops) and 4/5 culture.

Training & Growth Investment (on top of pay)
We actively invest in making you a sharper teammate. Over time, we’ll put thousands of dollars into your development (courses, coaching, playbooks, and tools) that we choose together based on what will make you more effective in this seat.
We also protect uninterrupted deep work blocks for you every week (no rando ---------- etings, no Slack spam). You’re paid to ship outcomes, not sit in back to back Zoom calls.

Important: This is an ON FIELD role. You will not have a team under you to delegate to. 
95% of your time is doing the work yourself, 5% is light coordination. If your default mode is to make plans and pass tasks to others, this will be a bad fit. 

Compensation here is performance based. Raises are earned through clearly exceeding KPIs and demonstrating core values consistently. If you want “stability” to mean “I can coast and still get raises,” this won’t feel stable. If you want stability to mean “the better I get, the more valuable and secure I become,” you’ll like it here.

How To Apply (24-Hour Test)
Our Hiring Process (So There Are No Surprises)

If your 24 hour answers impress us, the next steps are:
A short interview focused on your past execution.
A live 60 minute work trial inside our real tools where you’ll work directly with the Ops Manager. We do this because talk is cheap. If you don’t like being tested on real work before getting hired, this won’t be a fit.

Do NOT click quick-apply. In the next 24 hours, you must:

Reply with answers to ALL of these (short but specific; bullets are fine):
Q1: Describe the most complex project you personally drove fro ---------- ssy start to completion where you did most of the execution yourself, not just assigning tasks. Include at least one example of how you set your own deadlines and moved forward when other people were slow to respond.” 
Q2: Share 3 links or screenshots (Google Docs, Sheets, Asana boards, trackers, automations, etc.) that you built yourself which show how you organize and drive work. Briefly explain each.
Q3: You have 30 tasks due this week and realize you’ll miss 5. Walk me through, step-by-step, how you handle it with your manager and stakeholders.
Q4:  Below are 6 messy “tasks” we actually see (all dumped into one list):
– “Fix Asana, it’s a mess”
– “Seller lead texting follow up??”
– “Finance com/projections cadence d2 ”
– “Inventory backlog insane”
– “Need better reporting”
– “Update SOPs
In this role you will NOT have anyone to delegate to assume it’s just you and the Ops Manager.
In a Google Doc, please:

Rewrite these into clear tasks with: Owner (you or Ops Manager), Due Date (next 7–30 days), and a concrete Done Check + proof required. “Write your task descriptions as if you are committing: ‘I will do X by Y time. If Z doesn’t happen by [time], here’s my fallback.’ Generic ‘follow up / request access’ answers will fail you.” 
Put them in the order you’d actually do them and explain why in 3–5 bullet points.

Link the Google Doc here (view access).

Spend no more than 30–45 minutes. If you can’t figure this out on your own, this role will be too hard in real life – that’s okay, just skip applying.

Attach your PDF resume/Google Drive link.
