Proactively handle operational issues/fires in designated lanes
Maintain Reporting at Set Cadence, Follow-up loops across projects
Add leverage: plug AI into our workflows (Asana, docs, reports) to make us faster and cleaner without being asked
We do NOT need more SOPS-although you might tighten them on the fly. We need someone to do the operations. If you want to sit and write playbooks all day, this is the wrong seat-that will be an earned responsibility later. If you aren’t ready to work hard daily do not apply. 

How We Set You Up To Win (First 90 Days)
We don’t just throw you in the deep end. In your first 30 days, you’ll shadow the Ops Manager, learn our Asana + reporting systems, and get a clear scorecard so you always know what “great” looks like. By 30 days, you’ll own your own project lanes with weekly 1:1s for feedback, support, and removing blockers so you can move faster instead of guessing what we want.

Real Growth Example 
One of our Filipino teammates started with us on basic execution work at ~$800/month. Over time, he took ownership of his queue, fixed broken processes, and became the person we trusted to “just handle it.” As his scope grew, so did his comp and stability. Now he makes ~$2,500/month (and we’re working on getting him even more) because he stuck with it and actually grew. A lot of people say they want that, but their resumes show 6–12 month hops. The pattern here is simple: prove you can own outcomes over time, and we grow your responsibilities, trust, and pay accordingly.

Schedule, Hours & Time Zone (No Surprises)
This is a full time, US hours role (roughly US Eastern business hours) with a true 50–60 hour workweek during this growth season. Expect most weeks to be closer to that 60 hour mark. If you want a strict 40 hour cap, this is the wrong seat; if you want a place where extra effort is noticed, measured, and rewarded, you’ll feel at home here. This is not just a job. This is a commitment.

What Winning Looks Like
By Day 30: Ops Manager is spending ~95% of his time on projects, not Slack/Asana/admin fires, because you’re handling those and executing projects alongside him. Profit and leading metrics are moving up faster because of you

By 12 months: You are Ops Manager’s go to operator & on track to become Ops Manager. Nothing important moves in Ops without you knowing. You own the Asana board, key ops projects, and reporting cadence. You’re the person we trust to “just handle it,” and your comp reflects that increased ownership. 

If you become that go to operator, you’ll have real say in how we run Ops – you won’t just be ‘executing tickets,’ you’ll help design the system.”

Who This Is NOT For
You prefer to ‘coordinate’ and assign tasks to others instead of doing the work yourself with excellence.
You want to manage people before you’ve truly proven you can personally execute at a high level. 
You want a chill, low-pressure 9–5
You need to be told what to do all day and hate being judged by objective numbers
You mark tasks complete without proof or next steps
You change jobs every 6–18 months and blame the company/manager/market/
You get defensive or freeze when given direct feedback or pressure
You are currently juggling multiple full time clients and want to “squeeze us in” on the side.
You are mass applying to dozens of jobs and just want to see who bites first.


Who This IS For
You’ve stayed 2–3+ years in at least one role and can prove outcomes
You like pressure, highly competitive environments, being the one who “gets it done” when others stall
You’ve built systems (trackers, automations, SOPs), not just followed them
You are tech-comfortable: Asana (or similar), CRMs, zaps, AI tools, simple web/ops/marketing stacks
You’re willing to bet on one company instead of juggling lots of smaller, easier gigs.
You hate corporate BS– endless meetings, vague goals, and politics – and would rather be rewarded for what you actually create.
You’re happy to grind 50–60 hours/week no issues. We’re in a BIG growth & push season. It will be like this for the next year and then we’ll see. Everyone wants to grow a lot, but it’s actually happening here.  

Comp & Growth
(All of this is written into your contract.)
Months 1–3: Ramp / Trial
• $800/month base, guaranteed, while you learn our systems and we test fit.
• No bonus in this phase. You’re paid to prove you can hit the KPI floors.
Month 4+ – Performance Based Upside
• Base stays at $800 until you’ve hit KPI floors for 2 straight months. After that, base increases to $900/month going forward.
• On top of base, you can earn:
$400 bonus in months where you hit 3 out of 4 KPIs (with Output as one of them).
$1,000 bonus in months where you hit 4 out of 4 KPIs on the weekly scorecard.
• In practice, that means:
Solid performance months ~$1,200–$1,300/month
Killer months (4/4 KPIs)~$1,800–$1,900/month
All bonuses are 100% KPI based  (shipped outcomes, taking load off the Ops Manager, zero CEO/manager rescues, tight open loops) and 4/5 culture.

Training & Growth Investment (on top of pay)
We actively invest in making you a sharper teammate. Over time, we’ll put thousands of dollars into your development (courses, coaching, playbooks, and tools) that we choose together based on what will make you more effective in this seat.
We also protect uninterrupted deep work blocks for you every week (no rando ---------- etings, no Slack spam). You’re paid to ship outcomes, not sit in back to back Zoom calls.

Important: This is an ON FIELD role. You will not have a team under you to delegate to. 
95% of your time is doing the work yourself, 5% is light coordination. If your default mode is to make plans and pass tasks to others, this will be a bad fit. 

Compensation here is performance based. Raises are earned through clearly exceeding KPIs and demonstrating core values consistently. If you want “stability” to mean “I can coast and still get raises,” this won’t feel stable. If you want stability to mean “the better I get, the more valuable and secure I become,” you’ll like it here.

How To Apply (24-Hour Test)
Our Hiring Process (So There Are No Surprises)

If your 24 hour answers impress us, the next steps are:
A short interview focused on your past execution.
A live 60 minute work trial inside our real tools where you’ll work directly with the Ops Manager. We do this because talk is cheap. If you don’t like being tested on real work before getting hired, this won’t be a fit.

Do NOT click quick-apply. In the next 24 hours, you must:

Reply with answers to ALL of these (short but specific; bullets are fine):
Q1: Describe the most complex project you personally drove fro ---------- ssy start to completion where you did most of the execution yourself, not just assigning tasks. Include at least one example of how you set your own deadlines and moved forward when other people were slow to respond.” 
Q2: Share 3 links or screenshots (Google Docs, Sheets, Asana boards, trackers, automations, etc.) that you built yourself which show how you organize and drive work. Briefly explain each.
Q3: You have 30 tasks due this week and realize you’ll miss 5. Walk me through, step-by-step, how you handle it with your manager and stakeholders.
Q4:  Below are 6 messy “tasks” we actually see (all dumped into one list):
– “Fix Asana, it’s a mess”
– “Seller lead texting follow up??”
– “Finance com/projections cadence d2 ”
– “Inventory backlog insane”
– “Need better reporting”
– “Update SOPs
In this role you will NOT have anyone to delegate to assume it’s just you and the Ops Manager.
In a Google Doc, please:

Rewrite these into clear tasks with: Owner (you or Ops Manager), Due Date (next 7–30 days), and a concrete Done Check + proof required. “Write your task descriptions as if you are committing: ‘I will do X by Y time. If Z doesn’t happen by [time], here’s my fallback.’ Generic ‘follow up / request access’ answers will fail you.” 
Put them in the order you’d actually do them and explain why in 3–5 bullet points.

Link the Google Doc here (view access).

Spend no more than 30–45 minutes. If you can’t figure this out on your own, this role will be too hard in real life – that’s okay, just skip applying.

Attach your PDF resume/Google Drive link.

title: AI Video Production & Content Growth Specialist
description:
Part-Time | Remote (Philippines) | Long-Term Opportunity

Compensation

* $8–10 USD per hour
* Approximately 10–15 hours per week to start
* Long-term opportunity
* Performance-based increases available
* Opportunity for additional hours as responsibilities grow

---
 IMPORTANT

To confirm you read this posting, begin your application with:

**MAX BUILDS SYSTEMS**

Applications that do not include this phrase may not be reviewed.

You must also submit a short Loom video (60–90 seconds) introducing yourself and explaining your experience with HeyGen, ElevenLabs, and video editing.

---

About Us

We are a rapidly growing U.S.-based commercial insurance agency specializing in trucking and non-emergency medical transportation (NEMT) companies nationwide.

We are building a modern, technology-enabled insurance platform using AI, automation, content, and exceptional customer service.

We are looking for a highly capable AI Video Production & Content Growth Specialist to help us create professional videos, build reusable content systems, and support sales, marketing, recruiting, and training initiatives.

This is a long-term opportunity for someone who enjoys building systems, learning AI tools, and creating high-quality content efficiently.

---

What You'll Do

You will help transform ideas, scripts, recordings, meetings, interviews, and educational content into professional marketing and training assets.

Your responsibilities will include:

AI Video Creation

* Create AI avatar videos using HeyGen
* Create and manage AI voices using ElevenLabs
* Produce professional videos for sales, marketing, and education
* Create personalized prospect and client videos

Video Editing

* Edit videos using CapCut or Adobe Premiere Pro
* Add captions, graphics, transitions, branding, music, and B-roll
* Create short-form and long-form versions of content
* Optimize videos for social media and email campaigns

Content Repurposing

Turn one piece of content into multiple assets:

* Social media videos
* LinkedIn content
* YouTube Shorts
* Educational clips
* Marketing videos
* Training content

Asset Management

Build and maintain:

* Video templates
* Brand assets
* B-roll libraries
* Music libraries
* Voice libraries
* Avatar libraries
* Content libraries

Internal Projects

Create videos for:

* Client education
* Sales/Marketing
* Employee training
* SOPs
* Sales support
* Company initiatives

---

Typical Week

Examples of work may include:

Monday

* Create renewal campaign video
* Edit social media clips

Tuesday

* Create personalized prospect video
* Build SendSpark content

Wednesday

* Create educational trucking or NEMT content
* Organize content assets

Thursday

* Edit recruiting or training videos

Friday

* Create market update videos
* Repurpose existing content into multiple formats

No two weeks will be exactly the same.

---

Required Skills

You should have experience with:

* HeyGen
* ElevenLabs
* CapCut and/or Adobe Premiere Pro
* Canva
* AI-powered content creation
* Video editing
* English communication
* File organization
* Content creation workflows

---

Preferred Skills

Bonus points if you have experience with:

* SendSpark
* ChatGPT
* Claude
*  ---------- 
* n8n
* LinkedIn content
* YouTube content
* Social media marketing
* Graphic design
* Content strategy

---

What Success Looks Like

A successful candidate can take:

* A rough idea
* A script
* A meeting recording
* A voice memo

And quickly turn it into:

* Professional video
* Social media clips
* Training content
* Marketing assets
* Reusable templates

With minimal supervision.

---

First 90 Days

Month 1

Build:

* Video brand kit
* Intro/outro animations
* Caption styles
* Video templates
* Trucking renewal videos
* NEMT renewal videos
* New venture video templates

Month 2

Build:

* FAQ video library
* Safety video templates
* Recruiting videos
* Employee training templates
* Market update templates

Month 3

Launch:

* Ongoing content production system
* Content asset library
* Repurposing workflow
* Monthly content calendar

---

Top Performers May Eventually

* Lead our entire video production function
* Help build AI-powered content systems
* Manage content production workflows
* Receive additional hours and compensation
* Become a key long-ter ---------- mber of our team

---

Application Requirements

Please submit:

1. A brief introduction
2. Your hourly rate
3. Your experience with HeyGen
4. Your experience with ElevenLabs
5. Your experience with CapCut or Premiere Pro
6. Links to at least 3 videos you personally created
7. A Loom video (60–90 seconds) introducing yourself
8. The AI tools you use most frequently

---

Paid Test Project

Qualified applicants will be invited to complete a paid test project.

The project will include:

* One AI avatar video
* One edited social media video
* One short-form video version

Compensation will be provided for the test project.

---

Who We Are Looking For

We are looking for someone who is:

* Reliable
* Organized
* Creative
* Detail-oriented
* Fast
* Tech-savvy
* AI-focused
* A builder and problem-solver

If you enjoy using AI to create content, improve systems, and help build a growing company, we would love to hear from you.

Speed & Efficiency Matter

We value quality, but we also value efficiency.

We are looking for someone who knows how to leverage AI tools, templates, and systems to produce high-quality work quickly.

The ideal candidate is not someone who spends hours perfecting a simple video.

The ideal candidate understands how to use tools like HeyGen, ElevenLabs, CapCut, Canva, ChatGPT, and other AI tools to create professional content efficiently and consistently.

We care about outcomes, quality, attention to detail, and continuous improvement—not simply the number of hours worked.

Hours & Workload

This is a part-time position with an expected workload of approximately 10–15 hours per week to start.
Workload may vary based on projects, content initiatives, and business needs. Some weeks may require additional hours, while other weeks may require fewer hours.
We are looking for a long-term tea ---------- mber who is comfortable working in a flexible, project-driven environment.
As the company continues to grow, there may be opportunities for additional hours and expanded responsibilities.

title: SENIOR MARKETING & CHANNEL SPECIALIST | ELEVATED HOSPITALITY PARTNERS
description:
SENIOR MARKETING & CHANNEL SPECIALIST

ABOUT ELEVATED HOSPITALITY PARTNERS

Elevated Hospitality Partners is a growing vacation rental management platform supporting multiple local hospitality brands across Texas, New Mexico, and California. We manage high-quality vacation homes with a focus on strong homeowner partnerships, thoughtful guest experiences, local market expertise, and disciplined operational execution.

As we continue to grow, we are building a centralized marketing function that supports all of our markets, improves the performance of our listings, strengthens our homeowner acquisition efforts, and helps us present a consistent, premium brand across every channel.

ROLE OVERVIEW
We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented, and creative Senior Marketing & Channel Specialist to support marketing across our vacation rental portfolio.

This role will report to our Chief Commercial Officer and work closely with our market teams, revenue management, onboarding, field operations, and external marketing partners.

This is a hands-on marketing role focused on listing optimization, channel content quality, website updates, homeowner acquisition marketing, referral channel support, brand consistency, and select social media support. The ideal candidate is a strong writer, comfortable with digital tools, highly organized, and excited to help build scalable marketing processes for a growing hospitality business.

This role is especially important because our listings, websites, homeowner-facing content, and referral channels directly impact our ability to grow revenue, attract new homeowners, and create a premium brand experience across every market.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

LISTING OPTIMIZATION & CHANNEL CONTENT
* Own the quality, consistency, and optimization of property listings across Guesty, Airbnb, Vrbo, Google Vacation Rentals, direct booking websites, and other distribution channels.
* Write and refine listing titles, descriptions, summaries, photo captions, amenity highlights, and market-specific content.
* Review listings for completeness, accuracy, conversion quality, and brand consistency.
* Help determine the best hero images, photo order, captions, and property highlights for each listing.
* Coordinate with market teams to confirm property details, amenities, local recommendations, and unique selling points.
* Manage the listing optimization checklist for new and existing homes.
* Support the launch of new listings by ensuring marketing content is complete, accurate, and on-brand before going live.
* Review underperforming listings and recommend content, photo, or positioning improvements.
* Manage the relationship with photography partners to help ensure property photography is high quality, consistent, and aligned with our brand standards.
* Coordinate photography needs, shot lists, photo feedback, and follow-up requests with market teams and external photographers.

WEBSITE & DIRECT BOOKING SUPPORT
* Maintain and update property pages, market pages, owner-facing pages, and other website content.
* Ensure direct booking websites are accurate, polished, and aligned with current listings and brand standards.
* Support the creation of landing pages for market-specific campaigns, homeowner acquisition, and referral partners.
* Help improve website content for SEO, readability, and conversion.
* Coordinate with website vendors or internal tea ---------- mbers when technical changes are needed.
* Support updates to Google Business Profiles and other local search assets.

HOMEOWNER ACQUISITION MARKETING
* Support marketing efforts designed to attract new vacation rental homeowners.
* Help create and maintain homeowner-facing materials, including one-pagers, brochures, market summaries, case studies, listing improvement examples, and sales support materials.
* Support email campaigns, nurture sequences, and other marketing efforts for prospective homeowners.
* Help package the EHP value proposition clearly across markets, including our approach to home care, revenue management, guest experience, and owner communication.
* Create market-specific content that helps homeowners understand why our local teams are the right partner for their vacation rental.

REFERRAL CHANNEL MARKETING
* Support marketing efforts for referral partners, including real estate agents, brokers, developers, designers, contractors, and other local professionals who may refer homeowners to us.
* Help create referral partner collateral, outreach materials, email campaigns, and follow-up content.
* Maintain referral channel marketing assets and ensure they are professional, clear, and easy for partners to share.
* Support the marketing side of our lifetime value of leads strategy, helping us stay visible and valuable to referral sources over time.
* Coordinate with leadership and market teams to identify partner opportunities and create simple, repeatable campaigns.

BRAND & CONTENT CONSISTENCY
* Help maintain consistent brand voice, tone, and visual standards across all markets and channels.
* Create and manage templates for listings, owner marketing, referral partners, social media, presentations, and website content.
* Use tools like Canva and Google Workspace to produce clean, professional, on-brand marketing materials.
* Ensure each local brand feels personal and market-specific while still reflecting the quality and standards of Elevated Hospitality Partners.

REPORTING & PROCESS MANAGEMENT
* Maintain a marketing tracker for listing optimization, website updates, referral marketing, owner acquisition campaigns, and active projects.
* Provide regular updates to the Chief Commercial Officer on progress, priorities, and blockers.
* Track key marketing activity, including listings optimized, new listings launched, website pages updated, referral materials created, and campaigns supported.
* Help build repeatable marketing processes as we scale across markets.
* Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, consistency, and quality across our marketing work.

SOCIAL MEDIA
* Support social media content across our brands, with a primary focus on homeowner brand awareness and homeowner acquisition rather than guest marketing.
* Create and maintain a simple content calendar focused on brand credibility, homeowner education, market presence, property highlights, team stories, and referral partner visibility.
* Repurpose listing content, market insights, owner-facing content, and local stories into social media posts.
* Create professional, on-brand graphics and short-form content using Canva or similar tools.
* Ensure social media supports the broader business goal of building trust with homeowners and referral partners.

WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE
* Listings are polished, accurate, compelling, and consistent across channels.
* New homes are launched with complete and high-quality marketing content.
* Website and direct booking content stays current and professional.
* Homeowner acquisition materials are organized, clear, and easy for the sales team to use.
* Referral partner marketing is consistent and professional.
* Photography partners receive clear direction and deliver brand-aligned property photos.
* Marketing projects are tracked, prioritized, and completed on time.
* The company has stronger marketing systems that can scale across multiple markets.

IDEAL CANDIDATE
* Excellent written English and strong editing skills.
* Experience in digital marketing, hospitality marketing, vacation rental marketing, real estate marketing, or a similar field.
* Strong attention to detail and ability to manage many listings, projects, and deadlines.
* Experience writing marketing copy for websites, listings, emails, or social media.
* Comfort working with digital platforms, website builders, channel tools, spreadsheets, and project trackers.
* Strong Canva or basic graphic design skills.
* Good understanding of brand consistency and premium presentation.
* Ability to work independently, ask smart questions, and organize work clearly.
* A proactive, problem-solving mindset.
* Interest in hospitality, travel, real estate, and vacation rentals.

PREFERRED EXPERIENCE
Experience with any of the following is helpful, but not required:
* Airbnb, Vrbo,  ---------- , or Google Vacation Rentals
* Guesty or another property management system
* Vacation rental or short-term rental marketing
* Real estate or property management marketing
* Website CMS tools
* Google Business Profile
* Google Analytics
* Meta Business Suite
* Email marketing tools
* SEO basics
* Photography coordination or creative direction

TOOLS YOU MAY USE
* Guesty
* Airbnb
* Vrbo
* Google Vacation Rentals
* Google Workspace
* Canva
* Meta Business Suite
* Google Business Profile
* Website builder tools
* Project management or tracking tools
* AI writing and productivity tools

COMPENSATION
* Full-time remote role based in the Philippines
* Compensation: $1,200-$1,500 USD per month, depending on experience
* 13th month pay
* Full-time schedule
* 90-day performance and compensation review
* Long-term growth opportunity as our marketing function expands

WORKING HOURS
• This is a full-time role aligned to U.S. Central Time.
• Required schedule: 9:00am-5:00pm Central Daylight Time, Monday-Friday.
• 9:00am-5:00pm CDT = 10:00pm-6:00am PHT.
• Because the Philippines does not observe daylight saving time, the Philippines time equivalent may shift when the U.S. changes between CDT and CST.
• Candidates must be comfortable working overnight Philippines hours on a consistent basis.


APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS

To apply, please submit the following:

1. RESUME OR WORK HISTORY
Please share your resume, LinkedIn profile, or a short summary of your relevant work experience.

2. MARKETING PORTFOLIO OR EXAMPLES
Please include examples of marketing work you have created or managed. This could include:

* Vacation rental listings
* Airbnb, Vrbo, or  ----------  listing copy
* Website pages
* Real estate or hospitality marketing materials
* Social media content
* Email campaigns
* Brochures, one-pagers, or Canva designs
* Before-and-after examples of listings or marketing content you improved

We are especially interested in examples related to short-term rentals, vacation rentals, hospitality, real estate, travel, or local service businesses.

3. VACATION RENTAL / OTA EXPERIENCE

Please briefly answer the following:
* Have you worked with Airbnb, Vrbo,  ---------- , Google Vacation Rentals, Guesty, or other vacation rental platforms?
* Have you optimized vacation rental listings before? If yes, what changes did you make?
* What do you look for when trying to improve the performance of a vacation rental listing?
* What process or checklist do you use to make sure a listing is complete, accurate, and attractive to guests?
* How do you think about the differences between Airbnb, Vrbo, direct booking websites, and other OTAs?

4. LISTING OPTIMIZATION CASE STUDY
Please review the following Airbnb listing:  ---------- 

After reviewing the listing, please provide a concise recommendation for how you would improve it.

Your response should include:
* A brief summary of your overall impression of the listing.
* An optimized listing title. Please provide one title you believe would perform well on Airbnb and briefly explain why.
* A rewritten and optimized version of the listing. Please rewrite the full listing copy, including the main description, property/space details, location information, guest access, and any important notes you believe should be included. The listing should be written to clearly present the property, highlight its most compelling features, and drive conversion to booking.
* Your top 5 recommended improvements. These can relate to copy, photo order, amenities, guest expectations, OTA performance, direct booking potential, or anything else you believe would improve the listing.
* Your approach to listing optimization. Briefly explain the process you would normally use when reviewing and improving a vacation rental listing.

Please keep your response practical and concise. We are looking for clear writing, strong judgment, and an understanding of how to improve vacation rental listing performance.

WHY JOIN US
This is an opportunity to join a growing hospitality business at an early stage and help build the marketing engine for a multi-market vacation rental platform. You will have the chance to make a direct impact on listing performance, homeowner acquisition, referral partner marketing, and brand growth across several unique vacation rental markets.

We are looking for someone who is thoughtful, organized, creative, and excited to help build something that can scale.

title: Senior AI Systems Architect
description:
Senior AI Systems Architect (Contract-Based)

Build a Private AI Brain for a Knowledge-Driven Organization

We are not looking for a prompt engineer.
We are not looking for someone who “uses ChatGPT well.”

We are looking for a senior AI systems architect who can design and implement a structured, private AI assistant trained strictly on our internal knowledge base.

If you have not built real AI systems before, this role is not for you.

THE MISSION

We are a knowledge-intensive organization with extensive proprietary educational, analytical, and operational materials.

We want to build a private AI assistant that:

-Understands our internal frameworks and methodologies
-Synthesizes information across multiple documents
-Assists in research and structured analysis
-Drafts outputs aligned with our internal logic
-Operates within strict guardrails
-Uses only our curated materials unless explicitly instructed

This is not basic document search.
This is building a controlled AI research and analysis system.

WHAT YOU WILL BUILD

-A structured AI knowledge architecture
-A private internal AI research assistant
-A system capable of synthesis, analysis, and structured output
-Proper retrieval and reasoning workflows
-Guardrails to minimize hallucination and knowledge drift
-Clear documentation so our team can use and maintain the system

If your solution is “just upload everything into ChatGPT,” this role is not for you.

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

You must have real experience with:

-OpenAI API
-Building AI systems for real clients or companies
-Retrieval-based systems (RAG or similar frameworks)
-Working with large structured and unstructured data
-Designing systems, not just writing prompts

Bonus if familiar with:

-Gemini
-NotebookLM
-AI workflow automation
-Knowledge base architecture

We are looking for builders and system thinkers.

WHO SHOULD NOT APPLY

-Prompt engineers without backend experience
-VAs who simply use AI tools
-Applicants without proof of real-world AI systems built

ENGAGEMENT STRUCTURE

-Contract / project-based
-Milestone-driven
-Potential ongoing retainer relationship based on performance

HOW TO APPLY

To be considered, you must complete the application form below:  ----------  [DO NOT CLICK THE LINK. COPY IT AND PASTE IT IN A NEW TAB.]

Important:
-You must submit a Loom video inside the form presenting an AI system you built.
-Applications without a completed form will not be reviewed.
-Do not send direct messages or attachments through OnlineJobs.

title: Looking for a Technical VA for 7-Figure Marketing Agency
description:
Marketing Agency Operator — Technical Integrations (Work Directly With the Founder)

I started this marketing agency last year with a $4,000 loan and scaled it to seven figures in annual revenue within 18 months. I'm hiring one person to work alongside me long-term and gradually take over key parts of what I do day-to-day.
This is a hands-on role on a fast-moving team, and you'll learn the exact systems behind that growth. You can see how we operate on my YouTube channel:  ---------- 

Compensation & benefits

$1,000/month base salary
Performance bonus / commission on top of base for strong work
Direct mentorship and access to the playbook and SOPs that took this business to seven figures in one year (people pay me $500/hour for consulting)
Real room to grow into a managerial position worth $5k/month as the business grows


What you'll be doing

Technical integrations — this is the core of the role. Connecting tools using APIs and webhooks, and wiring up anything marketing-related: Pixels, CAPI, GTM, webhooks, Zapier. APIs, webhooks, CAPI, GTM, Pixels, and connecting marketing tools. Google Sheets (SUPER important). Connecting marketing platforms is necessary.
Working inside our marketing stack: lead-distribution software (e.g., LeadProsper, LeadsHook) and funnel builders.
Building and maintaining systems in Google Sheets.
Using AI tools to handle technical and coding tasks.

Google Youtube Bing Ads is a huge bonus!!!!

This is intermediate integration work — not advanced, heavy custom development.

You will also replace lowlevel editing tasks and spreadsheet filling and some research.

title: Personal Assistant / Generalist VA — AI Savory, Travel, Email, Recruiting, Social Media (Work Directly With Founder)
description:
I’m a solo founder running multiple businesses (apps, e-commerce, client projects) and I travel frequently. I need a sharp right hand who can take entire areas off my plate — not someone who needs a checklist for everything.
The tasks are deliberately BROAD. One day you research and book hotels and flights, the next day you answer customer support emails, screen job applicants, or draft social media posts. If “I’ve never done that before” scares you, this is not your job. If it excites you, keep reading.
What you’ll handle:
 • Travel & personal: Research hotels, flights, visas, appointments — present me 2–3 best options with prices, not 20 links
 • Email & support: Manage inboxes, answer customer support requests with templates you help build, flag only what truly needs me
 • Recruiting: Post jobs, screen applications, schedule interviews, manage candidate communication
 • Social media: Draft posts and captions (with AI tools), schedule content, basic Canva graphics
 • Research & admin: Compare tools/suppliers, organize files and docs, track invoices, whatever comes up
How we work:
 • Async-first via Slack/Telegram and Loom — almost no meetings
 • You get the goal and the context, you figure out the how
 • AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude) are part of the daily workflow here — you should already use them and want to get better at it
 • Direct line to me, no middle managers

Requirements
 • AI affinity is a hard requirement: You already use ChatGPT, Claude, or similar tools daily for your work (writing, research, drafts) — and you can tell me exactly HOW. You’re curious to build AI into every workflow, not afraid of it
 • 2+ years as VA, executive assistant, or in a role juggling many different task types
 • Excellent written English — you’ll write emails customers and candidates actually read
 • Extremely organized: nothing falls through the cracks, you follow up without being reminded
 • Proactive communicator: you flag problems early and suggest solutions, not just report issues
 • Reliable internet, own laptop, comfortable with new tools (Notion, Slack, scheduling tools)
Bonus points:
 • Customer support experience (e-commerce/SaaS)
 • Recruiting or HR experience
 • German language skills (not required, but a big plus)


What you get
 • A founder who pays on time, every time
 • Variety — no two weeks look the same, you’ll learn how real businesses run
 • We start small with a paid trial period, then grow hours and responsibility based on performance
 • For the right person, this develops into a stable long-term role with raises and benefits over time

How to Apply
Applications are filtered automatically. To prove you read this:
 1. Start your application with the word “ANCHOR”
 2. Answer in 2–3 sentences: Describe one concrete way you used ChatGPT or another AI tool to do your work faster or better. What was the task, what tool, what was the result?
 3. Tell me about the widest range of tasks you’ve handled in one role — give 3 real examples
 4. State your expected hourly rate and weekly availability
Generic copy-paste applications without the keyword will be ignored.
I’m not looking for a task-doer. I’m looking for someone who makes my life easier without being asked twice. If that’s you, apply now.

title: Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator
description:

Hourly Rate: Commensurate with Experience and Fit
Business: Short-term and Mid-Term Rentals Furnished Rentals

Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator

TempStayUSA is seeking a highly organized, tech-savvy Revenue Operations & Growth Coordinator to support lead generation, social media operations, prospect outreach, research, and business growth initiatives.

This role sits at the intersection of marketing, operations, lead generation, social media, and business development. The ideal candidate is equally comfortable building relationships, managing systems, researching opportunities, organizing workflows, and supporting growth initiatives across multiple channels.

Key Responsibilities

* Conduct thoughtful outreach through OTAs, email, social media, and other channels
* Build and nurture relationships with prospects over time
* Track communication and follow-up activities across multiple platforms

* Monitor engagement and respond to comments, messages, and inquiries
* Track growth metrics and performance analytics
* Support hashtag research and audience growth initiatives

Most Important Requirements

**Relationship Building & Prospect Nurturing**

Success comes from building authentic relationships, maintaining thoughtful follow-up, and creating trust through consistent communication.

You must be comfortable managing communication and tracking activities across OTAs, social media platforms, email campaigns, CRM systems, and project management tools.

Experience managing social media operations, engagement, analytics, scheduling, and audience growth is required. You must be able to describe how you support this role and what YOUR metrics are in building audience. 

**Tech-Savvy & AI-Oriented**

We use a lot of technical processes - AI Agents for many things, Zapier for many integrations. Experience with tools such as:

* Apollo
* Zoho Social

*CapCut

Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, BDC, short-term rentals, hospitality, guest communications, or property management is strongly preferred. Understanding the industry, terminology, and customer mindset is important for success in this role.

Our Ideal Candidate

* Organized
* Detail-oriented
* Curious
* Proactive
* Relationship-focused
* Comfortable with technology
* Strong at follow-up
* Excited to work in an AI-assisted operating environment

If you enjoy building relationships, solving problems, learning new tools, and helping grow a hospitality business, we'd love to hear from you.

Must have:
Strong written English
Organized + proactive
Reliable daily execution + reporting

“What would you do in your first 7 days to generate more inquiries?”

Applications without Loom will not be reviewed.

title: Ad Campaign Specialist (Multi-Channel E-commerce Advertising)
description:
----------  is an Australian, tech-led start-up in the e-commerce space. We are looking to hire an individual with a problem-solving mindset and a "can-do" mentality to join our growing team.

Are you passionate about digital advertising and data-driven growth? We are looking for an experienced Ad Campaign Specialist to manage and optimize advertising campaigns across multiple online platforms. The ideal candidate will have strong hands-on experience with marketplace advertising, social media advertising, and search engine marketing, with a proven ability to improve sales, traffic, and return on ad spend (ROAS).


ABOUT THE ROLE

As our Ad Campaign Specialist, you will be responsible for planning, launching, monitoring, and optimizing paid advertising campaigns across various e-commerce marketplaces and digital channels. You will work closely with our operations and e-commerce teams to drive customer acquisition, increase brand visibility, and maximize advertising performance.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
• Create, manage, and optimize advertising campaigns across:
     • Amazon Advertising
     • eBay Promoted Listings
     • Facebook Ads
     • Instagram Ads
     • Google Ads (Search, Shopping, Display, Performance Max)
     • Australian online marketplaces and advertising platforms
• Monitor campaign performance and make data-driven optimization decisions.
• Conduct keyword research, competitor analysis, and audience targeting.
• Improve campaign metrics including CTR, CPC, CPA, conversion rates, and ROAS.
• Manage advertising budgets efficiently across multiple platforms.
• Prepare regular performance reports and provide actionable recommendations.
• Identify new advertising opportunities and growth channels.
• Collaborate with product listing and content teams to improve campaign effectiveness.
• Stay up to date with industry trends, platform updates, and best practices.

REQUIREMENTS
• Proven experience managing paid advertising campaigns.
• Strong knowledge of Amazon Ads, eBay advertising, Facebook Ads Manager, and Google Ads.
• Experience with marketplace advertising and e-commerce marketing.
• Strong analytical and reporting skills.
• Ability to interpret campaign data and make strategic recommendations.
• Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
• Strong communication skills and ability to work independently.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
? Experience managing advertising campaigns across multiple marketplaces.
? Knowledge of Australian e-commerce platforms and online marketplaces.
? Experience with Amazon Seller Central, eBay Seller Hub, Shopify, or other e-commerce platforms.
? Understanding of SEO, conversion rate optimization (CRO), and customer acquisition strategies.
? Experience using AI tools and automation platforms to improve advertising efficiency.
? Ability to identify opportunities beyond advertising, including product positioning, pricing strategies, and marketplace growth.

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We value people who:
• Think critically and solve problems proactively.
• Take ownership of their work and deliver results.
• Are comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment.
• Continuously learn and adapt to new platforms and technologies.
• Have a genuine passion for e-commerce and digital marketing.

The successful candidate will have the unique opportunity to learn from and influence seasoned entrepreneurs who have built several successful companies. As you take on more responsibility, you will also have the chance to grow financially and develop into additional positions.

Please add "push boundaries" as the last words in your application.

Stock2Cash is looking to build a fun, exceptional team. You have to be a reliable, positive, and enjoyable person to work with!

We're fully set up to support our entire team working from home. We know the only thing scarier than starting a new job is starting a new job remotely, but we've got you ;)

title: Marketing Specialist
description:
About Us
Month2Month is a fast-growing hospitality and property management company focused on maximizing occupancy, optimizing listing performance, and delivering exceptional guest experiences across all platforms.


Role Summary
As an Omnichannel Marketing Specialist, you are responsible for maximizing the visibility, search ranking, and click-through performance of property listings across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, ShowMojo, and MLS.

Your core objective is to drive traffic ? improve ranking ? increase click-through rate ? support occupancy growth.

You will operate as the digital merchandiser of our portfolio, using data-driven strategies, platform algorithms, and visual optimization to improve listing performance and minimize vacancy.

This is a highly execution-driven role, not a branding or social media position.

Key Responsibilities

1. Omnichannel Listing Optimization & Merchandising

 Continuously audit and optimize listings across Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, ShowMojo, and MLS
 Ensure 100% listing coverage for:
 All vacant homes
 Homes becoming vacant within ?7 day
 Select and sequence the top 5 listing photos to maximize CTR:
 Focus on bright living areas and bedrooms
 Build a strong visual “story” for first impression
 Apply standardized cover image badges to improve click-through performance
 Create and relaunch listings for underperforming properties:

 Ensure accurate policies (pet rules, smoking, utility caps, tenant requirements)
 Maintain consistency across platforms

2. Cross-Channel Pricing & Promotion Strategy
 Execute pricing strategies across channels:
 Airbnb / VRBO ? seasonal and demand-based pricing
 Zillow / Direct ? competitive long-term positioning
 Apply targeted discount rules to stimulate traffic and bookings
 Support long-term (3+ months) pricing and promotion strategies
 Manage “Urgent Homes” portfolio:
 Daily optimization (pricing, photos, titles, positioning)
 Focus on traffic recovery and vacancy reduction

3. Data Analysis & Performance Optimization

 Track key funnel metrics daily:
 First Page Impressions
 Listing Views
 Click-Through Rate (CTR)

 Identify underperforming listings and take corrective actions

 Continuously test and iterate:
 Photo order
 Pricing adjustments
 Listing content

4. Reporting & Insights

 Submit a Daily Performance Report including:
 Traffic and listing performance
 Lead volume across channels
 Key actions taken and results
 Provide insights and recommendations to improve listing performance and occupancy

Description

Month2Month is a performance-driven organization where listing visibility and traffic directly impact occupancy and revenue.
As an Omnichannel Marketing Specialist, you will play a critical role in managing and optimizing how our properties are presented across all platforms.
This is not a traditional marketing role focused on branding or social media. Instead, it is a hands-on execution role focused on platform algorithms, listing performance, and measurable results.
You will be expected to take ownership of listing performance, proactively identify opportunities, and continuously improve metrics such as impressions, CTR, and traffic.

We are looking for someone who is detail-oriented, data-driven, and highly responsive, with the ability to execute quickly and consistently.

Required Qualifications

 Bachelor’s degree or above
 Experience in property management, short-term rental, hospitality, or related industry
 Strong attention to detail and execution ability
 Data-driven mindset with ability to interpret performance metrics
 Ability to manage multiple listings simultaneously
 Strong sense of urgency and ownership
 Comfortable working in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment

Preferred Qualifications

 Experience with Airbnb, VRBO, Zillow, or similar platforms
 Understanding of platform ranking algorithms and listing optimization
 Familiarity with AI tools for:
 Listing content generation
 Workflow automation
 Basic design skills (Canva, Photoshop) for image enhancement and badges
 Experience with A/B testing or performance optimization

title: SharePoint Specialist (Project-Based)
description:
SharePoint Specialist (Project-Based)
Remote (Philippines) | Project-Based | Multiple projects available

ABOUT THE ROLE

Best IT is an Australian managed service provider working with businesses that are preparing their environments for AI. Right now, we are doing a lot of Data Governance and SharePoint overhaul work, rebuilding client environments so they have a clean, structured, and secure path to enabling Microsoft Copilot and other AI tools.

We have an internal Project Manager and Lead Technician overseeing each engagement. We need a skilled SharePoint Specialist to work alongside them, handling the hands-on build and configuration work across multiple client projects.

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING

- Restructuring SharePoint site architecture for clarity, security, and Copilot readiness
- Auditing and cleaning up permissions, sharing settings, and access controls
- Implementing information architecture, metadata, and taxonomy frameworks
- Migrating content between SharePoint sites and libraries
- Creating and configuring document libraries, lists, and pages to meet governance requirements
- Applying sensitivity labels and data classification aligned to Microsoft Purview standards
- Working to a defined scope and project checklist under the direction of our Lead Tech and PM
- Documenting completed work clearly for handover

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

- Strong hands-on experience with SharePoint Online (Microsoft 365)
- Good understanding of SharePoint permissions, sharing, and security best practices
- Familiarity with Microsoft 365 information architecture and governance concepts
- Experience with SharePoint content migration (using tools like AvePoint or similar is a plus)
- Understanding of how SharePoint structure relates to Microsoft Copilot and AI readiness
- Comfortable working independently and executing tasks from a brief or technical spec
- Clear communicator in English, written and verbal
- Reliable, detail-oriented, and able to meet project milestones

NICE TO HAVE

- Experience with Microsoft Purview (sensitivity labels, DLP, compliance)
- Familiarity with Power Automate for SharePoint workflow automation
- Any Microsoft 365 or SharePoint certifications
- Previous work in a managed service provider environment

HOW IT WORKS

This is a project-based engagement. You will be brought in for specific client projects, working remotely within Australian business hours. Depending on quality of work and fit, there is strong potential for ongoing projects as our Data Governance pipeline is active and growing.

TO APPLY

Please apply through onlinejobs.ph and include the following in your application:
- A brief summary of your SharePoint experience
- Examples of SharePoint projects you have completed (client names can be anonymised)
- Your availability and preferred hourly rate in USD

title: Video Editor for Faceless Youtube Channels (Archival & B-Roll Master)
description:
My name is Niko, and we are scaling a network of high-growth YouTube channels that focus on historical, cultural, and educational storytelling

example(not our channel):  ---------- 

We are not looking for a standard "faceless channel" editor who just throws stock clips over a voiceover. We are looking for a Visual Historian/Storyteller who can take a deep-dive script and turn it into a cinematic experience.

The "Simple" Paradox: While the technical execution may seem simple, the taste required is immense. You must know how to use 1960s archival photos, subtle textures, and intentional pacing to keep a viewer captivated for 40 minutes.

What You’ll Be Responsible For:

* Narrative Sourcing: Finding the perfect historical image or clip—not just the first "barbecue" result on Pexels, but an authentic 1960s South archival photo that matches the soul of the script.

* Pacing & Retention: Mastering the "slow-burn" edit. You know when to let a photo breathe and when to use subtle motion (Ken Burns effect) to maintain focus.

* Soundscapes: Crafting immersive audio. You’ll use foley, ambient background noise, and era-appropriate music to ground the viewer in the story.

* Thumbnails: Creating high-quality, "clickable" art that looks like a documentary poster (experience in this style is mandatory).

Position Details & Expected Output:
* Schedule: Monday to Saturday (Flexible hours).
* Output: 1 high-quality video ever 2 days. Note: While the style is "clean," we expect a high level of intentionality in every frame.
* Salary: $400 USD per month + performance-based bonuses (e.g., $150 bonuses for 100k+ views milestones).
* Growth: This is a long-term play with scaling pay as the network grows.

Requirements (Crucial):
* Documentary Sensibilities: You understand how to edit for a mature, curious audience.
* Visual Taste: You can distinguish between "cheap stock footage" and "cinematic b-roll/archival assets."
* Independent Creator Mindset: You can spot a boring section of a 40-minute script and save it with a clever visual hook or a shift in sound design without being micromanaged.
* Reliability: You can maintain a "4 videos per week" rhythm without sacrificing the aesthetic quality.

Ideal Candidate:
* You have an eye for historical accuracy and "vibe" consistency.
* You are a master of the "Ken Burns" effect, film grain overlays, and clean, elegant typography.

How to Apply:
1. Start your application with your favorite animal.
2. Portfolio: Provide 2–3 examples of work that show storytelling. If your portfolio is just fast-paced "gaming" edits or "bright/loud" automation videos, please do not apply. We want to see "Video Essay" or "Documentary" styles.
3. Context: Briefly explain your experience with long-form retention (20+ minute videos).
4. Logistics: Confirm your availability for a 6-day-a-week workflow.

title: GHL EXPERT
description:
**Job Title:** GoHighLevel Specialist (Part-Time, Ongoing, $400-$500/mo, Start ASAP)

**Job Description:**

Health and wellness practice needs a GoHighLevel specialist to build and maintain funnels, a full website, landing pages, and email automations. This is ongoing work, not a one-time project.

Hours: 10 -12 hours/week with option to increase.

First projects: Build sales pages, thank you pages, and order pages for two funnels (a digital course and a lab test funnel). Set up full email sequences with conditional automations for both. Later, build a complete website inside GHL.

Ongoing: New funnels, page updates, email edits, workflow troubleshooting, and website maintenance. Future projects will likely include setting up and managing a community forum inside GHL, lead tracking, and text automations. 

We have templates for all of the pages, and content will be provided.

YOU MUST HAVE:

1. 12+ months building funnels and websites for clients inside GoHighLevel (not WordPress, ClickFunnels, Kajabi, or Kartra).
2. Hands-on experience with GHL workflows: triggers, wait steps, if/else branches, tags, and pipelines.
3. Experience building full websites (not just single landing pages) inside GHL.
4. Portfolio of GHL pages and/or websites you built (live links or screenshots).
5. Ability to start within 5 business days.

Bonus: Experience with GHL's community/membership features is a strong plus.

TO APPLY: ANSWER THESE 3 QUESTIONS
(Applications that skip any question will not be reviewed.)

1. Show your work. Send 2 to 3 live links or screenshots of GHL funnel pages or websites you built. For bonus points, include a short Loom video walking me through one build.

2. Show a workflow. Attach a screenshot from inside GHL's workflow builder showing a real automation you created. Tell me in 2 sentences what it does.

3. Solve this scenario. A customer purchases a course. They get a welcome email immediately, then login instructions 24 hours later. If they have not logged in after 72 hours, they get a reminder. If they have logged in, no reminder is sent. Walk me through exactly how you build this in GHL: which triggers, which workflow steps, which conditions.

NOTE: If your experience is mainly on other platforms and you plan to learn GHL on this job, this is not the right fit. I am looking for someone who already works in GoHighLevel daily.

title: Executive Operations Assistant
description:
About the Business
We are a Florida-focused curative title real estate investing company. We work on properties with complicated ownership, probate, heirship, tax, lien, and title issues. Our business depends on clean follow-through, accurate documents, organized files, consistent communication, and disciplined execution from signed purchase agreement through attorney handoff, probate progress, document completion, and closing readiness.

Role Overview
This is not a generic virtual assistant role. We are looking for a calm, steady, detail-driven Executive Operations Assistant who can become the operational control point for the COO. The right person will keep the COO organized, coordinate transaction and notary workflows, monitor probate case progress, maintain complete deal files, and audit documents before key handoffs.

You should enjoy structure, checklists, clean folders, accurate notes, careful follow-up, and quiet execution. You do not need to be loud, salesy, or highly social. You do need to be reliable, precise, patient, consistent, and comfortable holding people accountable to next steps.

Core Responsibilities
1. Executive Assistant to the COO
• Protect the COO’s time by organizing priorities, reminders, follow-ups, meetings, and action items.
• Prepare daily priority lists, meeting notes, agendas, and post-meeting task summaries.
• Monitor assigned inboxes, messages, calendars, documents, and task lists for urgent items and missed follow-ups.
• Turn verbal instructions into clear written tasks, checklists, and next actions.
• Maintain calm, professional communication while supporting a fast-moving operator.

2. Transaction / Notary / Closing Coordination
• Coordinate mobile notary appointments with sellers, heirs, and notaries after purchase agreements are signed.
• Prepare and organize signing packages, notary instructions, FedEx labels, tracking links, and scanback requirements.
• Track execution of Joinders, Quitclaim Deeds, Assignments of Interest, Affidavits of Heirs, copies of IDs, and related documents.
• Review scanbacks for completeness before forwarding packages to the proper internal tea ---------- mber, probate attorney, or closing-related party.
• Maintain transaction status, next steps, blockers, and deadlines in the CRM/project management system.

3. Probate Case Coordination
• Monitor probate-related deal progress and maintain case status updates.
• Communicate with probate attorneys and internal tea ---------- mbers to track what is pending, filed, missing, signed, recorded, or ready for next action.
• Maintain open-items lists for each probate file, including missing heirs, missing documents, pending attorney tasks, and court/status updates.
• Organize probate documents, attorney correspondence, family/heir notes, and supporting evidence in the correct folders.
• Escalate stalled cases before they become forgotten cases.

4. Deal File Management / Curative File Control
• Keep every deal file clean, complete, and easy to understand.
• Maintain digital folders, naming conventions, document checklists, title issue notes, heir charts, seller information, and transaction records.
• Ensure each file has a clear current status, next action, responsible person, and due date.
• Prepare clean handoff packages for attorneys, title companies, internal reviewers, or closing coordination.
• Prevent scattered information, duplicate documents, missing IDs, incorrect versions, and unclear file history.

5. Quality Control / Document Audit
• Audit documents before signing, after scanback, before attorney handoff, and before title/closing handoff.
• Check names, dates, signatures, notarizations, legal descriptions, attachments, IDs, missing pages, and consistency across documents.
• Flag issues clearly and early instead of assuming someone else will catch them.
• Maintain QC checklists and document audit logs.
• Help the team reduce costly errors, closing delays, and repeated requests to sellers or heirs.

The Person Who Will Thrive in This Role
• You are calm under pressure and do not need constant excitement to stay engaged.
• You naturally create order out of scattered information.
• You are comfortable with repetitive follow-up, checklists, and status tracking.
• You notice small details that other people miss.
• You are professional, direct, and respectful when following up with sellers, notaries, attorneys, and internal tea ---------- mbers.
• You can manage up: if the COO misses a decision, deadline, or open item, you will respectfully bring it back to the front.
• You prefer accuracy over speed, but you can still move quickly when the process is clear.
• You are loyal, stable, consistent, and serious about long-term work.

This Role Is Probably Not a Fit If...
• You need a highly social, chatty, people-heavy role to feel energized.
• You dislike repetitive checklists, document review, file organization, or administrative follow-through.
• You prefer improvising over following a proven process.
• You get bored when the work requires precision, patience, and consistency.
• You avoid following up because you do not want to bother people.
• You are uncomfortable working behind the scenes in a support and coordination role.
• You want a sales, negotiation, or lead-generation role instead of an operations support role.

Required Skills and Experience
• Excellent written English and professional communication skills.
• Strong organization, task tracking, and file management ability.
• High attention to detail when reviewing names, documents, dates, IDs, signatures, and instructions.
• Comfortable using CRMs, Google Drive, Google Sheets, email, calendars, messaging tools, and project/task systems.
• Ability to work Central Time Zone hours consistently.
• Reliable internet connection and quiet work environment.
• Experience as an Executive Assistant, Transaction Coordinator, Legal Assistant, Real Estate VA, Probate Assistant, Title Assistant, or Operations Coordinator is preferred.
• Experience with U.S. real estate, probate, legal documents, notary coordination, or title-related work is a strong advantage, but trainability and accuracy matter most.

Tools You May Use
• Pipedrive or similar CRM
• Google Workspace: Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar
• Phone/messaging systems such as Quo, WhatsApp, or similar
• Project/task management tools
• FedEx label and tracking workflows
• Shared digital folders and document checklists

Success Metrics
• No unsigned, missing, or incomplete document packages sent forward without being flagged.
• Every active transaction has a current status, next step, owner, and due date.
• Notary appointments, FedEx labels, scanbacks, attorney handoffs, and follow-ups are tracked without gaps.
• Probate cases do not stall silently; blockers are identified and escalated.
• Deal files are clean, organized, and ready for review at any time.
• The COO has fewer dropped balls, fewer reminders to give, and more operational visibility.

First 30/60/90 Days - Timeframe & Expected Progress

First 30 Days
Learn the business model, tools, document types, folder structure, transaction workflow, and daily communication rhythm. Begin supporting COO priorities and tracking simple tasks.

First 60 Days
Own notary coordination, scanback tracking, deal file organization, and basic QC checklists with supervision. Begin maintaining probate status trackers and open-items lists.

First 90 Days
Operate as the daily control point for COO support, transaction coordination, probate tracking, file hygiene, and document audit workflows. Identify process gaps and help improve SOPs.

Schedule and Compensation
• $5/hour starting compensation plus deal bonuses.
• Central Time Zone working hours required.
• Long-term opportunity for someone who becomes dependable, accurate, and deeply trusted.
• Deal bonuses are tied to successful transaction progress and closed deals, based on internal company policy.

How to Apply
To apply, reply with the subject line: "Executive Operations Assistant - Detail Control" and include the following:
1. A short paragraph explaining why you are a strong fit for a detail-heavy operations support role.
2. Your experience with executive assistance, transaction coordination, real estate, legal documents, probate, title, notary coordination, or file management.
3. A specific example of a time you caught a small detail that prevented a bigger problem.
4. A specific example of how you track multiple open tasks, deadlines, and follow-ups.
5. Your availability in Central Time Zone hours.
6. Your internet speed, computer setup, and whether you have a quiet work environment.
7. Confirm that you are comfortable with $5/hour plus deal bonuses.
8.     Provide DISC test results:  ---------- /disc-personality-test/
9.     Copy of resume.

Small Detail Test
At the end of your application, write this exact sentence:
"I understand that this role requires calm consistency, accurate follow-up, and clean files."

Applications that do not follow the instructions above may not be reviewed. This role is for someone who pays close attention to details before the job even begins.

title: Full Stack Developer
description:
We're a growing digital agency that builds websites and web applications for our clients. We're hiring our first full-time developer to take ownership of the work — your code will go straight to production for real clients from day one.
If you like having real impact, learning fast, and working without someone constantly telling you what to do, you'll fit right in.
What You'll Do

Build and maintain web applications end-to-end (frontend + backend).
Ship new features, fix bugs, and keep existing client projects running smoothly.
Work directly with the team to understand requirements and deliver clean solutions.
Suggest technical improvements — we want someone who thinks, not just executes.

What We're Looking For

2+ years of real experience building full-stack web applications (we care about what you've shipped, not your diploma).
Strong skills in JavaScript/TypeScript.
A modern frontend framework: React (Next.js is a big plus) or Vue.
Backend experience with Node.js (or another backend you know well).

One Loom video showing one of your projects

title: Digital Marketing Manager (Shopify, Meta, TikTok)
description:
What we're looking for:

FIT Strong & Healthy is seeking a Digital Marketing Manager to lead our brand's digital marketing efforts and support our day-to-day e-commerce operations. Your role involves developing marketing strategies, helping manage our e-commerce platforms, and driving online sales growth across Amazon, Shopify, Walmart, TikTok Shop, and eBay.

We're looking for someone with strong strategic thinking, sharp analytical skills, and hands-on digital marketing expertise. Your responsibilities span social media management, traffic generation, conversion rate optimization, and supporting operational execution. You'll bring creativity, data-driven thinking, and a collaborative mindset — focused primarily on marketing and advertising, while helping with the operational side of the business as needed.

Beyond experience, we're looking for someone professional, organized, and a strong communicator in both written and verbal English.

This role is 100% work from home.

About FIT Strong & Healthy:
FIT Strong & Healthy is a US-based, woman-owned e-commerce brand selling high-quality, eco-friendly products that support healthy eating and an active lifestyle — including borosilicate glass water bottles, glass meal prep containers, and bento boxes. We sell across Amazon, Shopify, Walmart, TikTok Shop, and eBay, with a growing Instagram community  ----------  customers are health-conscious adults (25–50) who meal prep, prioritize food-safe reusable products, and value clean design.

In this role, you will:
• Develop strategic plans for digital marketing campaigns.
• Launch, monitor, and optimize digital advertising campaigns (Meta Ads, Amazon PPC, and beyond).
• Manage social media presence to boost engagement and brand visibility.
• Create, schedule, and publish social media posts (static and video) across Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, Pinterest, and YouTube.
• Own customer relations and communications — respond to comments, DMs, and questions on social media, plus customer emails and messages from e-commerce platforms — in a timely, on-brand, and helpful way.
• Develop and analyze metrics to track digital content and campaign performance.
• Collaborate with influencers and content creators for compelling content.
• Stay current with emerging digital marketing best practices and trends.
• Manage budgeting, forecasting, and planning for digital campaigns.
• Monitor and evaluate campaign performance, tracking key metrics and reporting results against goals.
• Contribute to developing and implementing comprehensive e-commerce strategies aligned with business objectives for online sales growth.
• Help manage, maintain, and update our e-commerce platforms, supporting a seamless, user-friendly online shopping experience.
• Own and maintain product catalogs across all e-commerce platforms (Amazon, Shopify, Walmart, TikTok Shop, and eBay) — listings, copy, images, A+/Enhanced content, pricing, and inventory accuracy.
• Monitor and analyze market trends, customer behavior, and competitor activities to identify improvement opportunities and drive growth.
• Apply industry best practices, emerging technologies, and digital marketing techniques to optimize website performance.
• Create and manage marketing campaigns — including email marketing, social media advertising, SEO, and affiliate/influencer marketing — to drive traffic and increase conversions.
• Analyze data, including web analytics and customer insights, to identify areas for improvement and increase sales.
• Develop strong relationships with key partners, including suppliers, vendors, and service providers.
• Collaborate closely with the owner and other tea ---------- mbers.
• Prepare regular reports on e-commerce performance, including key metrics, sales trends, customer behavior, and campaign effectiveness.
• Take on additional projects as needed to support broader business goals.
Your Impact:
• Increased traffic, follower count, engagement, and conversion rate — resulting in improved online sales performance.
• Revenue growth and improved profitability through effective e-commerce strategies, digital campaigns, and optimization.
• Develop and execute an effective digital marketing plan within the allocated budget.
• Improved customer acquisition and conversion rates from digital channels.
• Measurable ROI from digital marketing efforts.

About You:

Top priorities for this role (must-have):
• Strong, hands-on experience with Shopify — store management, theme/design updates, apps, product setup, collections, and orders.
• Strong, hands-on experience with TikTok Shop — catalog setup, content, ads, and the affiliate/creator program.
• Strong, hands-on experience with Meta Ads (Facebook + Instagram) — building, launching, scaling, and optimizing campaigns.

Core qualifications:
• Minimum 2 years of e-commerce marketing and management experience.
• Comprehensive knowledge of digital marketing strategies and tactics — including SEO, social media advertising, email marketing, and affiliate/influencer marketing.
• Working knowledge of Amazon Seller Central is a plus. Familiarity with Helium 10 is also a plus, but not required.
• Additional experience with Amazon, Walmart, and eBay is a plus.
• Proficiency managing social media accounts (creating, editing, and posting static and video content) across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Pinterest, and YouTube.
• Experience running campaigns on additional advertising platforms — Amazon Ads, TikTok Ads, Pinterest, and YouTube — is a plus.
• Proficiency in photo and video editing.
• Excellent communication, problem-solving, and project management skills.
• Strong analytical skills for data-driven decisions.
• Working knowledge of AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, image and video generation, AI-powered marketing and automation tools) and the ability to apply them to speed up content creation, research, copywriting, and day-to-day workflows.
• Proficiency in MS Office and relevant software applications.
• Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Management, or a related field.

Your Traits:
• Adaptable to changing situations and market dynamics.
• Quick learner, passionate about staying current with industry trends and best practices.
• Creative problem solver who generates innovative solutions.
• Highly organized and detail-oriented — able to juggle multiple projects and priorities.
• Self-motivated and results-driven; takes initiative and drives projects forward without hand-holding.
• Strong team player — collaborates effectively with the owner, colleagues, and external partners.
• Analytical thinker who derives insights from data and makes data-driven decisions.

WHO We are Looking For:
• You have a great personality with a genuine desire to learn and grow
• You are kind and always polite to people
• You love social media and building relationships
• You can be 100 percent dedicated and involved

Shift and Schedule:
• Full-time, 40 hours per week
• Must overlap at least 5 hours daily with US Central Time business hours (9 AM – 5 PM CST)
• Most sustainable shift: 2 AM – 11 AM Manila time (12 PM – 5 PM CST overlap)
• You'll work on your own schedule outside the required overlap hours

Compensation:
• $1,000 – $1,500 USD per month (negotiable based on experience)
• 13th month bonus tied to KPIs after 6 months
• Long-term, full-time position

How to Apply:
In your application, please include the following — applications missing any of these will not be reviewed:
1. The word "GLASS" at the very top of your application (so I know you read this)
2. A short paragraph about a Shopify store you've personally managed (store name, what you did, what results you got)
3. A short paragraph about a Meta Ads campaign you ran (product, audience, budget range, results)
4. Your typical daily work hours in Manila time
5. Your monthly salary expectation in USD
6. A link to a portfolio, LinkedIn, or any work samples
7. Your résumé attached

I'll respond to qualified applicants within 5 business days.

Hiring Process:
• An interview will be required.
• There will be a 90-day probation period after the hire date.
