About Us
PulseLink is a fast-growing UK-based marketing agency helping aesthetic clinics (medical spas, body sculpting clinics, facial aesthetic clinics) scale profitably using paid ads, AI-powered appointment setting, and a done-for-you service model.

We're currently working with 13 active clients across the UK and we're about to break into the US market — which is the entire reason this role exists.

This is not a job at a 100-person agency where you'll be one cog in a machine. We're small, hungry, and building this thing properly. The right hire here gets to build the foundation of a much bigger operation alongside the founder.

About the Role
We're hiring an Operations & Tech Assistant to own the technical backend of the business.

Right now, the founder (Oli) is spending 80% of his time on backend ops — building automations, configuring GoHighLevel sub-accounts for new clients, fixing tech that breaks, migrating workflows onto a new platform stack. That's the bottleneck stopping us from signing more clients and breaking into the US.

Your job is to take all of that off his plate — and become the person responsible for keeping the entire backend running smoothly as we scale.

You'd be the first dedicated person in this seat at PulseLink, which means:

Real ownership over the tech stack and how it's built
Your fingerprints on the SOPs the next 10–20 hires follow
Direct line to the founder — no middle management
Pay and responsibility grow with you as we scale

Key Responsibilities
Client Onboarding — Set up new clients in GoHighLevel: configure sub-accounts, pipelines, calendars, forms, welcome automations. Target: every new client launched within 7 days.
 ----------  Automation Migration — We're currently moving the entire business onto a new automation stack. You will own this migration end-to-end.
AI Texting Agent Management — We use AI to text leads on behalf of every client. You'll train it, debug it, and optimise it so it works for every clinic as we scale.
Daily Tech Tickets — Fix integrations, calendar syncs, pixel issues, broken automations. When something breaks at 8am, you're the person who diagnoses and fixes it.
SOP Creation — Build documentation for every recurring process so the next hire can follow it without asking questions.
R&D / Process Improvement — Bring 2+ ideas per month on how we can run faster, leaner, or smarter. We don't want a task-completer — we want a builder.
Client Communication — Talk to clinic owners on Slack and occasionally Zoom about tech-related questions. Clear, professional English is non-negotiable here.

Skills & Tools Required
Strong experience with  ----------  (formerly Integromat) — you've built multi-step automations before, not just turned on templates
Solid working knowledge of GoHighLevel — sub-accounts, pipelines, workflows, custom fields, integrations
Experience with AI tools in a professional context — bonus if you've used Claude Code or Cursor for building/editing scripts
Familiarity with AI conversational agents (chat bots, AI SMS responders) — building, training, or maintaining
Fluency with Google Sheets (formulas, lookups, trackers)
Excellent written English — you'll be talking to UK clinic owners
Ability to manage 10+ open tickets without dropping the ball
Resourcefulness — you Google before you ask, you bring solutions not problems, and you'd rather ask for forgiveness than permission

Nice-to-have (not required)
Beauty / aesthetics industry experience (we'll train you)
Past experience at a marketing agency
Notion / ClickUp for ops management
Meta (Facebook) Ads Manager familiarity for troubleshooting pixel issues

What We Offer
$700–$900/month base depending on experience, paid weekly via Wise/PayPal
Monthly performance bonuses — client retention bonus, SOP compliance bonus, R&D bonus
Pay scales as the company grows and as you take on more responsibility
100% remote, work from anywhere in the GMT-friendly timezone
Direct mentorship from the founder
Real ownership over your domain — no micromanagement, just outcomes

Do NOT Apply If…
You dislike high-pressure environments
You struggle to adapt quickly to change
You're uncomfortable receiving direct feedback
You need to be told what to do every step — common sense and ownership are non-negotiable
You're looking for a "chill remote job" — this isn't one
You don't speak/write fluent English

title: Creative & Operations Unicorn
description:
WANTED: The Ultimate Creative & Operations Unicorn (A Rare Breed Only) 
Think... Operations/Executive Assistant Meets Creative Genius

Let’s be real for a second. Most job ads are incredibly boring, and most Virtual Assistant roles are just checking boxes on a list. This is not that job.

At The Event Engineer, we help visionary entrepreneurs scale past the 7-figure mark by building world-class event systems. We don’t do "average," and we don’t do generic. We are growing fast, and right now, our founder, Lisa Barry, needs a true partner-in-brilliance.

We are looking for a rare human who is a 50/50 split between a Meticulous Systems Wizard and a Brilliant Creative Genius.
If you are a thinker who thrives on autonomy, loves tech, and gets bored doing the same easy task every day....welcome home.

The Stack You’ll Own (Your Playground):
ClickUp & Slack: You’ll be the guardian of our digital office. You keep projects moving, organize task flows, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks.
GoHighLevel (GHL): You'll build, optimize, and play with high-converting landing pages, sales funnels, and marketing automations.
Claude (Your AI Bestie): We don’t just use AI; we co-work with it. You will use Claude for copywriting, deep-dive strategy, coding tweaks, and data analysis. You are the pilot; Claude is your hyper-intelligent co-pilot.
Canva & Video Editing: You’ll look at our social media analytics, figure out a killer marketing strategy, and then actually bring it to life by editing scroll-stopping short-form videos and designing high-ticket marketing assets.

Who You Are:
An Autonomous Thinker: You don't need a manager breathing down your neck or giving you a daily to-do list. You see a gap, you engineer a solution, and you execute it.
A Strategic Storyteller: You actually understand marketing psychology. You know why a video went viral or why a funnel converted, and you can repeat that success.
A Little Bit EA: You’re not above doing light executive assistant support to keep daily operations smooth. You just want the business to win.
Design-Obsessed: If it looks cheap, it doesn’t get published. Period.

Qualifications:
2–3+ years working with high-ticket coaches, creators, or agencies.
Absolute mastery of GHL, ClickUp, Slack, and Canva.
Next-level video editing skills (especially short-form Reels/TikToks).
Flawless English and a gift for "voice-matching" (you can write exactly how Lisa talks).
You know how to prompt Claude like an absolute pro to speed up your workflow.

The Vibes & The Package:
Location: 100% Remote (Based in the Philippines ????????)
Hours: 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM AEDT (Full-Time—matching Aussie hours!)
Pay: $1,000 AUD / month starting rate + performance-based growth opportunities as we scale the empire together.

How to Apply (No Generic AI Copy-Pasters Allowed! This is IMPORTANT)
We want to see the real human behind the screen. If you send us a 100% ChatGPT-generated cover letter, it goes straight to the trash. To apply, send us:

Your Portfolio: Show us your absolute best short-form video edits, graphic designs, or copywriting assets.
DISC Profile: Attach a screenshot or PDF of your DISC assessment results (we love knowing how your brain ticks!).
Subject Line: Make the email subject line: "[Your Name] | The Operations & Creative Unicorn"

Ready to build something iconic? Apply now!

title: Short-Term Rental Liaison & Digital Marketing Specialist
description:
We are a growing property management and housekeeping company looking for a sharp, proactive Short-Term Rental (STR) Liaison to take over the day-to-day operations and digital presence of our rental properties in Nova Scotia, Canada.

If you love the fast-paced world of hospitality, have a flawless eye for detail, and know how to market properties to maximize bookings, this long-term position is for you. You will act as the central hub for our STR operations, taking the daily workload completely off the business owner's shoulders.

Daily Operational Responsibilities:

 - Quality Control: Review turnover cleaning reports, photos, and inspection videos submitted by field staff to ensure pristine, hotel-grade standards before guests arrive.

- Property Management Systems (PMS): Manage listings, calendars, and automations across Airbnb, VRBO,  ---------- , and our property management software (Hospitable).
 - Guest & Client Relations: Act as the primary point of contact for guest inquiries, booking requests, and property owners, handling all communication smoothly and professionally.
 - Logistics & Inventory: Track stock orders, manage supply levels for properties, and troubleshoot daily scheduling hitches or maintenance alerts.

Marketing & Digital Presence Responsibilities:
 - Social Media Management: Create engaging posts, reels, and content to advertise and promote our properties across social platforms.

 -Listing Optimization: Write compelling copy, update listings, and implement marketing strategies to increase visibility and occupancy rates across all booking websites.

 -Website Management: Assist with updating and maintaining our promotional website to drive direct bookings.

Requirements:

 * Proven experience managing short-term rentals using major platforms (Airbnb, VRBO,  ---------- ) and PMS software (Hospitable or similar).

 * Background in digital marketing, social media content creation, or website promotion specifically for real estate, hospitality, or travel.

 * Incredible attention to detail—you don't miss a thing when reviewing photos or checklist reports.

 * Strong problem-solving skills; you can handle an unexpected scheduling or guest issue calmly and independently.

 * Excellent written English and a warm, hospitable communication style.

 *Work Schedule: Must work the owner's exact hours: Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Atlantic Standard Time (AST).

To apply, please reply with the word **"ORCHESTRATOR"** at the very top of your message so I know you read the full post. Please include a brief summary of your STR platform experience, any marketing/social media work you've done for rentals, and your desired monthly salary range.

title: Senior MSP Engineer (Remote)
description:
PLEASE READ: Applicants MUST SUBMIT APPLICATION VIA THIS LINK to be considered:

 ---------- 

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Are you someone who thrives on precision, organization, and ensuring IT systems run smoothly and securely? Do you enjoy solving complex technical challenges and take pride in delivering seamless IT operations for clients?

We are looking for a passionate Senior MSP Engineer to join our team and help drive exceptional IT service delivery and innovation.

We’re itGenius, a leading cloud technology consulting firm and Google’s top partner in Australia. We’ve helped small and mid-sized businesses across the globe transform how they work with Google Workspace, AI, and automation. Our business model combines consulting expertise with scalable managed services that empower modern teams to do their best work.



If you’re an A-player—apply now!


? What's in it for you:

???? Competitive pay — December bonus, performance incentives & annual salary increases

????? Paid leaves plus all government-declared holidays

???? Annual team conferences — travel, food & accommodations covered

???? Training budget and clear career pathways

???? HMO allowance or PH government-mandated benefits



Job Overview:

The Senior MSP Engineer is a critical technical role responsible for delivering high-level IT support, network management, and infrastructure solutions for Managed Services clients. This position requires deep expertise in cloud services, networking, and security. The role includes acting as a future team leader by setting a high technical example, mentoring junior staff, and contributing to the development and alignment of the technical support team.



Accountabilities:

As the Senior MSP Engineer, you will be held accountable for:

Technical Service Delivery & Resolution

Focus: Maintaining quality, efficiency, and adherence to service standards.
Key Measurement Indicators (KPIs): Average resolution time, first-contact resolution rate, SLA adherence rate (90% or higher), CSAT scores (9/10 or higher), reduction in escalated issues.
Operational Optimisation & Reporting

Focus: Driving process improvement, data analysis, and accurate documentation.
Key Measurement Indicators (KPIs): Efficiency gains from new processes, accuracy and timeliness of reports, knowledge base article utilisation, adherence to team budgets.
Team Development & Leadership Potential

Focus: Setting a technical example, mentoring, and supporting team performance and growth.
Key Measurement Indicators (KPIs): Effectiveness of coaching and mentoring, overall team productivity and efficiency (10% improvement YOY), successful completion of training initiatives, team morale and retention rates.


Key Responsibilities:

Your day-to-day responsibilities will include:

1. Advanced Technical Support & Troubleshooting

Provide Level 2/3 technical support and remote assistance to Managed Services clients via phone, email, and onsite visits.
Diagnose and resolve complex software, hardware, and operating system issues on servers and workstations (Windows, Linux, macOS).
Utilise Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tools (e.g., Datto RMM, N-able, Nagios, Cacti) for proactive issue resolution and system analysis.
Troubleshoot and configure VoIP, IPTV, and various internet service-related problems.
2. Network and Infrastructure Management

Install, configure, and manage network infrastructure, including LAN/WAN, WiFi, VLANs, and VPNs.
Specialise in Unifi/Ubiquiti Networks for office setups and multi-tenant network management.
Configure and manage site-to-site VPN connections.
Conduct network and security assessments, providing recommendations for improvement.
Work with various network equipment, including Cisco and Juniper switches.
3. Cloud Services and Security

Administer and manage Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 environments for small and medium-sized businesses.
Lead client onboarding, including Google Workspace migrations.
Implement and ensure compliance with security policies, including those aligned with SMB 1001 security certification standards.
Perform domain security audits and manage Chrome Enterprise solutions.
4. Documentation and Process Management

Document IT i ---------- , solutions, and infrastructure upgrades using ticketing and documentation systems (e.g., Datto Autotask, IT glue, RT Ticketing system).
Ensure strict adherence to Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
Contribute to effective project management for IT infrastructure upgrades and Managed Services implementation.


Leadership & Mentoring Expectations

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong leadership potential and contribute to the team’s development by:

Mentoring and Coaching: Providing guidance and technical coaching to junior helpdesk tea ---------- mbers.
Leading by Example: Setting a high standard for technical execution, problem-solving, and service delivery.
Team Collaboration: Fostering a high-performing and collaborative team environment.
Taking Initiative: Speaking up, taking ownership, and showing full accountability for technical outcomes.
Future Leadership: Demonstrating readiness to take on further supervisory and management responsibilities as the team structure evolves.


Required Qualifications

5+ years of experience in an MSP or IT Support environment
Strong Level 3 engineering background with proven technical depth
Demonstrated experience mentoring or leading junior engineers
Solid understanding of cloud platforms (Google Workspace, M365)
Proven experience with network setup, configuration, and troubleshooting
Experience delivering onsite technical support to business clients
Certification(s) preferred: Google Workspace Administration Certification, CompTIA Network+, Security+, CCNA, Microsoft, or equivalent networking/cloud certifications are highly valued.



Required Skills

Strong Level 2/3 technical troubleshooting across Windows, Linux, and macOS environments
Advanced networking skills: LAN/WAN, WiFi, VLANs, VPNs, firewalls, and routing
Hands-on experience with Unifi/Ubiquiti Networks, Cisco, and Juniper switches
Proficiency using RMM tools (Datto RMM, N-able, Nagios, Cacti)
Strong understanding of cloud services: Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Chrome Enterprise
Experience managing site-to-site VPNs and multi-site network environments
Experience with SMB 1001-aligned security frameworks.
Experience with VoIP/IPTV Troubleshooting
Ability to conduct security assessments and implement SMB-level security standards
Excellent documentation skills using Autotask, IT Glue, or similar tools
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities
Excellent communication skills for client-facing and team interactions
Ability to work onsite in Melbourne and perform hands-on technical work
Leadership, mentoring, and team collaboration skills
Ability to prioritise, manage workload, and maintain SLA commitments


Nice-To-Have Qualifications

Experience with project-based infrastructure upgrades or migrations
Familiarity with Asana, Intercom, Autotask, IT Glue, or advanced ITSM systems
Prior leadership exposure or formal Team Lead responsibilities
Additional cloud or network certifications (e.g., CCNP, Google Workspace Admin, Azure)


Work Schedule

8-hour shift
Morning shift
Monday to Friday
Weekends off


You’ll thrive here if you’re interested in:

The evolving world of cloud-based work (Google Workspace, Google Drive, Gmail, etc.)
Supporting small business growth (1–30 employees)
Applying technology to improve productivity and collaboration

title: Senior Paid Media Specialist (Part Time - Meta Ads Focused)
description:
We are looking for a Senior Paid Media Specialist who understands that ads don’t stop at the click.

We are looking for a Meta Ads guru! Someone who knows Meta ads inside out and who can articulate in plain English what is good and what isn't regarding campaigns. 

You will know creative and targeting and how to set benchmarks for your campaigns. How to link Meta Ads to HubSpot and how to get the most out of a landing page. 

We’re searching for someone who goes beyond CPCs and CTRs and knows how to optimise campaigns based on pipeline, sales outcomes, and revenue attribution.

You’ll be responsible for managing and scaling campaigns across Google, Meta and LinkedIn for a portfolio of growth-focused clients.

If you enjoy diving deep into CRM data, attribution, conversion tracking and lead quality, this role will suit you perfectly.

What You’ll Do

Manage and scale paid campaigns across Google Ads, Meta & LinkedIn for a portfolio of service-based and B2B clients.

Take full ownership of campaign performance from first click through to closed revenue.

You will:

• Manage 4-5 client accounts with budgets ranging from $1k – $40k/month
• Optimise campaigns based on lead quality, pipeline and revenue outcomes
• Connect paid media with CRM systems such as HubSpot, GoHighLevel, Podium and Lana
• Analyse sales pipeline data to improve lead-to-opportunity and opportunity-to-sale conversion rates
• Implement closed-loop attribution to understand true campaign ROI
• Build high-quality Looker Studio reports with insights, commentary and strategic recommendations
• Run structured testing across audiences, creative, offers and landing pages
• Work closely with our strategy and creative teams to improve funnel performance
• Continuously improve CPA, CPL, SQL rate and ROAS

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys digging into the numbers and understanding what actually drives revenue.

What We’re Looking For

This role requires someone who understands performance marketing at a commercial level, not just platform management.

You should have:

• 5+ years hands-on experience running Meta campaigns
• Strong experience in lead generation marketing for service-based businesses
• Experience connecting paid media with CRM platforms (HubSpot, GoHighLevel)
• Experience analysing lead quality and sales pipeline data
• Advanced reporting skills using Looker Studio, GA4 and Google Sheets
• Strong understanding of conversion tracking, attribution and GTM
• Experience running B2B campaigns on LinkedIn Ads
• Ability to write engaging copy that converts


Hiring Process

Intro call
Short paid practical exercise
Final interview

This role will suit someone who enjoys working at the intersection of paid media, CRM data and revenue analytics.

title: Executive Assistant to the CEO & Operations Coordinator
description:

As the business continues to grow, we are looking for an exceptional Executive Assistant to become the CEO’s right hand and help ensure the business operates efficiently, professionally, and proactively.

We are looking for someone who takes ownership, follows through, solves problems independently, communicates proactively, and enjoys creating structure and organisation in a fast moving business.

The Opportunity

You will work directly with the Founder and CEO, supporting both executive and operational functions across the business.

You will be responsible for helping manage customer orders, supplier communications, logistics, administration, customer service, executive support, and daily operational coordination.

You will be a key person ensuring tasks are completed, customers are looked after, suppliers are followed up, and the CEO can focus on growth and strategy rather than day to day administration.

What Success Looks Like

After 90 days, the successful candidate will:

Applications that do not include this phrase may not be considered.

We are looking for a long term tea ---------- mber who wants to grow with the business and become a key part of our leadership support structure.

title: Lead Generation & Cold Outreach Specialist
description:
Email + LinkedIn Outreach | Full-Time | $600–$800/month DOE

We are looking for an experienced Lead Generation & Cold Outreach Specialist to help us build targeted prospect lists, run outbound campaigns, and generate qualified conversations through both cold email and LinkedIn outreach.

This role is ideal for someone who understands that successful outreach is not just about sending messages — it is about targeting the right people, writing strong personalized outreach, protecting deliverability, following up consistently, and turning prospects into booked calls.

Experience with Instantly, Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and recruiting or staffing agencies is a major plus.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

You will own and manage outbound lead generation from research to booked meetings, including:

* Building clean, targeted lead lists using Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, and other lead sources
* Researching companies, decision-makers, and prospects based on our ideal customer profile
* Creating and managing cold email campaigns in Instantly
* Setting up and maintaining inboxes, sending schedules, warm-up, rotation, and throttling
* Running LinkedIn cold outreach campaigns, including connection requests, follow-ups, and message sequences
* Writing and improving cold email and LinkedIn outreach copy
* Personalizing outreach based on prospect, company, role, or industry
* Validating leads, removing duplicates, and maintaining list hygiene
* Monitoring deliverability, bounce rates, spam issues, and inbox placement
* Running A/B tests on subject lines, messaging, offers, and follow-up sequences
* Tracking outreach performance and reporting results weekly

Key Metrics You’ll Track

You should be comfortable reporting on:

* Leads sourced
* Emails sent
* LinkedIn connection requests sent
* Connection acceptance rate
* Open rate
* Reply rate
* Positive reply rate
* Meetings booked
* Bounce rate
* Deliverability improvements

What We’re Looking For
Required:

* 3+ yrs Proven experience with B2B lead generation
* Experience running cold email outreach campaigns
* Experience using LinkedIn for cold outreach and prospecting
* Strong hands-on experience with Apollo
* Experience with Instantly or similar cold email platforms
* Strong understanding of email deliverability best practices
* Ability to build targeted, high-quality lead lists
* Strong written English and attention to detail
* Organized, reliable, and able to work independently
* Stable internet connection and professional work habits

Highly Preferred:

* Experience with LinkedIn Sales Navigator
* Experience supporting a recruiting, staffing, B2B service, or lead generation agency
* Experience booking meetings for sales teams
* Ability to write outreach copy that gets replies
* Strong understanding of targeting decision-makers for service-based businesses

What Success Looks Like

You will be successful in this role if you can:

* Build accurate, targeted lead lists
* Find the right decision-makers
* Launch cold email and LinkedIn campaigns that generate replies
* Protect and improve email deliverability
* Write outreach that feels relevant and human
* Consistently generate qualified conversations and booked calls
* Use data to improve campaign performance over time

Compensation

* $600–$800/month, depending on experience
* Full-time role
* 40 hours per week
* Long-term opportunity for the right person

How to Apply

To be considered, please send the following:

A short introduction about your background
Tools you have used, such as Apollo, Instantly, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, or others
Specific results you have achieved, such as:

* Reply rate
* Positive reply rate
* Meetings booked
* LinkedIn acceptance rate
* Deliverability improvements


We are looking for someone with real hands-on experience in lead generation, cold email, and LinkedIn outreach — not someone who is still learning the basics.

If you know how to find the right prospects, write strong outreach, manage campaigns, and turn cold leads into qualified conversations, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Backlink & AI Visibility Specialist
description:
DON'T APPLY IF >>>>> Please read carefully. We would like applicants to have tests, IQ and identity above 80%. Also please submit your best samples as they are factored for alignment with our quality. 

Backlink & AI Visibility Specialist (SEO · AEO · GEO)
We're a digital marketing agency specializing in trades and construction businesses, and we're hiring a Backlink & AI Visibility Specialist to build and grow off-page authority for our own brand and our clients' brands — and to make sure those brands are accurately cited and surfaced by AI-powered search engines, large language models (LLMs), and answer engines.
This is a hybrid role that bridges traditional link building with two emerging disciplines: Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO). You'll work across multiple client accounts, so you should already have a vetted list of backlink sources and placements you've personally used with success.
Experience: 2–5 years in link building or SEO
Employment type: Full-time (contract options available)
Focus areas: Link building + AEO/GEO + AI search & LLM citation

Core link-building responsibilities

Research and prospect link targets using Ahrefs, Semrush, and Moz — but prioritize niche relevance and real editorial traffic over raw DA/DR scores. A relevant link from a trade publication beats twenty links from high-authority sites no one reads
Run journalist-source and digital-PR outreach (email, LinkedIn) using current platforms — Qwoted, Source of Sources (SOS),  ---------- , and #JournoRequest on X (HARO/Connectively shut down in Dec 2024; don't rely on it) — to earn guest posts, editorial links, resource-page placements, and press coverage
Conduct competitor backlink analysis to identify gaps and replicate genuinely earnable, high-value opportunities
Monitor backlink profiles, identify toxic links, and submit disavow requests via Google Search Console
Work with the content team to develop linkable assets — original data studies, surveys, infographics, and long-form guides that publishers actually want to cite

Local citations & directory management

Manually build and manage local citations and business directory listings across Canadian and North American platforms — the kind of work a tool like BrightLocal automates, done by hand for accuracy and full control
Set up new accounts and complete listings from scratch where needed, keeping NAP (Name, Address, Phone) data consistent across every directory
Work from a provided master list of target directories for manual entry, and proactively flag additional niche- or region-relevant directories worth adding
Keep listings current over time — updating details, removing duplicates, and verifying entries

AEO, GEO & LLM citation responsibilities

Answer Engine Optimization (AEO): Optimize content to be selected as the direct answer in Google AI Overviews and AI Mode, Bing Copilot, Perplexity, voice assistants, and featured snippets
Generative Engine Optimization (GEO): Ensure our brands, products, and content are accurately represented and cited in outputs from generative AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini
Audit how — and whether — our brands are cited in LLM outputs (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity) and track citation frequency, accuracy, and sentiment over time
Earn mentions and citations on the sources AI answer engines index most heavily — authoritative publications, industry wikis, Reddit, Quora, and reputable government/educational domains

Note: many of these placements are "no-follow" and won't pass traditional link equity. For AEO/GEO that's expected — the goal is AI citation and entity strength, not PageRank. We want someone who understands that distinction.


Structure content for extraction: lead with a direct 40–60 word answer block, use FAQ schema, speakable schema, and clear entity definitions, and write in an objective, declarative voice (AI engines down-weight "we think / in our opinion" hedging)
Keep content fresh and specific — recency and concrete statistics are heavily weighted, especially by Perplexity, which refreshes near-daily and cites 4–8 sources per answer
Make sure AI crawlers can actually read the page: critical text rendered server-side (not injected by client-side JavaScript), and the right crawler access configured (e.g. OAI-SearchBot for ChatGPT search eligibility, distinct from GPTBot training access)
Develop and execute digital PR strategies aimed at earning mentions on high-E-E-A-T domains that serve as training and retrieval sources for AI models
Strengthen entity recognition across AI systems by optimizing Knowledge Panels, Wikidata entries, and third-party review platforms (G2, Trustpilot, Clutch)


How we work — our standards (non-negotiables)
Link building in 2026 is about earning citations and trust, not gaming volume. We expect you to:

Earn, don't manipulate. No PBNs, link farms, paid link networks, comment spam, or scaled/automated link schemes. Google's spam updates penalize these, and one bad shortcut can damage a client account we've spent months building.
Manually vet every target. Real editorial standards, real organic traffic, real topical relevance — not just a metric in a tool.
Disclose paid placements with rel="sponsored" where applicable.
Build relationships, not one-offs. Repeat publisher relationships beat cold-blasting templates that editors delete on sight.
Honour the human edit pass. Any AI-assisted outreach or content draft gets a human voice + accuracy review before it goes out. Drafts are never sent or published raw.

How we measure success
This role is tracked against clear outputs, reviewed monthly. You'll work from a shared tracker (provided) and keep it current. Core KPIs:

Links/citations earned — count, by type (editorial, guest, resource, directory, digital PR) and by client
Quality of placements — relevance + real traffic, not just DR/DA
Live link survival rate — placements that stay live and indexed
Citation tracking (AEO/GEO): a monthly prompt audit — run 20–30 buyer-intent prompts per client across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Google AI Overviews/AI Mode, logging whether the brand is cited, citation accuracy, and sentiment over time
Local citation health — directory listings live, NAP-consistent, duplicate-free
Toxic-link monitoring — flagged and disavowed as needed

Detailed recurring tasks, cadence, and tracking columns live in the accompanying VA task tracker.

What we're looking for

2–5 years of hands-on link building or SEO experience
A documented track record of placements you can show us (live URLs)
Working knowledge of AEO/GEO is a strong plus; if you don't have it yet, you must be able to learn it fast
Comfortable with high-volume, detail-accurate manual data entry — citation and directory work rewards consistency and care
Honest grasp of what does and doesn't work — e.g., why self-placed Wikipedia links get reverted and how legitimate, policy-compliant entity presence is actually built

Details

Full-time, with contract options available
pay range, weekly hours, and any time zone overlap you require]


How to apply
To be considered, your application must include:

Your 3–5 best link-building or digital-PR samples — live URLs, each with a one-line note on why it mattered. Samples are weighted heavily; they're how we judge fit against our quality bar.
ID Proof and IQ scores of 80% or higher on your profile

title: Projects & Operations Manager (Contractor)
description:
PHP 150 to PHP 175 per Hour

Asana, Freshsales CRM and Automation
Remote contractor engagement | Open to Philippines-based applicants | Australian client

We run three connected businesses out of Australia and there are a lot of moving parts. We need one organised, switched-on person to own the systems behind all of it so nothing slips through the cracks.
If you genuinely enjoy taking a messy pile of emails, ideas, and half-finished tasks and turning it into a clean tracked project that actually gets finished, you are going to like this role.
This is a contractor engagement, not a job. You manage your own hours, equipment, invoicing, and tax. But we are looking for someone for the long run, not a one-off.
Who you would be working with
The businesses span accommodation, tourism, events, podcasting, and digital marketing:

iBooked.Online builds websites and book-direct marketing strategies for accommodation businesses.
The Accommodation Show is a weekly podcast featuring leaders from across the short-term rental and accommodation industry.
STRIVE Conference is an industry event series with speakers, sponsors, partners, and attendees, currently expanding across APAC.
On top of those there is a steady stream of client work, marketing, partnerships, and content projects.
What you would actually do
Your job is to make sure work gets tracked, people get followed up, deadlines get hit, and information lives in the right place instead of scattered across our inboxes. In practice that breaks down into a few areas.
Run our projects in Asana
Asana is where delivery happens. You would build and maintain clear projects for events, podcast production, website work, marketing, and internal jobs. That means turning conversations, emails, and meeting notes into real tasks with owners, due dates, and priorities. You would spot overdue tasks and missing assets before they become problems, keep the boards genuinely useful rather than cluttered, and send us a regular summary of what is done, what is coming up, and what is stuck.
Run our pipeline in Freshsales CRM
Freshsales is where relationships and follow-ups ----------  sponsors, partners, leads, speakers, suppliers. You would keep records accurate, track next actions and pipeline stages, and make sure no opportunity dies because someone forgot to follow up. We also want your help building simple CRM processes and sensible automations rather than just data entry.
Coordinate the STRIVE conferences
Events are where the most balls are in the air. You would manage speaker and sponsor deliverables, chase bios, headshots, presentation files, logos, contracted inclusions, ticket allocations, invoices, and marketing deadlines, and keep the operational checklists and timelines current.
Keep the podcast and content moving
You would manage the workflow for guests, recordings, transcripts, show notes, and promo, so every episode moves smoothly from booking to launch without us having to remember what stage it is at.
Coordinate website and marketing projects
You would track client content, images, approvals, and outstanding actions, and coordinate between clients, designers, developers, and marketing contractors so projects stay on schedule.
Improve how we work
When you spot a process we keep doing manually, we want you to say so and fix it. Templates, checklists, SOPs, and practical automations between Freshsales, Asana, and Google Workspace. You do not need to be a developer. You do need to look at a repetitive task and think "this should be automated."
The systems you would use

Asana for project and task delivery
Freshsales CRM for contacts, pipelines, and follow-ups
Automation and AI: Zapier or Make, Google Workspace, Asana rules and templates, Freshsales workflows, and AI tools like ChatGPT for summaries, drafts, and documentation
Who we are looking for
The right person:

Has real experience in project management, operations, or delivery coordination
Is strong in Asana, or a very similar project management tool
Has CRM experience, ideally Freshsales or Freshworks, but HubSpot, Pipedrive, or similar is fine
Writes excellent, professional English
Can run their own deliverables and deadlines without being chased
Goes looking for missing information, overdue tasks, and gaps instead of waiting to be told
Is comfortable following up external people directly
Understands the difference between recording a task and actually driving it to done
You do not have to be an expert in both Asana and Freshsales today. The ideal candidate is genuinely strong in one, comfortable with pipeline-style CRM work, and quick to learn the other and build sensible workflows between the two.
Bonus points for
Experience with any of these will stand out: Freshsales setup and automation, advanced Asana (rules, dashboards, reporting), Zapier or Make, event or conference coordination, sponsor and partnership management, podcast production, website agency or digital marketing project management, SOP documentation, AI-assisted workflows, and working with Australian, New Zealand, UK, or US clients.
Hours and availability
This is flexible and based on what is active. Expect roughly 15 to 20 hours per week to start, rising and falling with projects and the conference calendar.
No fixed daily hours, but you will need to attend the occasional meeting, stay responsive around deadlines, and have some practical overlap with Melbourne, Australia time. You are free to work with other clients as long as there is no conflict of interest.
Rate
Hourly contractor rate in PHP, invoiced for approved hours worked.
Indicative range: PHP 150 to PHP 175 per hour, depending on your experience, ability to own projects, CRM skills, and practical automation capability. Demonstrated Freshsales and Asana experience puts you toward the top of that range.
Confidentiality
You would handle business, sponsor, partner, and client information, content calendars, and account access. You will need to sign a contractor agreement covering confidentiality, data protection, account security, intellectual property, and conflicts of interest. Any access you are given is used only for agreed work and kept secure.


How to apply: (Read this section)

We are running an industry conference involving 25 speakers, 10 sponsors, potential partners and sales opportunities, presentation files, speaker photos and bios, sponsor logos and contracted inclusions, ticket allocations, invoices, marketing deadlines, and several external contractors.
Tell us how you would organise this using Asana and Freshsales. Cover:

What you would track in Asana
What you would track in Freshsales
How you would manage deadlines and missing information
How you would track sponsors and partner opportunities
What you would automate
What you would report to us each week

We want a practical answer from real thinking and experience, not a generic AI-written one. Applications that skip this question may not be read.


Subject line
Use this exact subject line:
Independent Contractor Application | Asana + Freshsales | [Your Name]

title: Inventory / Purchasing Analyst
description:
Location: Fully Remote
Pay Rate: $12 – $15 per hour

Topline Heating and Air is seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Inventory / Purchasing Analyst to take ownership of inventory management and purchasing decisions. This is not a data-entry role. We are looking for someone who can analyze inventory trends, forecast demand, and make informed purchasing decisions to support operational efficiency and budget goals.

Key Responsibilities
Analyze inventory levels, usage trends, and line-item accuracy across systems
Identify discrepancies and ensure inventory data integrity
Forecast inventory needs based on historical data, demand patterns, and business requirements
Create and manage purchase orders aligned with forecasting and budget guidelines
Work closely with operations and finance teams to ensure inventory availability and cost efficiency
Review supplier orders and adjust purchasing strategies as needed
Monitor stock levels to prevent shortages or overstock situations
Support continuous improvement of inventory tracking and purchasing processes

What We’re Looking For
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience in inventory management, purchasing, or supply chain preferred
Ability to move beyond data entry into analysis and decision-making
High attention to detail and accuracy with numbers and records
Comfortable working with spreadsheets, inventory systems, or ERP tools
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities

Why This Role Matters
This role plays a critical part in ensuring the right materials are available at the right time while maintaining cost control. Your insights will directly influence purchasing decisions and operational success.

To apply, please email your resume to  ----------  with a subject line "Inventory / Purchasing Analyst". Please summarize your experience in the email content. Applicants who fail to follow instructions will not be considered for the position.

title: GoHighLevel & Growth Systems Manager

title: Business Coordinator
description:
Boost Auto is a New Zealand-based automotive consultancy operating at the intersection of marketing, sales performance, and emerging technology. This role exists to provide direct operational and research support to the Principal, enabling the business to execute at a higher level and with greater precision.

The incoming Business Coordinator will take ownership of day-to-day research functions, some book-keeping, operational workflow, and tool management — freeing the Principal to focus on client delivery, strategic development, and business growth.

The ideal candidate will be a business graduate looking for additional work with 2-4 years post grad work experience, they'll be an analytical critical thinker, disciplined with high standards of English literacy and a confident communicator. They will also be a confident user of Ai tools (mainly Claude, some Copilot). A knowledge or interest in cars is an advantage but not a pre-requisite.  

Key Responsibilities
1. Research and Market Intelligence
• Conduct structured research into the New Zealand automotive market, including registrations, trends, emerging brands, and competitor activity.
• Compile and present findings in clear, formatted summaries suitable for client delivery or internal decision-making.
• Monitor industry news, regulatory changes, and OEM developments relevant to Boost Auto’s client base.
• Support the Principal with data sourcing and analysis for proposals, reports, and presentations
2. Operations and Workflow
• Manage and maintain the business task board in Trello, ensuring priorities are current and actions are tracked.
• Support the development and maintenance of workflow automations to reduce manual administration.
• Coordinate scheduling, follow-ups, and project tracking across active engagements.
3. AI Tool Support
• Assist in the practical application of AI tools to streamline research, content production, and business processes.
• Identify opportunities to automate repetitive tasks using AI-assisted workflows.
• Stay current on relevant AI tool developments and recommend adoption where appropriate
4. General Business Support
• Provide administrative and coordination support as required by the Principal.
• Assist with document preparation, formatting, and version control.
• Handle ad hoc tasks with initiative and without requiring constant direction.


We use Trello, JotForm, Claude, MS365 and Xero (accounting software). You do not need Xero experience, but you have to understand the basics of accounting book-keeping. 

10 hours per week minimum. We offered 4 weeks paid leave (2 of which must be at Christmas). There is a possibility of additional work from time to time.

title: PrePress & Customer Service Specialist ( AU Client)
description:
Job description:

About the Role

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented PrePress & Customer Service Specialist to join our team. This role is ideal for someone with strong technical prepress expertise, excellent communication skills, and the ability to work collaboratively across multiple departments.

The successful candidate will be responsible for reviewing and preparing customer artwork for production, ensuring files meet print specifications, and identifying potential issues before they impact manufacturing. In addition, this role will provide backup support to the Customer Service team when required, including order processing and customer communication.

We are looking for a quick learner who can develop a strong understanding of our pricing structures, print parameters, products, and business processes while maintaining exceptional quality standards.

Key Responsibilities

PrePress & Artwork Management

Monitor jobs for potential production risks and proactively recommend solutions.

Process customer orders accurately and efficiently.
Respond to customer enquiries via email and other communication channels.
Assist customers with artwork-related questions and print specifications.
Maintain professional and timely communication with clients.
Technical Skills & ExperienceEssential

Strong understanding of print production processes, preferably within digital and flexographic printing environments.

Colour profiling
Knowledge of:
Trapping

Overprinting
Die lines
Imposition

Ability to identify artwork errors, inconsistencies, and potential production issues before printing.
Experience working within a fast-paced production environment.
Preferred

Experience in label printing, packaging, or commercial print production.
Exposure to digital and flexographic printing technologies.
Previous experience supporting customer service or order processing functions.
Personal Attributes

Excellent spoken and written English communication skills.
Exceptional attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.
Ability to explain technical concepts in clear, simple language.
Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Ability to confidently seek clarification when requirements are unclear.
Highly organised with strong record-keeping and version control practices.
Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines.

Artwork files are consistently prepared accurately and on time.

title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
description:
Senior Full Stack Engineer

Role Definition
One of the core contributors for the development of core payment processing systems and admin dashboard functionality for our payment platform. This role requires deep expertise in both backend payment processing architecture and frontend dashboard development, with primary focus on backend systems that handle financial transactions at scale.

Core Responsibilities
•	Payment Processing Architecture: Design and implement secure, PCI-compliant payment processing systems supporting credit cards, ACH, and digital wallets
•	Transaction Management: Build comprehensive transaction lifecycle management including processing, refunds, voids, and settlement reconciliation
•	Payment Gateway Integration: Architect and implement integrations with multiple payment processors while maintaining flexibility to switch providers
•	Admin Dashboard Backend: Develop robust APIs for transaction monitoring, customer management, and merchant administration
•	Database Design: Design and optimize database schemas for high-volume transaction processing and real-time reporting
•	Security Implementation: Implement tokenization, encryption, and fraud detection systems meeting PCI DSS compliance requirements
•	API Development: Create RESTful APIs with consistent error handling, authentication, and webhook capabilities
•	Performance Optimization: Ensure sub-3-second transaction processing times and system scalability for high-volume periods

Key Accountabilities & KPIs
•	Transaction Processing Performance: Maintain <3-second processing times for 99.9% of transactions
•	System Reliability: Achieve 99.9% uptime for payment processing services
•	API Quality: Deliver APIs with <100ms average response times and comprehensive error handling
•	Security Compliance: Pass all PCI DSS audits and security reviews without critical findings
•	Integration Success: Successfully integrate with primary payment processors within timeline
•	Code Quality: Maintain >80% test coverage for payment processing modules
•	Database Performance: Optimize queries to handle 10M+ transactions with <500ms response times

Required Technical Skills
•	Backend Technologies: 5+ years with C#/.NET Core, ASP.NET Web API, Entity Framework
•	Database Expertise: Advanced SQL Server/MSSQL, database design, performance optimization
•	Payment Processing: Experience with payment gateways (Stripe,  ---------- ), PCI compliance, tokenization
•	Frontend Development: Proficient in React, TypeScript/JavaScript, modern CSS frameworks
•	API Design: RESTful services, authentication (OAuth 2.0, JWT), webhook implementation
•	Security: Understanding of encryption, secure coding practices, vulnerability management
•	Cloud Platforms: Experience with Azure services, particularly App Services and SQL Database
•	Version Control: Git workflows, code review processes, branching strategies

Preferred Capabilities
•	Previous fintech or payment processing industry experience
•	Experience working for startup or high-growth organizations, with the ability to thrive in fast-paced, evolving environments
•	Experience with high-volume transaction systems (>$1M monthly processing)
•	Familiarity with fraud detection systems and risk management
•	Understanding of financial regulations and compliance requirements
•	Experience with microservices architecture and event-driven systems
•	Knowledge of performance monitoring tools (Application Insights, New Relic)

Why OutsideHire?
•	An inclusive and dynamic work environment that values creativity and collaboration
•	Opportunities to work on cutting-edge projects that make an impact
•	Perks: Competitive salary, remote work, professional development support, etc.
•	A team that truly values your skills and contributions

title: Senior AI-First Mobile App Developer (Contract, Remote)
description:
We are hiring a senior mobile app developer to lead the build of a new consumer mobile product from MVP through launch. This is a hands-on contract role for someone who can move quickly, work independently, and use modern AI-assisted development tools as a core part of their workflow.
Because this role will involve confidential product information, shortlisted candidates will be required to sign a non-disclosure agreement before full product details, workflows, and technical specifications are shared.

Role Overview
This role is ideal for a builder who is comfortable owning execution across frontend, backend integration, and release preparation for a mobile app. We are specifically looking for a developer who already uses AI coding tools such as Cursor, Claude Code, and similar platforms to accelerate development, debugging, refactoring, documentation, and testing.
The right candidate should be able to take a product from scoped requirements to a functional production-ready application, using AI tools intelligently without sacrificing code quality, architecture, security, or maintainability.

Key Responsibilities
Build and ship a full mobile application for iOS and Android.
Own day-to-day implementation across core app features, API integrations, data flows, and release readiness.
Use AI coding tools in a practical, high-output way to speed up development while maintaining strong engineering judgment.
Translate product requirements into clean technical architecture and execution plans.
Write production-quality, maintainable, well-structured code.
Set up and improve development workflows, including debugging, code reviews, testing, and documentation.
Collaborate with product and stakeholder teams to clarify scope, timelines, risks, and tradeoffs.
Identify blockers early and propose solutions quickly.
Help define the MVP build path, technical decisions, and launch priorities.
Support deployment, bug fixing, QA handoff, and app store submission readiness.

Required Qualifications
Ability to work EST hours 9AM-5PM Eastern Timezone.
Strong experience building and shipping full mobile applications from scratch.
Ability to develop a complete app, not just isolated tickets or UI components.
Proven proficiency with AI-assisted coding tools such as Cursor (main tool), Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, or similar.
Strong judgment around when to rely on AI-generated code and when to manually architect or refine solutions.
Experience working across frontend application logic, backend/API integration, authentication, state management, and performance optimization.
Solid understanding of software architecture, debugging, version control, and testing practices.
Ability to read, evaluate, and improve code generated through AI workflows.
Strong communication skills and comfort working in a fast-moving, async remote environment.

Preferred Background
Experience building cross-platform mobile apps using frameworks such as React Native, Expo, or Flutter.
Experience shipping consumer subscription apps or data-driven mobile products.
Familiarity with analytics, push notifications, third-party integrations, and production monitoring.
Experience building secure applications with privacy-conscious engineering practices.
Previous experience as a lead developer, founding engineer, or highly autonomous senior contractor.

Recommended Tech Stack
The stack below reflects our current preferred direction. Candidates do not need deep expertise in every tool listed, but should be comfortable working within a modern TypeScript-based mobile stack and making sound technical decisions across the full product lifecycle.
Mobile: React Native + Expo, TypeScript, Expo Router, Zustand + TanStack Query, NativeWind or Tamagui, React Hook Form + Zod, Victory Native or similar charting libraries, React Native Reanimated, Expo SecureStore + MMKV, WatermelonDB or SQLite-based local storage, Expo Notifications, Expo Linking, Sentry React Native, and PostHog React Native.
Backend / API: Node.js, Next.js API routes or Hono, TypeScript across the full stack, tRPC, Supabase Auth, PostgreSQL, Drizzle ORM, Zod for shared validation, Inngest or  ---------- v for background jobs, Supabase Storage, and Resend, SendGrid, or similar transactional email tooling.
AI / Developer Workflow: Cursor (main tool), Claude Code, GitHub Copilot or comparable AI-assisted development tools, Anthropic Claude and OpenAI APIs or comparable LLM integrations, plus prompt/version management and AI observability tooling where appropriate.
Infrastructure / DevOps: Render, Supabase, GitHub Actions, Expo EAS Build / Submit, Sentry, Better Stack or similar monitoring tools, and Cloudflare or a comparable CDN / security layer.
Product / Operations: RevenueCat, PostHog, ClickUp, Slack, Figma, and Notion or equivalent documentation tooling.

What We’re Looking For
Fast execution without sloppy code.
Strong product instincts, not just technical output.
Comfort using AI as a serious development multiplier.
Someone who can turn ambiguous requirements into shipped software.
Someone who understands how to balance speed, quality, and pragmatism in an MVP environment.

title: Amazon Catalogue Operations Specialist (UK & EU Marketplaces)
description:
Auraglow is a UK-based lighting brand selling our own products across Amazon UK and multiple Amazon EU marketplaces.

We are looking for a full-time Amazon Catalogue Operations Specialist to take ownership of our Amazon listings across the UK and EU.

This is not a generic VA role and not a PPC position. The role is focused on catalogue structure, listing health, compliance, variations, flat files, and execution.

We need someone who can work independently, use initiative, document their work clearly, and take ownership of Amazon catalogue issues without needing daily hand-holding.

You will be responsible for the day-to-day health and maintenance of our Amazon listings, including:

Creating, maintaining, and fixing parent/child variations across Amazon UK and EU marketplaces
Ensuring listing parity between UK and EU marketplaces
Using flat files for bulk listing creation, deletion, and updates
Monitoring and resolving issues in the Build International Listings (BIL) report
Manually creating or correcting EU listings where BIL fails
Deploying A+ Content across marketplaces using provided assets
Updating existing listings with improved AI-assisted translations
Resolving suppressed, inactive, or blocked listings
Managing Amazon compliance-related listing issues, including missing attributes, documentation requests, and marketplace-specific policy warnings
Cleaning up old or discontinued listings
Maintaining accurate task logs and issue reports

This role does not involve:

Amazon PPC or advertising management
Pricing strategy
Customer service
General admin work
Social media management

This is a specialist Amazon catalogue operations role.

Please only apply if you have hands-on experience with most of the following:

Amazon Seller Central
Amazon UK and EU marketplaces
Flat file uploads and processing reports
Parent/child variation creation and repair
Suppressed listing resolution
Amazon compliance warnings and Account Health issues
Build International Listings / EU marketplace parity
Brand Registry accounts
Managing large SKU catalogues

We are looking for someone who has personally fixed catalogue problems, not just monitored them or passed them to Seller Support.

Working Hours & Pay:
Full-time role: 40 working hours per week
Monday to Friday
UK working-hours overlap required
Salary: $1,200–$1,600 USD per month, depending on experience
Paid via Wise
Long-term role for the right person
Pay review after 6 months based on performance and contribution

This is intended to be a full-time role with one company, not a multi-client freelance position. During agreed working hours, we expect your focus to be on our business only.

There will be a paid trial period before confirming the long-term role.

During the trial, we require:

Daily task updates
Clear issue logging
Weekly progress summaries
Time tracking via Hubstaff during working hours

This is not intended as micromanagement. It is to ensure clear visibility, accurate payroll, and proper task tracking for a remote full-time role.

What We Value:

Reliability
Clear communication
Attention to detail
Ownership
Honesty about blockers
Ability to work independently
Calm problem-solving when Amazon issues are unclear

Amazon catalogue work can be messy. We need someone who can investigate issues properly, document what they find, and propose sensible next steps.

Please answer the following questions in your application:

Which Amazon marketplaces have you personally worked on?
What is your experience with Amazon flat files?
Describe the most complex parent/child variation issue you have personally fixed.
Describe an Amazon compliance or suppressed listing issue you have personally resolved.
Are you available full-time and willing to work exclusively for one company during agreed working hours?
Are you comfortable using Hubstaff during the trial period?
What is your expected monthly salary in USD?

Applications that do not answer these questions properly will not be considered.

Final Note:

We are looking for a long-term tea ---------- mber who can take real ownership of Amazon catalogue operations.

If you are experienced, reliable, organised, and comfortable solving Amazon catalogue issues independently, we would like to hear from you.

title: Comping Manager
description:
Company Overview: We are a real estate investment company specializing in acquiring vacant land. We are seeking a dedicated and detail-oriented Texting Virtual Assistant to join our team. This role involves sending out texts to property owners and responding to inbound messages, ensuring prompt and professional communication.

Key Responsibilities:

Role Overview
We're hiring a full-time Appraiser to underwrite properties and determine accurate market values using our proprietary AI-driven valuation software. You'll be the human judgment layer on top of our tech — running properties through the platform, validating outputs, and delivering reliable value determinations that drive our acquisition decisions.
Responsibilities

Underwrite incoming properties to determine market value using our AI-driven software
Review and validate software-generated valuations, applying judgment to edge cases
Analyze comparable sales, market trends, and property characteristics to confirm or adjust outputs
Maintain consistency and accuracy across a high volume of property evaluations
Flag data discrepancies or anomalies that could affect valuation quality
Communicate value determinations clearly to the acquisitions and disposition teams
Communication Management: Maintain a high level of professionalism in all communications, ensuring responses are clear, concise, and aligned with company guidelines.

Documentation: Record and update all communication logs, ensuring accurate and up-to-date records of interactions.

Follow-Up: Manage follow-up texts with leads as needed, keeping the conversation ongoing and engaging.

Reporting: Provide regular updates on messaging activity, response rates, and any notable interactions to the team.

Qualifications:

Experience: Previous experience in a similar role, particularly in a remote setting, is preferred.

Communication Skills: Excellent written communication skills with a focus on clarity and customer engagement.

Tech-Savvy: Comfortable using various software and messaging platforms. Experience with CRM systems is a plus.

Time Management: Ability to manage multiple conversations simultaneously and prioritize tasks effectively.

Attention to Detail: Strong attention to detail, ensuring all communications are accurate and professional.

Reliability: Must be dependable, with the ability to work independently and meet deadlines.

Working Hours: Flexible hours, with the expectation of timely responses during agreed-upon working periods.

Compensation: Competitive, based on experience and performance.

How to Apply: If you are interested in this position, please send your resume  ---------- 
Please record a **1–2 minute Loom video** answering the questions below. Keep it casual but professional, we just want to get a sense of your communication style and personality.

**Record your Loom video with your camera on**, and then share the link when you submit your application.

Questions to answer:

1. What excites you most about working as a texting virtual assistant for a real estate investment company?
2. How do you make sure your written communication sounds professional, friendly, and human — not robotic?
3. Tell us about a time you managed multiple chats, emails, or messages at once. How did you stay organized and timely?
4. Have you ever used CRMs, texting platforms, or automation tools before? If so, which ones?
5. Why do you think you’d be a great fit for Land property solutions culture and fast-paced environment?

- High attention to detail and accuracy
- Ability to review data carefully and identify inconsistencies
- Good written English communication skills
- Comfortable sending professional emails to customers
- Reliable, punctual, and able to stay on top of daily deadlines
- Experience with invoicing, billing, reconciliation, or payment follow-ups

Work Schedule

title: Microsoft 365 Solutions Specialist (SharePoint, Teams & Governance)
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*

About the Organization 

EJF Real Estate Services and The Nabo Group are growing organizations focused on real estate investment and property/HOA management across multiple regions. Our operations rely heavily on structured document management, secure collaboration, and scalable systems to support property portfolios, boards, and internal teams. 

We are undergoing a transformation to modernize and standardize our Microsoft 365 environment, with a strong focus on SharePoint Online, governance, and operational efficiency. 

Overview 

We are seeking an experienced Microsoft 365 Solutions Specialist (SharePoint, Teams & Governance) to design and implement a scalable, secure, and governance-driven collaboration environment tailored to a multi-entity real estate and property management organization within the Corporate IT Department. 

This role will focus on building a structured and standardized environment across SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, and Microsoft Entra ID, enabling efficient document management, controlled access, and future-ready AI and automation capabilities. 

The ideal candidate is a strategic architect, not just an administrator, with experience designing environments that support distributed teams, regional operations, and complex permission models.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Architecture & Design:

•  Design enterprise-grade SharePoint Online architecture aligned to:  
 -  Properties / communities (HOAs)  
 -  Regions / portfolios  
 -  Internal departments (Finance, Operations, Legal, etc.)  
•  Implement a hub-and-spoke model with clear hierarchy and navigation  
•  Define scalable information architecture:  
 -  Metadata vs folder structures  
 -  Content types and taxonomy  
 -  Standardized document libraries  
•  Establish site provisioning models and templates for repeatable property onboarding  
•  Align SharePoint with Microsoft Teams and OneDrive usage patterns  

Governance & Compliance:

•  Develop and document a SharePoint governance framework tailored to property management operations:  
 -  Naming conventions (properties, regions, departments)  
 -  Site lifecycle management (onboarding/offboarding properties)  
 -  Permission models (board members, internal staff, vendors)  
•  Implement data governance and compliance controls:  
 -  Retention policies for property records and financial documents  
 -  Sensitivity labels and secure document handling  
 -  External sharing controls for board members and third parties  
•  Ensure alignment with real estate and property management operational needs  

Microsoft 365 Integration:  

•  Architect seamless integration across:  
 -  SharePoint Online  
 -  Microsoft Teams (for property and internal collaboration)  
 -  OneDrive (personal vs organizational data boundaries)  
 -  Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) 
•  Design scalable identity and access strategies:  
 -  Role-based access (internal staff, board members, vendors)  
 -  Group-based provisioning  
 -  Conditional access awareness  

Automation & AI Readiness:

•  Structure SharePoint for Microsoft Copilot readiness (clean, well-organized data)  
•  Identify opportunities for:  
 -  Power Automate workflows (approvals, document routing, onboarding)  
 -  Process automation for property management operations  
•  Improve search, discoverability, and document intelligence  

Migration & Optimization:

•  Assess current SharePoint and file storage environments  
•  Identify gaps, duplication, and inefficiencies  
•  Develop a migration and consolidation strategy  
•  Optimize usability for both:  
 -  Internal teams  
 -  Non-technical users (property managers, board members)  

Documentation & Knowledge Transfer:

•  Deliver:  
 -  Architecture diagrams  
 -  Governance framework documentation  
 -  Implementation and rollout roadmap  
•  Provide knowledge transfer to internal IT leadership and support teams  


Required Qualifications:

•  5+ years of experience with SharePoint Online (modern experience required)  
•  5+ years across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem  
•  Proven experience designing enterprise or multi-entity SharePoint architectures  
•  Strong understanding of:  
 -  Microsoft Teams & SharePoint integration  
 -  Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD)  
 -  Complex permission and access control models  
•  Experience with:  
 -  Metadata and taxonomy design  
 -  Governance frameworks  
 -  Scalable site provisioning strategies  


Preferred Qualifications:

•  Experience supporting property management, real estate, or HOA environments  
•  Experience with multi-entity or portfolio-based organizations  
•  Familiarity with:  
 -  Microsoft Purview (retention, compliance, labeling)  
 -  Power Platform (Power Automate, Power Apps)  
 -  Microsoft Copilot readiness and data structuring  
• Microsoft certifications (nice to have):  
 -  MS-100 / MS-101  
 -  MS-700  
 -  SharePoint / M365 certifications 

Working Hours: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm EST; Monday – Friday (US time zones)

Benefits: HMO and PTO after 90 days.

1. Please check out the company website before submitting an application:  ---------- .

3. Please indicate that you're hourly rate in USD..
4. Please send a link to your video recording introducing yourself & your relevant experience based on the job description, and include your OnlineJobs.PH profile link.

Kindly send your application to  ----------  the subject line, kindly indicate the position SharePoint / Microsoft 365 Specialist - your name.
