Looking forward to working with you!

title: Operations Coordinator (VA)
description:
PLEASE SEE HOW TO APPLY AT THE BOTTOM

About Alpha Key Digital


Alpha Key Digital is a digital marketing agency specializing in patient acquisition for cosmetic surgeons, dermatologists, medical spas, and cosmetic dentists. We’ve been in business for 10 years, serve 12 retainer clients, and are growing. We’re a small team that operates with precision. No bloat. No unnecessary meetings. Clear communication, clean systems, and work that gets done when it’s supposed to get done.



We are looking for a Virtual Assistant to take operational, administrative, and coordination tasks off the founder’s plate so he can focus on client strategy, business development, and growth. This is a high-trust role. You will be the person who keeps the engine running behind the scenes.



The Role: Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator. Full-time. Remote. Must have significant overlap with US Eastern business hours.



You are the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks. The founder runs the client relationships and the strategy. You run the operational backbone that supports it: email triage, project management, scheduling, contractor coordination, content posting, and prospect list management. You don’t wait to be told what to do. You see the gap and you close it.



This is not a customer-facing role. You will not be talking to clients directly. You will be the person behind the curtain making sure every deliverable, every email, every task, and every calendar event is where it’s supposed to be so the founder can walk into any conversation fully prepared.



You’d Be a Great Fit If You Are



The person who builds the checklist before anyone asks for one. You organize by instinct, not because someone gave you a system to follow. You create the system.
Reliable to the point where people stop checking your work because they already know it’s done. You don’t need reminders. You are the reminder.
Someone who reads the full email before responding. You catch the detail in the third paragraph that everyone else misses. You ask the clarifying question before it becomes a problem.
Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision. You don’t need daily check-ins to stay productive. You need clear expectations and then space to execute.
Proactive about surfacing problems early. If something is off, you flag it immediately instead of hoping it resolves itself. You know the difference between handling it yourself and escalating it.
Disciplined with time. You batch similar work together. You don’t context-switch 15 times a day. When you’re in a task, you finish it before moving on.
Protective of other people’s calendars. You treat the founder’s schedule like a strategic asset, not a suggestion. If something doesn’t belong on the calendar, you say so.
Someone who takes pride in clean systems. Your Asana is current. Your inbox is processed. Your files are named correctly. You don’t leave loose ends.


What You Actually Do





Email and Communication Management



Triage the founder’s inbox daily. Flag urgent items, draft responses for routine emails, archive what doesn’t need attention.
Manage Slack communication with the white-label development team (E2M) and contractors. Relay updates, follow up on late deliverables, keep threads organized.
Ensure no email or message sits unanswered for more than 24 hours during business days.


Project Management and Task Coordination

Own Asana: create tasks from briefs, assign to the right tea ---------- mber, track deadlines, follow up on overdue items, and keep the board clean.
Coordinate deliverables between the founder, PPC contractor, SEO team, and E2M web development team. You are the traffic controller.
Run a weekly check on all active client projects to confirm nothing is behind schedule. Surface delays to the founder before the client notices.


Calendar and Scheduling

Manage the founder’s calendar. Schedule client meetings, prospect calls, internal huddles, and personal appointments.
Protect time blocks. The founder has dedicated strategic work time that should not be interrupted by meetings. You enforce that.
Confirm and send reminders for all meetings. No missed appointments.


LinkedIn and Prospect List Management

Schedule LinkedIn posts (batch-created by the founder) using a scheduling tool.
Export connection lists from Ulinc (LinkedIn automation tool) and organize prospect data in spreadsheets.
Maintain and clean prospect lists. Tag, sort, and update contact records as the outbound pipeline grows.
Send LinkedIn event invitations to targeted lists for webinars (up to 1,000/week). Copy, paste, send.


Content and Social Media Support

Post pre-approved content to social media channels on schedule. You are not creating the content. You are making sure it goes live when it’s supposed to.
Upload and schedule video content (case studies, testimonials) to appropriate platforms.
Basic Canva edits for social media graphics if needed.


Reporting Support

Pull data from Google Looker Studio dashboards for monthly client reports. You are not analyzing the data. You are making sure the reports are assembled and ready for the founder to review.
Track basic metrics the founder requests (Ulinc connection rates, LinkedIn post engagement, email open rates).


What You Are Not Doing

Talking to clients. The founder owns all client relationships until a Client Success Manager is hired.
Setting strategy or making campaign decisions. You coordinate. You don’t d ---------- .
Financial work. No bookkeeping, invoicing, or payment processing.
Writing original content, ad copy, or marketing strategy. You post and schedule what others create.
You are the operational backbone that frees the founder to do the work only he can do.



Qualifications Required

Two or more years of experience as a virtual assistant, operations coordinator, or executive assistant supporting a founder or small team
Proficiency in Asana or a comparable project management tool (ClickUp, Monday, Trello)
Proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)
Proficiency in Slack or a comparable tea ---------- ssaging tool
Strong written English. Clear, professional, no errors.
Reliable internet connection and a quiet, distraction-free workspace
Available during US Eastern business hours (minimum 6 hours of overlap with 8am-5pm ET)
Proven ability to work independently, manage your own time, and meet deadlines without being chased


Preferred

Experience supporting a digital marketing agency or a B2B service business
Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator and LinkedIn posting/scheduling
Familiarity with Google Looker Studio (pulling reports, not building them)
Experience with Canva for basic graphic edits
Experience with Zapier or Make for workflow automation
Familiarity with CRM tools (GoHighLevel, Close, HubSpot, or similar)
Experience coordinating with offshore or white-label teams across time zones


Tools You Will Use Daily

Asana: project and task management
Gmail / Google Workspace: email, docs, sheets, calendar
Slack: internal team communication
LinkedIn: posting, scheduling, connection list management
Ulinc: exporting connection data, managing prospect lists


Weekly

Google Looker Studio: pulling client reporting data
Canva: basic social media graphic edits
Zoom / Loom: scheduling and coordinating video calls and async video


Compensation and Details

Full-time position
Remote (global, any location with reliable internet)
Must have significant overlap with US Eastern business hours (minimum 6 hours, 8am-5pm ET)
Compensation commensurate with experience and location
Reports directly to the founder
Start date: as soon as the right person is found


How to Apply



Apply at the link below. You will complete a short application form and then record video responses to 7 questions. The whole process takes about 15 minutes.



 ---------- 



Three things we look for in every application:

Did you read the full job description? (We can tell.)
Can you communicate clearly and professionally in writing?
Do you show evidence of working independently without needing to be managed?


Generic applications will not receive a response.

Apply here:

 ----------

title: Inbound Sales for print on demand/print shop role
description:
***Experience working for a print shop/embroidery/apparel client is required. Please don't apply if you've never worked in that industry.

***Some basic experience with Adobe Illustrator (working with mockups) is a plus, but not required.



About the Role:
We are hiring a dedicated Inbound Sales VA to take full ownership of converting high-intent inbound leads coming directly from our social media channels.

You will not be cold calling or handling random tech support. Instead, you will respond to warm inquiries from U.S. business owners looking for custom apparel and promotional gear. Your mission is to respond instantly, qualify their design and volume needs via chat and phone, handle objections, and close high-value bulk orders. You will manage the entire sales lifecycle within GoHighLevel (GHL).

What You’ll Do:
Inbound Social Media Closings: Monitor and rapidly respond to warm inbound leads from Instagram, Facebook, email, and web forms during U.S. business hours.

CRM Data Hygiene: Actively manage and update the sales pipeline within GoHighLevel (GHL)—logging interactions, setting tasks, and ensuring no lead falls through the cracks.

Consultative Selling: Help U.S. clients choose the right products (caps, tees, laser-etched gear), present bulk pricing/minimums, and close orders with a target 30–40% win rate.

Order Upselling: Maximize revenue by upselling complementary products (e.g., pitching custom shirts or laser-etched mugs to a client ordering hats).

Team Collaboration: Coordinate directly with our Texas-based leadership and production teams to ensure order accuracy before fulfillment.

What We’re Looking For:
Industry Experience: 2+ years of solid sales experience specifically within the custom apparel, embroidery, screen-printing, or promotional products industries (required).

GHL Mastery: Direct, hands-on proficiency using GoHighLevel (GHL) to manage sales pipelines and automations.

Flawless English Communication: Exceptional verbal and written English with a neutral or Western accent. You must be highly confident closing deals via chat/text and over the phone with American business owners.

The "Speed to Lead" Mindset: A proactive closer who understands that a lead from social media needs to be engaged within 5 minutes to convert.

Rock-Solid Technical Setup: A reliable workstation, noise-canceling headset, high-speed fiber internet (minimum 30 Mbps), and a dependable power/internet backup source for typhoon or power outage contingencies.




Details:
-Work from home
-$6/hour
-Mon-Fri 40 hours
-8am-5pm Central (9pm-6am Philippines time) (paid for 8 hours of work)
-Lots of opportunities for growth. We're looking for individuals who want to be a part of a team for many years to come.





HOW TO APPLY:
1. Please send an email with your resume attached and a brief overview of your relevant experience in the body of the email to ---------- with the subject line “Josh's VA”.

2. If you have Adobe Illustrator experience, please write “Adobe Illustrator exp” at top of email (if you don’t, don’t write it).

-If you have print shop/embroidery/POD experience, please write "print exp" at top of email (if you don't, don't write it).

3. At the bottom of the email, write the USD hourly rate (or salary, but specify hourly, monthly, etc and currency) of your last two roles listed on resume. For example:
-Most recent role: $7/hour (USD-full time)
-2nd most recent role: $4/hour (USD-part time)

**Please note, you earning less or more at previous roles DOES NOT negatively or positively affect your chances of getting this role whatsoever. This is simply information needed for us internally as we try to understand your career path. PLEASE BE HONEST, as this may be officially verified later on in the hiring process.


*Please be very specific in the email, explaining your relevant experience. If you don't follow the above 3-step instructions in how to apply, your application will not be reviewed.



DO NOT APPLY IF:
-If you’re not comfortable with a screen tracker that takes screenshots of your work shift (Time Doctor or similar)
-If you have a conflicting job that interferes with this schedule

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
If your instinct when something breaks is to fix the pipeline before fixing the symptom — this role was built for you.

To apply please fill out the application form here:  ---------- /bepartofourteam.

About the company

We're a family-owned business with all-local Philippines leadership and team. We've been 100% work-from-home since day one — not a pandemic pivot. We've grown by valuing our people, building long-term relationships, and doing great work for great clients.

The Role

You're not here to deploy code and move on. You're here to make sure the systems that deliver that code are reliable, secure, automated, and continuously improving.
We're looking for a DevOps Engineer who thinks in pipelines, not tasks. You'll own the infrastructure that keeps our development and client environments running — CI/CD pipelines, Linux systems, cloud platforms, monitoring, and security. When something breaks, you find the root cause. When something works, you ask how to make it more reliable, more automated, and less dependent on human intervention next time.

The Mindset We're Looking For

The best DevOps engineers we've worked with share a few traits:
They treat manual processes as technical debt. If something requires a human to do it repeatedly, their instinct is to eliminate that dependency — not just document it
They take ownership. From the  ---------- nt an infrastructure problem is theirs, it's theirs until it's resolved, documented, and hardened against happening again
They think in systems. They don't just fix what's broken — they ask what else could break, and why, and build accordingly
They communicate clearly. Status, blockers, and deployment risks are shared openly — before being asked for
They keep learning. The DevOps landscape moves fast. Twelve months from now they know more than they do today — because they made sure of it

Who Thrives Here

People who are technically sharp, operationally minded, and genuinely excited by the challenge of building infrastructure that just works. You don't wait to be handed a list of things to automate — you find them. You're comfortable with ambiguity, resourceful under pressure, and you care more about system reliability than staying in a defined lane.
We're a growing MSP, which means not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. For the right engineer, that's not a warning — it's the opportunity to build things the right way from the ground up.

What You'll Do

You'll walk into an environment where development pipelines, Linux infrastructure, cloud systems, and client integrations all need an engineer who can own them end-to-end. Your job isn't just to keep things running — it's to make them run better, more reliably, and with less human intervention every month.
That looks like:

Designing, building, and maintaining CI/CD pipelines that make deployments fast, reliable, and repeatable — so shipping code is a non-event, not a risk
Managing and administering Linux servers (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) across development and client environments — knowing these systems well enough to troubleshoot them at any layer
Administering web servers (Nginx, Apache) and databases — keeping them performant, secure, and maintainable
Implementing client-requested integrations — understanding what's being asked, translating it into a reliable technical solution, and delivering it cleanly
Conducting root cause analysis on production errors — not just fixing what's visible, but understanding what allowed it to happen and building the safeguard that prevents it
Building scripts and automation (Bash required; Python and PHP a plus) that eliminate repetitive manual work from the team's daily operations
Monitoring infrastructure, resource utilization, and security events — building alerting systems that catch problems before they become i ---------- 
Designing and enforcing DevSecOps practices — making security a built-in part of the pipeline, not an afterthought
Working with cloud platforms and virtualization — managing hypervisors and cloud service providers with the confidence of someone who's done it in production

What We're Looking For

Proven experience in Linux server administration (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) — you know these systems well enough to manage, troubleshoot, and optimize them without a guide
Hands-on CI/CD experience — you've built and maintained real pipelines, not just used them; you know what makes a deployment safe and what makes it a liability
Strong version control administration — Git, remote repositories, branching strategies, code review workflows; you manage these with intention, not just habit
Web server administration experience — Nginx and Apache, configuration, performance tuning, and troubleshooting
Database administration experience — you can manage database servers, optimize queries, and keep data environments healthy
Bash scripting proficiency (required) — you write automation that works, that others can read, and that makes the team faster; Python and PHP experience is a plus
Solid cloud computing and virtualization experience — working with hypervisors and cloud service providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, or equivalent) in real production environments
Infrastructure and event monitoring experience — you build alerting systems that surface the right information at the right time, not noise
Understanding of DevSecOps — you treat security as part of the build, not something bolted on at the end
Strong diagnostic skills — you find the root cause of a production issue, not just the fastest path to closing the ticket
Self-directed once you understand the environment — but you ask smart questions early to get there
Honest communicator — you surface risks and blockers clearly and early, even when the news isn't good
Long-term mindset — you want to build something that lasts, not move on when things get complex


How Onboarding Works (And How That Changes)

We're honest about how we work with new tea ---------- mbers.
In your first weeks and months, you'll have real hands-on support from the team. We'll work closely with you to understand our infrastructure, our standards, our tools, and how we operate. We expect questions — and we'll give you the time to learn.
As you show what you can do — and you will — that involvement steps back quickly. Trust grows fast here. People who demonstrate ownership, judgment, and quality work earn autonomy quickly. People who consistently deliver end up with significant latitude to shape the infrastructure, the pipelines, and how we build.
We don't want to over-direct people who don't need it. But we also don't believe in throwing someone in without context and calling it autonomy. We earn each other's trust through real work — then we get out of the way.


What Success Looks Like

At three months: You have a clear picture of the infrastructure landscape — what's healthy, what's fragile, and what needs to be rebuilt. At least one significant manual process has been automated. Your documentation is already what the team references.
At six months: Deployments are more reliable and less stressful than when you joined. Monitoring is catching problems before they become i ---------- . The team is spending less time on reactive firefighting and more time building.
At twelve months: The infrastructure is measurably more stable, secure, and automated than before you joined. You've built systems the team depends on — and you're already identifying the next layer of improvement. The company is investing in your next step.


How We Work

We trust our people to do great work and we give them the space to do it. There's real work to be done — clients and internal teams depend on the infrastructure you manage — but we understand that doing meaningful work means making decisions, and sometimes those decisions won't be right on the first try. That's how we find the right path. We don't punish thoughtful mistakes. We learn from them and move forward.
We're a growing MSP, so not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. That's part of why we need someone like you — to help us build the infrastructure, automations, and systems that make us better as we grow.


This Role Is Not for You If:

You need a defined list of tasks to know what to work on
You deploy the fix without asking what caused the failure
You treat documentation and runbooks as someone else's job
You're comfortable with fragile systems as long as they're currently running
Your outside commitments regularly affect your availability and focus
You're looking for a low-pressure remote role with predictable, well-defined work
There's no judgment in that list — it's just not the environment. We'd rather be honest now than waste each other's time.


What We Offer


Competitive salary based on experience
100% remote — no corporate commute, ever
Weekly pay
Tech and internet allowance
Paid vacation leave
HMO
Access to the latest AI tools (Claude, Code, and more) to work smarter and automate more
Career growth

title: Implementation Manager
description:
CALLING ALL IMPLEMENTATION TITANS! ARE YOU READY TO JOIN THE TOP 1%? 

ArboStar is looking for the best - the unstoppable, detail-obsessed project dynamos who define what a perfect client launch looks like.

ArboStar is an innovative SaaS platform that helps tree care, landscaping, and field service companies grow their businesses through smart automation and powerful tools. We make daily operations easier while helping our clients make the planet greener.

To maintain our high standard of client satisfaction across the globe, we are expanding our elite Implementation Team. We're seeking the best-of-the-best Implementation Manager who can guarantee a seamless, world-class onboarding journey for every new client.

What You’ll Do
As an Implementation Manager, you’ll guide new clients - primarily tree care and landscaping businesses -through a seamless onboarding experience. Your responsibilities will include:
- Leading onboarding and setup for new clients, ensuring smooth adoption of ArboStar tools
- Maintaining proactive communication and follow-ups throughout the onboarding journey
- Performing custom system configurations, integrations, and workflow setups
- Assisting with data migration and creating necessary client forms
- Collaborating with developers and submitting or reviewing Jira requests
- Conducting live product training sessions and explaining platform functionalities
- Monitoring client payments and performing timely follow-ups if needed
- Identifying and executing upsell opportunities for additional products and features
- Updating internal and external knowledge base articles
- Partnering with Sales, Customer Success, and Product teams to deliver a unified client experience

What We’re Looking For
- 2+ years of experience in Implementation, Customer Support, Onboarding, Training, or Member Services (preferably in SaaS or tech)
- Exceptional English (C2 level) with little to no accent - confident in speaking, writing, and presenting
- Excellent presentation and communication skills
- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple clients independently
- Experience collaborating with developers or QA teams using Jira or similar tools
- Proficiency with CRM and project management systems (HubSpot, Pipedrive, Asana, Google Suite, etc.)
- Comfortable working remotely in the Eastern Time Zone (9 AM – 5 PM ET)

Nice to Have
- Experience working with tree care, landscaping, or field service companies
- Familiarity with data migration or workflow automation
- Understanding of onboarding metrics such as completion, engagement, and retention

Compensation & Benefits
- 10 paid vacation leaves per year
- A paid day off on your birthday or special day

title: On-page SEO team leader
description:
About us
Black Swan Media Co is a US-based AI automation and SEO agency. We work with local service businesses — HVAC, plumbing, auto body — and help them rank and generate leads using proven on-page systems. We run lean, move fast, and hold high standards. This is not a generic content mill role.

What this role actually is
We're looking for a sharp, experienced SEO professional to lead our on-page team. You won't just execute - you'll own quality, train the team, and be the final set of eyes before anything goes live. You'll work directly with the agency owner and have real influence over how we do things.

This is a leadership role first, SEO role second. If you've never managed other VAs or reviewed someone else's work before publishing, this is not the right fit yet.

Your responsibilities
Lead and supervise a small team of on-page SEO specialists
Review and approve all on-page deliverables before they go to clients
Develop and maintain SOPs for meta titles, H-tag structures, schema markup, and internal linking
Perform technical on-page audits and deliver clear, actionable findings
Write optimized meta titles and descriptions that actually get clicks
Implement and validate schema markup (LocalBusiness, FAQ, Service types)
Conduct keyword research and map terms to page intent correctly
Communicate clearly with the agency owner via async updates (Loom + Slack)
Flag problems before they become client issues
Work hours are M-F 5 am EST - 12 pm EST

What you need to have
3+ years of hands-on on-page SEO experience (not just theory)
Proven experience leading or reviewing other SEO tea ---------- mbers' work
Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console, Claude/ChatGPT for AI content, understand entities + entity density, and silo structures.
Solid understanding of schema markup and structured data - you can write it, not just install a plugin
Able to explain SEO decisions clearly in plain English
Reliable internet, available during agreed overlap hours with US Eastern Time
Comfortable using AI tools to assist work - but you know when AI is wrong
Experience with wordpress sites
Familiarity with local SEO ranking factors and GBP optimization

?? Read this before applying

We receive a high volume of applications. Most are ignored because they are clearly copy-pasted or AI-generated. We will know. To be considered, you must follow the application instructions below exactly. Applicants who do not follow the instructions will not receive a response - no exceptions.

How to apply
Send a message through OnlineJobs.ph. Your message must include all of the following:

Subject line: Start it with the word FALCON (this tells us you read the full post)
2–3 sentences on why you are qualified to lead a team, not just execute tasks
One specific example of an on-page change you made that improved rankings — include the type of page, what you changed, and approximately what happened to rankings
A short Loom video (2–3 minutes max) walking us through any live page you've optimized — explain your decisions out loud
No cover letter templates. No generic intros. If your first sentence is "I am writing to express my interest," stop and start over.

title: Senior Graphic Designer cum Video Editor
description:
About us: 

PTOE Global Solutions helps businesses grow, operate, and scale through managed business solutions. We support business owners by providing the systems, people, content, technology, and execution support they need to run their business more effectively and sustainably. 

PGS also supports internal ventures and client brands across coaching, SaaS, faith-based communities, family services, and business transformation. We are looking for a strong creative leader who can turn strategy, messaging, and ideas into high-quality visuals and video content. 

Our mission is to help our clients build lasting legacies while orchestrating a "harmony over hustle" approach to business. We operate with a collaborative, democratic culture where modern techniques, mindfulness, and strategic innovation meet. If you are passionate about talent development, digital efficiency, and driving high-impact, human-centric transformations, you will find a purpose-driven home with us. 


Role Summary: 

The Senior Graphic Designer / Video Editor Creator will be responsible for creating professional visual assets, branded graphics, marketing materials, social media content, and video content for PGS and its clients.  

This role is ideal for someone who is both creative and strategic, able to design visually appealing content while understanding business objectives, brand positioning, audience engagement, and conversion goals. 

The person must be able to create polished designs, edit engaging videos, support marketing campaigns, and maintain brand consistency across multiple businesses and platforms. 


Graphic Design: 

Create high-quality visual assets for: 
Social media posts and campaigns 
Website graphics and banners 
Landing pages and sales pages 

Email marketing graphics 
Presentation decks 
PDF guides, lead magnets, and downloadable resources 
App store graphics and promotional images 
Brand assets, icons, thumbnails, and templates 
Client-facing reports and business documents 
Event, webinar, podcast, and community graphics 
Develop and maintain brand consistency across PGS and client brands.  
Create reusable design templates for faster content production. 
Work with marketing, content, product, and delivery teams to translate ideas into clear, professional visuals. 



Video Editing & Content Creation: 

Edit short-form and long-form video content for: 
YouTube 
Instagram Reels 
TikTok 

Facebook 
LinkedIn 
Website videos 
App demos 
Training videos 
Webinars 
Podcasts 
Client testimonials 
Promotional videos 
Community content 
Add captions, graphics, lower thirds, transitions, music, animations, and branding elements. 
Create engaging video clips from longer content. 
Prepare video assets optimized for different platforms and formats. 
Support the creation of launch videos, explainer videos, product demos, and promotional campaigns. 


Brand & Creative Direction: 
Help define and improve visual standards for each brand managed by PGS. 
Ensure all creative work aligns with the brand’s purpose, audience, tone, and business goals. 
Provide creative input on campaign ideas, content themes, and brand positioning. 
Guide junior designers, content creators, or contractors when needed. 
Review creative assets for quality, consistency, and professionalism. 


Marketing & Campaign Support: 
Work closely with the marketing strategist and content team to support campaign execution. 
Create visuals that support lead generation, audience engagement, community growth, and product awareness. 
Design creative assets for launches, promotions, funnels, newsletters, and social media campaigns. 
Help transform written content into visual stories, carousels, reels, videos, and branded marketing assets. 


Collaboration & Workflow:  

Manage multiple design and video requests across different brands and client projects. 
Organize design files, source files, templates, and creative assets properly. 
Meet deadlines while maintaining high-quality output. 
Take feedback professionally and revise designs/videos as needed. 
Use project management tools to track tasks and deliverables. 


Required Skills & Experience 

10+ years of professional graphic design experience 
3+ years of professional video editing experience 
Strong portfolio showing design and video work 
Advanced skills in tools such as:  
Adobe Photoshop 
Adobe Illustrator 
Adobe Premiere Pro 
After Effects 
Canva 
Figma 
CapCut or similar video editing tools 
Strong understanding of branding, layout, typography, colour, and visual hierarchy 
Experience creating content for social media platforms 
Experience creating marketing and sales assets 
Ability to edit videos with captions, transitions, music, motion graphics, and branded elements 
Ability to work with multiple brands and adapt to different styles 
Strong attention to detail 
Strong communication and time management skills 
Ability to take direction and also bring creative ideas forward 
Experience working in a remote team environment 


Preferred Qualifications:  

Experience working with SaaS, coaching, community, faith-based, or service-based brands 
Experience designing app-related graphics or app store promotional assets 
Experience creating YouTube thumbnails, reels, shorts, and podcast clips 
Experience with motion graphics and simple animation 
Experience with AI creative tools for image, video, and content production 
Experience creating presentation decks and business proposals 
Experience building brand kits and design systems 
Understanding of digital marketing, funnels, and conversion-focused design 


Ideal Candidate Profile: 

The ideal candidate is creative, organized, mature, and able to work across multiple brands without losing consistency or quality. They understand that design is not just about making something look good, it must communicate clearly, build trust, support marketing goals, and help the business grow. 

They should be comfortable working with business owners, marketing teams, content creators, product teams, and delivery teams. They should be able to take rough ideas and turn them into polished creative assets. 



Core Soft Skills: Strong communication, collaborative leadership style, and an ability to thrive in agile environments. 

Should be able work with the complete Adobe suite including Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign and Priemer Pro 


What we offer: 

Competitive renumeration.
Flexible work hours & remote options.  
Opportunity to work on multiple exciting projects in a dynamic environment.  
Collaborative and innovative team culture.

title: Product & Pricing Analyst (eCommerce & FMCG)
description:

We’re looking for a detail-oriented and analytical person to join our team, focused on product pricing research and competitor analysis. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with data, spotting trends, and turning numbers into clear insights and recommendations.

Key Responsibilities

What We’re Looking For

- High attention to detail
- Ability to work independently and think critically
- Comfortable doing repetitive research tasks accurately
- Good written English
- Experience in eCommerce or FMCG pricing
- Experience analysing competitor pricing
- Ability to suggest automation or process improvements

Role Details:
- Full-time (5–8 hours per day)
- UK working hours (flexible within reason)

o Alpro Oat Milk 1 Litre
o Aptamil 1 First Milk Formula 800g

title: Operations Administrator
description:
About Us
We are a leading health and safety training centre based in Northern Ireland. We are committed to delivering high-quality training and compliance solutions. To support our growing operations, we are seeking a dedicated and versatile Opera-tions Administrator to join our team remotely.

The Role
We are looking for a highly organised, proactive, and honest individual to oversee the day-to-day operations of our training centre. This is a multifaceted role requir-ing a unique blend of administrative rigour, creative design capability, financial accuracy, and process improvement skills. The successful candidate must be computer proficient and capable of working independently in a home-based en-vironment, with no other outside employment.

Key Responsibilities
Operations & Administration
• Oversee the day-to-day operational workflow of the training centre, ensur-ing all administrative processes run smoothly and efficiently.
• Manage course scheduling, delegate bookings, and trainer coordination.
• Act as the first point of contact for client enquiries, providing excellent cus-tomer service via phone, email, and digital platforms.
• Maintain accurate and organised digital filing systems, ensuring all records are compliant with data protection regulations (UK GDPR).
• Prepare and issue course certificates, delegate materials, and pre-course documentation.
Graphics & Design
• Create and edit professional marketing materials, including course bro-chures, social media graphics, presentation templates, and client-facing documents.
• Design internal process documents, flowcharts, and visual aids to support training delivery and operational procedures.
• Ensure all design work aligns with the company’s brand identity and main-tains a high standard of professionalism.

Bookkeeping & Finance Support
• Perform basic bookkeeping tasks, including processing invoices, tracking payments, and reconciling accounts using accounting software.
• Assist with credit control, following up on outstanding payments in a pro-fessional and timely manner.
• Prepare financial summaries and reports for management as required.

Process Improvement & Automation
• Analyse existing administrative and operational processes to identify inef-ficiencies.
• Build, document, and implement new workflows and automated systems to streamline repetitive tasks.
• Leverage technology to reduce manual data entry, improve accuracy, and enhance overall productivity.

General & Ad-Hoc Duties
• Provide general clerical support, including data entry, report generation, and minute-taking for meetings.
• Adapt to additional tasks as required, supporting the wider team to meet business objectives.
• Proactively identify and resolve operational issues before they escalate.

Person Specification
Essential Criteria
• Computer Proficiency: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office 365 (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams, PowerPoint) and Google Workspace. Ability to quickly learn and master new software and CRMs.
• Graphic Design: Proven experience with design tools such as Canva, Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop/InDesign), or similar to produce profes-sional marketing and internal materials.
• Bookkeeping: Experience with basic bookkeeping principles and famili-arity with accounting software such as QuickBooks, or Sage).
• Process Improvement: Demonstrable experience in building workflows and automating processes 
• Organisational Skills: Exceptional ability to manage multiple priorities, meet deadlines, and maintain attention to detail in a fast-paced environ-ment.
• Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a professional and courteous manner.
• Integrity: Must be honest, diligent, and trustworthy, with the ability to handle sensitive financial and client data responsibly.
• Home Working Capability: A quiet, dedicated home workspace with reliable high-speed internet access.

Desirable Criteria
• Previous experience working in a health and safety training environment or similar professional services sector.
• Qualification in bookkeeping or business administration.
• Knowledge of various softwares and tools 

Terms of Employment
• Working Arrangements: This is a fully remote, work-from-home posi-tion.
• Exclusivity: This is a full-time role. Due to the nature and demands of the position, no other concurrent employment or freelance work is permitted. Full commitment and focus on operations are required.
• Hours: Standard office hours, Monday to Friday. Flexibility may be required occasionally to meet business needs.
• Equipment: The successful candidate must have their own computer and reliable high-speed internet connection. Specific software licenses may be provided by the company.

How to Apply
Please submit your CV and a cover letter detailing your experience in operations, design, bookkeeping, and process workflows. Include examples of design work (portfolio or links) if available.

title: Data Analyst
description:

transforming, and loading (ETL) financial data from various sources into our systems, conducting thorough data validation and cleansing procedures, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to meet strict deadlines.

This position is 100% remote.


ABOUT THE COMPANY
Our mission is to help business owners achieve their personal, professional, and goals through the growth of their businesses. We work in dozens of verticals and provide strategic business guidance through courses, live events, partnerships, and investments. Our core values are the backbone of our business and guide our hiring process: we are inspirational, accountable, transparent, disciplined, aligned, and results-oriented. COMPETENCIES

title: Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator
description:
About Alpha Key Digital
Alpha Key Digital is a digital marketing agency specializing in patient acquisition for cosmetic surgeons, dermatologists, medical spas, and cosmetic dentists. We’ve been in business for 10 years, serve 12 retainer clients, and are growing. We’re a small team that operates with precision. No bloat. No unnecessary meetings. Clear communication, clean systems, and work that gets done when it’s supposed to get done.
We are looking for a Virtual Assistant to take operational, administrative, and coordination tasks off the founder’s plate so he can focus on client strategy, business development, and growth. This is a high-trust role. You will be the person who keeps the engine running behind the scenes.
The Role: Virtual Assistant / Operations Coordinator. Full-time. Remote. Must have significant overlap with US Eastern business hours.
You are the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks. The founder runs the client relationships and the strategy. You run the operational backbone that supports it: email triage, project management, scheduling, contractor coordination, content posting, and prospect list management. You don’t wait to be told what to do. You see the gap and you close it.
This is not a customer-facing role. You will not be talking to clients directly. You will be the person behind the curtain making sure every deliverable, every email, every task, and every calendar event is where it’s supposed to be so the founder can walk into any conversation fully prepared.
You’d Be a Great Fit If You Are
The person who builds the checklist before anyone asks for one. You organize by instinct, not because someone gave you a system to follow. You create the system.
Reliable to the point where people stop checking your work because they already know it’s done. You don’t need reminders. You are the reminder.
Someone who reads the full email before responding. You catch the detail in the third paragraph that everyone else misses. You ask the clarifying question before it becomes a problem.
Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision. You don’t need daily check-ins to stay productive. You need clear expectations and then space to execute.
Proactive about surfacing problems early. If something is off, you flag it immediately instead of hoping it resolves itself. You know the difference between handling it yourself and escalating it.
Disciplined with time. You batch similar work together. You don’t context-switch 15 times a day. When you’re in a task, you finish it before moving on.
Protective of other people’s calendars. You treat the founder’s schedule like a strategic asset, not a suggestion. If something doesn’t belong on the calendar, you say so.
Someone who takes pride in clean systems. Your Asana is current. Your inbox is processed. Your files are named correctly. You don’t leave loose ends.
What You Actually Do

Email and Communication Management
Triage the founder’s inbox daily. Flag urgent items, draft responses for routine emails, archive what doesn’t need attention.
Manage Slack communication with the white-label development team (E2M) and contractors. Relay updates, follow up on late deliverables, keep threads organized.
Ensure no email or message sits unanswered for more than 24 hours during business days.
Project Management and Task Coordination
Own Asana: create tasks from briefs, assign to the right team member, track deadlines, follow up on overdue items, and keep the board clean.
Coordinate deliverables between the founder, PPC contractor, SEO team, and E2M web development team. You are the traffic controller.
Run a weekly check on all active client projects to confirm nothing is behind schedule. Surface delays to the founder before the client notices.
Calendar and Scheduling
Manage the founder’s calendar. Schedule client meetings, prospect calls, internal huddles, and personal appointments.
Protect time blocks. The founder has dedicated strategic work time that should not be interrupted by meetings. You enforce that.
Confirm and send reminders for all meetings. No missed appointments.
LinkedIn and Prospect List Management
Schedule LinkedIn posts (batch-created by the founder) using a scheduling tool.
Export connection lists from Ulinc (LinkedIn automation tool) and organize prospect data in spreadsheets.
Maintain and clean prospect lists. Tag, sort, and update contact records as the outbound pipeline grows.
Send LinkedIn event invitations to targeted lists for webinars (up to 1,000/week). Copy, paste, send.
Content and Social Media Support
Post pre-approved content to social media channels on schedule. You are not creating the content. You are making sure it goes live when it’s supposed to.
Upload and schedule video content (case studies, testimonials) to appropriate platforms.
Basic Canva edits for social media graphics if needed.
Reporting Support
Pull data from Google Looker Studio dashboards for monthly client reports. You are not analyzing the data. You are making sure the reports are assembled and ready for the founder to review.
Track basic metrics the founder requests (Ulinc connection rates, LinkedIn post engagement, email open rates).
What You Are Not Doing
Talking to clients. The founder owns all client relationships until a Client Success Manager is hired.
Setting strategy or making campaign decisions. You coordinate. You don’t decide.
Financial work. No bookkeeping, invoicing, or payment processing.
Writing original content, ad copy, or marketing strategy. You post and schedule what others create.
You are the operational backbone that frees the founder to do the work only he can do.
Qualifications
Required
Two or more years of experience as a virtual assistant, operations coordinator, or executive assistant supporting a founder or small team
Proficiency in Asana or a comparable project management tool (ClickUp, Monday, Trello)
Proficiency in Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Drive, Calendar)
Proficiency in Slack or a comparable team messaging tool
Strong written English. Clear, professional, no errors.
Reliable internet connection and a quiet, distraction-free workspace
Available during US Eastern business hours (minimum 6 hours of overlap with 8am-5pm ET)
Proven ability to work independently, manage your own time, and meet deadlines without being chased
Preferred
Experience supporting a digital marketing agency or a B2B service business
Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator and LinkedIn posting/scheduling
Familiarity with Google Looker Studio (pulling reports, not building them)
Experience with Canva for basic graphic edits
Experience with Zapier or Make for workflow automation
Familiarity with CRM tools (GoHighLevel, Close, HubSpot, or similar)
Experience coordinating with offshore or white-label teams across time zones
Tools You Will Use Daily
Asana: project and task management
Gmail / Google Workspace: email, docs, sheets, calendar
Slack: internal team communication
LinkedIn: posting, scheduling, connection list management
Ulinc: exporting connection data, managing prospect lists
Weekly
Google Looker Studio: pulling client reporting data
Canva: basic social media graphic edits
Zoom / Loom: scheduling and coordinating video calls and async video
Compensation and Details
Full-time position
Remote (global, any location with reliable internet)
Must have significant overlap with US Eastern business hours (minimum 6 hours, 8am-5pm ET)
Compensation commensurate with experience and location
Reports directly to the founder
Start date: as soon as the right person is found
How to Apply
Apply at the link below. You will complete a short application form and then record video responses to 7 questions. The whole process takes about 15 minutes.
 ---------- 
Three things we look for in every application:
Did you read the full job description? (We can tell.)
Can you communicate clearly and professionally in writing?
Do you show evidence of working independently without needing to be managed?
Generic applications will not receive a response.

title: GoHighLevel CRM & Automation Specialist (Remote) About Us
description:
About Us
Well Skin Clinic is a leading medical aesthetics clinic in the UK, specialising in advanced skin treatments, laser hair removal, body contouring, injectables, and skin rejuvenation.
We are looking for a highly organised and experienced GoHighLevel specialist to build and manage our patient communication and follow-up systems.
This is a remote role with flexible hours and the opportunity for ongoing work.

The Role
You will be responsible for building and maintaining our GoHighLevel CRM and automation platform to improve patient engagement, consultation conversion rates, and client retention.
Initially, the focus will be on building consultation follow-up systems for patients who have attended appointments but have not yet booked treatment.
Responsibilities
Build and manage GoHighLevel workflows and automations
Create and optimise CRM pipelines
Build landing pages and forms within GoHighLevel
Configure email, SMS and WhatsApp automation campaigns
Set up lead scoring, tagging and segmentation
Build reporting dashboards and conversion tracking
Create automated follow-up sequences for different treatments
Maintain and improve existing workflows
Assist with CRM database organisation and management
Integrate GoHighLevel with third-party systems where required
Required Skills
Proven GoHighLevel experience
Strong understanding of workflows, triggers, tags and automation logic
Experience building sales or consultation pipelines
Landing page and funnel creation experience
Experience with WhatsApp Business integration
Excellent English communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Ability to work independently and make recommendations

Preferred Experience
Experience working with aesthetic clinics, healthcare businesses, dental practices or medical clinics
Experience with consultation-to-sale conversion systems
Knowledge of email marketing best practices
Experience with Zapier, Make, APIs or other integrations

First Project
Your first project will be to build a complete consultation follow-up system that includes:
Consultation attendance tracking
Treatment-specific nurture campaigns
Before-and-after result galleries
Automated email and WhatsApp communication
Follow-up task creation
Conversion reporting dashboard

To Apply
Please provide:
A brief introduction about yourself
Examples of GoHighLevel systems you have built
Screenshots or videos of workflows you have created
Any experience working with clinics, healthcare or aesthetics businesses
Your hourly rate
Your weekly availability
Applications without examples of previous GoHighLevel work will not be considered.

title: Junior Software Engineer / Technical Assistant (Python / Django)
description:
We’re looking for a Junior Software Engineer / Technical Assistant to work closely with a senior engineer on building and maintaining internal tools, web applications, and automation systems.

This is not a “follow a perfectly defined ticket” role. You’ll often be given partially defined problems and expected to help shape the solution, ask the right questions, and move things forward independently.

What you’ll be doing

You’ll work on a mix of:

* Building and maintaining Django-based web applications
* Implementing features end-to-end (backend + basic frontend where needed)
* Debugging issues and improving existing systems
* Writing scripts and small automation tools
* Helping turn vague requirements into structured technical tasks
* Researching and evaluating tools or implementation approaches
* Supporting general technical and operational work as needed

You will work directly with a senior engineer and be expected to communicate clearly, ask questions early, and keep progress visible.

?

What we’re looking for

We care less about years of experience and more about how you think and work.

Core traits:

* Responsive and reliable in communication
* Strong attention to detail (reads instructions carefully, follows through precisely)
* Genuinely eager to learn and improve quickly
* Comfortable working with ambiguity and incomplete requirements
* Honest about what you know and don’t know
* Able to think through problems logically, not just execute instructions
* Consistent and dependable over time (we are looking for a 2–3 year working relationship)

Technical skills (baseline):

* Python (required)
* Django (or willingness to become strong in it quickly)
* HTML / CSS / basic frontend understanding
* Git and basic version control workflows
* Basic SQL/database understanding

Nice to have:

* Experience with REST APIs
* Familiarity with deployment basics
* Exposure to AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT, etc.)

?

What success looks like

In this role, success means:

* You can take a loosely defined task and turn it into a working implementation
* You communicate blockers early instead of getting stuck silently
* You steadily improve autonomy over time
* You produce clean, understandable code with clear reasoning
* You become someone I can reliably delegate work to without constant oversight

?

Working style

* Remote
* Flexible EEST-aligned working hours
* Direct collaboration with a senior engineer
* Fast feedback loops and frequent iteration

This is a high-trust, high-learning role. You will be expected to think, not just code.

?

Interview process

1. Intro call
    * Communication, motivation, basic technical discussion
2. Take-home assignment (Python/Django)
    * Small Django task tracker project
    * 4-hour timebox
    * Must include Git commit history and AI usage documentation
3. Review call
    * Walkthrough of implementation
    * Live changes and modifications to requirements
    * Debugging and reasoning discussion
4. Optional trial period
    * Real tasks in a live environment

?

Important note on working process

We value transparency in how you work.

Candidates are encouraged to use AI tools (ChatGPT, Copilot, Cursor, etc.), but we expect clear documentation of AI usage and prompts. The goal is to understand how you think and how you leverage modern tools—not to avoid them.

We also place strong emphasis on:

* Commit discipline and iterative development
* Clear communication during work
* Ability to explain decisions, not just implement them

title: Automation Specialist – AI Workflows & Systems
description:
**About the role**
We are building smart, automated systems for our business and need someone who can help design and maintain them. This is a long-term remote position. We want someone who grows with us.

No degree required. Skills and mindset matter more.

**What you will be doing**

- Ability to think in processes — breaking a task into logical steps
- Comfortable with basic concepts like webhooks, APIs, and JSON (you don't need to code, but you need to understand how data flows)

**What we offer**

Start your application with the word WORKFLOW so we know you have read this entire post. Applications that do not include this word or do not answer the questions above will not be reviewed.

title: AI Workflow Specialist (Claude / ChatGPT) — Part-Time ? Full-Time, Long-Term, UK Company
description:
Help an established UK company put AI to work — and grow with us as we do
We're Unlocking Language, a London-based speech and language therapy provider founded in 2013. We have around 50 therapists supporting 100+ schools and clinics, and we're working toward a big mission: delivering 1 million hours of therapy by 2030.
To get there, we're building AI into how we run the business — and we're looking for someone to own that. This starts at 20–25 hours a week and is intended to grow into a full-time, long-term role as the work expands. If you want stability, real ownership, and the chance to build something from the ground up with a mission-driven team, this is for you.
What you'll do

Set up and configure our company accounts on Claude and ChatGPT, including feeding in our knowledge and documentation
Set up Claude Cowork and Claude Code for the team
Build workflows that automate repetitive tasks using these tools
Design and deploy AI agents to handle real operational work
Help the wider team actually use what you build

Tools you'll work with
Ninox, Xero, GHL, Lark, and Google Workspace.
Must have

Demonstrable, hands-on expertise in at least one major AI tool (Claude or ChatGPT). You'll be asked to show us real examples of what you've built.
Fluent English, written and spoken
Strong organisation and the ability to work independently

Nice to have

Genuine expertise across both Claude and ChatGPT
Experience building automations or AI agents end to end

The details

Hours: 20–25 per week to start, growing to full-time over time
Schedule: Your hours must overlap UK business hours (GMT)
Pay: $7–9 per hour, depending on our assessment of your skills
Type: Part-time now, leading to full-time — this is a long-term hire, not a one-off project

How to apply
Start your application with the phrase "AI builder ready" so we know you've read this far. Then tell us, in a few sentences:

One AI workflow or agent you've actually built — what it did and what tool you used
A link, screenshot, or short description we can look at as proof

We read every application that follows these steps. Applications that don't start with the phrase won't be reviewed. We're excited to meet the person who'll help us build this.

title: Airbnb Property Manager
description:
Virtual Assistant (Airbnb & Serviced Accommodation Operations Manager)

Job Title

Experienced Airbnb & Serviced Accommodation Virtual Assistant

Location

Remote (Philippines)

Employment Type

Full-Time
7 Days

Salary

$800 Monthly 
Uncapped Direct Bookings Commission 

?

Job Advert

Join a Growing UK Serviced Accommodation Business

We are looking for an experienced and highly organised Virtual Assistant based in the Philippines to manage the day-to-day operations of a growing Airbnb and serviced accommodation business.

This is not an entry-level position. We are looking for someone who already has experience working with Airbnb hosts, serviced accommodation operators, holiday lets, short-term rentals, or property management companies.

You will become a key member of the business, helping deliver exceptional guest experiences, maximise occupancy, and ensure properties operate efficiently.

If you have a strong attention to detail, excellent communication skills, and experience using Airbnb and property management software, we would love to hear from you.

?

About Us

We operate high-quality short-term and long-term accommodation across Glasgow, Scotland, serving:

* Contractors
* Corporate travellers
* Families
* Tourists
* Insurance guests
* Relocation clients

Our goal is to provide hotel-quality accommodation with exceptional customer service.

?

Key Responsibilities

Guest Communication

* Oversee AI Programme which primarily responds to guest enquiries quickly and professionally 
* Convert enquiries into bookings
* Convert Airbnb & Booking.com bookings into Directs
* Resolve guest issues and complaints where required

Booking Management

* Manage booking calendars
* Monitor occupancy levels
* Adjust minimum stay requirements
* Update listing information
* Prevent double bookings
* Coordinate last-minute reservations

Revenue & Pricing

* Monitor occupancy and pricing
* Make pricing changes to maximise revenue on Pricelabs
* Review competitor listings
* Assist with dynamic pricing strategies

Operations Management

* Coordinate cleaners and maintenance teams
* Track maintenance issues

Listing Management

* Create and optimise Listings
* Improve listing descriptions
* Edit Photos using Chat GPT 
* Upload and organise photos
* Monitor listing performance
* Increase conversion rates

Administrative Tasks

* Create & Update SOPs and checklists
* Manage contractor databases
* Track invoices and expenses
* Produce weekly performance reports

Business Development

* Research corporate accommodation opportunities
* Research contractor accommodation opportunities
* Assist with outreach campaigns
* Identify opportunities to increase occupancy

?

Required Experience

Essential

* Minimum 3 years Airbnb or short-term rental experience
* Experience managing multiple properties
* Excellent written English
* Experience dealing with guests professionally
* Using Chat GPT
* Strong organisational skills
* Ability to work independently
* Experience coordinating cleaners and maintenance teams

Software Experience

Experience with some or all of the following:

* Airbnb
* Booking.com
* VRBO
* Lodgify
* PriceLabs
* Google Workspace
* Microsoft Office
* Slack
* WhatsApp Business

?

Preferred Experience

* UK serviced accommodation experience
* Contractor accommodation experience
* Revenue management experience
* Dynamic pricing experience
* Direct booking website management
* Experience with automation tools such as Make.com, Zapier or n8n
* Social media marketing experience

?

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

The successful candidate will be measured on:

* Average guest response time
* Occupancy levels
* Guest review scores
* Number of issues resolved
* Revenue growth
* Booking conversion rate
* Cleaner performance management
* Listing optimisation improvements

?

Working Hours

Preferred:

* Flexible Working Hours
* Weekend & Nightly monitoring required
* Flexible schedule with overlap to UK business hours

?

What Success Looks Like

After 90 days you will:

* Oversee all guest communication independently
* Coordinate cleaners and maintenance without supervision
* Maintain occupancy targets
* Improve guest satisfaction scores
* Produce weekly business performance reports
* Identify opportunities to increase revenue and efficiency

?

Application Questions

To Apply

Only applicants with direct Airbnb or serviced accommodation experience will be considered.

title: Operations & Growth VA — Serviced Accommodation Business — 21 Hours/Week
description:
Role: Operations & Growth Virtual Assistant
Business: QP Stays
Location: Remote
Hours: 21 hours per week to start — 3 hours per day
Schedule: Daily check-ins required, including weekends
Rate: To be discussed based on experience
Start date: ASAP

Important — Independent Thinking Required

This role is best suited to someone who already has experience in guest support, serviced accommodation, short-term rentals, hotel operations, property management, or a similar operational role.

I work full-time alongside running the business, so I will not always be available to walk through every small task in real time. There will be clear SOPs, checklists and escalation rules, but I need someone who can think for themselves, prioritise tasks, solve simple problems, and make sensible decisions within agreed boundaries.

This role is not suitable for someone who needs step-by-step instruction for every small task.

I’m looking for someone reliable, proactive, organised, and experienced enough to keep basic daily operations moving even if I am unavailable for a few hours or a day.

You must know when to act, when to log information, and when to escalate.

About QP Stays

QP Stays is a growing serviced accommodation business based in Coventry, UK. We manage properties across Airbnb,  ---------- , VRBO and direct bookings, mainly serving contractors, business travellers, relocation guests, families and longer-stay guests.

We are looking for a reliable, organised and proactive Operations & Growth VA to help with the day-to-day running of the business, guest communication, admin, lead generation, and light social media support.

This is not a basic admin-only role. You must be comfortable dealing with guest messages, check-in issues, cleaning coordination, maintenance updates, booking admin, daily operational checks and outreach.

The right person will be calm, organised, reliable, confident with systems, and able to think clearly when problems happen.

Main Responsibilities

Guest Operations

Monitor guest messages across Airbnb,  ---------- , VRBO and direct bookings
Respond to guest enquiries using approved templates and SOPs
Support pre-arrival checks and online check-in completion
Chase incomplete guest check-in forms when required
Help resolve basic guest issues calmly and professionally
Escalate urgent issues, complaints, damages, refund requests or anything unusual
Monitor guest reviews and flag negative feedback before responding

Cleaning & Property Operations

Check cleaning schedules and completion updates
Monitor Turno cleaning updates and cleaner notes
Flag cleaning issues, missing items, restocking needs or maintenance problems
Help keep property records updated
Log maintenance issues clearly with photos, notes and status updates
Coordinate basic maintenance follow-ups where required

Admin & Systems

Keep Airtable records updated
Update booking, maintenance, expense and lead records
Organise Gmail, Google Drive and property documents
Track daily tasks and provide updates
Help maintain simple operations dashboards
Follow SOPs and checklists accurately

Lead Generation & Outreach

Research local companies in Coventry, Warwickshire and surrounding areas that may need accommodation
Find contacts at construction companies, contractor firms, relocation agencies, insurance companies, healthcare providers, universities and local businesses
Build and update lead lists in Airtable or spreadsheets
Send approved outreach messages
Track replies and follow-ups
Help book calls or viewings for interested leads
Keep lead records organised with contact status and next follow-up dates

Marketing & Social Media Support

This is a bonus part of the role, not the main focus.

The VA may also help with:

Organising property photos and videos in Google Drive
Creating simple Canva graphics
Drafting captions
Scheduling social media posts
Recycling existing content into new posts
Creating review/testimonial posts
Maintaining a simple weekly content calendar
Helping reduce reliance on external social media support over time

Experience with Canva, Meta Business Suite, Instagram, Facebook, TikTok or scheduling tools is a bonus.

Tools We Use

Training will be provided, but you must be comfortable learning new systems.

Uplisting — bookings, messaging, calendar and channel management
SuiteOp — online guest check-in and ID verification
Turno — cleaning scheduling and cleaner updates
Airtable — bookings, maintenance, expenses, leads and operations tracking
Google Workspace — Gmail, Drive, Docs and Sheets
Slack — internal communication
WhatsApp — communication where required
PriceLabs — dynamic pricing, read-only unless instructed

You do not need experience with every tool, but experience with Airbnb,  ---------- , Turno, Airtable, Slack or any property management system is a strong advantage.

What You Will Not Be Responsible For

You will not approve or action the following without permission:

Refunds
Guest compensation
Pricing changes
Booking cancellations
Contractor jobs over £150
Banking or financial transactions
Legal matters
Investor communication
Major guest complaints
Damage claims without approval

If unsure, you must escalate.

What We Are Looking For

Essential

Excellent written English
Reliable daily availability
Strong guest/customer communication skills
Good organisation and attention to detail
Ability to follow SOPs and checklists
Comfortable working independently
Calm and practical when problems happen
Able to prioritise urgent guest issues
Comfortable with weekend check-ins
Confident using online tools and learning new systems
Able to communicate naturally on video calls without reading from AI or scripts

Preferred Experience

Experience in any of the following is preferred:

Airbnb
 ---------- 
VRBO
Short-term rentals
Serviced accommodation
Hotel reception or guest support
Property management
Cleaner coordination
Maintenance coordination
Lead generation

Email outreach
LinkedIn prospecting
Appointment setting
Canva or social media scheduling

Working Hours

This role starts at 21 hours per week.

The starting structure is 3 hours per day, 7 days per week.

You do not need to be online all day, but you must be available for agreed daily check-ins and responsive during agreed working windows.

Weekend availability is required, mainly for light check-ins, guest issues, cleaning updates and urgent escalations.

The role may grow over time as QP Stays expands.

Ideal Candidate

You will be a good fit if you:

Are reliable and show up when agreed
Communicate clearly and proactively
Like working from systems and checklists
Can handle guest issues calmly
Know when to escalate instead of guessing
Can manage small daily tasks without being chased
Are comfortable doing both operations and growth tasks
Want to grow with a small but ambitious property business

Application Instructions

To apply, please send:

A short cover note explaining your relevant experience
Your CV or resume
A list of tools you have used before
Your weekly availability in UK time
Your expected hourly rate
Any experience you have with lead generation, outreach, social media or Canva
Confirmation that you are comfortable with short weekend check-ins

Important Recruitment Note

Shortlisted applicants will complete a short paid trial task and video interview.

During the interview, you will be asked live guest scenario questions. Please do not use AI, scripts or prepared notes during the live interview. I use AI myself and have no issue with it for written support, but this role requires real-time guest judgement, natural communication and the ability to think clearly under pressure.

About the Trial Period

The role will begin with a paid trial period.

During the trial, access will be limited and responsibilities will be introduced gradually. The first focus will be guest communication, daily checks, cleaning coordination, Airtable updates and learning the QP Stays systems.

If the trial goes well, the role can expand into more lead generation, outreach, reporting and marketing support.

title: Virtual Admin & Sales Support Assistant for Canadian Cleaning Company
description:
We are a cleaning company based in Ottawa, Canada, looking for a reliable, organized, and well-spoken Virtual Admin & Sales Support Assistant to join our team part-time.

Help close leads and convert inquiries into booked cleaning jobs

Track conversations and make sure no customer is missed
Help organize daily admin tasks and customer communication
Assist with review requests after completed jobs
Support basic sales/admin workflows as needed

Excellent spoken and written English
Comfortable speaking with customers by phone
Friendly, professional, and confident communication style
Strong attention to detail

title: Brand Partnerships & Monetization Assistant (Part-Time / High Commission)
description:
*** PAYMENT SCHEDULE: This position is paid Bi-Monthly (twice per month) on a fixed, predictable corporate schedule. ***
*** COMMISSION PERK: In addition to the base hourly rate, this role includes a flat 10?sh Commission on every single paid brand deal, sponsorship, or corporate UGC contract you successfully secure and close. ***

POSITION OVERVIEW:
The VEXXIA Lifestyle Entity is seeking a fearless, high-agency, and strategically aggressive Brand Partnerships & Monetization Assistant. This is a 15-hour-per-week part-time position designed for a true Revenue Lead who thrives under low micromanagement. 

We are NOT looking for an order-taker. We are looking for a proactive commercial manager who will build our monetization assets, hunt down deals, protect our brand compliance, and directly TELL the creator what assets are needed to close five-figure contracts.

NOTE: Because this role involves handling highly confidential brand contracts, backend revenue metrics, and proprietary strategy, a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is a mandatory condition of employment.

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:
1. High-Volume Outbound Pitching: Actively source, research, and cold-pitch an aggressive target of 20 brands per day (100 per week) using highly targeted B2B outreach strategies. You will relentlessly target brand PR rooms and marketing directors to secure premium lifestyle sponsorships, luxury travel integrations, and lucrative UGC contracts.
2. Asset Creation & Maintenance: Own our commercial presentation. You will be entirely responsible for creating, designing, and constantly updating our lifestyle Media Kit and a digital UGC Portfolio showcase link that displays our elite content creation work to corporate clients.
3. TikTok Showcase & Affiliate Hub Management: Actively manage our lifestyle TikTok Showcase and backend affiliate panels. You will handle inbound brand sample requests, coordinate listing details, and maximize digital store revenue.
4. Platform Monetization Architecture: Direct the strategy and structured rollout for our upcoming Patreon community, lifestyle live-stream optimization, and Amazon Storefront monetization. 
5. The Algorithm Boundary Safe-Guard: Act as our proactive compliance director. You will thoroughly research explicit platform guidelines so you can direct the creator on exactly how to safely discuss lifestyle/wellness topics without triggering automated algorithm flags, muted audios, or stream bans.
6. Content Direction (Telling the Creator What to Film): You must track your sales pipeline and aggressively communicate with the creator. You will provide clear briefs on exactly what content, raw footage, or script reads are missing to close or fulfill active brand contracts.

REQUIRED SKILLS & PERSONALITY STYLE:
* High-Agency Leadership: You do not wait for instructions. You analyze the brand assets, execute the volume blitz, and tell the business owner what needs to be delivered to make money.
* Total Resilience: You view outbound sales as a pure numbers game. Rejection doesn't phase you; you follow up relentlessly until you get a firm answer.
* Elite Organization: Flawless tracking via spreadsheets so no brand lead or sample request ever falls through the cracks.

APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS:
If you are an independent go-getter who wants to completely run the commercial division of a fast-growing media property while earning serious commission cash, we want you.

To apply, please reply with a quick summary of your sales or email pitching experience, along with any relevant samples of media kits or pitches you’ve written. IMPORTANT: To prove your attention to detail, you must start your response to this listing with the words "GOLD MINE" in all capital letters. Applications without this keyword will be automatically deleted.

We look forward to crushing these targets with you!

title: AI-Assisted Business Development Researcher (Help Us Build New Ventures & Filipino Jobs)
description:
About Us

We're a small, established US-owned company with a team based in the Philippines, hiring through OnlineJobs.ph for over a decade. Part of our mission is genuine: we want to create more good-paying, good-environment jobs for Filipinos. To do that, we need to keep finding new business ideas worth building — and we use AI as a thinking partner to do it.

What You'll Do

You'll use AI (ChatGPT, Claude, and similar) the way a sharp analyst would — not to write fluff, but to generate, research, and pressure-test new virtual/online business ideas. 

Concretely:

Use AI to brainstorm and then stress-test new business concepts (market size, competition, what could go wrong)
Research markets, niches, and tools, and summarize findings clearly and honestly
Build simple, realistic models of how an idea could work — costs, revenue, staffing, and how many jobs it could create
Present a handful of well-reasoned opportunities, not a hundred shallow ones
Be a genuine thinking partner — push back when an idea is weak

You're a Great Fit If You Have

Real fluency using AI tools for research and analysis (show me how you use them)
Strong critical thinking — you can tell a good idea from a shiny one
Excellent written English and clear, structured thinking
Curiosity about business models, online ventures, and how money is actually made
Honesty over agreeableness — I want truth, not flattery

Nice to Have

Background in business, research, marketing, or finance
Experience writing business plans or market analyses
Familiarity with the home-services or US small-business space

To Apply send me: 

(1) one online business idea you think could create jobs in the Philippines, in 4–6 sentences, 
(2) one honest reason it might fail, and 
(3) a note on which AI tools you used to think it through. 

Please start your application with the word "VENTURE" so I know you read this fully. Applications without it won't be reviewed.
