title: Executive Assistant to the Principal Designer
description:
DO NOT EMAIL DIRECTLY - Agencies & Applicants who emails Ishita directly will be rejected & blocked immediately.


Executive Assistant to the Principal Designer (Ishita Lalan)

Company: Ishita Interiors
Location: Remote (US time zones preferred)
Type: Full-Time
Reports to: Ishita Lalan, Principal Designer

About Ishita Interiors

Ishita Interiors is a fast-scaling interior design studio known for bold, emotionally resonant spaces with a strong point of view. Led by Principal Designer Ishita Lalan—whose roots in architecture and Bollywood set design bring a cinematic lens—we create elevated interiors that feel unforgettable and function beautifully. This is a real studio with real velocity—multiple projects moving at once, lots of stakeholders, and constant decisions.


The Role (Read this first)

This is a high-velocity, high-ownership Executive Assistant role supporting a creative principal. Ishita is the visionary—fast, creative, and idea-rich. You are the operational anchor: structured, decisive, and relentless about follow-through.

You will be stepping into a world that can feel chaotic at times (because creative work moves fast). Your superpower is making sense of chaos—turning scattered inputs into clean priorities, clear communication, and a plan that the team can execute.

If you are naturally a high-D / driver personality (decisive, direct, action-oriented), love creating systems, and take pride in being the person who keeps everything moving—this role will feel like home.

What You’ll Do
1) Inbox + Communication Command Center (Top Priority)
Own Ishita’s inbox daily: triage, organize, label, respond, draft, escalate
Keep a tight follow-up loop so nothing drops (clients, vendors, team)
Draft crisp, professional communication that matches the brand voice: warm, confident, direct
Turn unclear requests into clear next steps by asking the right questions and closing loops

2) Calendar + Priority Management
Own scheduling and protect Ishita’s focus time
Build daily/weekly clarity: what matters now, what can wait, what needs escalation
Prepare Ishita for meetings with context, agendas, and “what decision is needed”

3) Design Project Support (Studio Ops for Creative Work)
Track project milestones, approvals, open decisions, and dependencies across multiple projects
Maintain simple project trackers: timelines, action items, who owns what, and due dates
Coordinate with clients, vendors, contractors, and internal team to keep projects moving
Capture meeting notes, decisions, and action lists—and drive follow-through to completion

4) Organization + Systems Implementation
Create repeatable systems: templates, checklists, SOPs, naming conventions, file structure
Improve how the studio runs: fewer dropped balls, less back-and-forth, faster execution
Translate Ishita’s ideas into structured plans that others can implement

5) Pressure Handling + Rapid Problem Solving
Stay calm when something changes last minute (because it will)
Anticipate risks, surface constraints early, and present options (Plan A/B/C)
Communicate issues clearly with impacts, timelines, and recommended next steps


You’ll Thrive Here If You Are
Elite communicator. Clear writing, sharp summaries, confident tone. You love closing loops.
An exceptional organizer. You create clarity quickly and keep many moving pieces synchronized.
Calm under pressure. You don’t spiral when things change—you prioritize and execute.
A driver (high-D). You take initiative, make decisions, push progress, and hate dropped balls.
A fast learner. Tools, design vocabulary, and new workflows don’t intimidate you.
Process-minded. You don’t just survive chaos—you build systems that prevent it next time.

Qualifications
- 2+ years in an Executive Assistant, Operations Assistant, Project Coordinator, Producer, or similar role supporting a senior leader
- Strong proficiency with Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, Docs)
- Excellent written communication and professional judgment
- Comfortable learning and operating inside tools like Notion / Asana / ClickUp / Trello (any is fine)
- Ability to flex occasionally for time-sensitive deadlines (rare, but part of the job)


Nice to Have (Not Required)
Experience in interiors, design studios, architecture firms, creative agencies, or construction-adjacent environments
Familiarity with procurement workflows, vendor coordination, timelines, and approvals
Comfort with basic docs/slides and keeping shared drives organized


What Success Looks Like (30/60/90)

30 Days
Inbox and calendar are stabilized: organized, responsive, and prioritized
A simple system exists for action items, follow-ups, and meeting notes
Ishita is noticeably less reactive day-to-day because you’ve created structure

60 Days
Project trackers are consistently up to date (milestones, approvals, open decisions)
Communication threads are clean and proactive (fewer “just checking in” loops)
Weekly cadence is running smoothly: status updates, blockers, and decisions are visible

90 Days
Ishita’s schedule and focus time feel protected and intentional
Dropped-ball rate is near zero
You’re proactively improving systems and preventing problems before they happen


Compensation & Growth
Competitive salary (shared in later stages; commensurate with experience and location)
Growth path: EA ? Senior EA / Operations Lead


How to Apply (Required)

Please submit all of the following:
1) Resume (PDF)
2) Loom Video (Required — 4–6 minutes max)

Record one Loom (screen-share on). Include a link to the video in your application.
We’re evaluating: clarity, prioritization, tone, judgment, and how you create structure fast.

In your Loom, answer all 5 prompts below:

Prompt 1: Inbox Triage + Drafting
“You open Ishita’s inbox and there are 35 unread emails from clients, vendors, and internal team. Walk us through exactly how you would triage it in the first 30 minutes, and draft responses (on screen) to any two emails:
- one client email needing an update + reassurance
- one vendor email with missing info or a delay

Show your folder/label system, what you escalate, and what you close out immediately.”

Prompt 2: Chaos ? Plan
“Describe a time you walked into a messy situation (unclear owner, shifting priorities, moving pieces). Tell the story in 60–90 seconds, then explain the framework you used to stabilize it: what you clarified first, what you documented, who you aligned, and how you tracked owners + deadlines.”

Prompt 3: Design Project Coordination Scenario
“Scenario: It’s Tuesday. Ishita has a concept presentation Friday. A vendor says a key lighting option is backordered, the client is asking for ‘one more revision,’ and a contractor needs dimensions for a built-in by end of day.
Create a quick ‘command plan’ for the next 48 hours:
- priorities ranked (1–5)
- who you’ll contact and in what order
- Plan A/B/C for the lighting issue
- the exact update you’ll send to Ishita (tight bullets)
Bonus: show what tool you’d put this in.”

Prompt 4: Writing Test (Live)
“Live on Loom, draft a short client update email (6–10 sentences) that covers: status, what’s done, what’s next, what you need from them, and the next milestone. Assume the client is anxious—keep it warm, confident, and direct. Then rewrite it as an ‘executive brief’ (bullets, 8 lines max).”

Prompt 5: Systems + Follow-Through
“Show your personal system for ensuring nothing drops: follow-ups, reminders, tracking decisions, and dependencies. If you can, screen-share a template you’ve created (sanitized). If not, build one live in a doc.”

3) (Optional, but encouraged)
A sanitized example of a tracker, SOP, or template you’ve used to run projects or follow-ups (Doc/Notion/Sheet/PDF).

Important: We won’t move forward without a Loom. This role lives and dies by communication clarity and follow-through.

title: Business Development Representative
description:
The Impact You Will Have
As a Business Development Representative, you’ll play a critical role in helping identify, qualify, and connect with the right firms and decision-makers in the B2B Specialty Tax Software space. Your research, insights, and cold phone-based outreach will directly fuel our sales team’s pipeline, ensuring that we engage with the right partners at the right time. By keeping our CRM clean, providing tailored intelligence, and supporting outreach efforts, you’ll accelerate our ability to grow relationships and deliver value to the market.
About the Company
A software platform that automates and streamlines Specialty Tax Studies for CPAs, specialty tax firms, and real estate investors. We combine deep engineering expertise with modern technology to deliver audit-defensible results at scale. Our mission is to make specialty tax services more accessible, more profitable, and more efficient for firms of all sizes.

What We Look For
We’re looking for someone who is:
Proactive – anticipating needs before being asked.
Accountable – taking ownership of accuracy, deadlines, and communication.
Curious – always asking “why” and pushing for better results.
Efficient – leveraging tools and automation to eliminate repetition.
Collaborative – working as a true partner to the Senior Account Executive.
A Strong Communicator – 100% fluent, conversational English required.
Tool Proficient – experienced with HubSpot, Apollo, and LinkedIn Sales Navigator 

Core Responsibilities
Prospect & Firm Research
Validate firm status: identify if firms offer services requiring our software, outsource these services, or are prospects for in-house adoption.
Use multiple sources: firm websites, LinkedIn, press releases, etc.
Capture context relevant to sales (alliances, decision-makers, recent activity).
Keep HubSpot records accurate and updated.

Contact Identification
Target 2–3 key decision-makers per firm (tax partners, real estate leads, technology contacts).
Prioritize quality over quantity — avoid bulk, unqualified entries.

Outreach & Qualification
Conduct live outreach (email/phone/voicemail) and follow-ups from campaigns.
Run light qualification calls to vet interest or fill missing intel.
Draft clean, tailored outbound emails by firm type and role.
Support AEs with follow-up sequences and webinar invites.

Persona-Based Targeting
Tailor messaging for different roles:
CIOs – efficiency, vendor consolidation, compliance.
Tax Partners – profitability, staffing, realization.
Study Practice Teams – operational efficiency, time savings.
Engineers – reducing redundant work, adopting modern tools.

Sales Enablement Partner
Support the Senior AE by aligning contacts and notes with live deals.
Join weekly check-ins with updates and questions.
Understand sales context and pipeline priorities.

Tool Proficiency
Manage bulk imports and structured tags to keep data clean.

Admin & Ops Support
Maintain CRM hygiene.
Assist with follow-ups, scheduling, and webinar logistics.
Support marketing initiatives and alliance outreach.

Benefits
Competitive compensation package
Remote-first flexibility
Training and growth opportunities in SaaS and sales enablement
Exposure to cutting-edge tax automation technology
Collaborative, supportive team culture

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
We believe diversity fuels innovation. We are committed to building a team that reflects varied backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applicants of all identities and encourage an inclusive, respectful, and supportive workplace for everyone.

Please fill out the form to proceed with your application:  ----------

description:

title: Brand & Visual Designer - Decks, One-Sheets, Web Mockups, Long-Term Work
description:
I'm a Nashville-based video producer looking for a skilled graphic designer to build a long-term working relationship with. I run a conference video and photography company, and I need a go-to designer for brand and visual design work as it comes up.

This is not a high-volume role. Project frequency varies — some months may have multiple small pieces, others may be quiet. This works best for someone who appreciates flexible, ongoing work rather than guaranteed weekly hours. I'm looking for someone I can send projects to consistently over time, not a one-off hire. The first few projects will be smaller pieces so we can establish a working rhythm before I send larger work.


What You'll Be Working On

Ongoing work will include:

Branded slide deck templates (blank decks I can reuse across client presentations)
One-sheet and PDF design (client-facing marketing materials)
Document formatting — Google Docs and similar, styled to look polished and professional
Website section mockups in Figma before I build them in Wix
Occasional brand asset creation as needed

This is static graphic design. No motion graphics or animation needed.


Requirements

Strong, fluent written and spoken English
Several years of professional graphic design experience (not entry-level)
Expert proficiency in Adobe Illustrator, InDesign, and Photoshop
Strong design fundamentals: typography, layout, composition, and visual hierarchy
Experience creating brand materials — decks, one-sheets, PDFs, or similar
Reliable communicator who flags problems early
High-speed, reliable internet connection


Nice to Have

Figma proficiency for website section mockups
Experience building in Wix. My sites are built on Wix, and someone who can implement their own designs there would be a significant plus, though not required
Experience with corporate or conference brand work
Clean, minimal, premium design sensibility


How We'll Work

Communication via WhatsApp and email
File and asset handoff via Dropbox or Google Drive
Payment via Wise, per project, paid promptly on delivery
I'll provide brand references, existing materials, and clear direction
You'll deliver polished, near-final work; I'll handle final implementation


To Apply

Please include all five items below. Applications missing any of these won't be reviewed.
1. Portfolio link. Share examples of static graphic design work — brand materials, decks, one-sheets, layouts, or similar. I want to see how you handle typography and layout. If your portfolio is primarily motion graphics, social media content, or video work, this role is not the right fit.
If you have your own website or portfolio site, include that link too. How you present your own work tells me a lot.
Links must be publicly viewable. Behance, your portfolio site, or similar. Google Drive links that require permission requests won't be reviewed.

2. Speed test. Screenshot from speedtest.net showing your current download and upload speeds.

3. Brief note. A few sentences about your design experience and the kinds of projects you most enjoy.

4. Rate. Your typical per-project rate for a single-page one-sheet designed from scratch, and for a 10-slide branded deck template.

5. Computer setup. Briefly share your current computer: make, model, year, RAM, and primary storage type (SSD or HDD). Just want to confirm your system runs Adobe Creative Suite smoothly.

I read every application personally and will respond to candidates I'd like to talk further with.

title: Medical Operations Manager & Systems Architect
description:
Medical Operations Manager & Systems Architect
Telehealth | Women’s Health | Remote

About OptiME Health

 ---------- 

OptiME Health is redefining healthcare for women.

We are a provider-led telehealth practice focused on metabolic health, hormone optimization, longevity, body composition, and preventative medicine.

Our philosophy is simple:

Build strong bodies that outlast age.
We combine evidence-based medicine, diagnostics, GLP-1 therapy, hormones, nutrition, accountability, and behavior change into one integrated patient experience.

We are looking for a highly experienced healthcare operations professional who can help build and scale the systems behind our patient experience.

This is not a traditional virtual assistant position.

We need an operator! 

Someone who understands healthcare workflows, patient journeys, automation, EHR implementation(PRACTICE BETTER or similar) and practice operations.

?

Your Mission

Your mission is to help build the operational backbone of OptiME Health.

You will architect the systems, workflows, automations, and patient experience that allow our practice to scale while maintaining a high-touch, concierge-level experience.

You will work directly with the Founder and become one of the first key operational hires.

?

Phase 1: Build & Architect

During your first 14-30 days, you will lead implementation and optimization of:

Practice Better
* Programs
* Packages
* Scheduling
* Forms
* Questionnaires
* Consents
* Charting templates
* Documentation workflows
* Task automation
* Patient journeys
* Reporting dashboards

Go High Level (GHL)
* Pipelines
* Lead stages
* Opportunity tracking
* Email automation
* SMS automation
* Workflow design
* Patient nurture sequences
* Internal notifications

AI & Automation
* AI voice systems
* AI receptionist workflows
* Lead qualification systems
* Intake automation
* Appointment booking automation
* Follow-up automation
* SOP creation

Documentation & Operations

* SOP creation
* Training documents
* Workflow mapping
* Process improvement
* KPI reporting systems

?

Ongoing Responsibilities

Patient Experience

* Oversee onboarding experience
* Monitor patient communications
* Ensure follow-ups happen on time
* Coordinate scheduling
* Track patient milestones
* Improve retention and engagement

Healthcare Operations

* Manage Practice Better workflows
* Coordinate lab workflows
* Coordinate pharmacy workflows
* Monitor operational bottlenecks
* Maintain reporting dashboards
* Identify process improvements

Revenue & Conversion Support

While this is not a sales role, experience with patient conversion is highly valuable.

You should understand:

* Lead nurturing
* Consultation follow-up
* Conversion tracking
* Objection handling
* Patient onboarding psychology

?

Required Experience

Must Have

Minimum 3 years healthcare operations experience

AND

At least one of the following:

* Built Practice Better from scratch
* Built Healthie from scratch
* Built SimplePractice from scratch
* Built Jane App from scratch

You must have personally configured:

* Intake forms
* Consents
* Programs
* Scheduling
* Automations
* Reporting

We are not looking for someone who simply used the software.

We are looking for someone who built and managed it.

?

Strongly Preferred

Experience with:

* Practice Better
* Go High Level
* OpenAI / AI automations
* Zapier
* Make
* Twilio
* Functional medicine
* Telehealth
* Women’s health
* Hormone replacement therapy
* GLP-1 programs
* Weight management clinics
* Longevity clinics

?

Personality Fit

You are:

* Highly proactive
* Systems-oriented
* Detail obsessed
* Process driven
* Solution focused
* Independent
* Excellent at problem solving

You see broken systems and immediately think:

“Here’s how we can fix this.”

?

Compensation
Please include:

1. Hourly rate
2. Desired monthly salary
3. Availability
4. Time zone
5. Number of hours available weekly

?

APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS

To be considered, complete every question below.

Applications that skip questions will be automatically rejected.

REQUIRED APPLICATION QUESTIONS

1. Have you personally built Practice Better, Healthie, Jane, or SimplePractice from scratch?

Describe exactly what you built.

2. What healthcare practices have you worked with?

Include specialty and provider count.

3. Describe the most complex automation you have built.

What software did you use?

4. Have you worked with Go High Level?

Explain your role and experience.

5. Have you integrated multiple systems together?

Examples:

* EHR + CRM
* CRM + AI
* CRM + SMS
* Scheduling + Automations

Explain.

6. Have you created SOPs and training documents?

Provide examples.

7. Have you worked in:

* Functional medicine
* Women’s health
* Weight loss
* GLP-1
* Hormone optimization

Explain.

8. What KPIs would you track for a telehealth practice?

List your top 10.

9. Explain the ideal patient journey from lead to retention for a women’s telehealth clinic.
10. Record a 2-minute Loom video explaining:

“If I hired you tomorrow, what would be the first 30 days of improving OptiME Health operations?”

title: Remote Cloud & Infrastructure Engineer (MSP | WFH)
description:
Job Description
The Remote Project Engineer is dedicated to completing innovative technology projects, enhancing system capabilities, and fostering a culture of collaboration to directly address the unique challenges of the area. This position is key in delivering project solutions to clients that exceed expectations through careful understanding of client technologies and a thoughtful approach to problem solving. The ideal candidate typically has 5 years of experience designing, testing, and deploying complete technology solutions.

Job Responsibilities
Expected Results

Enhance team collaboration and communication, contributing to the success of every project.

Prior Experience
Proven project execution expertise with a track record of successful implementations.
Strong technical proficiency in infrastructure components and systems.
Strong technical proficiency in configuring and deploying various infrastructure changes, including firewalls, servers, networking, and wireless.
Excellent problem-solving skills and a proactive approach to system optimization.
Outstanding communication and collaboration abilities for effective stakeholder engagement.
Client-focused mindset with a commitment to ensuring client satisfaction.

Ability to work in a fast-paced, and at times high-pressure consulting environment.
Experience using Professional Services Automation (PSA) tools or ticketing platforms such as ConnectWise is a plus but not required for this position.
Prior experience working in a Managed Service Provider (MSP) environment supporting multiple Clients is a plus but not required for this position.
Experience using Remote Monitoring and Management tools (RMM) such as Kaseya or LabTech/ConnectWise Automate is a plus but not required for this position.

Required Areas of Expertise

Windows Server Environment Setup
Active Directory and Active Directory Services
DNS and DHCP
Hypervisor Setup and Configuration with Hyper-V
Cloud Platform Deployments

NAS solutions from QNAP and Synology and Dell

Hypervisor Setup and Configuration

Nutanix Acropolis
Cloud Platform Deployments
QuickBooks Online
Apple MDM
Citrix ShareFile
Equipment Setup and Deployment
Network Switches from Cisco and Dell Enterprise
Firewalls from SonicWall and Fortinet
Wireless Access Points (WAPs) from Cisco Meraki and Ubiquiti

Job Requirements
A home office set up that meets the following:
Computer: A working computer with a minimum of 8GB RAM and one of the following:
Windows 11 with an Intel i5 or i7 (8th generation or newer), or
AMD Ryzen 5 or 7 (2000 series or newer), or
macOS 12 or newer.
Internet: Stable internet connection with at least 5 Mbps download and upload speeds.
Audio Equipment: A quality headset with a noise-cancellation feature for meetings.
Mobile Device: A smartphone capable of installing the Microsoft Authenticator app.
Webcam: An HD webcam for video calls.
2nd Monitor 
UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply) if using a desktop (Not needed if using a laptop)
A very strong cultural fit the ITS way (Our core values will be discussed during the interview) 
Excellent command of the English language both spoken and written 
Able to work full time for 40 hrs. a week:  8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST (Monday to Friday)
Able to pass our online behavior and cognitive assessments. 
Preferably, at least two of the following certifications: CompTIA A+, CompTIA Network+, CompTIA Security+, 3cx Basic Certified Engineer.
Able to provide NBI Clearance (for PH applicants).

Compensation
Pay rate starts at $8.00/hour and may vary by experience and location.

Benefits
Comprehensive Health Plans / HMO (Employee & Dependent/s)
Life Insurance
Flexible Benefit Reimbursement (Dental/Vision/Mental Health/Wellness/Government Contributions)
Paid Time Off (starts at 15 days per year)
Maternity/Paternity Leave
Paid US Holiday
Night Shift Bonus
Salary Advancement/Loan
Health & Wellness Program

title: Meta Ads Creative Director — Meta Ads, Creative Strategy & KPI Management
description:
We run a DFY lead generation system for P&C insurance agencies. Our product combines Meta ads, funnels, CRM, automations, and reporting to help agencies generate and track quote opportunities.

We are looking for a Growth Operator who can own the performance loop across Meta ads, KPIs, and creative direction.

This is not just a media buying role. You need to understand Meta ads, direct response creative, KPI diagnosis, and how to use AI tools to create and iterate high-performing UGC-style ads and short-form videos.

Responsibilities:

* Monitor client campaign KPIs daily
* Track CPL, cost per booked call/quote opportunity, CTR, CPM, CPC, frequency, and lead flow
* Flag red/yellow/green account health status
* Launch and monitor approved Meta campaigns and creative tests
* Identify winning and losing creatives
* Research competitor ads and new angles in the insurance/local lead gen space
* Write hooks, ad scripts, and creative briefs
* Direct our video editor and video creator on what to make next
* Use AI tools to create or brief UGC-style videos, b-roll, avatars, ad concepts, hooks, and variations
* Maintain a weekly creative testing roadmap
* Prepare weekly performance recaps
* Spot tracking, funnel, or CRM issues before clients complain
* Recommend what to kill, scale, remake, remix, or test next

Required:

* Experience with Meta lead generation campaigns
* Strong direct response instincts
* Ability to read campaign data and diagnose bottlenecks

* Comfortable with AI creative tools like Sora, Veo 3, Higgsfield, Runway, Midjourney, ElevenLabs, CapCut, Canva, ChatGPT, Claude, Descript, OpusClip, HeyGen, Arcads, or similar

* Comfortable working with editors, video creators, and CRM dashboards

Bonus:

* GoHighLevel experience
* Local lead gen experience
* Insurance, mortgage, home services, or financial services experience
* Experience building AI-assisted creative workflows

Compensation: $750-$1200/month Plus Bonuses with growth opportunities

title: Event & Conference Recap Video Editor - Adobe Premiere, Long-Term Work
description:
JOB OVERVIEW
I'm a Nashville-based video producer looking for a skilled, experienced video editor to build a long-term working relationship with. I produce recap videos for corporate conferences and events, and I'm hiring a primary editor to handle this work going forward.

Volume is currently around 1 project every 1-2 months, with potential to grow as my client base expands. This is not one-off freelance work. I'm looking for someone I can train on my system, pay consistently, and grow with over time. The first 2-3 months will be test projects so we can establish process and rhythm before scaling up.


What You'll Be Editing

Most projects are conference and event recap videos that combine b-roll footage with interview segments. A typical project includes:

B-roll edited to music
Interview-driven storytelling
Deliverables typically 60 seconds to 3 minutes


Requirements

Strong, fluent written and spoken English
Several years of professional editing experience (not entry-level)
Proven experience editing event recaps or similar event-driven content (interviews + b-roll cut to music)
Adobe Premiere Pro
Comfortable working with large file volumes and proxy workflows
High-speed, reliable internet connection
Strong sense of pacing, music editing, and visual storytelling
Able to take a rough creative direction and deliver a near-final edit with minimal back-and-forth
Reliable communicator who flags problems early


How We'll Work

Communication via WhatsApp and email
File transfer via Dropbox or Google Drive
Payment via Wise, per project, paid promptly on delivery
I'll provide creative briefs, reference videos, music, and footage organized clearly
I provide proxy files for editing, so you'll work on optimized media (not raw 4K footage)
You'll deliver a near-final edit; I'll handle final tweaks and client review
First 2-3 months will be smaller test projects so we can build a working rhythm together


To Apply

Please include all five items below. Applications missing any of these won't be reviewed.

1. Portfolio link. Share ONE event recap video you've edited (b-roll + interviews cut to music). Bonus if it has clear narrative threading. If you don't have event recap work specifically, share the single edit you've done that's closest to this style. If you don't have anything in this neighborhood, please don't apply.
The link must be publicly viewable. YouTube, Vimeo, your portfolio site, or similar. Google Drive links that require permission requests won't be reviewed.

2. Speed test. Screenshot from speedtest.net showing your current download and upload speeds.

3. Brief note. A few sentences about your editing experience and the kinds of projects you most enjoy.

4. Rate. Your typical per-project rate for a 2-3 minute recap video with mixed b-roll and interview footage.
I read every application personally and will respond to candidates I'd like to talk further with.

5. Editing setup. Briefly share your current editing computer: make, model, year, RAM, and primary storage type (SSD or HDD). I provide proxy files so you'll be editing on optimized media, not raw footage. Just want to confirm your system runs Premiere smoothly.

I read every application personally and will respond to candidates I'd like to talk further with.

title: Marketing Automation Manager / Email Marketing Manager
description:
Job Title: Marketing Automation Manager / Email Marketing Manager

Working Days: Monday to Friday (4hrs/day)

Job description
We're looking for a driven and strategic Marketing Automation Manager to lead and scale our customer retention and lifecycle marketing across multiple e-commerce brands. In this role, you will take ownership of end-to-end marketing automation leveraging platforms like Klaviyo and Shopify to design high-impact, data-driven retention strategies. You will combine analytical thinking with creative execution to drive customer engagement, lifetime value, and revenue growth.

This is an opportunity to move beyond execution into full strategic ownership, influencing how we communicate with customers across their entire journey.

What You'll Do
• Retention & Lifecycle Strategy: Own and develop end-to-end lifecycle marketing strategies across multiple brands, including customer journeys, lifecycle flows, segmentation frameworks, and retention roadmaps. Move beyond basic automations to build sophisticated, revenue-driving programs.
• Marketing Automation & Platform Management: Lead the management and optimization of Klaviyo (Email & SMS) and Shopify integrations. Build, test, and scale advanced flows, campaigns, and automation logic using customer data and behavioral insights.
• Data Analytics & Performance Optimization: Analyze campaign and flow performance across Klaviyo, GA4, and Shopify. Translate data into actionable insights, continuously optimizing for key metrics such as conversion rate, AOV, repeat purchase rate, and customer lifetime value.
• Campaign Planning & Execution: Own the monthly and quarterly campaign calendar, aligning with business goals, product launches, and promotional events. Ensure timely and high-quality execution across all retention channels.
• Segmentation & Personalization: Design and implement advanced segmentation strategies to deliver highly personalized customer experiences at scale.
• Research & Innovation: Stay updated with global DTC and e-commerce retention trends. Continuously test new ideas, tools, and strategies to improve engagement and performance.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with design, content, and tech teams to ensure seamless execution. Provide clear briefs, manage timelines, and maintain high standards of output.
• Team & Process Ownership: Drive operational efficiency by building processes, frameworks, and documentation for scalable marketing automation.

What We're Looking For
• 4-7 years of experience in Email/CRM/Marketing Automation, preferably in e-commerce/D2C
• Hands-on expertise in Klaviyo and Shopify
• Strong understanding of customer lifecycle marketing and retention strategies
• Proficiency in analytics tools such as GA4 and platform reporting dashboards
• Experience in managing multi-brand or high-volume campaign environments
• Strong project management and stakeholder coordination skills
• Data-driven mindset with a balance of creativity and execution

Nice to Have
• Experience with additional CRM or automation tools
• Exposure to A/B testing frameworks and experimentation
• Understanding of omnichannel marketing (push, WhatsApp, paid retargeting, etc.)

title: Senior Technical Lead (Part-Time)
description:
About The Product

We are building a B2B SaaS platform focused on marketing attribution, conversion tracking, revenue measurement and privacy-conscious data collection.

The platform helps businesses understand the difference between platform-reported performance and actual business outcomes by connecting advertising platforms, websites, CRM systems and revenue sources.

Because the platform operates in a privacy-sensitive environment and processes attribution, tracking and revenue data, accuracy, reliability and data integrity are critical.

Our customers rely on the platform to make marketing and business decisions. A bug, release issue or implementation mistake can impact reporting, attribution, revenue calculations, privacy-related functionality and customer trust.

We are looking for a senior technical professional who can help ensure the platform remains stable, secure, maintainable and production-ready as it grows.

----------

Required Experience

* 7+ years of professional software engineering, platform engineering, release engineering, QA engineering or technical leadership experience
* 5+ years working with production SaaS applications
* 5+ years reviewing production code and pull requests
* Experience acting as a Technical Lead, Senior Engineer, Platform Engineer, Engineering Manager or similar role
* Experience making architectural and technical decisions
* Experience improving engineering standards and development processes
* Experience with production releases and CI/CD workflows
* Experience working with customer-facing production systems

----------

Must Have

* Strong TypeScript experience
* Strong Next.js and React experience
* Strong backend review experience
* Strong understanding of APIs and backend service design
* Strong PostgreSQL and SQL knowledge
* Experience with GitHub workflows and pull request reviews
* Experience with GitHub Actions and CI/CD pipelines
* Experience reviewing code for maintainability, security and scalability
* Strong understanding of software architecture principles
* Strong understanding of QA and release management processes
* Strong written and spoken English

----------

Preferred Experience

* Bun
* Hono
* Prisma
* Kysely
* PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS)
* Redis / BullMQ
* Kubernetes
* ArgoCD
* Grafana
* Loki
* Prometheus
* Stripe Billing
* Application security
* Privacy/GDPR-sensitive products
* SaaS analytics, attribution or data platforms

----------

This Role Is Probably Not A Fit If

* You primarily work as a manual QA tester
* You have never reviewed other developers’ code
* You have never been responsible for release quality
* You have never worked on production SaaS systems
* You are not comfortable challenging engineering decisions
* You need detailed instructions for every task
* You are looking for a ticket execution role rather than a technical leadership role

----------

The Role

We are looking for a Senior Technical Lead who will act as an independent technical reviewer and second set of eyes for a growing SaaS platform.

This is not a primary development role.

You will not be responsible for building most product features.

Instead, your responsibility is ensuring that engineering quality, architecture, releases, infrastructure and technical standards remain at a high level as the platform grows.

You will work closely with the lead developer and founder while maintaining an independent technical perspective.

You should be comfortable:

* Reviewing code
* Reviewing architecture decisions
* Identifying technical debt
* Challenging implementation decisions
* Improving engineering standards
* Improving release quality
* Improving testing quality
* Identifying security and privacy risks
* Providing technical guidance when required

Think of this role as a combination of:

* Senior Engineer
* Technical Lead
* QA Lead
* Release Manager
* Platform Reviewer

----------

Technical Stack

Languages

* TypeScript
* SQL (PostgreSQL)

Frontend

* Next.js 14
* React
* App Router
* Tailwind CSS
* shadcn/ui

Backend

* Bun
* Hono
* Prisma
* Kysely
* Better Auth

Data & Queueing

* PostgreSQL 16
* JSONB
* Row Level Security (RLS)
* Redis
* BullMQ

Integrations

* Stripe Billing
* Usage Metering
* Resend
* React Email

Infrastructure

* Hetzner Kubernetes Engine
* Kubernetes Secrets
* S3-compatible storage
* GitHub Actions
* ArgoCD
* Grafana
* Loki
* Prometheus

You do not need to be an expert in every tool, but you should be senior enough to understand how these systems interact and identify risks, bottlenecks and improvement opportunities.

----------

Responsibilities

Technical Review & Oversight

You will regularly review development work and provide technical feedback.

You should be comfortable reviewing:

Frontend

* Next.js
* React
* TypeScript
* User flows
* Responsive behaviour
* Product requirements

Backend

* Bun
* Hono
* APIs
* Authentication flows
* Database interactions
* Business logic
* Data processing workflows

Your focus is not only whether code works, but whether it is:

* Maintainable
* Scalable
* Secure
* Well structured
* Consistent with project standards

You should be able to identify:

* Technical debt
* Architectural risks
* Security risks
* Privacy-related implementation risks
* Improper handling of sensitive data
* Permission and access control issues

----------

Quality Assurance & Testing

You will help ensure new functionality works correctly before it reaches production.

This includes:

* Feature validation
* Regression testing
* Acceptance testing
* Release testing
* Bug reproduction
* Integration testing
* User flow testing
* Tracking validation
* Revenue attribution validation

You should be comfortable validating:

* Frontend applications
* APIs
* Tracking systems
* Revenue calculations
* CRM integrations
* User permissions
* Reporting systems
* Privacy and consent-related functionality

----------

GitHub, CI/CD & Release Management

You will help maintain a professional software delivery process.

This includes:

* Branching strategies
* Pull request workflows
* Protected branches
* Merge policies
* GitHub Actions
* CI/CD pipelines
* Release validation
* Deployment workflows
* Staging reviews
* Production release reviews
* Rollback planning

Your goal is to reduce risk and increase confidence in releases.

----------

Infrastructure & Platform Oversight

You do not need to be a dedicated DevOps engineer.

However, you should be comfortable reviewing infrastructure health and identifying operational risks.

This includes:

* Kubernetes environments
* Docker environments
* Monitoring dashboards
* Logs and alerts
* Resource usage
* Secrets management
* Access control reviews
* Security-related deployment risks

You should be able to identify issues before they become production i ---------- .

----------

Hands-On Technical Contributions

While the primary focus of this role is review, quality assurance and technical leadership, you should also be comfortable making occasional improvements and fixes when appropriate.

Examples include:

* Small frontend fixes
* Small backend fixes
* CI/CD improvements
* GitHub workflow improvements
* Infrastructure improvements
* Monitoring improvements
* Configuration changes
* Documentation improvements

You will not be responsible for building major product features, but you should be comfortable contributing directly to the codebase when needed.

----------

What Success Looks Like

* Engineering standards have improved
* Releases are predictable and low risk
* Code quality is consistently high
* Technical debt is visible and managed
* Security and privacy risks are identified early
* Infrastructure is stable and well monitored
* Bugs and regressions are caught before production
* Developers receive actionable technical feedback
* The founder has confidence that the platform is being built correctly

title: Junior Finance Assistant (Accounting Graduate)
description:
APPLICANTS WHO APPLY THROUGH THE LINK BELOW WILL BE SCREENED
***Only complete submissions will be considered.***

Who we are:
Join our growing team at gigCMO Ltd, a dynamic growth consultancy providing strategic marketing leadership and expertise to start-ups, scale-ups, and SMEs, propelling them toward profitable revenue growth. Nestled in the heart of the City of London, United Kingdom, we specialise in supporting B2B clients in expanding their reach domestically and internationally. Our dedicated team, operating under the banner of Magnacca International, is strategically based in the Philippines. We chose this location for its pool of talent, strong social skills, enthusiasm, and unwavering loyalty.

Position Overview:
As a Junior Finance Assistant at gigCMO Ltd, you will play a crucial role in ensuring our operations' financial integrity and efficiency. Reporting to the financial controller, you will be responsible for various financial and analytical tasks working closely with your Finance Associate colleague as well as the Controller, CEO and COO.

Key Responsibilities:
Perform project-specific financial analysis.

title: Product Manager - SaaS
description:
gigCMO is a dynamic growth consultancy providing strategic marketing leadership and expertise to start-ups, scale-ups, and SMEs, propelling them toward profitable revenue growth. Nestled in the heart of the City of London, United Kingdom, we specialise in supporting B2B clients in expanding their reach domestically and internationally. Our dedicated team, operating under the banner of Magnacca International, is strategically based in the Philippines. We chose this location for its pool of talent, strong social skills, enthusiasm, and unwavering loyalty.

We are seeking a dedicated and dynamic Product Manager to lead and enhance our Digital Church Platform®. The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing the product development lifecycle, managing development resources effectively, and ensuring rigorous final testing processes before releasing software updates. You will also play a critical role in translating the strategic vision held by the founder into actionable roadmaps and product deliverables.

Key Responsibilities:

- Manage the Product Development (PD) pipeline, ensuring alignment with organisational goals.
- Prioritise and allocate development resources efficiently to align with strategic priorities.
- Conduct comprehensive final testing and quality assurance to guarantee smooth, error-free software updates before public release.
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to deliver product enhancements that meet user needs and drive platform adoption.
- Communicate clearly and proactively with stakeholders regarding project timelines, milestones, and release schedules.
- Translate strategic vision and high-level ideas from leadership into actionable product roadmaps, user stories, and specifications. 
- Facilitate clear and consistent communication between non-technical stakeholders and technical teams, ensuring all parties are aligned.
- Set up and manage internal systems (e.g. Basecamp and HubSpot) to track backlog, sprints, and milestones.
- Gather feedback from churches and users to shape future features and improvements. 
- Monitor product performance or user feedback loops to inform decisions and prioritisation. 
- Monitor incoming requests via helpdesk and chat channels, triage issues, and ensure timely resolution either directly or by coordinating with the development team.

Qualifications:

- Proven experience in Product Management, ideally within digital platforms or SaaS environments.
- Strong organisational skills with an ability to manage multiple development priorities simultaneously.
- Exceptional attention to detail, particularly in software testing and quality assurance.
- Demonstrable capability in managing software product lifecycles effectively.
- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, able to engage stakeholders at various levels.
- Previous experience in digital community platforms or faith-based digital services is advantageous but not essential.
- Experience writing product requirements, specs, and user stories to support development.
- Understanding of user needs in faith-based or nonprofit contexts, particularly around compliance, admin, or community tools, is highly desirable. 
- A mindset geared toward scalability, continuous improvement, and hands-on problem solving in an early-stage SaaS environment.

Application Requirements:

- This role demands a full-time commitment and is not compatible with another full-time job. To be considered, kindly fill out the form using this link:  ---------- 
- Incomplete submissions will not be considered.

Compensation: Php 30,000 - 40,000 (depending on whether the applicant meets 100% of the requirements)

title: Content Writer
description:
GIGCMO LTD is looking for a Content Writer to support the Canadian Indigenous Investment Forum (CIIF), a year-round investment forum connecting Indigenous-led businesses and communities with institutional investors across the UK, Europe, and North America. The role also involves supporting content initiatives across our other client brands as needed.

To be considered for the role, you should:
- Be comfortable writing about finance, current events, and history, with a genuine interest in international news. Think Bloomberg or Financial Times. 
- Have experience writing across a variety of formats. We particularly value long-form work such as case studies, opinion pieces, and newsletters, alongside short-form content like social media posts, photo captions, and infographics.
- Know how to interpret data and use it to tell a story. You don't need to be an analyst, but you should be confident enough to draw insights from numbers and shape it into something readable. 
- Take an audience-centric approach to writing. That means researching who you're writing for, understanding their interests and concerns deeply, and producing content that speaks to them. 
- Develop fresh content ideas and editorial plans independently and proactively. You'll contribute to content calendars and other editorial planning activities, and we value someone who can bring ideas to the table rather than waiting for them.
- Be a team player. You'll collaborate closely with creatives, other writers, web developers, and others across a cross-functional team. 
- Be willing to learn and use new tools. We work with HubSpot and a range of other tools and platforms, and we want someone curious enough to pick them up quickly. 

Qualifications
- A degree in journalism, creative writing, communications, or literature is preferred but not required. 
- Strong editing and proofreading skills, with the ability to self-edit and tighten your own drafts.
- Familiarity with SEO and on-page optimisation.
- A strong portfolio of written work.
- Comfortable working UK hours (3 pm – 12 am Philippine time).

Kindly fill out the form using this link:  ----------

title: Integration & Automation Developer
description:
Position Overview
Are you the kind of developer who gets excited building systems that actually work — not just writing tickets in a corporate queue? We are hiring our very first internal Integration & Automation Developer, and this is a rare opportunity to own the entire technical foundation of a growing company from day one.

You will not be one of dozens of developers. You will be THE developer. If you thrive on real ownership, shipping things that matter, and seeing the direct impact of your work on a business you care about — keep reading.

What You Will Own

You will be the core technical anchor of our internal operations: architecting and maintaining our database, building automated workflows in the Microsoft Power Platform (Power Automate and Power BI), and connecting our internal tools through seamless REST API integrations.

Key Responsibilities

Power Automate - Workflow & System Automation: Design, build, and scale automated Cloud and Desktop flows to connect internal applications, sync business data, trigger smart notifications, and eliminate manual work across the organization.

Power BI - Reporting & Dashboards: Build and maintain powerful, clear dashboards and reports that transform raw business data into real insights for decision-makers. You will own the data story.

API & System Integrations: Build reliable, well-structured connections between our internal systems and third-party cloud tools using REST APIs, webhooks, and JSON — making sure data flows exactly where it needs to go.

Internal App Development: Design and deploy custom internal tools, forms, and data-driven apps using Microsoft Power Apps — built to actually solve the problems our team faces every day.

What We Are Looking For

Microsoft Power Automate: Deep, hands-on experience building complex, reliable Cloud and Desktop flows. You know how to architect flows that don't break, scale well, and connect systems cleanly.

Microsoft Power BI: Proven ability to build dashboards and reports connected to live data sources. You can model data effectively and present it in a way that non-technical stakeholders actually understand.

API & Integration Skills: Strong working knowledge of REST APIs, webhooks, JSON data structures, and authentication standards like OAuth and API keys.

Database Experience: Comfortable working with relational databases — querying, maintaining, and building integrations on top of structured data.

Why This Role Is Different

True Ownership: You are not a small cog in a massive machine. You will own the technical landscape, set the direction, and see the direct impact of your work every single day.

100% Remote Work: Work from the comfort of your home with a stable, predictable schedule. Working hours are 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM EST.

Long-Term Career Growth: As our first internal technical hire, you are not just taking a job — you are building a career with a company that is investing in you for the long haul.

No Bureaucracy: We move fast, communicate directly, and cut through the red tape. Your ideas will be heard and your work will matter.

A Quick Note on How We Work
This role is built on trust, direct collaboration, and long-term partnership. A few things to know before you apply:

Direct Relationships Only: We hire individuals directly. We are not looking to work with agencies, outsourcing firms, or representatives acting on behalf of other developers.

Our Commitment to Focus: This is a full-time, dedicated internal role requiring 100% focus during working hours.

Interactive Technical Screening: We want to see how you think in real-time. Our technical evaluation is a casual, collaborative live video call where you will keep your camera on and walk us through a Power Automate and Power BI scenario.

How to Apply (Required Screening Questions)
To be considered, please answer ALL of the following questions in your cover letter.

Please confirm that you are looking for a single, dedicated full-time role and do not currently hold overlapping corporate full-time contracts.

Are you completely comfortable with a live, camera-on, screen-share technical evaluation where you will walk through a Power Automate and Power BI scenario in real-time?

Describe a Power Automate flow or Power BI report you built that had a direct, measurable impact on a business process. Be specific — what was the problem, what did you build, and what changed?

Please record and submit a short video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself. Tell us who you are, what draws you to this specific role, and briefly walk us through one integration or automation project you are proud of. Upload it to YouTube (unlisted), Google Drive, or Loom and paste the link here. This helps us get a sense of how you communicate — an important part of this collaborative role.

title: US Tax Associate
description:
Build your tax expertise. Support high-level clients. Grow with a top-tier firm.


About the Role

If you’re looking to build strong fundamentals in U.S. taxation, work on real client cases, and grow inside a high-performance accounting firm—this role is for you.

DiLucci CPA Firm has been serving business owners and real estate investors since 2005. We’re known for going beyond compliance—using the tax code strategically to help clients keep more of what they earn while giving them clarity, confidence, and long-term direction.

As a Tax Associate, you will support the preparation of U.S. tax returns, assist with client documentation, and work closely with senior tea ---------- mbers to ensure accurate and timely filings.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone early in their tax career who is detail-oriented, coachable, and eager to grow in U.S. tax.


What You’ll Do

Tax Preparation & Support
- Prepare individual and/or business tax returns (1040, 1065, 1120S)
- Assist in gathering, organizing, and reviewing client financial data
- Ensure accuracy and completeness of all tax filings

Documentation & Organization
- Maintain clean and organized client files and records
- Upload, track, and manage documents within internal systems
- Support the team in keeping workflows efficient and up to date

Client Support
- Assist in responding to client inquiries and requests
- Help ensure a smooth and professional client experience
- Follow up on missing documents and required information

Team Collaboration
- Work closely with senior tax professionals and managers
- Apply feedback quickly to improve accuracy and efficiency
- Support the team during high-volume tax periods

What We’re Looking For
- 1–3 years of US tax or accounting experience
- Experience in tax returns (1040 required; 1065/1120S a plus)
- With CPA, EA, or Tax Certifications is a plus
- Strong attention to detail and accuracy
- Good written and verbal English communication skills
- Highly organized and able to manage multiple tasks
- Comfortable using accounting or tax software
- Willingness to learn quickly and grow in U.S. tax
- Reliable, proactive, and team-oriented

Why Join Us
- 100% Remote
- Work directly with seasoned U.S. tax professionals
- Hands-on experience with real client work
- Clear growth path into senior tax roles
- Supportive, fast-paced learning environment


Our Core Values
- Genuine Care
- Great Communication
- Speed to Learning & Implementation
- Fun Tax Energy (FTE)

How to Apply:
Submit and answer the following:
1.  Resume Link
2.   Are you able to start ASAP?
3.  Are you open to extended hours specially during peak periods and deadlines?
4. Briefly share your relevant  US Taxation experience.


If you’re looking for a role where you can build real tax skills, work with a strong team, and grow your career in U.S. accounting—we’d love to hear from you.

title: COO - D2C Beauty Brand
description:
Chief Operating Officer (COO) – E-commerce D2C Brand (Remote – Philippines)

Full-time · 40+ hours per week · Long-term · High-responsibility role

We are a fast-growing e-commerce beauty brand called Facelove Cosmetics serving thousands of customers worldwide every day. To accelerate our growth and further professionalize our internal operations, we are looking for an experienced and highly capable Chief Operating Officer (COO) who will take full responsibility for all operational processes within the company.

This role is only for someone who has proven, measurable results in previous companies, thrives on taking ownership, and excels at building strong teams and systems. Since you need to have an extraordinary amount of attention to detail, please start your application with the word "leader".

What You’ll Be Responsible For (Core Responsibilities):
As COO, you are the operational backbone of the company. You work directly between the founders and the team, ensuring a strong, efficient, and scalable operation.

Store Operations:
- Oversee and optimize daily store operations
- Monitor fulfillment, supplier relations, inventory, and performance
- Identify bottlenecks early and proactively solve them on your own

Content Operations:
- Structure and streamline all content workflows
- Manage planning, deadlines, and team accountability
- Ensure consistent, high-quality, on-time content delivery

Internal Systems & Processes:
- Build SOPs, workflows, and automation systems
- Implement tools to improve efficiency across departments
- Create scalable systems that run like a well-oiled machine

Team Leadership & Accountability:
- Lead daily/weekly calls and manage cross-functional teams
- Communicate clearly and deliver actionable next steps
- Coach teams, maintain accountability, and drive performance

What We Are Looking For (Skills & Experience):
We need someone who does not need to be trained from scratch, someone who brings real experience and combines it with hunger, ownership, and strong leadership.

Requirements:
- Minimum 3 years experience in a high-performance e-commerce setting
- Strong leadership and team management skills
- Excellent communication: clear, decisive, solution-oriented
- Experience building SOPs, systems, and organizational structures
- Experience scaling a D2C E-commerce brand from low 8 figures into the 9 figures 
- Proven ability to manage multiple departments at once
- Thrives in fast-paced environments with many moving parts
- Full-time availability (40+ hours) with flexibility on weekends

What You Can Expect:
- Salary depending on experience: $6000 – $8000+ per month
- Long-term working relationship with a global D2C brand
- A key leadership role directly under the founders
- Room for initiative, innovation, and strategic input
- A high-performance culture with clarity, speed, and high standards
- Monthly review meetings + weekly operational check-ins
- Work with a driven team aiming for rapid growth and operational excellence

Ready to Elevate Our Operations to the Next Level?

title: Luxury Travel Operations Coordinator (Remote)
description:
Position Overview

As a Luxury Travel Operations Coordinator, you will be responsible for managing time-sensitive VIP airport and travel services for passengers traveling through airports worldwide. The role involves coordinating complex travel arrangements, handling urgent operational requests, and ensuring flawless service delivery for high-profile and luxury travel clients.

Our services operate within a highly time-sensitive environment where precision, efficiency, and responsiveness are essential. Every booking requires careful coordination across multiple parties, airports, and service providers worldwide. Success in this role depends on maintaining exceptional accuracy, strong situational awareness, and the ability to execute flawlessly under tight deadlines.

This position requires exceptional attention to detail, strong organizational skills, sound judgment under pressure, and the ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously. You must be comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where situations can change rapidly and where quick, accurate decision-making is essential.

We are looking for a highly responsible, proactive, and dependable professional who can independently manage operational responsibilities while maintaining excellent communication with clients, suppliers, and internal teams.

Key Responsibilities

- Respond promptly to all incoming calls, emails, and messages related to pre-service and post-service requests.
- Manage new bookings, verify travel details, and ensure accurate data entry across company systems.
- Confirm that all required information is collected, verified, and communicated clearly to clients and operational partners.
- Coordinate VIP airport services and monitor requests to ensure successful execution.
- Create and send invoices, track payments, and maintain accurate client records.
- Assist clients with special travel requests, urgent changes, and last-minute operational adjustments.
- Communicate proactively with the manager on duty regarding urgent situations, service issues, complaints, or operational escalations.
- Ensure all bookings, modifications, and cancellations comply with company procedures and service deadlines.
- Promote and cross-sell additional services and return-trip assistance when appropriate.
- Maintain a high level of responsiveness and professionalism while managing multiple active cases simultaneously.

Qualifications

- Previous experience in the travel industry is required (travel agency, airline, airport operations, luxury travel, or related field).
- Excellent written and spoken English communication skills.
- Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy under pressure.
- Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and the ability to work across multiple systems simultaneously.
- Good knowledge of world geography, international travel, and airport operations.
- Strong problem-solving and decision-making skills.
- Ability to prioritize effectively and manage multiple urgent requests at the same time.
- Comfortable working independently with minimal supervision.
- Access to a computer or laptop, reliable high-speed internet, and a working phone for calls.
- Ability to work flexible schedules, including early mornings, evenings, weekends, and public holidays.
- Professional, calm, and composed approach when handling high-pressure situations.

title: Senior Lead Generation Specialist & Outbound Technical Operator
description:
About the Role
We are seeking a world-class Senior Lead Generation Specialist & Outbound Technical Operator to serve as the strategic engine and audience growth hub of our firm. We specialize in placing high-performing Virtual Assistants and Executive Assistants with elite US-based clients.

This is a highly technical, data-driven execution role. You will own our entire outbound infrastructure, build advanced prospecting pipelines, maximize deliverability at scale, and manage our day-to-day growth systems. We will provide the core copywriting and messaging strategy, while you own the technical execution, spintax deployment, and continuous A/B testing to maximize performance.

If you are a master of email deliverability, data engineering, and outbound systems, and you want to step into a high-visibility execution role with elite compensation, this is for you.

Your Core Responsibilities:
Infrastructure Architecture & Deliverability Mastery: Own the absolute health and scalability of our cold email infrastructure (15+ domains, 50+ inboxes, and growing). Monitor deliverability daily, manage advanced inbox rotation/warmups, and proactively solve spam or sender reputation issues before they touch our data.
Data Operations & List Hygiene: Oversee advanced data scraping, list cleaning, and multi-step verification protocols to ensure optimal bounce rates and protect our sender reputation.
Campaign Execution & Rigorous Testing: Manage high-volume sending schedules and load pre-written sequences. You will take the copywriting we provide and run rigorous A/B testing on both copy variants and technical variables (sending windows, spintax, angles, and inbox distribution) to constantly optimize and maximize the positive reply rate.
Workflow Optimization: Build out smooth backend workflows, document advanced internal SOPs, and maintain the data pipelines that keep our outreach metrics flawless.
Pipeline Accountability: Own the primary metric of keeping our internal sales team fed with high-intent, warm "hand-raisers" who want to buy our services.

Requirements:
5+ Years of Elite B2B Outbound Experience: A proven track record of running sophisticated, high-volume B2B cold email marketing and outbound data operations.
Advanced Tech Stack Mastery: Deep, practical expertise with modern outbound tools (e.g., Smartlead, Instantly, Clay, Apollo, DeBounce, MillionVerifier, list-enrichment APIs).
Technical Deliverability Expertise: Expert-level knowledge of DNS management, SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and Custom Tracking Domains. You know how to play the cat-and-mouse game with ISPs to stay out of the promo/spam folders.
The "Data Scientist" DNA: You live in clean spreadsheets. You make decisions based on numbers, conversion percentages, and A/B test results, not gut feelings.
Exclusivity (No Moonlighting): This is a high-level, full-time position. Juggling other clients, freelancing, or agency side-gigs is strictly prohibited. We want 100% of your focus, and we compensate accordingly.

Shift & Location:
Schedule: Flexible working hours, but requires a structured daily overlap with US Business Hours (EST) for leadership syncs and campaign monitoring.

Compensation & Premium Benefits:
Base Salary: $1,200 – $1,450 USD / month – We are looking for elite talent and are prepared to pay top-of-market rates for the right expert.
Uncapped Bonus Structure: Generous performance bonuses and commissions tied directly to the closed deals generated from your infrastructure.
Growth Trajectory: As the first major technical hire in this department, you will have a direct opportunity to grow into a leadership role as the company expands.

HOW TO APPLY:
Please reply with the subject line: [Growth Ops Application - (Your Name)] and include:
Your updated Resume highlighting your technical outbound experience.
A link to your LinkedIn Profile.
A brief breakdown of the most complex cold email infrastructure you have personally built and managed (number of domains/inboxes) and the specific tactics you used to maintain high deliverability and manage A/B testing at scale.

title: E-commerce Operations & Brand Launch Coordinator
description:
Part-time | 20 hrs/week | $600–700/mo | Intermediate Level | Long-term**

---

We're a small company that helps brands and organizations launch custom branded apparel and e-commerce stores. You'll work directly with the founder.

We're looking for someone with a strong visual eye, good judgment, and the kind of work ethic where things get done without being asked twice.

**What you'll do:**

- Read and triage the inbox — d ----------  what needs the founder's attention and what you can handle directly, and draft responses
- Gather requests from email and Slack, maintain a visible task list the whole team can see, and keep every job and its status current
- Execute client store launches using our internal tools — we'll train you on the software, but you need strong visual judgment: choose garment colors that work with client branding, size logos correctly, and review client websites to understand their aesthetic before making decisions
- QA your own work before marking anything done — you check for typos, accuracy, and completeness

**What we're looking for:**

- **A strong visual eye — this is the most important thing.** When you see a logo on a garment, you know if the colors work or clash. You can look at a client's website, logos, and artwork, and immediately understand their visual identity — colors, aesthetic, brand feel — and translate that into product decisions. If design sense isn't something you have, this role isn't the right fit.
- When something is unclear, you figure it out or ask a smart question. You build your own systems to get more efficient rather than waiting to be told how to do something.
- You proactively update the status of your work — people never have to follow up to find out where things stand.
- You check your own work. Mistakes happen, but for you they're rare.
- Google Workspace, Slack, Canva, AI tools. Shopify and Printful are a plus — we'll train the right person.
- Strong written English.

**To apply:** Send a short intro (3–5 sentences) about your background and why this role fits you. Then answer this: *You're launching a store for a wellness brand. Their website is mostly sage green, cream, and warm terracotta. The client hasn't specified garment colors. How would you d ----------  which colors to choose, and why?*

Applications that don't answer the question will not be reviewed.

title: GoHighLevel Marketing & Automation Specialist

A2P 10DLC & Phone/SMS Setup

Monitor SMS deliverability and help troubleshoot registration or compliance issues
Marketing Support
Create and manage email and SMS marketing campaigns
Assist with lead generation campaigns and nurture sequences
Help organize marketing assets, templates, and client communication workflows

Track campaign performance and suggest improvements
Client Support & Training
Record short training videos for clients and internal tea ---------- mbers

Join client calls when needed to explain systems, workflows, funnels, or automations
Communicate clearly and professionally in English, both written and spoken
Provide updates on projects and ask thoughtful questions when something is unclear
Required Skills & Experience

The ideal candidate must have:

Strong experience using GoHighLevel
Experience building workflows, funnels, websites, forms, surveys, calendars, and pipelines
Understanding of email marketing, SMS marketing, lead nurturing, and CRM automation
Experience with A2P 10DLC setup or willingness to learn quickly
Ability to troubleshoot automations and CRM issues
Strong attention to detail
Proficient English communication skills
Ability to record Loom-style training videos
Ability to speak with clients professionally when needed
Strong organization and project management skills
Ability to work independently and meet deadlines
Preferred Skills

These skills are not required, but they are a plus:

Experience with insurance, healthcare, Medicare, financial services, or professional service businesses
Experience with Canva, Zapier, Make, Calendly, Zoom, RingCentral, Stripe, or WordPress
Experience creating SaaS snapshots inside GoHighLevel

Experience setting up white-label or SaaS Mode in GoHighLevel
Experience creating SOPs and training documentation
Experience managing multiple client accounts
Work Schedule

This is a remote position for a candidate based in the Philippines.

Schedule may be part-time or full-time depending on experience and availability. Some availability during U.S. Eastern Time business hours is required for meetings, client support, and training.

What We Are Looking For

We are looking for someone who is dependable, organized, proactive, and comfortable taking ownership of projects. You should be able to think through the client journey, not just complete tasks. We want someone who can help us build clean, professional, automated systems that improve communication, lead follow-up, and client experience.

To Apply

Please send the following:

Your resume or work history
Examples of GoHighLevel funnels, workflows, automations, or websites you have built
A short video introduction explaining your GoHighLevel experience
Your hourly rate or monthly salary requirement
Your availability in U.S. Eastern Time
Any experience you have with A2P 10DLC, AI automation, or client training videos

title: Real Estate VA
description:
I am looking for a reliable, highly organized, and proactive Virtual Assistant to help manage the day-to-day operations of my rental properties. This is a remote, part-time position focused entirely on administrative upkeep, utility management, accurate bookkeeping, and tenant communication.
The ideal candidate has a background in real estate or property management, enjoys keeping data organized, and possesses excellent communication skills.
Key Responsibilities
 Financial & Bill Management: Oversee and execute the punctual payment of property utility bills (power, gas, water, etc.). Ensure all expenses are tracked, logged, and synced accurately in our tracking system.

title: Project Manager (Task Execution-Focused)
description:
About Allied Assist

Allied Assist is a remote operations support company helping businesses stay organized, efficient, and fully executed behind the scenes. We support multiple clients across operations, administration, communication, systems, and project execution to ensure tasks move forward accurately and on time.

We are looking for a highly organized, execution-focused Project Manager who thrives in fast-paced environments, is technically capable, proactive, resourceful, and enjoys figuring things out.

This role is ideal for someone who naturally researches solutions, learns new systems quickly, and takes ownership of moving tasks from start to completion.


Role Overview

This role is centered around execution, coordination, problem-solving, and operational support.

You will work directly with clients, internal teams, and Virtual Assistants to keep projects moving, resolve issues, organize workflows, and ensure execution happens consistently.

We need someone who does not stop at “I don’t know” — but instead researches, investigates, tests solutions, and finds answers.

Clients may bring operational questions, technical issues, process concerns, system challenges, or administrative requests, and this role requires someone comfortable learning, troubleshooting, and driving resolution.

This is not a strategy-only management role and not a purely people-management position.

This is a hands-on execution role for someone who loves organization, ownership, research, and getting things done.


Key Responsibilities
Manage day-to-day operational tasks and project execution across multiple client accounts
Track deliverables, timelines, action items, and task completion
Research solutions for client concerns, technical issues, workflow gaps, and operational questions
Troubleshoot problems by investigating systems, documentation, tools, SOPs, and available resources
Coordinate with Virtual Assistants and specialists to ensure work completion
Follow up proactively on pending items, blockers, and deadlines
Organize and maintain tasks, SOPs, records, and workflows in project management systems
Communicate with clients professionally through email, chat, calls, and Zoo ---------- etings
Assist clients with administrative, operational, technical, and process-related requests
Learn new tools, systems, and workflows quickly when client needs change
Document findings, solutions, and process improvements
Keep workflows organized and execution moving efficiently
Use tools such as ClickUp, Slack, Hubstaff, Google Workspace, Excel/Sheets, AI tools, and various client systems


Requirements
3–5 years experience in project coordination, operations support, executive assistance, project management, client support, or similar roles
Strong execution mindset with excellent follow-through
Technically capable and comfortable learning new systems quickly
Strong research and problem-solving skills
Naturally resourceful — able to investigate and find answers independently
Excellent organizational skills with ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously
Strong attention to detail and accountability
Comfortable troubleshooting workflows, systems, and operational challenges
Experience communicating professionally with clients via email, chat, and Zoom
Ability to manage priorities in a fast-paced remote environment
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Experience with ClickUp or similar project management systems preferred


Nice to Have
Technical support or troubleshooting background
Experience with process documentation and SOP creation
Experience supporting remote teams or Virtual Assistants
Familiarity with AI tools and productivity platforms
Experience with automations, workflows, CRMs, or operational systems
Experience working directly with clients in a service environment


Work Setup
Fully remote
Must have stable internet and a quiet workspace
Must be available during core working hours (8am–5pm EST)
Compensation
Starting rate: $4.00/hour
Paid weekly via Wise
Performance-based raises at 30/60/90 days
Long-term growth opportunities available for strong performers
