Applications that do not follow the instructions above may not be considered.

We look forward to meeting motivated individuals who are ready to build a long-term career in the U.S. mortgage industry.

title: WEBSITE/WordPress Developer and Local SEO Specialist (SEO, AEO, GEO, GBP, Citations)
description:
JOB DESCRIPTION
We are a US-based marketing agency building the complete digital presence for a new professional services client: a CFO-level accounting firm in the greater Atlanta, Georgia market. We need one skilled, self-directed hire who can build the WordPress site and own the full local search presence from launch through ongoing optimization. This is a long-term role with real ownership and room to grow.

PHASE 1: WEBSITE BUILD (Weeks 1)
Build a clean, fast, conversion-ready WordPress (or your recommendation) site (5 to 7 pages) on the provided hosting
Install and configure Rank Math Pro or Yoast SEO (v19+)
Pass Core Web Vitals on mobile and desktop
Integrate lead capture forms on every service page
Connect Google Analytics 4 and Google Search Console from day one
Implement IndexNow protocol for immediate Bing indexing (this feeds ChatGPT live search)
Configure robots.txt to allow AI crawlers including ChatGPT-User

SCHEMA AND STRUCTURED DATA (this is a hard requirement, not optional)
LocalBusiness JSON-LD on homepage and contact page: name, address, phone, hours, geo coordinates, URL, image, priceRange, areaServed — must match GBP exactly
FAQPage schema on every service page
Person schema for the business principal
Organization schema with sameAs properties linked to all verified profiles
Service schema on each individual service page

PHASE 2: GBP, NAP, AND CITATIONS
Full Google Business Profile setup and optimization: all fields complete, services reviewed and corrected, photos uploaded and labeled, GBP posts on a regular cadence, Q and A section seeded with structured answers
Submit to all tier-one directories: Google, Bing Places, Apple Maps, Yelp, Facebook, BBB
Submit to accounting and professional services industry directories
Submit to local Cherokee County and greater Atlanta directories
Target 40 to 60 verified citations in the first 90 days
Audit NAP consistency across all platforms quarterly and correct any drift

PHASE 3: AEO AND GEO OPTIMIZATION (ongoing)
Place a 40 to 60 word direct answer block at the top of every service page section
Build and maintain prompt-aligned FAQ sections on all service pages
Maintain a 7 to 14 day content freshness cycle with genuine page updates
Define 15 to 25 target prompts for the local market and track AI citation performance monthly
Track three GEO KPIs each month: mention rate, citation rate, and answer position across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and Google AI Overviews

If this works, I have 20 other websites that will need adjusting and keep you busy :)

BACKLINKS
White-hat only: local press, chamber of commerce, business association profiles, relevant guest content
No link farms, no PBNs, no shortcuts

TOOLS YOU MUST KNOW
Google Search Console, GA4, Rank Math or Yoast SEO, Semrush or Ahrefs, BrightLocal or Whitespark, Google Rich Results Test and Schema Validator, IndexNow. Ability to manually test AI citation visibility in ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini is required.

TO APPLY: Send a Loom video, 3 minutes maximum, covering these three things: Show one local SEO project you built from scratch, your experience implementing JSON-LD schema, and how you would get a brand new local business appearing in Google AI Overviews within 30 days. Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

title: Odoo ERP Expert / Consultant for System Review & Optimization
description:
This should attract a senior Odoo developer who can own your ERP long-term rather than just complete one project.

# Senior Odoo Developer / ERP Architect (Long-Term Partnership)

## About Us

We are a rapidly growing manufacturing and distribution company operating on a heavily customized Odoo ERP platform. Odoo is the operational backbone of our business, managing purchasing, inventory, logistics, sales, accounting integrations, customer service, vendor management, reporting, and custom workflows.

We are looking for a highly skilled Odoo developer who can become our long-term technical partner. This is not a one-time project. We need someone who can continuously improve, maintain, troubleshoot, and expand our ERP as our business grows.

## What We Need

We need an experienced Odoo developer who can:

* Design and build new custom modules
* Modify and extend existing Odoo functionality
* Maintain and improve custom workflows
* Debug and resolve production issues quickly
* Optimize performance and database operations
* Build custom reports and dashboards
* Create integrations with third-party systems and APIs
* Assist with Odoo upgrades and migrations
* Improve user experience and operational efficiency
* Provide ongoing support and strategic recommendations

Our goal is to have a developer who understands our business processes and becomes an extension of our team.

## Current Environment

Our Odoo environment includes significant customization across multiple departments, including:

* Purchasing
* Inventory Management
* Warehouse Operations
* Manufacturing Processes
* Vendor Management
* Customer Service
* Logistics & Freight Operations
* Reporting & Analytics
* Accounting Integrations
* CRM and Sales Operations

We have numerous custom modules, automated workflows, and business-specific processes that require both maintenance and ongoing enhancement.

## Required Experience

You should have:

* 5+ years of Odoo development experience
* Strong Python skills
* Strong PostgreSQL knowledge
* Experience developing custom Odoo modules
* Experience with Odoo Studio and custom code
* Experience with API integrations
* Experience with Odoo version upgrades and migrations
* Strong debugging and troubleshooting abilities
* Excellent written English communication

## Preferred Experience

Bonus points for experience with:

* Manufacturing companies
* Distribution businesses
* Inventory-heavy operations
* Warehouse management
* Logistics and transportation workflows
* EDI integrations
* Shipping carrier integrations
* Accounting and financial integrations
* Business process automation

## How We Work

We move quickly and expect proactive communication.

We are looking for someone who:

* Thinks like an ERP architect, not just a programmer
* Can recommend better solutions instead of simply following instructions
* Understands business processes and operational efficiency
* Documents their work clearly
* Writes maintainable, production-quality code
* Can handle both small fixes and large development projects

## Initial Engagement

The first phase will include:

* Reviewing our current Odoo environment
* Understanding our custom modules and workflows
* Identifying technical debt and improvement opportunities
* Completing several maintenance and enhancement tasks

If the relationship is successful, this can become a long-term ongoing engagement with consistent work.

## To Apply

Please include:

1. Your Odoo experience (years and versions worked with)
2. Examples of custom Odoo modules you have built
3. Examples of complex business workflows you have implemented
4. Experience with manufacturing, inventory, or logistics operations
5. Your availability (hours/week)
6. Your hourly rate
7. A brief description of the most complex Odoo project you have worked on

We are looking for a true long-term partner who can help us continue building a world-class ERP platform.

A tip: I'd also add a screening question that instantly filters out 80% of applicants:

**"Describe an Odoo customization you built that significantly changed a company's business process. What was the problem, what did you build, and what measurable result did it achieve?"**

The strongest Odoo developers answer with business outcomes. The weaker ones only talk about code.

title: Beginner Level WordPress Developer
description:

title: Executive Assistant — Fast-Paced Startup CEO
description:
The Role
You won't be handed a playbook. You'll help write it.
This role exists because the CEO needs a trusted operator who can absorb context quickly, make good calls with limited information, and execute without hand-holding. You'll sit at the intersection of strategy and execution — owning the CEO's time, filtering noise, moving things forward, and occasionally being the one person who holds the whole picture together.
If you thrive in structure and need clear processes before you act, this isn't the right fit. If you're energised by working at the edge of what you know and figuring things out as you go, keep reading.

What You'll Actually Do
Time & Schedule Management

Own the CEO's calendar with a sharp eye for time efficiency — protect deep work blocks, batch meetings intelligently, and eliminate unnecessary gaps and conflicts
Proactively plan each day before it starts — not reactively managing what lands, but anticipating what's coming and setting the CEO up for it
Brief the CEO before every meeting: context, objective, who's in the room, what outcome is needed
Guard the CEO's time like it's the company's most valuable resource — because it is

Communication

Manage high-volume inbound across email, Slack, and other channels — triage, respond, escalate, or action without being asked
Draft outbound communications on behalf of the CEO — clear, fast, and in the right tone for the audience
Ensure nothing important goes unanswered and no thread gets dropped
Be the connective tissue between the CEO and the Sales, Development, and Marketing teams — keeping information flowing, follow-ups happening, and people accountable

Cross-Team Coordination

Work daily alongside Sales, Development, and Marketing — understand their priorities, track dependencies, and flag anything that needs CEO attention
Run task and project tracking in ClickUp — you'll live in this tool and keep it clean, current, and actually useful
Manage campaigns, automations, and workflows in GoHighLevel (GHL) — this is non-negotiable
Follow up with tea ---------- mbers so the CEO doesn't have to chase anyone

Operations & Projects

Prepare meeting agendas, briefing notes, and action summaries
Handle logistics end-to-end: travel, events, client meetings, team offsites
Manage sensitive commercial, legal, and team information with full discretion
Spot what's falling through the cracks and close the gap before it becomes a problem


What We're Looking For
Non-negotiables:

Proficiency in ClickUp — you use it daily and keep it genuinely organised
Proficiency in GoHighLevel (GHL) — automations, pipelines, workflows, communications
You act before being asked — you anticipate, not react
Exceptional written and verbal communication — fast, clear, and appropriate for the audience
You can shift priorities mid-sentence and not miss a beat
You make confident decisions with incomplete information
Full ownership mentality — no excuses, no blame, just solutions

Strong advantage:

Experience supporting a founder or C-suite executive in a startup environment
Exposure to SaaS, tech, or fast-growth companies
Experience coordinating across Sales, Dev, and Marketing functions
Familiarity with AI tools and a willingness to use them to work smarter


What You Won't Find Here

A slow onboarding with weeks of shadowing before real responsibility
A manager who has time to check in on your progress every day
Rigid processes that tell you exactly what to do


What You Will Find

A CEO who trusts you and gets out of your way once you've earned it
Real proximity to strategy and decision-making
A fast-moving team that cares deeply and gets things done
Genuine career upside as the company scales


How to Apply

A short video introduction (2–3 minutes) — no scripts, no production value needed. Just tell us who you are, why this role, and give us one example of a time you took initiative and made something happen without being asked. Upload to YouTube, Loom, or Google Drive and include the link.
Your CV
One sentence: What does "owning someone's time" mean to you?

Applications without a video will not be considered.

This includes handling customer and dealer enquiries, processing orders, maintaining internal systems and documentation, and assisting with a variety of operational and administrative tasks. This is a role for someone who enjoys taking ownership, learning how a business operates, and helping to keep things organised behind the scenes while delivering an excellent experience for customers and partners.

Key Responsibilities
- Respond to live chat enquiries
- Answer and manage customer phone calls
- Reply to customer emails professionally and promptly
- Process customer orders and handle related admin tasks
- Support dealer enquiries and administrative requests
- Maintain and update internal documentation, SOPs and knowledge bases
- Assist with operational projects and process improvement initiatives
- Support the team with additional administrative duties as required

Required Experience & Skills
- 5+ years of experience in a similar customer support or Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) role
- Excellent English communication skills (written and spoken)
- Strong skills in phone, email, and instant messaging platforms
- Reliable internet connection (please include a speed test screenshot)
- Include an image of a Cape Fur Seal with your application
- Competent with data entry and admin tasks

Soft Skills
- Highly organised and detail-oriented
- Self-starter who can work with minimal supervision
- Friendly, positive, and customer-focused attitude
- Open to learning and improving continuously


Why Join Us
This is an opportunity to become part of a growing electric bike company where your work will directly support both our customers and the day-to-day running of the business. You'll be trusted with responsibility, supported with training, and encouraged to take ownership of the tasks and projects you manage.

We offer opportunities for long-term growth, performance-based salary increases, and the chance to develop into a key operational role as the business continues to expand.


To Apply

Please submit the following with your application:
Your CV / Resume
Screenshot of your internet speed test (use Google search: “internet speed test”)
30-second voice recording demonstrating your spoken English
Results from an online English grammar test
Confirmation of 5+ years’ experience in a similar BPO/customer service role

title: Full-Stack Content & Product Marketer (B2B & SaaS)
description:
Role Overview
We operate at the intersection of technical infrastructure and career technology. We run two parallel, high-growth tracks: CDOps Tech (an elite B2B infrastructure firm deploying interim DevOps/SRE squads to top-tier startups) and our upcoming Career Intelligence Platform (a developer-focused SaaS ecosystem).

We are looking for a hybrid, Full-Stack Marketer who lives at the intersection of technical copywriting, WordPress management, visual asset design, and product launch marketing. You will own our entire digital footprint, responsible for translating deeply technical concepts into beautiful, conversion-oriented case studies, social pipelines, and launch loops.

Key Responsibilities
1. B2B Case Studies & Collateral (CDOps Tech)
Interview the founding technical team to distill complex infrastructure achievements (e.g., Kubernetes migrations, downtime reduction, optimizing event-driven architectures) into highly polished, readable case studies and lead magnets.

Write authoritative, peer-to-peer content that commands immediate respect from senior technical decision-makers (CTOs, VPs of Engineering).

2. Product Marketing & Launch Strategy (Career Intelligence Platform)
Build and execute the comprehensive go-to-market (GTM) strategy for the upcoming SaaS platform launch.

Craft the messaging architecture, landing page copy, product walkthrough collateral, and onboarding email sequences to drive user sign-ups and viral loops.

3. Website Management & Visual Design (WordPress & Figma)
Take complete ownership of our web properties. You will directly update, edit, and optimize our landing pages and publish new case studies within WordPress (using blocks or page builders) without requiring a developer.

Maintain on-page SEO best practices (meta-tags, URL structures, image optimization) using standard WordPress SEO tools.

Design crisp, minimalist visual graphics, presentation decks, and social media templates using Figma or Canva that match our clean, tech-focused brand identities.

4. Social Media & Campaign Management
Manage, write, and schedule a consistent publishing calendar across all official corporate handles (primarily LinkedIn and X/Twitter).

Repurpose long-form case studies and product updates into short-form, high-engagement text posts, threads, and visual carousels.

Requirements & Qualifications
Experience: 3–5+ years of direct experience working as a Product Marketer, Content Lead, or Growth Marketer inside a B2B SaaS, developer-tool, or cloud-infrastructure startup environment.

The Tech Stack: Hands-on mastery of WordPress (including block editors/builders and basic plugin management), design software (Figma/Canva), social schedulers, and basic marketing automation/CRMs (HubSpot/Mailchimp).

Linguistic Technical Fluency: You must comfortably understand basic infrastructure terminology (e.g., DevOps, SRE, cloud scaling, CI/CD pipelines, Kubernetes) so your copy sounds authentic to engineering leaders, not generic.

Creative Multi-Tasker: A distinct portfolio showing a mix of both long-form written work (like case studies or whitepapers) and visual design work (like web layouts or social media assets).

The Style: Zero tolerance for generic corporate buzzwords or marketing fluff. You write with brevity, impact, and absolute clarity.

Note: Only for Philippines Candidates that do not require sponsorship

title: Account Manager
description:
About Rockenue
Rockenue is a technology-powered commercial management company for hotels. We handle revenue management, distribution, sales, and tech implementation for a growing portfolio of properties across Europe.

We're looking for someone with energy, hunger, and common sense — not necessarily a long CV.
You'll take ownership of a portfolio of hotel accounts, acting as the day-to-day point of contact for property partners. You'll produce reports, manage distribution channels, solve problems, and keep everything running smoothly. We'll teach you the hospitality side. What we can't teach is drive, initiative, and the ability to figure things out.
If you're the kind of person who Googles the answer before asking the question, who takes pride in getting things right, and who genuinely gets a buzz from being on top of everything — keep reading.

What You'll Be Doing

Managing a portfolio of hotel accounts end-to-end, acting as the main point of contact for property owners and managers
Producing and delivering regular performance reports to clients
Working inside OTA extranets ( ---------- , Expedia, etc.), channel managers, and property management systems daily
Monitoring rate parity, availability, and distribution health across all channels
Coordinating with the internal team on pricing updates, promotions, and new channel activations
Identifying revenue opportunities and flagging underperformance
Using AI tools, chatbots, and automation to streamline workflows and increase output
Learning and mastering new hospitality tech platforms quickly


Who You Are (This Matters More Than Your CV)

Relentless work ethic. You take ownership. You don't clock-watch. When something needs doing, you do it — properly and fast.
Common sense over credentials. You can walk into an unfamiliar situation, assess it logically, and figure out the right move without being hand-held.
Tech-obsessed, not tech-tolerant. You genuinely enjoy learning new systems. Complex dashboards and unfamiliar software excite you rather than intimidate you.
AI is already part of your workflow. You actively use tools like ChatGPT, Claude, or similar to get more done. This isn't optional — it's central to how we work.
Proactive by nature. You spot problems before they're reported. You suggest improvements nobody asked for. You hate sitting idle.
Clear communicator. Excellent written and verbal English. You can explain something simply, flag an issue professionally, and keep a client confident.
Detail-oriented without losing the big picture. You catch the small errors but still understand why they matter commercially.

Hospitality experience is a plus but absolutely not required. If you've never touched a hotel extranet but you're sharp, motivated, and learn fast — we want to hear from you. We will train the right person.

Application Requirements
Your application must include a short video introduction (2–3 minutes) covering:

Who you are and a bit about your background
Why you'd be a great fit for Rockenue specifically
A brief example of how you use AI or automation tools in your current or recent work

Applications without a video will not be considered. This is the single most important part of your application — show us your energy and personality.

Compensation
Competitive monthly salary (paid in GBP/USD), commensurate with experience. Long-term position with real room to grow as the portfolio scales.

title: Sales Operations Project Manager
description:
We’re looking for a strong, proactive Sales Operations Project Manager who can lead a sales pod, drive accountability across closers and setters, and maintain seamless communication between Sales, Growth Ops, and Tech Ops.

This is a leadership and coordination role — you are the operational backbone that ensures the pod performs at a high level, SOPs are followed, data is accurate, and every tea ---------- mber stays aligned and supported. If you’re the kind of person who naturally takes ownership, thrives in fast-paced environments, and knows how to communicate with clarity and authority, you’ll excel here.

This is a full-time position, requiring consistent availability from 8am to 4pm Eastern Time, with solid internet, power, and backup.

All applicants must complete DISC and IQ assessments before applying.

Detailed Job Description Breakdown

Pod Leadership & Daily Operations
• Lead and manage the daily workflow of closers and setters to ensure the sales pod operates smoothly.
• Run daily huddles, weekly check-ins, and pipeline reviews with clear agendas and follow-through.
• Manage pod-level projects (e.g., SOP rollouts, funnel changes, tracking fixes) end-to-end, ensuring on-time execution.
• Monitor pod performance, identify bottlenecks early, and escalate issues with solutions—not problems.
• Own the onboarding and offboarding process for new sales staff, including systems access, SOP orientation, and performance expectations.
• Ensure all pod members maintain clean calendars, proper availability, and updated CRM/task workflows.

Quality Assurance, Accountability & Compliance
• Hold closers and setters accountable for submitting accurate EODs, updating dispositions, and following SOPs consistently.
• Conduct quality checks on sales calls, calendar availability, CRM hygiene, and follow-up sequences.
• Audit all sales data regularly (bookings, show rates, sales, payments, renewals) to ensure accuracy before reporting.
• Flag performance gaps early and coach tea ---------- mbers with clarity, empathy, and firmness.
• Ensure all pod documentation, scorecards, and checklists are consistently maintained and up to date.

Cross-Department Coordination
• Work directly with Front-End Tech Ops, Back-End Automation Specialists, and Growth Ops to resolve tech issues, data discrepancies, or workflow breakdowns.
• Communicate updates clearly and quickly across all stakeholders — closers, setters, Growth Ops, SOMs, Tech Ops, and leadership.
• Serve as the bridge for multi-team projects (automation fixes, tracking repairs, new funnels, offer migrations).
• Maintain a strong working relationship with salespeople (who may be impatient or stressed under pressure) while keeping communication tight, professional, and steady.

Reporting & Performance Management
• Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports for leadership, ensuring all numbers are accurate and validated.
• Track KPIs across the full funnel: leads ? setters ? calls ? sales ? renewals.
• Support leadership in evaluating pod performance, identifying improvement plans, and implementing corrective action.
• Ensure the sales pod meets revenue, activity, and quality targets consistently.

Skills & Qualifications
• Project Management experience is mandatory.
(Comfortable managing timelines, action items, dependencies, and follow-through across multiple teams.)
• At least 1–2 years experience in sales operations, sales admin, sales coordination, or a similar leadership/ops role.
• Strong leadership and interpersonal skills — able to hold people accountable with firmness and empathy.
• Excellent verbal and written communication; comfortable leading huddles, giving feedback, and jumping on calls.
• Highly organized, detail-driven, and able to manage multiple moving parts in a fast-paced environment.
• Tech-savvy and comfortable navigating CRM, calendar tools, funnels, spreadsheets, and SOPs.
• Experience working with sales teams (closers/setters) is strongly preferred.
• Solution-oriented, proactive, and able to anticipate problems before they happen.
• Thrives in an environment where communication, speed, and ownership are non-negotiable.

If this sounds like you…

If you love driving clarity, supporting high-performance teams, and being the operational heartbeat of a fast-moving sales pod, we’d love to meet you. This role is perfect for someone who wants to grow into higher-level ops leadership while playing a mission-critical role in our sales organization.

About Sales Possible

At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching companies to build world-class marketing and sales ecosystems. We’re not just a sales team provider — we architect full-funnel systems, optimize performance, and support our clients with cutting-edge automation, AI, and operations infrastructure.

We work with high-growth education brands and expert coaches, helping them scale sustainably while delivering exceptional customer experiences.

We’re a fast-moving, tight-knit team that values ownership, proactive communication, and continuous improvement. If you want to work in an environment obsessed with growth, clarity, and performance — you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Backend Automation & AI Engineer
description:
Role Details

Tools We Use
• Automation: Zapier, Make (Integromat), HighLevel, ClickFunnels
• Reporting: Google Sheets, Looker Studio
• AI (secondary): ChatGPT API, custom workflows
• Backend: Firebase,  ----------

_________

About Sales Possible

At Sales Possible, we partner with mission-driven online coaching businesses to scale their impact through world-class marketing, sales systems, and team training.

We don’t just teach sales—we build sustainable systems that empower both businesses and the people behind them. Our clients range from expert coaches to fast-growing education brands, and we take pride in helping them turn vision into results.

We’re a tight-knit, fast-moving team that values clear communication, ownership, and continuous growth. If you thrive in a proactive, performance-driven environment and want to build cutting-edge reporting and automation systems, you’ll feel right at home here.

title: Remote Survey Programmer — Voxco / JavaScript Specialist (Market Research)
description:
Remote Survey Programmer — Voxco / JavaScript Specialist (Market Research)
Employer: TKW Research Group (Australia / New Zealand)
Location: Remote — Philippines
Engagement: Independent contractor, full-time equivalent (approx. 40 hrs/week)

The Package — What You’ll Receive

TKW Research Group is a long-standing Australian/New Zealand market research firm, and we’re looking for a Survey Programmer to join us on a long-term contract basis. This is a real partnership, not a transactional gig — and a chance to contribute to something genuinely new in market research. The package reflects that:

• Front-row seat on a voicebot R&D project — you’ll contribute directly to a survey voicebot we’re developing with a tier-1 AI platform partner. Real conversational-AI exposure on your CV.

• Base retainer: ?90,000 – ?140,000 PHP per month, commensurate with experience and voicebot project involvement, paid in arrears.

• 13th-month service bonus equivalent to one month’s base fee, paid every December.
• HMO allowance of up to ?50,000 per year, fully reimbursing premium private health cover for you and one dependent.
• Internet & equipment stipend of ?3,000 per month to support your home office setup.
• 10 days of paid leave per annum after 12 months of service, plus observed Australian public holidays without reduction to your base fee.

• Full training paid by TKW Research Group — structured onboarding plus ongoing platform training (Voxco and related tools) so you can perform at the highest level.

• Direct relationship with senior project managers in Australia/New Zealand — no middle layers, no agency markup.

About the Role

We’re looking for a Survey Programmer who can take complex questionnaires and turn them into clean, well-tested, production-ready survey scripts. You’ll own the scripting pipeline end-to-end: build, test, launch, deliver data.

Direct experience with Voxco is ideal, but we’re equally open to strong programmers from Forsta/Confirmit, Decipher, or Qualtrics who can pick up Voxco quickly — and we’ll fund the training to get you there. What matters more is that you can write real JavaScript (not just drag-and-drop survey builder logic) and that you sweat the details on QA.

The Voicebot Project — What Makes This Different

Alongside your core scripting work, you’ll contribute to something most survey programmers never get to touch: TKW Research Group is partnering with a tier-1 multinational AI platform provider to develop a survey voicebot — conversational AI that conducts genuine, natural-sounding interviews at scale.

Your role on the project: building the underlying survey logic, question structures, routing, and validation rules that the voicebot will run. You’re not expected to be an ML engineer — you’re the survey expert who makes sure the conversational layer asks the right questions in the right order and captures clean data on the other side.

This is genuine R&D work with commercial backing. You’ll be one of a handful of survey programmers globally with hands-on conversational-AI experience on your CV.

What You’ll Do
• Translate questionnaires (Word, PDF, client-supplied) into interactive online and CATI scripts.
• Build complex logic: skip patterns, routing, piping, randomisation, quota management, custom validations.
• Write JavaScript, HTML5 and CSS3 to extend platform functionality where the out-of-the-box tools fall short.
• Test thoroughly — logic, edge cases, mobile rendering, accessibility — before anything goes live.
• Export and format data (SPSS, CSV, Excel) for the analysis team.

What We’re Looking For
• 3+ years programming surveys in a market research agency, fieldwork house, or specialised BPO.
• Strong JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3 — you should be comfortable debugging in browser dev tools.
• Hands-on experience with at least one enterprise platform: Voxco, Forsta/Confirmit, Decipher, or Qualtrics.
• Bonus points for SPSS syntax, Python, SQL, or experience with conjoint/MaxDiff modules.
• Genuine interest in conversational AI — you don’t need ML experience, but you should be curious about how survey methodology and voice AI intersect.
• Reliable home office: high-spec PC, dual monitors preferred, stable internet plus a backup option.
• Strong written English — you’ll be communicating with project managers in Australia and New Zealand.

How to Apply

Send your CV plus a short note covering:
1. The platforms you’ve worked on.
2. The most complex script you’ve built.
3. Your earliest available start date.
4. A sentence or two on why the voicebot project interests you.
?

title: Senior Finance Manager
description:
About the Role

If you are detail-oriented, process-driven, and excited about working in a high-growth, tech-enabled finance environment—we’d love to hear from you.

Accounts Payable

 CPA/CA qualification is required

title: Mobile Frontend Engineer — RoseJob
description:
About RoseJob

  RoseJob is job search that actually feels good. Upload your resume, and our matching engine (7-agent resume extraction over ~25K live jobs from 30+ sources) builds your profile — then you swipe through hand-picked roles, each card showing the skills you bring vs. what the job needs. Think "match, bloom, hire." We're early, the backend is real and shipping, and the mobile app is where the product comes alive.

  The role

  You'll be our mobile engineer — owning the RoseJob app end to end, from gesture physics to App Store release. This is a craft role: a swipe app lives or dies on feel, so we care as much about a buttery card animation and a delightful loading state (falling petals ? blooming bouquet) as we do about clean architecture. You'll work directly with the founder and the backend/matching team, and you'll have real ownership over how the product looks, moves, and ships.

What you'll build

  - The swipe deck — fluid card gestures (? like, ? pass, ? super-like) with  ---------- ntum, snap-back, and stacking, built on Reanimated + Gesture Handler running on the UI thread at 60fps.
  - The resume-upload ? extraction ? profile-review ? deck flow (our hard invariant: no swiping without a resume), including the document picker, upload states, and the "petals while we think" extraction experience.
  - The match card itself — our killer feature — rendering skill-expression overlaps cleanly and legibly.
  - The saved "bouquet" (liked stack), job detail, and apply hand-off.
  - The whole release pipeline: EAS builds, TestFlight, App Store + Play submissions, OTA updates, and the unglamorous parts (signing, review rejections, staged rollouts).

  Our stack

  Expo · React Native · TypeScript · expo-router · Reanimated · Gesture Handler · Zustand — talking to a FastAPI backend. You don't need to have used every piece, but you should be at home in this world.

  You'll thrive here if you have

  - Shipped real React Native apps to both the App Store and Google Play — and owned the releases, not just the features. (Send us links; we'll open them on a device.)
  - Strong TypeScript + React fundamentals (hooks, state, render performance).
  - Real fluency with gestures and animations — Reanimated worklets, Gesture Handler, 60fps interactions. This role is unusually animation-heavy; show us something that moves.
  - Comfort with the platform realities: iOS vs. Android differences, permissions, push (APNs/FCM), deep links, and surviving App Store review.
  - Enough native depth (Swift/Kotlin) to write or debug a native module and read a crash stack trace when Expo can't cover it.
  - An eye for polish and UX craft — you sweat the empty states, haptics, and the 100ms that makes a swipe feel right.

  Nice to have

  - Consumer / B2C app experience (especially anything swipe-, feed-, or card-based).
  - EAS Build/Submit + OTA update experience.
  - Animation/motion-design sensibility (Lottie, custom transitions).
  - Accessibility (VoiceOver/TalkBack) and i18n.
  - Comfort being the first/only mobile hire — scoping, shipping, and owning quality without a big team around you.

title: Full-Stack Growth Marketer (Meta + Google + AI Web) — AU Agency, Trades/construction Niche
description:
ABOUT BUILD UP FLOW
Build Up Flow (BUF) is an Australian growth agency working with trades, construction, and design businesses. Right now we're focused on the AU market. As we grow, we're expanding into the US and other English-speaking markets.
We're not a one-service vendor. We're an integrated growth partner running paid ads, recruitment, and systems for clients who care about pipeline, not impressions.
We move fast. We're direct. If you like fluff or hide behind reports, this isn't your seat.

THE ROLE
This is a rare seat. You'll own the full demand engine for a portfolio of BUF clients: Meta ads, Google Ads, SEO, and the websites the SEO lives on. Most of our clients need a proper foundation built (site, keywords, technical SEO, Search/PMax/Meta campaigns) then ongoing optimisation across all of it.
We want one sharp operator who can run paid social and paid search, build the site under it with AI, and treat the whole thing as one funnel instead of separate channels.
You'll use AI hard across everything: creative briefs, audience research, ad copy and hooks, keyword research, content briefs, on-page optimisation, schema, and full site builds (Lovable, v0, Bolt, Claude, GHL's AI builder, WordPress + AI, whatever fits). We don't want a traditional web developer. Your value is knowing what to build, wiring SEO into the structure, and making it look and convert like a pro did it, not writing custom code.
This is a part-time role starting at around 15-20 hours/week across our current client load. We add new clients regularly, and your hours, pay, and account load grow with us. We want someone who treats BUF like their own agency, not a side gig.

WAGE / SALARY
$10-20 USD/hour based on experience.

YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES
Paid: Build, launch, and manage Meta ad campaigns end-to-end (CBO, ABO, Advantage+, pick the right tool for the job)
Build and manage Google Ads campaigns (Search, Performance Max, Display, retargeting)
Run structured testing across creatives, hooks, audiences, and offers
Brief and iterate on ad creative (we have separate creative support, but you direct the brief)
Track lead quality, not just CPL

SEO and web:
Full-stack SEO: keyword research, on-page, technical, content briefs, internal linking, schema
Use AI to build and iterate on client websites fast, with SEO baked in from the structure up
Map keyword clusters to site architecture and content plans
Set up tracking properly (GA4, GTM, conversion tracking, call tracking)

Across both:
Use AI tools daily for real work, not novelty
Set up and run campaigns through GoHighLevel (pipelines, automations, reporting)
Document your process and updates in Notion (our whole knowledge base lives there)
Weekly reporting with a clear point of view on what's working, what's not, and what's next
Communicate directly with the BUF team via Slack / Loom / call

MUST-HAVES

3+ years actively managing Meta ad spend (don't apply if you've only "assisted")
3+ years managing Google Ads (Search + PMax minimum)
Strong SEO knowledge: technical, on-page, and content
Proven track record managing AU, US, or UK client accounts
Proven results in lead-gen campaigns, not e-commerce only
Proven ability to build professional websites using AI tools (show us sites you've shipped using Lovable, v0, Bolt, GHL, WordPress + AI, or similar)
Strong taste: you can spot AI-slop and produce output that looks like a designer built it
GoHighLevel: confident setting up sub-accounts, pipelines, automations, sites, funnels, and reporting. You can build it, not just use it
Notion: comfortable working inside it daily for SOPs, tracking, and documentation
AI tools: Claude (web + Projects), Claude Code, ChatGPT used daily for real work
Strong English written communication
Reliable internet + ability to overlap with Australian business hours (AEST)
You can read a dashboard and form an opinion, not just describe what you see

BONUS

Experience with trades, home services, solar, or construction clients
Experience writing direct-response ad copy
Local SEO and Google Business Profile optimisation experience
Familiar with n8n or other automation tools
Has built or contributed to a GHL snapshot/template

HOW TO APPLY
Start your application with the word "FULLSTACK" so we know you read this.

Send:
Your best paid result on each side (one Meta, one Google) with numbers: spend, CPL, conversion rate, ROAS where you have it
2-3 examples of ads you've run (screenshots or links)
A site you've built using AI tools, link it, tell us which tools you used
One ranking improvement you've driven (keyword, position movement, timeframe)
The AI tools you use weekly and one specific way you use them in your work
Your rate and weekly availability

Applications without the keyword get deleted.

title: Personal Support Contractor
description:
We are seeking a highly organised, detail-oriented and proactive Personal Support Contractor to provide ongoing administrative and bookkeeping support across a range of personal, trust and property-related matters.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working independently, managing multiple priorities, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

The successful candidate will be trusted with confidential information and play a key role in maintaining accurate records and keeping administrative processes running smoothly.

Key Responsibilities:

Trust Distribution Administration
Prepare and maintain trust distribution schedules and records.
Coordinate documentation and information required for trust-related activities.
Track key dates, actions and outstanding items.

Personal Bookkeeping (Across Multiple Entities)
Maintain accurate financial records across personal, trust and investment entities.
Categorise transactions and update bookkeeping systems.
Assist with year-end preparation and accountant requests.

Rental Property Administration
Maintain property-related records and documentation.
Track rental income, expenses and key property information.
Liaise with property managers and service providers as required.

Bank, Loan & Credit Card Reconciliations
Reconcile bank accounts, loan accounts and credit cards.
Investigate and resolve discrepancies.
Maintain organised supporting documentation.

Document & Record Management
Organise and maintain digital filing systems.
Ensure records are accurately stored and easily retrievable.
Manage document version control and archival processes.

Liaison & Information Chasing
Follow up with accountants, property managers, banks, lenders and other third parties.
Track outstanding requests and ensure timely responses.
Coordinate information gathering for various administrative and financial matters.

Simple Reporting
Prepare straightforward financial and administrative reports.
Summarise account balances, cash flow information and outstanding action items.
Provide regular status updates and progress reporting.

About You
You will ideally have:
Previous experience in bookkeeping, administration, executive assistance or financial administration.
Strong attention to detail and high levels of accuracy.
Excellent written English communication skills.
Experience reconciling financial accounts.
Strong organisational and follow-up skills.
Ability to manage confidential information with discretion.
Intermediate to advanced Excel or Google Sheets skills.
Experience working remotely and independently.
Experience with trusts, investment entities and rental property administration.
Experience supporting business owners, investors or high-net-worth individuals.

To Apply
Please submit:
A brief cover letter outlining your relevant experience.
Your CV/resume.

Only shortlisted contractors will be contacted.

title: Virtual Executive Assistant
description:
I'm the founder of a digital growth marketing company that helps mission-driven founders scale their businesses with more ease & less overwhelm - and I'm looking for a small team of highly-skilled Executive Assistants to help take over the marketing and management tasks that make a huge difference in the lives of the people that hire us.

I'm looking for a max of 5 amazing executive assistants available for part-time (potential for full time) with a salary starting at $500 USD per month and with long term success and robust training, moving up to $700 USD per month as an emerging leader in the company.

If you have the following skill sets, I encourage you to apply:

REQUIRED EXPERIENCE:
- Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in Digital Marketing, Video Editing, Social Media Management, Content Creation, and Executive Assistant Work (Administrative Tasks, Calendar & Email Management, etc)
You’re trusted to manage marketing deliverables while providing high-level executive support, keeping priorities organized, systems moving, and leadership focused on growth instead of day-to-day execution. You've done it all from managing schedules to email & calendar management. You have a meticulous eye for detail where Prioritization and Organization are your love language

- 1+ years of experience in Sales Funnels and Shopify
You're a professional who is passionate about helping changemakers simplify their lives by setting up systems that will allow them to operate their businesses with more ease and less overwhelm. You have experience with sales funnel and CRM software

- 1+ years of experience advertising on platforms like Instagram, Facebook, Tik Tok, within a Marketing team consisting of Paid Acquisition, Organic Marketing, Customer Retention, & Product Marketing
You're an emerging leader who has the potential to head a team in the future that will report directly to you. Having leadership experience is a MAJOR plus

- Creating SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures)
You’ll ensure each business function has detailed documentation for repeatable and scalable growth if it isn't already place. If you see gaps, you take the initiative to fill them and produce results without anyone knowing there was an issue (yea, all that and a bag of chips :-)

- Experience working at a digital/online product/platform: You’re familiar with online courses, digital products & platforms and popular tools in the business space ranging from landing page builders and email service providers to CRMs, and course hosting platforms aren’t foreign to you (We teach tools like Shopify, Convertkit, Kajabi, Teachable, Learnworlds, Calendly, etc)

ABOUT YOU:
- “You down with SOP? Yeah you know me!”—You’re an advocate for SOPs (Standard Operating Procedures) to make our systems repeatable & scalable.
- Your love language is processes & predictable, meteoric growth. We never question company progress towards the quarterly and yearly goals because you keep real-time metrics for all projects.
- You’re aware of new software and can look at current systems and identify how to automate tasks a company has been doing manually for months.
- You’re addicted to digital processes & automation in the world of social media because you love building connections in the digital space and you’ve seen how powerful it can be in business growth
- You’re driven by metrics & tracking. You know that you can’t improve something unless you measure it, so you develop ways to measure performance so your company is always predictably improving.
- Timeliness and deadlines are your jam. When we have a team call at 10:00am, you show up at 9:55am. When the deadline is Tuesday at 5:30pm, you have it done Monday.
- You overcommunicate clearly and confidently. If you agree/disagree with something, you communicate that without hesitation or overthinking. You’re no stranger to asking why we do things a certain way to ensure we’re intentional with our resources.
- You’re a leader and team player. Ego? What ego? If something needs to be done, you dive in to make sure all critical tasks are accounted and cared for.

If you’re nodding your head “Yes” to the above, proceed below to apply.

PERKS:
I'm committed to setting our staff up for success with access to trainings that will certify your skills in:
- Task & Calendar Management
- Launch/Sales Strategy Planning
- Sales Funnel Tech Setup (For E-Commerce or Service Based Businesses)
- Social Media Management
- Digital Media Marketing (Including Email and SMS Marketing)
- Lead Generation

After one week of training and assessment, you're officially certified under our internal training program, which means hitting the ground running with your new skills. Perks include:
- Part time work hours
- Work from anywhere in the world (as long as your internet signal is strong and computer is fast)
- Starting Salary at $500 USD a month ($6000 USD annual)
- Internal certification program to enhance your professional skills in your career (I have helped clients amass at least $10M from the same digital growth marketing strategies I will require you to learn)

Interested? Please reply to this post with the secret keyword "APPLES" so I know you can pay attention to detail - from there, I'll let you know next steps if you qualify.

title: Authorization Specialist – ABA Healthcare (CentralReach + US Insurance Calls)

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
Senior Data Engineer (Data Engineering, Data Warehousing & Analytics)
Position Summary

The IT Data Engineer is responsible for designing, building, maintaining, and modernizing the organization's data platform and reporting ecosystem. This role serves as the primary technical owner of data engineering initiatives, working closely with executive leadership, business stakeholders, and development teams to transform operational and third-party data into trusted, actionable business insights.

The position combines data engineering, data warehousing, reporting, and business partnership responsibilities. The successful candidate will help drive the organization's transition toward a modern, data-driven culture while supporting both current and future analytics initiatives.
Key Responsibilities
• Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines integrating internal and external data sources.
• Build scalable ETL/ELT processes and data quality controls.
• Maintain and enhance dimensional models, fact tables, and dimension tables.
• Create mapping tables and conformed dimensions for cross-system reporting.
• Develop and support Power BI dashboards, reports, and datasets.
• Partner with business stakeholders to gather requirements and deliver analytics solutions.
• Support migration from SQL Server-based warehousing to cloud-based platforms.
• Monitor and optimize reporting, query, and warehouse performance.
• Serve as a trusted advisor on data strategy and analytics initiatives.

Initial Priorities (First 6–12 Months)
• Learn business operations, products, inventory, and distribution processes.
• Integrate new third-party datasets into the data warehouse.
• Expand Power BI reporting and analytics capabilities.
• Improve data quality and standardization across systems.
• Develop a roadmap for cloud-based modernization and future data architecture.
Technical Environment
Current: SQL Server, Power BI, Star Schema Modeling, ETL Processes, Operational Systems.

Future: Microsoft Fabric, Azure Data Lake, Cloud Data Platforms, Modern Data Warehousing Architectures, Advanced Analytics Solutions.
Success Measures
• Strong understanding of business operations and stakeholder needs.
• Successful integration of new data sources.
• Increased reporting adoption and business value.
• Improved data quality and trust in reporting.
• Progress toward modernization and cloud adoption.
• Establishment of scalable data engineering practices.
   (Databricks is a plus)

Overall Mission
Build and evolve the organization's data platform, integrate new data sources, improve analytics and reporting capabilities, and help transform the company into a data-driven organization through modern data engineering and business intelligence solutions.

title: Outbound telemarketing & email Manager for GHL based team
description:
We are looking for a highly organised and proactive Outbound Marketing Manager to help build, manage, and improve a referral-development system for a growing Australian psychology and mental health business.

This role is ideal for someone with strong experience in GoHighLevel / GHL, outbound marketing operations, CRM management, appointment-setting campaigns, lead management, team coordination, and process improvement.

his is not a basic VA role. We are looking for someone who can help manage the operating system for outbound referral development.

Main Responsibilities

You will be responsible for helping manage the outbound referral development process inside GoHighLevel, including:

Managing lead lists and referral partner databases
Organising contacts by category, location, profession, source, status, and priority
Building and maintaining GHL pipelines
Managing appointment-setting workflows
Tracking outreach activity by staff and/or AI agents
Monitoring calls, conversations, booked appointments, attended meetings, referrals received, and conversion outcomes
Helping manage telemarketers / appointment setters
Reviewing CRM data for accuracy and completeness
Ensuring follow-up tasks are created and completed
Helping build email and SMS follow-up sequences
Managing reactivation campaigns for old or inactive referral partners
Helping improve call scripts, email templates, objection handling, and workflow documentation
Preparing weekly performance reports
Identifying bottlenecks in the referral-development process
Helping the business owner improve the system over time

You will also help coordinate a growing human and AI outbound team, including human telemarketers and AI outbound agents.

Required Skills

You must have strong skills in:

GoHighLevel / GHL
CRM management
Pipeline management
Lead database organisation
Appointment-setting workflows
Outbound marketing operations

Email and SMS campaign management
Reporting and KPI tracking
Team coordination
Process documentation
Attention to detail
Clear written English
Reliable communication
Highly Preferred Skills

Experience with any of the following would be very valuable:

Managing outbound appointment setters
Working with healthcare, mental health, NDIS, medical, allied health, legal, or professional services businesses
Building referral partner campaigns
GHL automations
GHL forms, calendars, workflows, tags, pipelines, opportunities, tasks, and reporting
AI voice agents or AI outreach tools
Call tracking and call summaries
Lead enrichment
Zapier / Make
Google Sheets

Email deliverability and campaign setup
SOP writing
Key Outcomes for This Role

The goal of this role is to help build a reliable referral-development machine that produces:

More referral partner conversations
More booked meetings
More attended meetings
More active referral partners
More client referrals
Better CRM data
Better follow-up
Better reporting
A stronger human and AI outbound team
KPIs We Care About

You will help track and improve:

Calls made
Conversations
Decision-maker conversations
Appointments booked
Appointment attendance rate
Referral partners activated
Referrals received
Clients converted
Revenue generated
Cost per appointment
Cost per referral
CRM completion rate
Follow-up completion rate
Who This Role Suits

This role suits someone who is:

Systems-minded
Very organised
Comfortable managing a CRM
Strong with GHL
Good at improving processes
Confident managing outreach campaigns
Interested in growing into an operations or marketing management role
Comfortable working with a health/psychology business where professionalism and confidentiality matter
Work Arrangement

This role will begin as a paid trial. If successful, it may become a longer-term position.

The role will likely start part-time and may grow depending on performance.

You will work remotely and communicate through online tools such as email, GHL, Google Workspace, and video meetings where required.

Application Instructions

To apply, please answer the following questions:

Describe your experience with GoHighLevel / GHL.
What parts of GHL have you personally set up or managed?
Have you managed outbound campaigns or appointment-setting teams before?
Have you worked with healthcare, NDIS, mental health, legal, allied health, or professional services businesses before?
What CRM pipelines, automations, workflows, or reporting dashboards have you created?
What is your expected hourly rate?
Are you available for a paid trial task?
Please include examples of GHL systems, campaigns, dashboards, workflows, or SOPs you have helped create, if available.

title: TikTok Shop Lead Operator — $2,000 base + 25% uncapped profit share + year-end bonus (US LLC handled)
description:
This is a reposted job with revised compensation base & updated job title. 
Compensation at a glance
$2,000/month base, paid weekly via Wise (guaranteed floor)
25% of monthly Net Profit you personally generate, paid monthly, uncapped
15% override on the net production of any helpers we hire under you
10% year-end bonus on your annual personal Net Profit, paid every January 31

Real comp examples: $3,250/mo at $5K NP · $5,750/mo at $15K NP · $9,500/mo at $30K NP (full math + worked example in the Compensation section below)

This pays 2–3x what every comparable TikTok Shop role on OnlineJobs.ph offers. Keep reading to see why we can pay this and what we expect in return.

Essentially: you are building an entire TikTok Shop affiliate company from the ground up, with full financial backing and US LLC infrastructure provided by us. You bring the operational skill. We bring the capital, legal structure, and US-side resources.

About this role:
This is not a typical VA role. This is a lead operator role for someone who wants to build a real TikTok Shop affiliate business and earn like a senior tea ---------- mber with real upside, not a flat-rate VA.

We're a US-based holding company launching a TikTok Shop affiliate operation. We provide the infrastructure — US LLC, EIN, US bank account, US-resident device/IP setup, US shipping addresses, working capital, and TikTok Shop seller verification. You build and run the operation. Product research, account warming, scripts, content production, posting, optimization, scaling. When the time is right, you propose new hires and we approve, fund, and onboard them under our LLC — and you earn a cut of what they produce.

You're the operator on the ground. The owner is hands-on with strategy, sets all base pay, approves all hires and fires, and owns final say on direction. You drive execution day-to-day with real autonomy inside that framework.

If you've ever run a successful TikTok Shop account and thought "I should be doing this with a real company behind me instead of $2/hr for an agency" — this is that opportunity, with the US LLC infrastructure already solved.

What success looks like
Month 1: Two TikTok Shop accounts warmed and posting 5+ videos/day each, with first commission revenue landed
Month 3: Three accounts running, 10+ videos/day total, $3,000+/month in net profit, your share clearing $750/month on top of base
Month 6: Established daily playbook, $15,000+/month in net profit, your share at $3,750/month on top of base (total ~$5,750/month)
Month 12: Scaled operation with 1–2 helpers you've trained, $25,000+/month net profit possible, your share clearing $6,000+/month plus base plus year-end bonus

Day-to-day responsibilities
You own day-to-day execution. That includes:

Product research — daily Kalodata / Shoplus / FastMoss workflow, your own filter stack, daily shortlist of 10 candidates, scoring top-performing videos for replicability

Sample sourcing — submit free sample requests and refundable sample purchases via TikTok Shop Creator Center to our US shipping addresses

Content production — generate 5 script variants per winning product, brief US-based on-camera recorders with structured shot lists, edit raw clips in CapCut

UGC creator hiring — you can brief and hire US-based on-camera talent through  ----------  (you brief them, they record, you edit and post — they don't need to keep the product). Budget for this comes out of operating expenses, approved by us.

Account operations — stagger-post 5–15 videos/day across 2–3 TikTok accounts (each on its own isolated device/IP), tag every video's affiliate product from Showcase before publishing, manage comment moderation and account hygiene

Pre-followed account acquisition — purchasing TikTok Shop-eligible accounts (5,000+ followers) is a legitimate part of the playbook. You'll source candidate accounts and submit them for our approval before purchase.

Drop-off & hook analytics — pull swipe-away / retention curves from TikTok analytics on every video, identify exactly where viewers leave, and use that data to rebuild hooks that hold attention past the first 3 seconds

Performance management — track 24-hour and 72-hour performance per video in shared Google Sheets, flag winners and losers per decision rules you build (with our sign-off)

Team training (when we hire) — when we approve a new hire, you train them, oversee their daily output, and earn a percentage of their net production on top of your base and your own profit share

Must-haves
1+ year hands-on TikTok Shop affiliate experience (you've personally operated accounts, not just managed someone else's)

Working knowledge of Kalodata, Shoplus, or FastMoss — you can name the filter stack you'd use to find a trending product without us prompting

Hook analytics fluency — you regularly look at where viewers swipe away from your videos and use that data to rebuild hooks. You can explain a specific time you fixed a low-retention video by studying the drop-off curve.

Exceptional written English (your scripts go to US viewers; your briefs go to US recorders)

Proven ability to ship volume — 5+ videos/day, every workday, without quality drop

Data-driven mindset — you read GMV growth, commission rate, and affiliate count signals fast and act on them

2+ years remote work experience with verifiable OnlineJobs.ph or Upwork references

Proficient in Slack, ClickUp or Notion, Google Sheets, CapCut, ChatGPT

4+ hour overlap with US Mountain Time

Reliable computer, fast internet, backup power

Comfortable with daily WhatsApp check-ins (morning plan + end-of-day recap) and a weekly direction call

Operator mindset — you treat this like real work that compounds, not a 9-to-5

Nice-to-haves
Prior experience under a US-LLC-backed structure operating US-market TikTok Shop accounts
Experience editing UGC-style product review videos
Experience hiring and briefing UGC creators (HourlyUGC, Upwork, Billo, etc.)
Experience buying and warming pre-followed TikTok accounts safely
Hands-on with Claude Code for scripting and automation tasks
Familiarity with US residential proxy / remote-desktop tooling (AnyDesk, RustDesk, BrightData)
Experience running multiple isolated social accounts without crossing fingerprints
Prior experience training and overseeing junior tea ---------- mbers

Resources we provide
US LLC, EIN, US bank account, US shipping addresses, TikTok Shop seller verification
Working capital for samples, account purchases, and initial creator briefs
Tools budget: Kalodata, Shoplus or FastMoss, ChatGPT Plus, Claude Code, CapCut Pro, any other tool you make a case for
US-based UGC talent budget via HourlyUGC and similar platforms
Daily strategy access to the owner via WhatsApp, weekly direction call

Compensation
Base salary: $2,000/month ($11.55/hr × 40 hrs/week), paid weekly via Wise — your guaranteed floor

Personal profit share: 25% of monthly Net Profit you personally generate, paid monthly, uncapped, for as long as you are with the company

Net Profit definition: Gross affiliate commission revenue minus all operating costs, including your $2,000/month base salary, contractor pay (UGC creators, helpers), software subscriptions (Kalodata, CapCut, ChatGPT, Claude Code, etc.), sample purchases, account purchases, banking fees, and US LLC overhead. Your base is paid by the company as a normal expense — it does NOT come out of your 25% share.

Worked example (a $5,000 Net Profit month):

Gross affiliate commission revenue: $8,500
Less your base salary: ?$2,000
Less samples & account purchases: ?$500
Less software (Kalodata, CapCut, ChatGPT, Claude Code): ?$300
Less UGC creator contractor pay: ?$500
Less banking and LLC overhead: ?$200
= Net Profit: $5,000

Your 25% share: $1,250
Your total monthly comp: $2,000 base + $1,250 share = $3,250
Comp at higher Net Profit tiers (base always paid separately on top):
At $5,000/month Net Profit: $2,000 base + $1,250 share = $3,250/month total

At $15,000/month Net Profit: $2,000 base + $3,750 share = $5,750/month total

At $30,000/month Net Profit: $2,000 base + $7,500 share = $9,500/month total

Team override (when we hire helpers under you): When the company hires a helper under your direction, the helper's net production is split three ways:

Helper: 25% of their own net production (same share structure as yours)

You (operator override): 15% of the helper's net production, paid monthly alongside your personal profit share

Company: the remaining 60%

The 15% override equals 20% of the company's 75% cut — a meaningful piece of every helper you train and retain. The better you train them and the longer they stay productive, the larger your monthly override grows. Worked example: if a helper generates $5,000/month in net production, helper earns $1,250, you earn an additional $750 override on top of your own profit share, company keeps $3,000.

Year-end bonus: At fiscal year-end (December 31), you receive a bonus equal to 10% of the personal net profit you generated for the company during that fiscal year, paid by January 31.

Example: if your personal accounts generated $120,000 in net profit across the calendar year, your year-end bonus = $12,000, on top of all monthly profit shares already paid

This is designed to reward staying through the full year — someone who's accrued meaningful YTD profit has a real reason to finish the year strong rather than walk in November

Bonus applies only if you're with the company on December 31 of that year

Performance floor: After the first 3 months (during which we are getting the process / pipeline up and running):
Your personal monthly net profit generation must clear your monthly base pay ($2,000). If you fall below base for 3 consecutive months OR any 4 months within a rolling 6-month window, the role is terminated unless we judge that exceptional extenuating circumstances apply (which we will weigh case by case).

Why this beats every comparable listing:
Typical TikTok Shop VA on OnlineJobs.ph: $800–1,500/month flat, no profit share, no bonus
Senior pod operator with commission: $1,500–2,000/month + 5% personal sales (~$1,750/month at our target revenue)
Most "profit share" listings: $2,000 base + 10?ove a high margin threshold (~$2,250/month at our target revenue)
This role at $15K/month personal net profit: $5,750/month + 15% override on any helpers you train (~$750/mo per $5K-producing helper) + year-end bonus accruing toward $12K+ — total annualized comp ceiling far above any flat-rate or pod role on this platform

Transparency: Net Profit is calculated from a Google Sheet you'll have read access to, updated daily. Every line item is visible. No black box.

How we work together
Daily WhatsApp check-ins. Each morning you send a short message with your plan for the day. Each evening you send a recap of what you completed. Takes 5 minutes total. This is non-negotiable.

Weekly direction call. One scheduled call per week (30–45 min) to discuss overall progress, priorities, and any decisions that need our sign-off.

Final decisions rest with us — all hiring, firing, base pay, account purchases, and revenue policy decisions default to the owner. You propose, recommend, build the case. We approve.

You have real autonomy inside that framework on day-to-day execution: content choices, hook iterations, posting cadence, niche pivots, product picks, contractor briefs.

Long-term mindset — the owner's other business has retained OnlineJobs.ph hires for 2+ years. We invest in operators who invest in us.

How to apply
Follow all application instructions below carefully.

In your application message, answer every question below, numbered, in order:

List every TikTok Shop affiliate account you've personally operated. Include handle and approximate monthly revenue at peak.

What product research tool do you use? Walk us through your exact filter stack — the specific filters, in order, that you'd apply right now to find a trending product worth promoting this week.

Describe one specific time you looked at a TikTok video's swipe-away curve, identified where viewers were dropping off, and rebuilt the hook to fix it. What did the original hook say, what did the data show, and what did you change it to?

Pick one TikTok Shop product you'd promote this week. Tell us (a) the product, (b) why you'd promote it (data-backed), and (c) the exact 3-second hook you'd open with.

Describe one independent operational decision you've made on a TikTok Shop account — what was the call, what was the data behind it, what was the outcome?

What's your current US Mountain Time overlap availability?

What tools are you currently paying for out of your own pocket?

Do you currently operate any personal TikTok Shop accounts? (Yes/No — full disclosure required if yes)

At the very end of your application, in the final line, type the word "Kalodata" so we know you read this entire post.

For applicants who do follow these instructions, we'll shortlist the strongest candidates for a paid 2-hour trial task ($16 paid via Wise), and then interview finalists directly with the owner.

We're hiring one person and we'd rather wait three weeks for the right one than three days for the wrong one.

If you've ever wanted to run a US-market TikTok Shop business with the LLC, banking, and infrastructure handled — this is your shot.

title: Executive GoHighLevel (GHL) Masterclass and Architecture Consultant
description:
We are looking for an elite, agency-level GoHighLevel (GHL) Architect and Paid Media Expert for a high-intensity, one-time strategic consulting project.

Instead of managing a team, you will be consulting directly with the agency’s leadership (CEO and COO). Your primary mission is to audit our core architecture, troubleshoot specific bottlenecked pipelines, and teach us your "insider secrets" so we can flawlessly manage, scale, and replicate our CRM builds moving forward.

We know top-tier talent comes at a premium, and we are fully prepared to pay your professional hourly rate for a true master of the platform.

---

The Mission & Scope of Work

This is a one-time, high-level consulting engagement (starting with roughly a 3-hour deep dive) focused on knowledge transfer and architectural diagnosis.

1. Advanced Architecture Audit & "Speed-to-Lead"

* Diagnose Lead Routing: Audit our current GHL filing architecture to discover exactly why leads aren't being reported to business owners fast enough, and show us how to fix it.
* Maximize Automation: Teach us how to implement missed high-conversion features, specifically elite "speed-to-lead" auto-texts, multi-channel follow-ups, and pre-qualifying lead structures.

2. Immediate Client-Specific Problem Solving

*Review and help us finalize a complex CRM build, correctly connect all social assets, and ensure our Meta Ads campaign is flawlessly integrated for launch.
* Troubleshoot our Meta Ads-to-GHL link to ensure the business owner receives lead data instantly.
* Advanced Data Visualization: Guide us on how to build sharp, visible client dashboards that pull and visualize data from Google Analytics and Google Search Console right alongside the GHL lead pipeline.

3. Framework for Scalability

The Blueprint: Teach us how to take our successful builds (like the lawyer CRM) and turn them into flawless, repeatable snapshots/templates that we can deploy identically for future service or medical clients.

---

Professional Qualifications

Elite GHL Mastery: Deep, insider knowledge of GHL architecture, snapshots, custom values, and advanced workflow triggers.
Niche Paid Media Expertise: Proven success running and integrating **Meta Ads** for service niches (experience with Personal Injury Lawyers is a massive plus).
The Ability to Teach: You aren't just a button-clicker; you can articulate *why* things work, explain complex workflows clearly, and pass your strategic knowledge onto agency executives.

---

Logistics & Structure

The Engagement: An intensive, one-time strategic consulting block (approx. 3 hours to start).
The Dynamic: You will be leading a session with the agency's COO (who will handle internal team execution moving forward) and the CEO.
Compensation: Open / Negotiable. We are looking for the best of the best and will match the hourly ask of a verified expert.

---

How to Apply

If you have the "real, studied, and tested" GHL expertise to take our agency's core architecture to the next level, please apply with your **requested hourly rate** and provide the following three items:

1. A Brief Intro: A very short, punchy summary of why you are a GHL expert.
2. A Relevant System You've Built: Describe a specific system or architecture you have built in GHL that directly resonates with what we are trying to build (e.g., advanced speed-to-lead, custom snapshots, or complex legal/service pipelines).
3. Your Portfolio: A link or documentation showing your past GHL builds, workflows, or dashboard setups.

title: Video Editor (PHP 369/HR - USD $6/HR | PART Time)
description:
Hey!

We are looking for a Video Editor ROCKSTAR who will turn our long form content into high-performing short form clips and weekly YouTube videos.

We are a marketing and education company that works with English speakers and already have a team of high-performing individuals from the Philippines. Mastery of the English language is a requirement and will be tested prior to hiring.

BENEFITS INCLUDE:
- 100% remote
- Weekly pay
- USD $6 / Hour = 369 PHP / Hour
- Paid training
- No work during the weekends
- Opportunity to go from part time to full time
- We pay for the AI and creative tools you will use (Adobe, Descript, Opus Clip, Canva Pro)
- Grow with a team that values initiative, speed, and high standards

WHAT YOU WILL DO:

Short form (main focus):
- Take long form content (webinars, trainings, YouTube videos, podcasts) and turn it into short form clips for Reels, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and LinkedIn
- Add attention-grabbing hooks, captions, emojis, B-roll, overlays, and pattern interrupts
- Create short testimonial videos from coaching calls, client interviews, and other raw footage
- Organize and manage video assets so clips are easy to find and reuse

Long form & channel support:
- Edit 1 piece of long form content per week (highlight key  ---------- nts, add B-roll, overlays, and graphics)
- Publish that long form video on YouTube (upload, title, description, tags, end screens, cards)
- Create and upload custom YouTube thumbnails that drive clicks
- Handle one-off editing requests as needed (simple promos, clips, intros/outros, etc.)
- Analyze performance of published videos in YouTube Studio (CTR, views, watch time, retention) and provide simple reports and recommendations

Collaboration & cadence:
- Meet once a week with the owner of the company for feedback, direction, and priorities
- Join 1 monthly content planning session with the team
- Use AI tools (that we pay for) to speed up editing, transcription, captioning, and repurposing while still polishing by hand

Expected volume (part-time ~20 hours per week):
- 20–30 finished short form clips per week (30–90 seconds each)
- 1 edited and published long form YouTube video per week
- Ongoing testimonial edits and one-off requests as needed

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

Experience & portfolio:
- Proven experience editing short form content from long form videos
- Strong portfolio showing:
Before/after edits from long form to short form
YouTube videos you have edited and thumbnails you have designed
Short testimonial / social proof videos (a plus)

Tool competence (we provide licenses):
You will work daily inside:
- Adobe Premiere Pro (primary editor)
- Basic Adobe After Effects (simple animations, lower thirds, transitions)
- Descript (transcription, rough cuts, repurposing)
- Opus Clip (AI clipping & short-form ideas)
- Canva Pro (YouTube thumbnails & simple graphics)
- YouTube Studio (publishing & analytics)
- Google Drive, Google Docs/Sheets (file management, tracking)
- ClickUp (task management)
- Slack (communication)

You don’t need to be a master of every tool on day one, but you must already be strong in at least one pro editor (Premiere / Final Cut / DaVinci) and be able to learn the rest quickly.
Having experience with video editing is a requirement to be considered for this role.

We will ask you what your favorite dog breed is, answer chowchow in your application so we know you have read the full job posting.

We will start with relatively limited hours (around 20 hours per week to begin) with the opportunity for it to grow as our relationship develops.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Mastery of the English language (spoken and written)
- Strong project management skills and ability to hit volume targets consistently
- Comfortable reviewing analytics and making basic performance recommendations
- Quiet environment to work

TECH REQUIREMENTS (MINIMUM):
- Computer: Windows 10/11 PC or Mac (released within the last 4–5 years)
CPU: Intel i7 / Ryzen 7 / Apple M1 or better (or equivalent)
RAM: Minimum 16 GB (32 GB strongly preferred)
Storage: At least 512 GB SSD, with 150+ GB free for active projects
GPU: Dedicated graphics card with 4 GB VRAM or more (e.g., NVIDIA GTX 1660 / RTX series or equivalent). For Mac, M1/M2 with integrated GPU is fine.

- Software capability:
Must be able to run Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects smoothly while having multiple Chrome tabs, cloud drives, and AI tools (Descript / Opus Clip / etc.) open.

-High Speed Internet: Stable enough for uploading/downloading large video files and live calls without interruption
Reliable backup option (mobile data or secondary ISP) is a plus

- Other:
Quality headphones and microphone for edit reviews and team calls
Quiet, dedicated workspace suitable for focused editing work

If your current setup struggles with Adobe Premiere or AI tools like Descript / Opus Clip, please upgrade first before applying. This role requires fast, reliable hardware to hit our weekly volume targets.

SCHEDULE:
You must be available to work 4 hours per day, following this schedule: 3 PM – 7 PM Philippines time.

title: Sales Operations Specialist
description:
About Ultra Labs
Ultra Labs is a U.S.-based Business Incubator/Accelerator focused on software and tech-driven businesses. We are searching for a proactive, highly organized Sales Operations Specialist to support and strengthen our sales operations across multiple projects and brands.

The Sales Operations Specialist will take ownership of sales data processes, ensure smooth execution across reporting workflows, and support data-driven decision making. What We're Looking For

Why Join Us?
