title: Marketing Coordinator — AI-Powered Client Delivery (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
About the role:

Inquivix helps international companies enter the Korean market through Naver SEO, paid advertising, content marketing, and influencer campaigns. We are hiring a Marketing Coordinator in the Philippines to join our remote team.

This is not a traditional marketing role. You will use AI tools (Gemini, Claude) as your primary production system to execute client deliverables every day. If you've never used AI tools for real work output, this role is not for you.

What you will actually do:

- Produce client marketing deliverables using AI — ad copy, creative briefs, campaign materials, landing page content
- Produce and review Korean-language content using AI (you do not need to speak Korean — you need to know how to use AI to produce and check Korean content effectively)
- Build marketing reports and performance summaries using AI (you do not need to be an analyst — you use AI to pull insights from data and present them clearly)
- Produce SEO blog content using structured AI workflows we've built
- Manage tasks in NiftyPM — update statuses, meet deadlines, flag blockers immediately
- Support your PM with whatever is needed to keep client accounts moving

Content production is part of the job, but it's the baseline — not the ceiling. You will handle real client work across multiple accounts.

What we value more than experience:

- How you think matters more than what you've done before
- We will teach you our processes. We cannot teach you how to use AI effectively or how to think clearly under pressure.
- If you can learn fast, follow SOPs precisely, and use AI as a daily production system — you will succeed here even without traditional marketing experience

Requirements:

- Experience using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) for actual work output — not just experimenting
- Clear, structured English writing — you will produce client-facing content daily
- Comfortable following documented processes and SOPs precisely
- Strong self-management — you work remotely, you meet deadlines, you communicate proactively
- Reliable internet and quiet workspace

Nice to have:

- Experience with SEO concepts
- Experience with Google Ads, Meta Ads, or Naver
- Experience with project management tools (Nifty, Asana, Trello)
- Basic HTML/CSS or WordPress knowledge
- Any technical or dev background — we find technical thinkers outperform traditional marketers in this role

Salary: 25,000 - 30,000 PHP/month. 90-day probation with structured review at 60 days. Salary adjustment on the table at 90-day mark based on performance.

title: Job Title: Chief of Operations / Operations Manager (Real Estate)
description:
Location
Remote | Full-Time

About Us

We are a fast-growing real estate team focused on delivering exceptional client experiences through efficient systems, strong leadership, and innovative technology. We leverage Virtual Assistants, automation, AI tools, and integrated business systems to scale operations and support our sales team.

We are seeking a highly organized, tech-savvy, and results-driven Chief of Operations / Operations Manager to oversee company operations, lead our Virtual Assistant team, optimize systems, and drive operational excellence across the organization.

Position Overview

The Chief of Operations / Operations Manager will serve as the operational leader of the company, responsible for managing daily business operations, leading a team of Virtual Assistants, improving workflows, implementing technology solutions, and ensuring all departments operate efficiently and effectively.

This role requires someone who understands real estate operations, has strong leadership skills, and possesses advanced knowledge of AI tools, automation platforms, and system integrations.

Key Responsibilities
Operations Management
Oversee and optimize all day-to-day company operations.
Develop, document, and improve Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
Identify operational bottlenecks and implement scalable solutions.
Create accountability systems and performance metrics across departments.
Ensure consistent execution of company goals and strategic initiatives.
Virtual Assistant Team Leadership
Manage, train, and mentor a team of Virtual Assistants.
Create workflows and task management systems for the VA team.
Conduct performance reviews and KPI tracking.
Ensure quality control and operational consistency.
Build a culture of accountability, efficiency, and continuous improvement.
Systems & Technology Management
Oversee the company's CRM, communication platforms, project management tools, and operational systems.
Manage system integrations between platforms such as CRM, dialers, lead generation tools, transaction management systems, marketing platforms, and reporting dashboards.
Troubleshoot operational and technical issues affecting productivity.
Evaluate and implement new software and technologies to improve efficiency.
AI & Automation Development
Utilize AI tools to streamline workflows and improve operational efficiency.
Design and implement AI-driven automations for lead management, reporting, communication, and task management.
Build integrations using tools such as Zapier,  ---------- , API connections, ChatGPT/OpenAI, and other automation platforms.
Stay current with emerging AI technologies and identify opportunities for implementation.
Create AI-powered systems that reduce manual work and increase team productivity.
Reporting & Analytics
Develop operational dashboards and reporting systems.
Track company KPIs and provide leadership with actionable insights.
Analyze team performance and operational efficiency metrics.
Present recommendations for continuous improvement.
Strategic Leadership
Partner with ownership and leadership teams to execute company vision.
Lead operational projects and system implementations.
Manage change initiatives and company-wide process improvements.
Ensure all departments are aligned with organizational goals.
Qualifications
Required
3+ years of Operations Management experience.

title: Implementation & Operations Specialist
description:
About Pharmesol
Pharmesol’s AI Pharmacy Assistant automates routine tasks and conversations so pharmacy teams can spend more time delivering exceptional patient care.
We build pharmacy-specific AI systems that understand pharmacy workflows, medications, and patient interactions. Our technology is already live in pharmacies across the U.S., helping teams reduce operational burden while improving the patient experience.
We are a fast-growing company operating at the intersection of healthcare and AI. The pace is high, the problems are complex, and the expectations are demanding. If you enjoy solving real operational problems and building systems that directly impact patient care, you will find this work deeply rewarding.

About the Role
We are looking for an Implementation Associate who will own the deployment of Pharmesol’s system with pharmacy customers.
This is not a coordination or project management role. You will be responsible for figuring out how Pharmesol should actually work within each pharmacy’s operations.
Every pharmacy operates differently. Departments change, workflows evolve, and edge cases appear constantly. Your job is to understand those details, design the correct workflow, and ensure the system behaves exactly as intended.
You will work directly with pharmacy teams to map workflows, configure automation logic, test scenarios, and ensure a successful launch.
At any given time you may be managing ~10 active implementations simultaneously, each with different operational nuances. The work requires exceptional attention to detail, strong reasoning ability, and the discipline to track many moving pieces without things slipping through the cracks.
You will work closely with engineering, operations, and sales, but you own the implementation outcome end-to-end.

Responsibilities
Customer Implementation
Lead implementation calls with pharmacy teams and operational stakeholders
Deeply understand each pharmacy’s workflows, operational constraints, and edge cases
Design how Pharmesol should integrate into those workflows
Configure and test automation logic to ensure the system behaves correctly
Identify missing information, inconsistencies, or operational risks during setup
Run scenario testing to validate system behavior before launch
Launch new customers and ensure the system performs as intended

Operational Problem Solving
Break down complex workflows into clear automation logic
Anticipate edge cases and operational exceptions before they cause issues
Diagnose problems quickly and determine whether the root cause is configuration, workflow, or system behavior
Work closely with engineering when product changes are required

Customer Ownership
Maintain clear communication with pharmacy teams during implementation
Ensure expectations are clear and timelines move forward
In some cases, continue managing the account after launch or transition it to another tea ---------- mber

About You
This role is best suited for someone who naturally thinks in systems and details. Much of the job involves noticing inconsistencies, asking the right follow-up questions, and working through operational edge cases before they cause problems.
You will likely do well in this role if the following feels natural to you:
You notice inconsistencies quickly. When someone explains a workflow, you instinctively identify missing steps, unclear assumptions, or edge cases.

You ask the right questions. You are comfortable probing until you fully understand how something actually works in practice.

You can translate real-world processes into clear logic. You enjoy breaking down complex workflows into structured steps that can be implemented in a system.

You stay organized across many parallel threads. You can manage ~10 implementations at once while keeping decisions, workflow logic, and next steps clearly documented.

You take ownership for outcomes. If something is unclear or not working as expected, you investigate and resolve it rather than waiting for direction.

You operate well in fast-moving environments. Priorities shift quickly, and you can context switch frequently without losing accuracy or attention to detail.

This Role May Not Be a Good Fit If
You struggle to context switch frequently. In this role, priorities shift often and you may move between multiple implementations throughout the day while still maintaining focus and accuracy.

You are uncomfortable receiving direct, candid feedback. We value fast learning and open communication.

Your default reaction when something goes wrong is to blame external factors or other people, rather than first asking what you could have done differently.

You prefer environments where responsibilities are narrowly defined. This role requires high ownership and the ability to figure things out independently.

Experience
2+ years of professional experience (excluding internships)
Previous experience working in a fast-paced startup environment required
Experience with U.S. healthcare operations strongly preferred
Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

Technical / Operational Skills
Strong analytical and problem-solving ability
Comfortable learning new systems and workflows quickly
Highly organized in tracking many concurrent details
You have personally built or configured AI or automation workflows (not just used ChatGPT for writing).

Hiring Process
Because this role requires a high degree of ownership and independent thinking, our hiring process is rigorous.
Final candidates should expect:
Three reference calls, including at least two former direct managers
Background check

Why This Role Is Unique
You will be working directly on the operational layer where AI meets real healthcare workflows.
The problems are rarely obvious, the environments are constantly changing, and the solutions require both careful thinking and practical judgment.
If you enjoy solving messy real-world problems and building systems that make frontline healthcare teams more effective, this role offers significant ownership and growth potential.

title: HubSpot Solution Architect
description:
CRM Magnetics is looking for a highly skilled HubSpot Solution Architect to lead complex client implementations and design scalable CRM systems for growing B2B companies.

We specialize in building advanced, high-impact HubSpot ecosystems — not basic setups. This role is for someone who wants to own solutions end-to-end, make decisions, and grow into a true architecture and leadership position.

About the Role
This is not just an execution role.

You will:
- Own full-cycle implementations — from discovery and architecture to delivery and optimization
- Design scalable CRM systems, not just configure tools
- Work directly with clients and influence business processes
- Take responsibility for technical decisions and final outcomes
We’re looking for someone who thinks like a system designer and problem-solver, not just a specialist.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead end-to-end HubSpot implementations (discovery ? architecture ? delivery ? optimization)
- Translate business needs into scalable CRM and automation solutions
- Design CRM structures, workflows, data models, and reporting systems
- Own solution decisions and ensure best practices across projects
- Manage and guide implementation specialists (task delegation, QA, support)
- Identify gaps, risks, and opportunities in client systems proactively
- Work directly with clients in English (calls, documentation, consulting)
- Handle complex integrations (APIs, third-party tools, custom logic)

Requirements:
- Advanced HubSpot expertise (CRM, workflows, reporting, integrations)
- Proven experience with complex implementations (not just basic setups)
- Strong solution-thinking mindset — ability to design systems
- Experience leading projects or managing tea ---------- mbers
- Strong problem-solving skills and ability to handle non-standard cases
- Fluent English (C1+) — client-facing role
- High ownership, responsibility and attention to detail

Nice to Have:
- RevOps / SalesOps / MarketingOps experience
- APIs, Zapier / Make, custom integrations
- SaaS / B2B background
- Experience designing scalable CRM architectures

Compensation & Growth
- Starting rate: $14/hour (Solutions Architect in Training)
- Training period is performance-based — no fixed timeline
- As soon as you demonstrate the ability to independently own projects ? immediate promotion and pay increase ($18+/hour)

We don’t slow people down — growth is based purely on capability and results.

Growth & Opportunity

We are a fast-growing agency with strong  ---------- ntum.

Our systems are already structured, and we’re actively improving:
- Documentation
- Operational efficiency
- Automation and AI deployment

This role is not capped.

The right person will have the opportunity to:
1) Shape internal processes and technical standards
2) Influence how solutions are designed across the company
3) Grow into leadership roles such as:
- Implementation Team Lead
- Head of Solutions Architecture
- Future executive-level roles as the company scales

Who We’re Looking For

Someone who:
- Doesn’t wait for instructions — finds solutions independently
- Can take full ownership of projects and drive them to completion
- Understands business logic, not just tools
- Thinks like an architect, not just a technician
- Wants to join early, grow fast, and build something meaningful

If you're a strong HubSpot expert ready to step into a true architecture and leadership track, we’d love to hear from you.

How to Apply

To be considered for this role, please include:

1) 2–4 relevant HubSpot projects or case studies you’ve worked on
2) A brief description of:
- The client/business context
- What you were responsible for
- The solution you designed and implemented
3) Any links, screenshots, or Loom walkthroughs (if available)

Applications without relevant project examples will not be considered.

We will not consider candidates without experience at Hubspot or without case studies.

title: Client Advocate
description:
Company Overview 

The Compliance Services Organization didn't exist. We built it.

MP1 Solutions pioneered the Compliance Services Organization (CSO) — a new category of operating partner built to replace the fragmented vendor model that has defined compliance programs for decades. Built on the foundation of MedPro Disposal, the nation's largest privately-held medical waste company serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states over the past 15 years, MP1 Solutions extends that operational infrastructure, vendor network, and customer trust into a category we're actively building — and leading. We're doing it with a technology-forward approach, actively developing the AI infrastructure that will make this model smarter, faster, and more scalable as we grow.

What we do: We operate as the single compliance partner for non-acute healthcare facilities — consolidating every obligation into one coordinated program, assigning one dedicated account manager as the point of accountability, and actively managing the underlying vendor ecosystem through continuous evaluation, negotiation, and optimization on the practice's behalf.

Why it matters: Regulated facilities across the country are managing compliance the same fragmented way they always have. We're changing that — and we're looking for people who want to help build what comes next.

Our technology: We're actively building the AI infrastructure that sits behind this model — automating compliance tracking, surfacing risks before they become problems, and creating a system that gets smarter with every client we add. If you want to build at the intersection of compliance and technology, you're in the right place.

Our impact: Our clients never have to worry about something falling through the cracks — obligations are coordinated, tracked, and documented in one place. We audit costs, eliminate overcharges, and deliver proof of compliance that always keeps practices inspection ready. Every client has one dedicated account manager who owns their program end to end. And unlike a traditional vendor relationship, our model is designed to get better over time — continuously re-evaluated, optimized, and improved with every client we serve.

Position Overview: 
We are seeking for Customer Service Representatives/Client Advocate with experience building relationships and trust via inbound & outbound calls. If you are goal-oriented and comfortable working from home over the phone or via email and are ready to be a part of a high-growth, rapidly expanding company with unlimited growth for motivated individuals, then please apply!

Mission statement: Identify, engage, and convert healthcare organizations with multiple locations into clients by offering centralized compliance solutions that drive efficiency and cost savings.

Key Responsibilities:
•  Manage inbound and outbound customer communications through phone, email, and other support channels while maintaining professionalism and responsiveness. 
•  Build strong relationships with healthcare clients by understanding their needs, providing accurate information, and delivering exceptional customer support. 
•  Resolve customer concerns, complaints, and service issues efficiently while ensuring a positive and solution-oriented client experience. 
•  Maintain accurate documentation of customer interactions, account updates, service requests, and resolutions within company systems and CRM platforms. 
•  Coordinate with internal departments to drive timely issue resolution, service fulfillment, and client satisfaction. 
•  Educate clients about company services, compliance programs, and available solutions to support long-term client success. 
•  Meet performance expectations related to productivity, responsiveness, quality assurance, and customer satisfaction metrics. 
•  Adapt quickly to evolving processes, priorities, and operational needs within a fast-paced and rapidly growing remote work environment. 

Qualifications:
•  Bachelor’s degree in a related field or equivalent combination of education and relevant work experience.  
•  Minimum of 2 years of customer service experience in a BPO environment.  
•  Strong verbal and written English communication skills with the ability to build rapport and trust with clients.  
•  Proven ability to multitask, prioritize tasks, and work independently with minimal supervision.  
•  Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, Salesforce and other CRM platforms, with the ability to accurately manage customer records, track interactions, update account information, and navigate multiple systems efficiently in a fast-paced environment.  
•  Experience handling inbound and outbound customer interactions through phone and email support channels.  
•  Reliable work-from-home setup, including a stable internet connection, backup power/internet, and a quiet dedicated workspace.  
•  Professional, adaptable, detail-oriented, and capable of thriving in a fast-paced environment.  

Compensation & Benefits:
• Salary Range: $500-$650 base (depending on level of experience) + $50 internet allowance + monthly incentives
• Permanent work-from-home
• 40 hours per week, aligned with U.S. business hours (CST, graveyard shift).
• Fixed weekends off (Saturday & Sunday)
• Work Anniversary Bonus
• No work with pay on US Holidays

Basic Technical Requirements:
•   Your own laptop/desktop PC with the following minimum specs: i5 5th gen and above/AMD equivalent; 8GB RAM; Mozilla or Chrome installed; Windows 10 upwards.
•  A consistent and stable internet connection of at least 20Mbps
•  With back-up computer, internet connection and power supply
•  Noise-canceling headset/headphones
•  A quiet and dedicated workspace at home

Application Process:
1. Your most updated resume.

2. Record a 1–2-minute audio or video introducing yourself and explaining why you are interested in the job. Should be recorded using Vocaroo or Loom, and the link should be shared with us for easy access. 

3. Submit to all of these emails:

 ---------- 
 ---------- 
 ---------- 

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement
MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions are equal opportunity employers. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: GoHighLevel Specialist + Website+Video & Social Media Creator
description:
GoHighLevel Specialist + Website+Video & Social Media Creator
Remote  ·  Full-Time  ·  US Eye-Care Medical Group (Florida)
Base $700–$1,100 USD/month  +  uncapped new-patient bonuses that can DOUBLE your base.

Read This First
This is not a generic VA job. We are hiring one exceptional person to run our marketing engine and grow it. We will train you, pay you on time, treat you like a teammate, and reward you directly every time your work brings in a new patient.
The harder and smarter you work, the more you earn. Your base is just the floor. Your ceiling is set by you.
If you are world-class at GoHighLevel AND can create scroll-stopping video and social content, keep reading. If you are a generalist who has “heard of” these tools, this is not your job — and that’s okay.
The Offer (Why the Best Apply Here)
Base salary $700 – $1,100 USD/month  (full-time, 40 hrs/week)
New-patient bonus $ USD for every qualifying new patient your work brings in (booked + shows up). No cap.
Realistic earning Drive 35-50 new patients in a month and you will earn up to 50% of your base . The more you produce, the more you make — every month.
Reviews Reviewed every 6 months. Strong performers reach the top of the base range within 90 days.
13th-month bonus Paid after 12 months.


Paid time off Paid US holidays after probation + paid leave.
Payment Bi-weekly via Wise — on time, every time.
What the bonus means in plain numbers: $ per qualifying new patient, uncapped. A productive month can earn you more in bonuses than your entire base. We want you to out-earn the salary — that means we’re both winning.
Schedule
• 45-50 hrs/week (we may start you at 20–30 hrs/week part-time, with a clear, fast path to full-time).
• Live overlap required: 9:00 AM – 200 PM US Eastern (9:00 PM – 1:00 AM Manila), Monday–Friday, for real-time work with the CEO and marketing lead. Some flexibility on exact hours.
• The rest of your hours are flexible — we care about output, not clock-watching.
About Us
We are a US-based ophthalmology / eye-care medical group with three clinic locations in Florida. We treat thousands of patients every month, focused on senior eye health, diabetic eye disease, and advanced retinal care.
We are stable, established, and real — not a fly-by-night agency. We have a small, dedicated in-house marketing team and an active marketing budget. You will work directly with the CEO and the marketing lead — no five layers of management between you and the people who make decisions and see your work.
The Role — In One Sentence
You will own our GoHighLevel (GHL) CRM end-to-end and produce the video and social content that brings senior and diabetic patients through our doors — then get paid a bonus on every patient you help bring in.
What You'll Actually Do (Day-in-the-Life)
 — GoHighLevel Operations (the backbone)
• Build and maintain workflows from scratch: patient recall, new-patient intake, no-show recovery, review requests, and our referral/ambassador program.
• Manage and clean pipelines, opportunity stages, and contact tags.
• Build SMS + email sequences (US-tone copy is supplied — you execute and polish, you don’t write from zero).
• Set up and maintain calendar integrations and lead routing.
• Build dashboards and weekly reports: leads in ? calls booked ? patients shown.
• Connect GHL to forms, landing pages, and Meta lead forms.
— Video & Social Media Creation (where you shine)
• Create and edit scroll-stopping short-form video for YouTube, Instagram Reels, Facebook, and TikTok — content that actually gets seniors (65+) and diabetics to call us.
• Run our organic social presence across all platforms, targeting patients within a 15–20 mile radius of each of our three offices.
• Edit real footage of our doctors, staff, and (consented) patients into warm, trustworthy, human content.
• Important: our audience is older adults and people managing a serious health condition. We use real people, real stories, and a caring tone — never AI avatars or AI voices. You’ll film, cut, caption, and publish content that feels like a trusted neighbor, not an ad.
• Track what works (saves, shares, calls, DMs) and make more of it.
 — Paid Ads Support (Meta / Facebook)
• Daily Ads Manager checks: spend, frequency, CPM, CTR, cost-per-lead.
• Upload and launch creative; build and duplicate campaigns/ad sets.
• Pull weekly performance reports.
• You execute under direction — you are not the lead strategist — but you must know Meta Ads Manager cold, including the Special Ad Category (Health & Wellness) rules that apply to US healthcare advertisers.

 — Reporting + Documentation
• Weekly Monday performance snapshot to the CEO.
• Build and maintain an SOP document as you go, so the system never depends on one person.
Must-Haves (Do Not Apply Without These)
1. 2+ years hands-on GoHighLevel — building workflows from scratch, not clicking templates. Be ready to show real workflows you’ve built.
2. Proven video editing + social content skills — short-form video for Reels / TikTok / YouTube. Be ready to show a portfolio or links to content you’ve made that performed.
3. Meta Ads and Google Manager experience — 1+ year managing live ad accounts. Bonus if you’ve worked under Meta’s Special Ad Category (Health & Wellness).
4. Excellent written and spoken English — you’ll write internal updates and US-facing SMS/email copy reviewed by the CEO.
5. Reliable internet (25 Mbps+), a quiet workspace, and a working webcam (weekly Zoom).
6. Long-term mindset — we’re hiring for years, not months. Job-hoppers will be cut early.
Nice-to-Haves (Bonus Points)
• US healthcare or medical-practice experience.
• HIPAA awareness (we train, but knowing what patient data can’t touch is a plus).
• Zapier /  ---------- .
• Canva / CapCut fluency for fast visual turnarounds.
• Experience with patient recall, no-show recovery, or appointment-booking funnels.
Who This Is Perfect For
• You’ve run GHL for 2+ agencies and you’re tired of juggling 8 clients — you want one company, deep work, and real impact.
• You can build systems and make a video that actually makes someone pick up the phone.
• You’re motivated by results: you’d rather earn $ a patient with no cap than sit on a flat salary.
• You want to grow into an Operations / Marketing Manager role within 12–18 months.
• You want a US employer who pays on time, communicates clearly, and treats you like a teammate.
Who This Is Not For
• Generalist VAs who’ve only “heard of” GHL.
• Agencies or teams reselling labor — we hire one human, directly.
• Anyone unwilling to do a paid 2-hour test task before a final offer (you keep the pay even if not hired).
How to Apply — Read Carefully (This Is Your First Test)
We get hundreds of applications. This is how we filter for people who actually read and follow instructions.
Subject line, exactly:
CLEAR VISION — GHL + Video Specialist — [Your Name]
In the body, include — in this order:
7. Codeword: “20/20” at the very top, so we know you read this.
8. 3 GoHighLevel workflows you personally built — 1–2 sentences each: the problem it solved and the result.
9. 2 video / social posts you created — links to the actual content, plus the result (views, saves, calls, leads).
10. 1 Meta Ads or Google campaign you ran — niche, monthly spend, what you optimized for, and the result.
11. Years of GHL experience + years of video/social experience + years of Meta Ads experience.
12. A Loom video (3 minutes max) walking through ONE workflow inside your own GHL account. Required — no Loom, no review.
13. Your full-time monthly rate in USD (and part-time hourly if relevant).
14. Earliest start date + how many hours of US Eastern overlap you can commit to.
Applications missing any of the above are rejected automatically.
Hiring Timeline
• Applications reviewed daily.
• Shortlist contacted within 5 business days.
• Round 1: 30-min Zoom with the Marketing Lead.
• Round 2: Paid 2-hour test task (you keep the pay even if not hired).
• Final: 20-min call with the CEO.
• Offer within 2 weeks of applying.
Why You'll Want This Job
• Stable US healthcare company — years in operation, not a startup that might vanish.
• Real budget, real tools — you won’t be told to “make it work with free Mailchimp.”
• Uncapped bonuses — your effort directly sets your income. Most months, your bonus can rival or beat your base.
• Direct CEO access — your work is seen and rewarded.
• Clear growth path — Operations / Marketing Manager role opening within 12–18 months for the right person.
• One company, one mission, deep focus — no client-juggling.

We are looking for one excellent person. Take the time to apply properly — and welcome to a role where the harder you work, the more you earn.

title: Remote Maintenance Coordinator
description:
About the Role
We are a U.S.?based real estate / property management company hiring a Remote Maintenance Coordinator in the Philippines. Your main job: own all maintenance requests from start to finish so issues get handled fast, tenants stay happy, and owners stay informed.

You will manage tickets, vendors, and communication. You are the “air traffic controller” for maintenance.

Key Responsibilities
1. Maintenance Request Intake & Triage

Monitor all incoming maintenance requests (portal, email, phone logs).
Log and organize requests in our system (AppFolio or similar).
Triage issues: emergency vs urgent vs routine based on our SOPs.
Gather missing details (photos, descriptions, access instructions) from tenants.
2. Work Order Creation & Scheduling

Create clear work orders with problem details, priority, and access notes.
Assign jobs to the right vendor / in?house tech based on skill, location, and cost.
Coordinate dates and times with tenants and vendors.
Confirm vendors have all info needed before dispatch.
3. Follow?Up & Ticket Management

Track all open work orders and follow up until completion.
Check status with vendors, reschedule if needed, and avoid jobs “falling through the cracks.”
Update work orders with notes, photos, invoices, and completion details.
Close tickets only after work is confirmed done and documented.
4. Tenant & Owner Communication

Keep tenants updated on status: scheduled, in progress, completed.
Communicate professionally and clearly via email, text, or phone (as needed).
Escalate serious / repeated issues to the Property Manager or owner quickly.
Provide simple weekly maintenance summaries (open tickets, completed, problem units).
Requirements
Based in the Philippines with excellent written and spoken English.
1+ year experience in property management, maintenance coordination, or similar admin role.
Experience with AppFolio or other property management software is a strong plus.
Very organized, strong follow?through, and comfortable handling many tickets at once.
Good problem?solving skills and calm under pressure (emergencies, upset tenants).
Stable internet connection and quiet work environment.
Able to work overlapping U.S. business hours.
Success Metrics
Average response time to new maintenance requests.
% of tickets completed within target time by priority level.
Number of overdue / stalled work orders.
Tenant / owner feedback on communication and speed.*

title: Gorgias Customer Service Manager – Must Have 30+ Agent Management Experience
description:
To apply for the position, please use the following link: popon.forms.app/employmentapplication-cx

Pop On Veneers is one of the fastest-growing direct-to-consumer smile brands in the United States. We help customers smile with confidence through custom-made cosmetic veneers and oral care products.

We are looking for an experienced Customer Service Manager to oversee our Philippines-based customer service team.

This is not a regular customer service agent role. We are only looking for candidates who have already managed a customer service team, preferably 30+ agents, in a remote or BPO-style environment.

The ideal candidate has strong hands-on experience with Gorgias, understands customer service KPIs, and knows how to manage agent productivity, quality, attendance, escalations, workflows, and performance.

Key Responsibilities:
- Manage and oversee a Philippines-based customer service team
- Supervise agents handling email, chat, and phone support
- Monitor agent productivity, attendance, response times, resolution times, backlog, and quality
- Review tickets and customer interactions to ensure support standards are being followed
- Coach agents, provide feedback, and help improve individual performance
- Manage Gorgias workflows, macros, automations, tags, views, and ticket organization
- Handle escalated customer issues and difficult cases
- Identify workflow problems and recommend process improvements
- Help train new agents and improve existing team processes
- Report team performance, issues, and recommendations to leadership

Requirements:
- Previous experience as a Customer Service Manager, Support Manager, Operations Manager, Team Manager, or similar leadership role
- Must have experience managing customer service agents, not just working as an agent
- Experience managing 30+ agents is strongly preferred
- Previous hands-on experience using Gorgias is required
- Strong understanding of customer service KPIs, QA, ticket management, and team reporting
- Experience managing remote or Philippines-based teams is strongly preferred
- Strong leadership, communication, and coaching skills
- Excellent written and verbal English
- Highly organized and comfortable managing a fast-paced support operation

Please do not apply if your only experience is as a customer service representative or virtual assistant. This role is for an experienced customer service manager who has managed a team before.

To apply for the position, please use the following link: popon.forms.app/employmentapplication-cx

title: Remote Property Manager – AppFolio Expert
description:
About the Role
We are a growing residential property management company looking for a detail?oriented, reliable Remote Property Manager based in the Philippines. Your primary goal: keep units full, rents collected, and issues handled quickly with excellent communication.

You will work closely with our Maintenance Coordinator, monitor AppFolio daily, and make sure vacancies are filled as fast as possible with qualified tenants.

Key Responsibilities
1. Rent Collection & Delinquencies

Monitor daily rent collections in AppFolio.
Verify 100% of tenants are paid or properly tagged (late / payment plan / notice) by set deadlines.
Send late notices, payment reminders, and coordinate with tenants on payment plans according to company policy.
Prepare weekly delinquency reports for ownership.
2. Maintenance Coordination Support

Review new maintenance requests in AppFolio and make sure they are correctly categorized and assigned to the Maintenance Coordinator / vendors.
Follow up on open work orders to ensure they are completed within target timeframes.
Communicate with tenants regarding scheduling, access, and status updates.
Flag recurring issues or vendor performance problems to management.
3. Leasing & Vacancy Management

Monitor upcoming move?outs and newly vacant units in AppFolio.
Coordinate with the team to ensure units are turned quickly and listed immediately once rent?ready.
Respond to rental inquiries (email, phone, portal) quickly and professionally.
Pre?screen applicants, collect documents, and move qualified prospects through the application and lease?signing process.
Track days?vacant and report bottlenecks.
4. Tenant Communication & Documentation

Respond to tenant questions and requests within agreed SLAs.
Document all interactions and decisions inside AppFolio (notes, tasks, follow?ups).
Escalate legal / serious issues to management promptly with clear documentation.
Requirements
MUST HAVE:
Strong experience using AppFolio (daily user, not just training).
1+ year experience in property management, leasing, or similar real estate operations role.
Excellent written and spoken English.
Strong attention to detail and follow?through.
Comfortable working U.S. business hours (or overlapping core hours).
Stable internet connection and quiet work environment.
Performance Metrics (How You’ll Be Measured)
% of rent collected by the 5th / end of month.
Average days?vacant per unit.
% of maintenance tickets resolved within target time.
Response time to tenants and applicants.

title: Catalog Operations VA, US E-comm Brand, Shopify and Amazon Sync Required
description:
SALARY

$4 to $6 USD per hour, paid bi-weekly via Wise or PayPal

HOURS

30 to 40 hours per week, overlap with US Mountain Time preferred

ABOUT US

Cedar Mill Firearms Family is a US-based e-commerce brand selling firearm storage and EDC accessories on Amazon, BigCommerce, and Shopify. We sell trigger locks, gun socks, dehumidifiers, cleaning kits, range bags, and safes. We do NOT source firearms, federal law prohibits Amazon firearm sales and we do not import them.

We sell on Amazon, BigCommerce, and Shopify, and we need a Catalog Operations VA who owns the multi-channel sync. Keep SKUs, inventory counts, and pricing aligned across all three. Distinct from the Amazon-specialist roles, this seat is channel-agnostic and lives in the master SKU sheet.

WHAT YOU WILL OWN

You will own the master SKU sheet and the multi-channel sync. Keep product data aligned across Amazon, BigCommerce, and Shopify (title, price, inventory count, variant attributes). Catch SKU drift within 24 hours. Maintain the inventory feed into Sellbrite (or equivalent) and resolve sync errors. Build the listing QA checklist that catches multi-channel errors before they hit live customers. Coordinate with the Amazon catalog specialist on Amazon-side fixes and with the BigCommerce and Shopify admin work directly.

WHAT YOU MUST ALREADY KNOW

REQUIRED, 3+ years multi-channel catalog operations (Shopify + Amazon at minimum; BigCommerce a strong plus).

REQUIRED, Sellbrite, Linnworks, ChannelAdvisor, or equivalent multi-channel sync tool experience.

REQUIRED, Hands-on Shopify variant management (you know the 100-variant cap and how to work around it).

REQUIRED, Inventory accuracy obsession. You audit before you trust.

REQUIRED, Self-directed. You build your own QA checklist after week 1.

PREFERRED, BigCommerce metafields and option-set experience.

PREFERRED, Shopify Flow or BigCommerce automation experience.

PREFERRED, Prior catalog-lead role with example SOPs you wrote.

BONUS, AI tool fluency (ChatGPT, Claude) for bulk diff generation across channels.

KPI TARGETS

Cross-channel SKU drift less than or equal to 24 hours.

Inventory accuracy greater than or equal to 99 percent on monthly audit.

Listing QA pass rate greater than or equal to 98 percent on launches.

Multi-channel sync error resolution less than or equal to 4 business hours.

HIRING PROCESS, 4 STAGES

Stage 1, apply via our Google Form, 15 minutes.

Stage 2, if shortlisted, 45 minute evaluation task, unpaid screening. Real multi-channel scenario: a SKU needs to sync across Amazon, Shopify, and BigCommerce with different field requirements, walk through the handling.

Stage 3, 45 minute video interview via Google Meet.

Stage 4, paid trial week at the agreed rate, then full hire decision.

We respond to every applicant within 5 business days regardless of outcome.

APPLY HERE

 ---------- 

IMPORTANT, SCAM PROTECTION

WE NEVER charge any fee to apply or to be considered. We NEVER ask for M-Pesa, GCash, registration, training, background check, or document payment. Our ONLY application channel is the Google Form link above. The hiring process is form, then evaluation task, then interview, then paid trial week. At NO stage do you pay us. Please do NOT message us on onlinejobs.ph chat about this role, your application will not be seen if you skip the form.

title: Staff Accountant
description:

Financial reporting and financial statement preparation.
Account reconciliations and general ledger maintenance.
Strong understanding of GAAP and accounting best practices.
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.

Experience in pharmacy operations, pharmacy accounting, or pharmacy-related healthcare organizations.

Desired Traits

High degree of integrity and professionalism.
Excellent problem-solving and analytical abilities.
Adaptable and comfortable in a dynamic, entrepreneurial environment.
Ability to communicate effectively with both financial and non-financial stakeholders.

title: HR & Accounting Administrator
description:
We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented, and proactive HR & Accounting Admin to support daily accounting and HR-related tasks, including payment tracking, invoice audits, commission reviews, payroll coordination, onboarding requirements, and dashboard/reporting updates.

This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, comfortable working with spreadsheets and dashboards, communicates clearly across departments, and has the confidence to speak up when something does not look right. The right person should be trustworthy, resourceful, and able to use critical thinking to identify issues, ask the right questions, and suggest process improvements when needed. 

Do not APPLY if: Please do not apply if your experience is limited to call center work only, with no background in admin, reporting, accounting, HR, or data-related tasks, or if you are only familiar with very basic Excel or Google Sheets functions and are not comfortable learning more advanced reporting.

Key Responsibilities
The HR & Accounting Admin will be responsible for supporting and managing tasks such as:

Accounting & Sales Reporting
-Track and confirm daily payment received reports.
-Audit Sales Team invoices and verify accuracy of billing details.
-Review monthly sales data and summarize key information.
-Organize, verify, and spot abnormalities or changes in sales reports.
-Coordinate with Sales and Medical teams regarding invoice or payment discrepancies.
-Manage patient program refunds, Stripe disputes, and cancellation requests.

Commission & Payroll Support
-Review Sales Team commission records on scheduled payroll periods.
-Verify sales data and ensure commission calculations are accurate.
-Coordinate with HR and Sales regarding commission concerns or missing information.
-Review VA timesheets, overtime records, and payroll details.
-Assist with payroll preparation using payroll spreadsheets and related tools.

HR & Administrative Coordination
-Support employee and VA onboarding requirements.
-Track completion of onboarding checklists and required documents.
-Assist with termination requirements when needed.
-Coordinate with Sales, Medical, Coaching, Call Center, and HR departments to ensure requirements are completed properly.

Dashboard & Reporting
-Create, maintain, and improve dashboards for tracking payments, invoices, sales, commissions, payroll, and task completion.
-Prepare clean and organized reports for management review.
-Use spreadsheets and reporting tools to summarize data clearly.
-Identify data inconsistencies and communicate findings promptly.

Required Skills & Qualifications
-Strong attention to detail, accuracy, and organization.
-Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills.
-Experience with spreadsheets, reporting, and dashboard creation.
-Ability to organize, verify, analyze, and summarize data clearly.
-Comfortable working with invoices, sales records, commissions, payroll details, and HR-related information.
-Strong written and verbal communication skills.
-Able to communicate professionally with Sales, Medical, HR, Coaching, Call Center, and other departments.
-Confident enough to speak up about discrepancies, missing information, unclear processes, or possible errors.
-Knows how to use and navigate ChatGPT for reporting, summaries, communication drafts, task organization, and process improvement.
-Resourceful and willing to learn new tools, systems, and workflows.
-Trustworthy and dependable when handling sensitive financial and HR information.
-Able to work independently, manage recurring tasks, and follow through without constant supervision.
Tools & Systems

Experience with any of the following is a plus:
-Google Sheets or Excel
-GHL
-Zoho
-Stripe
-Wise
-Timesheet tracking tools

Why Join Our Team?

At Dr. Jones’s Clinic, we help patients transform their metabolic health through advanced medical weight loss and coaching programs.

This role plays a critical operational role behind the scenes, ensuring our patient data, CRM systems, and internal reports stay organized and accurate.

You will gain experience working with advanced CRM systems, healthcare operations, and data management while supporting a fast-growing telehealth company.

Department: Admin Department 
Work Type: Full-time 
Working Hours: Must be available to work during MST business hours 
Role Type: Administrative, Reporting, Cross-Department Coordination 
Starting Rate: $6/hour upon official hiring 
Training/Test Period Rate: $4/hour during the training or test period 
Growth Opportunity: Rate may increase based on performance, reliability, quality of work, and ability to take ownership of tasks.

Application Requirement: Interested candidates are encouraged to send a sample dashboard, report, or any previous reporting work they have created that helped with tracking, analysis, or decision-making.


Email your application to:
 ----------

title: Marketing Operations Assistant (HubSpot / CRM / Automation)
description:
About the Role
We are a U.S.-based B2B software company in the energy industry.

You will work directly with our Head of Marketing to execute marketing operations, campaigns, and CRM management inside HubSpot.

This is not a social media or content-only role.
Your job is to translate marketing direction into clean, structured execution inside our systems.


What You’ll Be Responsible For
1. HubSpot CRM Management
- Clean and maintain contact and company data (duplicates, invalid emails, lifecycle stages) 
- Manage lists, properties, and segmentation 
- Perform ongoing data hygiene and enrichment ( ---------- , NeverBounce) 
- Ensure CRM accuracy and usability 

2. Marketing Automation
- Build and maintain workflows (lead routing, nurture sequences, follow-ups) 
- Execute drip campaigns (including state-specific campaigns) 
- Support outbound automation 
- Troubleshoot workflow and data issues 

3. Campaign Execution
- Build and send email campaigns, templates, and snippets 
- Support marketing campaigns and promotions 
- Assist with event follow-up campaigns 
- Connect tools (Appcues, forms, etc.) into HubSpot 

4. Lead Management
- Maintain and improve lead scoring (with guidance) 
- Review inbound leads and ensure proper routing 
- Identify and clean low-quality or invalid leads 

5. Reporting & Data
- Prepare reports on campaign performance 
- Ensure tracking and data consistency 
- Identify issues (broken workflows, missing data, etc.) 

6. Systems Support
- Assist with integrations (QuickBooks sync, internal tools) 
- Help maintain clean data flow between systems 


What Success Looks Like
- HubSpot data is clean, organized, and reliable 
- Campaigns are executed on time with minimal errors 
- Marketing operations run smoothly without constant oversight 
- You can take direction and turn it into completed work 


Requirements
- 2+ years experience with HubSpot OR similar CRM , and Marketing operations, admin, or automation support 
- Strong attention to detail 
- Strong written English 
- Ability to follow instructions and work independently 


Who This Role Is For
You like working with systems, structure, and data 
You are strong at execution and follow-through 
You can take direction and turn it into finished work 


Who This Role Is NOT For
Social media-only marketers 
Content writers without CRM experience 
Applicants who need constant supervision 


How to Apply (Read Carefully)
We receive a high volume of applications. We only review candidates who follow instructions exactly.

Step 1: Subject Line
Use ONE of the following Enerex core values as your subject line:

Earn Trust 
We > Me 
Own It 
Create Simplicity 
Celebrate Progress 

If your subject line does not match one of these exactly, your application will not be reviewed.

Step 2: Explain Your Choice
At the top of your application, answer:
Which value did you choose, and why does it resonate most with you? Use a real example.

Step 3: Answer These Questions
- What is your internet speed? Include a screenshot from speedtest.net. 
- Do you currently have another job? If yes: What do you do?  How many hours per week? 
- You log into HubSpot and find duplicate contacts , missing emails , and unclear lifecycle stages. What exact steps would you take to clean this up? (Be specific.)
- Give an example of how you improved or simplified a process. 
- What actions do you take to build trust with a remote manager? 

Include the word “structured” somewhere in your application.

Step 4: Resume & Short Video (2–3 minutes)
- Attached your updated resume.
- Record a Loom video answering:
>>> Why this role is a good fit for you 
>>> Your experience with HubSpot or similar tools 


Final Notes
- This is a long-term role with growth opportunity 
- Performance-based increases after 90 days 
- We value reliability, attention to detail, and accountability

title: US Tax  Plan Associate
description:

title: Technical Operations & Client Success Specialist (GHL / Xadira)
description:
# I will be reviewing every job post carefully. If you cannot take the time to respond without providing a templated job post, a job post that you copy pasted from someone else, or an AI generated job post, I cannot take the time to consider your Cover Letter and Proposal.

I understand there are a lot of applicants on Upwork but I will give real consideration to real applicants.

# Technical Operations & Client Success Specialist (GHL / Xadira)

## About Us

We operate a software platform called Xadira that helps Medicare insurance agents and financial professionals automate webinars, lead follow-up, appointment booking, CRM management, and client communications.

We are looking for someone who can become the operational backbone of our company.

This is NOT a simple customer support role.

You will work directly with the founder and gradually become the primary owner of client onboarding, troubleshooting, operational support, documentation, and system monitoring.

The goal is for you to become my "right hand" for all things related to Xadira operations.

If you enjoy solving problems, troubleshooting systems, improving processes, and taking ownership rather than waiting for instructions, this may be a great fit.

---

## Your Primary Responsibilities

### Client Onboarding

You will be responsible for helping new clients get fully set up and launched.

Responsibilities include:

* Creating and configuring new Xadira accounts
* User creation and permissions management
* Calendar integrations
* CRM configuration
* Lead routing verification
* State assignment setup
* A2P registration tracking
* Webinar setup assistance
* Launch readiness verification

---

### Client Support & Troubleshooting

You will become the first point of contact for most client support requests.

Responsibilities include:

* Responding to client emails and support requests
* Troubleshooting Xadira issues
* Troubleshooting GoHighLevel issues
* Troubleshooting CloseBot issues
* Diagnosing workflow failures
* Investigating appointment booking issues
* Investigating lead routing issues
* Resolving account configuration problems
* Escalating true software bugs when necessary

Many of our support requests involve:

* "No User Assigned" errors
* Opportunity creation failures
* Appointment booking issues
* Calendar issues
* Workflow automation issues
* User permission issues
* Lead routing issues

---

### Daily Operations Monitoring

You will proactively monitor system health and operational reliability.

Responsibilities include:

* Reviewing system alerts
* Reviewing workflow failures
* Reviewing AI booking failures
* Reviewing lead routing failures
* Reviewing unassigned contacts
* Reviewing opportunity creation failures
* Identifying recurring issues
* Escalating high-priority problems

The goal is to identify and solve issues before clients report them.

---

### Documentation & Training

Every problem solved should improve the system.

Responsibilities include:

* Creating SOPs
* Updating SOPs
* Recording Loom training videos
* Building help center content
* Creating troubleshooting guides
* Improving onboarding materials
* Building an internal knowledge base

We want problems solved once and documented forever.

---

### Client Communication

Responsibilities include:

* Communicating professionally with clients
* Providing troubleshooting updates
* Following up until issues are resolved
* Creating a positive client experience
* Managing expectations during investigations

Strong written English is extremely important.

---

## Required Skills

* Excellent English communication
* Strong troubleshooting ability
* Strong problem-solving skills
* Experience with GoHighLevel
* Ability to learn new systems quickly
* Strong attention to detail
* Ability to work independently
* Comfortable communicating directly with clients
* Comfortable recording Loom videos

---

## Preferred Skills

* CloseBot experience
* CRM administration experience
* Marketing automation experience
* Webinar funnel experience
* Medicare industry experience
* SOP creation experience
* Technical support experience

These are helpful but not required.

---

## What Success Looks Like

### After 30 Days

* Understand Xadira workflows
* Handle basic support requests
* Assist with onboarding
* Create your first SOPs
* Begin monitoring operational alerts

### After 60 Days

* Independently onboard new clients
* Resolve most support requests
* Investigate workflow issues
* Create documentation regularly
* Reduce founder involvement in day-to-day support

### After 90 Days

You own:

* New client onboarding
* User provisioning
* Calendar setup
* A2P tracking
* First-line support
* Daily operations monitoring
* Documentation management

You can independently resolve most operational issues and escalate only true software bugs or strategic decisions.

---

## To Apply

Please answer the following:

1. What experience do you have with GoHighLevel?
2. What CRM and automation platforms have you worked with?
3. Describe a technical problem you diagnosed and solved on your own.
4. Have you created SOPs or training documentation before?
5. Record a short Loom video introducing yourself and explaining why you would be a good fit.

### Troubleshooting Exercise

A client says:

"I am getting leads, but appointments are not being booked."

Walk me through exactly how you would investigate that problem.

### Important

title: Social Media & Affiliate VA — Content Repurposing
description:

I already produce a lot of short-form video; I need someone to get it everywhere it should be, properly linked and earning, and to help grow these accounts. 

The work: Taking content that already exists and getting it onto every platform the right way — uploaded, captioned for each platform, tagged with the correct products and affiliate links, and posted on a consistent schedule. 

A big part of the job upfront is working through a backlog in an organized way, then keeping the pipeline moving daily. Some of the tools I can teach the right person after hiring — I care more about someone sharp, reliable, and detail-oriented than someone who already knows everything.

Responsibilities:

Repurpose and cross-post short-form videos to YouTube Shorts, Instagram, Facebook, and Pinterest
Write platform-appropriate titles, descriptions, captions, and hashtags
Insert affiliate links/deeplinks and tag the correct products on every post
Upload Amazon Onsite product review videos and attach the right product
Communicate consistently about the queue, deadlines, and questions

Required:

Hands-on experience managing social accounts (YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and/or Pinterest)
Detail-obsessed with links, product tags, and file/folder organization (wrong links = lost income)
Solid written English for captions and descriptions
Reliable internet and able to manage your own queue day to day

Bonus Skill that help:

 ----------  for cross-posting
ViralVue and Amazon Associates / Onsite uploads
ManyChat DM automations
Geniuslink or other affiliate deeplink tools
Instagram/Facebook account growth & monetization

Schedule: Part-time to start, scaling to full-time for the right person. Flexible hours as long as the queue moves daily.
Pay: $4–6/hour USD, depending on experience.

To apply, reply with:

The word GECKO at the top of your message

Which of the platforms/tools above you've actually used, and roughly how long
Examples of accounts or creators you've helped manage or grow (links if you have them)
Your available hours per week and whether you're open to full-time now and/or later

Looking for a long-term partner, not a quick hire.
— Jaron

title: PPC Advertising Expert – Amazon, Walmart & Social Media
description:
Role Overview
We are seeking an experienced and analytical PPC Advertising Expert to manage and scale paid advertising campaigns across Amazon, Walmart, Meta, TikTok, and Google platforms.
This role demands a blend of data-driven decision-making, creative testing, and strategic optimization to maximize ROI and brand visibility. The ideal candidate understands both marketplace algorithms and consumer behavior—capable of driving traffic, conversions, and sustained profitability.
________________________________________
Key Responsibilities
Amazon & Walmart Advertising
• Plan, execute, and optimize Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display, and DSP campaigns.
• Manage Walmart Connect ads including search, display, and retargeting.
• Continuously analyze keyword performance, bids, and budgets to improve ACOS, ROAS, and TACOS.
• Implement strategies for new product launches, seasonal pushes, and liquidation campaigns.
• Monitor category trends, competitor ads, and pricing to identify growth opportunities.
Social Media & Paid Traffic
• Develop and manage Meta (Facebook/Instagram), TikTok, and Google Ads campaigns to support brand awareness and conversion goals.
• Build and test creative variations for audience segments using dynamic ad formats, video, and carousel creatives.
• Coordinate with the creative and social media teams to ensure ad messaging aligns with brand voice and product positioning.
• Implement pixel tracking, conversion events, and retargeting funnels.
Analytics & Reporting
• Track, measure, and report daily/weekly/monthly campaign performance.
• Provide actionable insights using tools like Amazon Brand Analytics, Helium10, DataDive, Google Analytics, and Meta Ads Manager.
• Maintain clear documentation of testing outcomes, ad structures, and learnings for continuous improvement.
• Forecast advertising spend and ROI based on historical performance and growth goals.
Collaboration & Strategy
• Partner with the content and design teams to ensure compelling, conversion-focused creatives.
• Work with product and inventory teams to coordinate ad pacing with available stock and seasonality.
• Advise leadership on marketplace advertising trends, new ad types, and emerging automation tools.
________________________________________
Qualifications
• 3+ years of hands-on experience managing PPC campaigns (Amazon required; Walmart & Meta preferred).
• Proven track record of improving ROAS, scaling profitable campaigns, and managing high-budget ad accounts.
• Expert knowledge of Amazon Seller Central, Walmart Connect, and Meta Ads Manager.
• Strong analytical skills and advanced proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets.
• Familiarity with AI-driven ad tools (e.g., Perpetua, Pacvue, Intentwise, or Klevu) is a plus.
• Excellent communication and presentation skills — able to translate data into strategy.
• History of working with strong USA based brands
________________________________________
Preferred Skills
• Experience in home textiles, décor, or lifestyle products.
• Creative mindset with strong understanding of A/B testing and consumer psychology.
• Working knowledge of automation tools and dashboards (Power BI, Looker Studio).
• Understanding of MAP pricing policies and brand protection.
________________________________________
Compensation
• Competitive salary based on experience
• Performance-based bonus structure

title: STR Head of Virtual Guest Operations & Integration
description:
Title- Head of Virtual Guest Operations & Property Integration 
Schedule: TBD EST TIMEZONE
Hours: 45 - 55 Hours
Rate of Pay: $10.75 + Bonus

About Us: 
We are a premier hospitality management and solutions group specializing in delivering 24/7 guest communication, revenue management, and listing optimization services. Founded to alleviate operational friction for property owners, we combine relentless ownership with elite service standards to drive revenue, maximize bookings, and maintain pristine asset performance. 

Our culture is defined by rapid, proactive action and a steadfast commitment to leading with kindness, professionalism, and a passion for mastery. By transforming complex asset logistics into seamless experiences, we empower our clients to build sustainable wealth while ensuring guests receive unparalleled care. 

Accountability:
KPI 1: Portfolio Rating Matrix: Maintain and protect a 4.9+ average guest rating across Airbnb, VRBO, and direct booking channels. Recapture and bulletproof Superhost Status across all accounts.
KPI 2: The Service Level Agreement (SLA): Enforce a zero-miss policy where all critical guest issues are addressed within 5 minutes, and general inbox response times average under 10 minutes.
KPI 3: Integration Velocity: Ensure 100?curate, flawless onboarding and system integration of up to 250+ new property listings over the next 12 months with zero administrative lag.
KPI 4: The Leadership Leverage Index: Drastically reduce the time the company owners spend on day-to-day operational noise. Success means owners are completely insulated from routine guest, cleaning, or maintenance escalations.
KPI 5: Revenue Contribution: Actively drive property profitability through the execution of a team-wide upselling framework (extending stays, early check-ins, late check-outs).

Tasks: 
*Daily Operations & Quality Audits
 - Inbox & Call Governance: Conduct daily spot-check audits of all customer service channels (emails, OpenPhone records, Hospitable/Cloudbeds inboxes) to ensure brand-standard tone, ultra-rapid response times, and total issue resolution.
 - Project Board Auditing: Execute the daily audit, update, and transfer of the  ----------  operational boards, ensuring complete visibility of active maintenance and tasks.
 - The Escalation Point: Act as the primary, high-tier phone escalation contact for complex guest issues. You don't just text; you pick up the phone, call the guest, deploy recovery strategies (e.g., ordering cookies, rapid maintenance dispatch), and transform a potential 2-star threat into a 5-star review.

*Weekly Governance & Coordination
 - Owner Alignment & Task Distribution: Lead the weekly owner alignment calls every Monday. Document all directives, map out weekly team priorities, assign roles, and distribute tasks seamlessly across the workforce.
 - Inter-Departmental Synergy: Collaborate weekly with the Revenue and Marketing teams to ensure maximum calendar occupancy, optimized pricing strategies, and flawless execution of top-tier bookings.
 - Logistics & Turnover Oversight: Supervise and streamline remote coordination with boots-on-the-ground cleaners, field handymen, and property inspectors.
 - Asset Protection: Oversee, document, and execute Airbnb damage claims and security deposit resolutions immediately following guest departures.

*Systems, Training & Scale
 - SOP Architecture: Author, maintain, and regularly optimize structured Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for every guest service, onboarding, and platform process.
 - Team Development: Exercise your hiring and firing authority to curate an elite team. Lead regular check-ins, conduct performance reviews, and run training refreshers for the 5+ virtual Guest Experience Specialists under your command.
 - AI & Workflow Automation: Partner with leadership to design, test, and implement AI agents, automated workflows, and software integrations to reduce manual administrative friction.

Expectations: 
*Industry Tenure: Minimum 3+ years of deeply hands-on experience within the Vacation Rental (STR) or Property Management industry, with explicit experience overseeing portfolios of 100+ units simultaneously.
*Leadership Background: Minimum 2+ years of direct experience managing, coaching, scheduling, and holding accountable remote/virtual teams of 5+ personnel. Must have experience holding hiring/firing authority.
*Advanced Tech & PMS Stack Mastery: Deep operational fluency using multiple Property Management Systems (PMS) concurrently. Cloudbeds experience is highly preferred, along with advanced knowledge of OwnerRez, Hospitable, PriceLabs, Slack,  ---------- , OpenPhone, and RingCentral.
*Smart Home Tech Literacy: Proven comfort navigating connected hardware ecosystems including Minut noise alerts, Ring Cameras, Nest Thermostats, Schlage locks, MyQ smart garages, and LastPass credential management.
*AI & Automation Forward: A proven track record of process improvement, displaying the ability to think critically about how to design workflows, leverage automations, and roll out AI agents to eliminate repetitive human labor.
*Secret Code Verification: Attention to detail is non-negotiable in this role. To prove you have read this entire specification, applicants must include the exact phrase [BOSSOPS-0526] in the subject line or first sentence of their application. 
*Communication Mastery: Exceptionally polished verbal and written English communication skills. You must possess the emotional intelligence required to calm an irate property owner or de-escalate an upset guest on a live phone call.

Wish List Items:
*Boutique Hotel Experience: Prior operational oversight within a boutique hotel setting, managing shared inventories or hybrid hotel/STR models.
*Revenue Management Experience: Familiarity with using tools to oversee the revenue of the portfolio, listing optimization, and maximizing the annual calendar. 


Salary: 
Position and Length: Contractor (Remote) 1-Year (12 months) 
Rate of Pay: $10.75 + Bonus
Schedule Hours Minimum: 45 Hours (Must Track Hours) 
Schedule Hours Maximum: 55 Hours (Anything Above Needs Approval) 
Agreed Schedule: TBD Eastern Time Zone 
Pay Schedule: Monthly or Bi-Weekly (With the submission of hours worked)

Growth Path: This role is built to scale alongside our portfolio. As we aggressively acquire more boutique hotels and vacation rental portfolios, the scope of this role will expand, offering clear paths to elevated compensation and leadership tier status. 

How To Apply:
Subject Line please add: HEAD VA STR
Please have an ID score of 70+
Please provide the secret code in the message 
Application Deadline:  June 12th, 2026
