title: Backend Software Engineer (AI Systems & Automation)
description:
Description
We're looking for an AI-enabled, product-minded Backend Software Engineer to strengthen the technical foundation of a global, digital-first higher education institution.

This is not a traditional backend role and it is not a purely internal one. More than 60% of your time will be spent working directly with a small number of high-value enterprise accounts through Slack and Zoom, camera on, alongside the business team and their product engineers. You will handle complex backend integrations, navigate regulated workflows, and design solutions for real client concerns in real time. The ability to communicate clearly and confidently on camera with technical counterparts at enterprise customers is not optional here.

The remaining time is spent strengthening the internal product: designing scalable systems, integrating AI into production workflows, and contributing to long-term architecture. AI usage must go beyond personal productivity. You need hands-on experience integrating AI into live systems, and practical experience using Claude Code or similar AI coding copilots in real development environments.

This role requires full alignment with US working hours.

Why You'll Want to Join
- You will be paid in USD (bi-monthly: every 15th and 30th)
- Paid Time Off in accordance with company policy
- Observance of Holidays per company guidelines
- 100% remote setup so you can work wherever you're most productive
- High-ownership engineering role inside a product-driven organization
- Opportunity to shape long-term architecture and AI-enabled workflows
- Direct collaboration with product, engineering, and enterprise clients

What You'll Work On
Enterprise Client Support and Technical Engagement
- Spend more than 60% of your time embedded with a small number of high-value enterprise accounts through Slack and Zoom
- Engage directly with technical counterparts at enterprise customers, camera on, to understand their needs and design solutions
- Handle complex backend integrations and regulated workflows on behalf of enterprise clients
- Translate client requirements into clear technical solutions and communicate tradeoffs confidently
- Collaborate with the business team and client-side product engineers to ensure solutions land correctly

AI-Integrated Product Architecture
- Design and implement backend systems that integrate AI directly into production workflows
- Use Claude Code or similar AI copilots as a core part of your engineering workflow
- Build AI-powered features that support core product functionality
- Identify automation opportunities across internal and client-facing systems
- Contribute to AI architecture decisions and long-term scalability planning

Backend System Design and Scalability
- Architect and build scalable, reliable backend services
- Design APIs and service layers that support complex product and client initiatives
- Work with distributed systems, databases, and cloud infrastructure
- Improve performance, reliability, and resilience across the product

End-to-End Product Ownership
- Take ownership of technical components from design through deployment and optimization
- Collaborate with product and design teams to translate user needs into robust technical solutions
- Proactively identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and architectural risks
- Contribute to long-term roadmap planning and infrastructure decisions

Engineering Standards and Team Contribution
- Write clean, maintainable, well-tested code
- Contribute to CI/CD pipelines, observability, and monitoring improvements
- Participate in architectural discussions and technical trade-off decisions
- Support junior and mid-level engineers through thoughtful code reviews and mentorship

What You Bring
- 4+ years of professional software engineering experience in a product-focused startup environment
- Backend-leaning engineering profile with comfort pushing full stack end-to-end when the work calls for it
- Demonstrated experience integrating AI into production systems and workflows
- Practical experience using Claude Code, strongly preferred, or comparable AI coding copilots in real development environments
- Proven ability to communicate clearly and confidently on camera with technical counterparts at enterprise or high-value accounts
- Strong system design fundamentals including databases, service architectures, and distributed systems
- Experience working in fast-paced or scaling startups
- Background in product companies rather than services, agency, or consulting-heavy environments
- Strong ownership mindset with ability to work autonomously
- Full availability during US business hours

Preferred but not strictly required:
- Experience with TypeScript, Python, or Java
- Exposure to GCP, Spanner, Redis, or similar cloud infrastructure
- Strong academic background from a reputable university

Nice to Have
- Experience building AI-powered user-facing features
- Background in EdTech or regulated industries
- Experience contributing to architectural modernization efforts
- Prior mentorship or technical leadership experience

How to Apply
Please include:
1. Your updated resume
2. A short Loom video (1 to 2 minutes) describing a production system where you integrated AI into backend architecture, how you used Claude Code or similar tools in your development process, and an example of a time you worked directly with a client or enterprise stakeholder to solve a technical problem

Only candidates who submit a Loom video will be considered.

If you are a backend-leaning engineer who integrates AI into real systems, communicates confidently with enterprise clients on camera, and thrives in fast-moving product environments, this role offers architectural influence, direct client impact, and meaningful technical ownership.

Application Process Overview
Our comprehensive selection process ensures we find the right fit for both you and our clients:
1. Initial Application - Submit your application and complete our prequalifying questions
2. Video Introduction - Record an video introduction to showcase your communication skills and work experience
3. Role-Specific Assessment - Complete a homework assignment tailored to the position (if applicable)
4. Recruitment Interview - Initial screening with our talent team
5. Executive Interview - Meet with senior leadership to discuss role alignment
6. Client Interview - Final interview with the client team you'd be supporting
7. Background & Reference Check - Professional reference verification
8. Job Offer - Successful candidates receive a formal offer to join the team

Each stage is designed to evaluate your fit for the role while giving you insights into our company culture and expectations. 

We'll keep you informed throughout the process and provide feedback at each step.

title: Backend Software Engineer (AI Systems & Automation)
description:
We are hiring an AI-enabled, product-oriented Backend Software Engineer to reinforce the core engineering backbone of a global, digital-first higher education platform.

This is a hybrid engineering and client-facing technical role, not a traditional backend position. A significant portion of your time will be spent directly engaging with a small set of high-value enterprise customers through Slack and Zoom. You will operate in real-time technical environments, collaborating with client-side engineers and internal stakeholders to deliver production-grade solutions across complex integrations and regulated workflows.

Clear, confident, camera-on communication with senior technical counterparts is a core operating requirement, not an optional skill.

The remainder of your time will focus on strengthening and evolving the internal platform by building scalable backend systems, embedding AI into production workflows, and shaping long-term architectural direction. AI is expected to be deeply integrated into your engineering practice, including hands-on use of tools such as Claude Code or equivalent AI coding assistants in real production contexts, not only for productivity but for system-level implementation.

This role requires full alignment with US business hours.

Why This Role Exists

Compensation is in USD and paid twice monthly on the 15th and 30th. The role includes standard paid time off and company holidays. It is fully remote, enabling flexible work from any location. The position offers high autonomy within a product-led engineering organization and direct exposure to enterprise customers operating in real-world production environments. It also provides the opportunity to influence long-term system architecture and AI-driven workflows.

Core Responsibilities
Enterprise Client Engineering and Technical Delivery

You will dedicate more than 60 percent of your time to direct engagement with enterprise accounts through Slack and Zoom. You will act as a technical bridge between client engineering teams and internal product engineering, designing and implementing backend integrations within regulated and high-complexity environments. You will translate enterprise requirements into robust, production-ready technical solutions while clearly communicating design decisions, constraints, and trade-offs with senior technical stakeholders.

AI-Native System Development

You will design and deliver backend systems with AI embedded into production workflows. You will leverage Claude Code or comparable AI engineering copilots as part of your daily development execution. You will build and enhance AI-powered product capabilities while identifying and operationalizing automation opportunities across both internal and client-facing systems. You will also contribute to evolving AI architecture standards and long-term system design direction.

Scalable Backend Architecture

You will design and maintain resilient backend services and APIs, working across distributed systems, databases, and cloud-native infrastructure. You will improve system reliability, performance, and operational observability while contributing to architectural decisions that impact long-term platform scalability.

End-to-End Product Ownership

You will own features and systems from design through deployment and iteration. You will collaborate closely with product and design teams to translate requirements into engineering execution. You will proactively identify system bottlenecks, risks, and inefficiencies while participating in roadmap planning and technical prioritization.

Engineering Excellence and Team Contribution

You will deliver clean, maintainable, and well-tested code while strengthening CI/CD pipelines, monitoring systems, and observability practices. You will participate in architectural reviews and technical decision-making and support other engineers through code reviews and mentorship.

Required Experience and Capabilities

You will bring at least four years of software engineering experience in product-driven environments, with a strong backend orientation and the ability to operate full-stack when required. You will have prior experience engaging directly with enterprise or high-value technical clients and a proven track record of integrating AI into production systems beyond experimental use. You will have hands-on experience with Claude Code or equivalent AI-assisted development workflows and strong system design capability across distributed systems, APIs, and data layers. You will be comfortable operating in fast-paced, scaling startup environments with a strong ownership mindset and the ability to work independently. Full availability during US business hours is required.

Preferred Qualifications

Preference will be given to candidates proficient in TypeScript, Python, or Java, with experience in cloud infrastructure such as GCP, Spanner, Redis, or similar platforms. A strong academic background or equivalent technical foundation is advantageous, as is exposure to regulated industries or EdTech environments.

Advantageous Experience

Additional value is recognized in candidates who have built AI-powered user-facing features in production, contributed to platform modernization or large-scale refactoring initiatives, demonstrated technical leadership or mentorship capability, or operated in high-accountability, customer-facing engineering roles.

Application Process

To be defined.

title: Amazon Wholesale Brand Outreach Specialist / Sourcing VA
description:
We are looking for an experienced Amazon wholesale VA to help us secure profitable brand-direct and authorized wholesale accounts. This is not retail arbitrage, online arbitrage, or lead scraping. We need someone who can independently research brands, contact decision-makers, open accounts, and analyze products for Amazon profitability.

Responsibilities
Find brand-direct and authorized wholesale opportunities.
Use tools like SmartScout, Keepa, SellerAmp, Helium 10, or similar.
Contact brands and distributors by email.
Follow up consistently until an account is approved or declined.
Complete wholesale applications and submit required business documents.
Review price lists and calculate profit, ROI, sales rank, competition, Buy Box consistency, and sales velocity.
Maintain an organized outreach tracker with weekly updates.
Requirements
Proven Amazon wholesale sourcing experience.
Experience opening brand-direct or authorized wholesale accounts.
Strong understanding of MAP policies, brand restrictions, Amazon compliance, and resale authorization.
Ability to analyze wholesale price lists and identify profitable SKUs.
SmartScout experience strongly preferred.
Fluent written and spoken English.
Professional email communication skills.
Existing brand relationships or direct supplier contacts are a major plus.

This is not an entry-level role. Applicants without proof of experience will not be considered.

Compensation
Base pay: $1/hour
Bonus 1: $150 after we review the brand, confirm profitability, and approve moving forward.
Bonus 2: $350 once the approved wholesale ASIN reaches $500 in cumulative Amazon sales.
Total potential bonus: $500 per approved wholesale contract.
Example:

2 approved wholesale contracts = $1,000 in bonuses
5 approved wholesale contracts = $2,500 in bonuses

Only legitimate, profitable brand-direct or authorized wholesale accounts qualify. Brands with thin margins, poor sales velocity, heavy competition, or no clear ROI will not count.

To Apply

Please include:

A summary of your Amazon wholesale experience.
Tools you have used.
Three outreach emails you personally wrote.
Proof of past wholesale approvals or supplier accounts you helped secure.
A short explanation of how you analyze a wholesale price list.

title: Amazon FBA Virtual Assistant (Online Arbitrage & Data Entry)
description:
Amazon FBA Virtual Assistant (Online Arbitrage & Data Entry)

Company: JKM Exchange LLC
Compensation: $400/month for 40 hours per week
Work Setup: Remote / Work From Home
Required Schedule: Monday – Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM Philippine Time (PHT). Note: This maps exactly to Sunday – Thursday, 7:00 PM to 4:00 AM Central Time to ensure you are sourcing during our peak marketplace listing drops.

Job Overview

We are a growing Amazon FBA retail arbitrage business looking for a highly detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to manage our product sourcing data, inventory logging, and shipment prep tracking. You will be responsible for validating profitable leads, ensuring our accounting software is perfectly up to date, and communicating our inbound shipments to our prep center.
Your Daily Responsibilities

Sourcing & Validation: Pull potential product leads using Replencatcher and Keepa Product Finder during peak marketplace hours.
Keepa Analysis: Review Keepa charts for price stability, BSR drops, and competition levels to validate profitable buys.

Inventory Data Entry: Accurately log every purchased item into InventoryLab (recording cost of item, unit number, date purchased, item condition, estimated resale price, and target ROI).
Prep Center Tracking: Update our prep center spreadsheet for every inbound order (logging item title, unit quantity, tracking number, order ID, and item condition).

FBA Shipment Routing: Prepare FBA batches in Amazon Seller Central and send the final shipment routing requests to our prep center.

Required Tools & Software Experience

Keepa (specifically Keepa Product Finder and chart analysis)
Replencatcher (We can teach you)
InventoryLab
Amazon Seller Central
Google Sheets / Excel

Requirements

Prior experience analyzing Keepa charts for Amazon FBA Online Arbitrage.
Strong attention to detail—data entry errors cost the business money.
Excellent written English for clear daily communication.
Reliable high-speed internet and a quiet workspace.
Ability to strictly work the required Monday-Friday PHT morning schedule.

How to Apply
Please send your resume and a brief overview of your experience using Keepa and InventoryLab. To ensure you have read this entire job description and possess the attention to detail required for this role, please include the phrase Q4 Arbitrage in the subject line of your message. Applications without this exact phrase in the subject line will be deleted unread.

title: Senior CAD Technician / CAD Department Head
description:
Philippine-Based Team | Full-Time

About Us:
We are a small, vibrant structural engineering firm based in the USA. We are building a talented, collaborative team based in the Philippines, and we invest heavily in custom workflow automation to help our drafters work smarter, not harder.

The Role:
We are looking for a Senior CAD Technician to serve as our CAD Department Head. You will establish our drafting standards and coordinate closely with our existing CAD technician and our all-Philippine team, reporting directly to the American owner. This is a hands-on leadership role for someone who wants to organize and run a department from the ground up.

What You’ll Do:
Establish and maintain company-wide Revit sheet templates, standard details, title blocks, and BIM protocols
Lead structural detailing for wood, steel, and concrete buildings
Serve as the primary point of contact for all CAD coordination between the Philippine team and US-based ownership
Review and QA drawings to ensure consistency with our developing standards
Mentor and coordinate with our current CAD technician as the department grows
Collaborate with our in-house software engineer to identify workflow bottlenecks and test Python-based automation scripts tailored to our projects

What You’ll Need:
5+ years of professional experience in Revit, preferably within structural engineering
Proven experience detailing wood, steel, and concrete structures
Demonstrated ability to set up sheet templates, view standards, and company detail libraries from scratch
Strong working knowledge of the Imperial measurement system (feet and inches) as used in US construction
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills, with comfort coordinating across time zones with an American owner.
Must be available for daily all-employee meetings during US work hours.
Reliable high-speed internet and a professional remote-work environment

Nice to Have:
Prior team lead, senior drafter, or department head experience
Familiarity with Dynamo, Revit API, or Python-based BIM automation
Knowledge of US structural drawing standards and typical US construction practices

What We Offer:
A key leadership role with real ownership over company standards in a growing firm
Direct support from a dedicated software engineer who builds custom Python tools to automate your workflows
A collaborative, small-team culture where your input directly shapes how we work
Stable, long-term remote employment with a Philippine-based team

To Apply:
Please send your resume, a brief cover letter describing your experience leading CAD standards or teams, and a sample drawing set or portfolio (PDF) demonstrating your Revit and structural detailing work.

title: Social Media & Content Manager for Home Improvement Brands + Personal Brand
description:
We are looking for a sharp, organized, creative Social Media & Content Manager to support multiple home improvement brands and a founder/personal brand.

This role starts part-time at approximately 25–40 hours per month, with the opportunity to grow into a larger part-time or full-time role for the right person.

This is not an agency role. We are looking for one dedicated person who wants to grow with us, learn our brands deeply, manage a content system, and help improve performance over time.

You will not work directly with our companies. You will report to and work closely with our Marketing Director/Founder. You may also have a direct report or support tea ---------- mber helping with execution, so you must be able to organize work clearly, review quality, give direction, and keep projects moving.

We own and operate several home improvement and construction-related companies, including outdoor living, turf, pavers, fencing, architecture/design, and a parent company brand. We also need support repurposing content for a founder/personal brand.

Some brands need consistent, professional presence. Other brands will be focused on serious growth, stronger reporting, better content quality, better engagement, and performance-based improvement.

Responsibilities:

* Manage social media content across multiple home improvement brands
* Maintain an updated content calendar for each assigned brand
* Create, repurpose, schedule, and publish content across Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and Stories
* Manage 3 posts per week for some brands and 5–7 posts per week for others depending on brand goals
* Create a variety of content styles, including reels, carousels, stories, before/after posts, project spotlights, funny/personality-based content, educational posts, testimonials, local trust posts, FAQs, offers, and promotional content
* Write original captions that match the brand voice and do not sound generic or AI-written
* Understand the voice, tone, audience, and positioning of each brand
* Help create and maintain brand style guides for each brand
* Repurpose founder/personal brand content into LinkedIn carousels, Instagram story slides, and other supporting assets
* Edit basic reels and short-form videos using current editing styles
* Create clean social graphics and carousels using Canva or similar tools
* Schedule content through SocialPilot, Metricool, or another scheduler we choose
* Monitor engagement, comments, DMs, and platform activity
* Flag important comments, DMs, reviews, or engagement opportunities
* Support basic community engagement and audience interaction when appropriate
* Track performance and prepare weekly/monthly reports
* Identify what content is working and recommend what to test next
* Use ManyChat or similar automation tools for engagement, lead capture, comment automation, and DM workflows when needed
* Use AI tools to move faster, improve ideas, organize content, and repurpose content without making the final output sound like AI
* Stay current on social media trends, hooks, editing styles, audio trends, platform updates, automation tools, and content formats
* Organize assets by brand, project, date, and content type
* Communicate clearly about missing assets, approvals, deadlines, and priorities
* Help manage a direct report or support person if assigned, including giving clear instructions and reviewing output before it is submitted

What We’re Looking For:

* Strong English writing skills
* Strong understanding of Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, Reels, Stories, and carousels
* Experience creating original content, captions, hooks, and content angles
* Ability to understand and adapt to different brand voices
* Good eye for design, layout, content flow, and visual consistency
* Experience with Canva, CapCut, SocialPilot, Metricool, ManyChat, and/or similar tools
* Comfortable using AI tools in a smart, practical way
* Strong understanding of engagement and how to create content that gets comments, saves, shares, DMs, clicks, or inquiries
* Ability to report on performance and explain what the numbers mean
* Current knowledge of social media trends and editing styles
* Strong organization and follow-through
* Ability to manage multiple brands without getting confused
* Ability to maintain content calendars and meet deadlines
* Comfortable working inside systems and improving them over time
* Able to manage or coordinate with a support person/direct report
* Willing to receive feedback and improve quickly
* Experience with home improvement, construction, real estate, architecture, design, local services, founder brands, or personal brands is a strong plus

This role is for someone who is both creative and operational.

We do not need someone who only posts content to keep pages active. We want someone who can help create better content, improve systems, increase engagement, watch performance, and grow with the company.

Some brands will be simple presence work. Others will have incentive opportunities tied to consistency, quality, engagement, reporting, and measurable growth.

To apply, please answer the following:

1. What is your hourly rate?
2. How many hours per month are you currently available?
3. Are you open to growing into more hours or full-time work if the fit is right?
4. How many other clients do you currently support?
5. How do you typically work with clients or marketing teams?
6. Have you ever managed a direct report, assistant, or support person? If yes, what did you manage?
7. What social media results are you most proud of?
8. What brands, creators, or accounts do you follow for inspiration?
9. What tools do you use for design, scheduling, editing, reporting, automation, and AI?
10. What experience do you have with ManyChat or other automation tools?
11. How do you use AI in your content process without making content sound AI-written?
12. How do you stay current on trends, editing styles, hooks, and platform changes?
13. Share 3–5 examples of posts, reels, carousels, stories, or campaigns you created or managed.
14. Tell us about a time you improved a client’s content quality, engagement, or results.
15. How do you stay organized when managing multiple brands and content calendars?
16. How would you approach creating a brand style guide for a company?
17. What would your first 30 days look like if you were hired for this role?
18. Put the word “Sandbox” in your subject line so we know you read the full post.

We will ask shortlisted candidates to complete a paid test assignment.

description:
HOW TO APPLY

We’re hiring an SEO & AI Search Specialist to grow organic visibility and revenue across traditional search and AI-driven answer engines. You’ll own strategy and execution for our Shopify stores— ----------  and  ---------- —covering technical SEO, content, entity/structured data, link acquisition, and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO)—earning inclusion and citations in Google AI Overviews, Bing Copilot, Perplexity, and ChatGPT/Gemini.

You’re excited because you will...

Strategy & Ownership
- Build and execute an integrated SEO + GEO roadmap tied to revenue for priority categories (sheet sets, duvet covers, quilts, decorative pillows).
- Define and track a query set for SERPs and AI answers; report share-of-voice and citation/inclusion rate in AI Overviews/answer engines.

On-Site & Technical
- Audit and improve site architecture, internal linking, faceted navigation, canonicals, and crawl budget for Shopify.
- Elevate Core Web Vitals and image optimization for PDPs/PLPs.
- Implement and maintain JSON-LD schema (Product, Offer, Review, Breadcrumb, Organization, FAQ, ImageObject) to strengthen entity understanding.

Content & Entity Building
- Create entity-first, evidence-backed content answer engines trust: buyer’s guides, comparisons, FAQs, care/how-to, sizing explainers, collection hubs.
- Ensure product attribute completeness (materials, thread count, fill, sizes, care, style) so AI systems can recommend or cite our pages.
- Collaborate with brand/editorial to publish on-brand copy optimized for humans and LLMs.

GEO-Specific Initiatives
- Test AI Overview/answer inclusion tactics (structured data, Q&A blocks, citations, author/entity pages, first-party images).
- Monitor and improve brand citation rate in answers for commercial and informational intents.
- Manage AI crawler permissions and robots directives (GPTBot, Perplexity, CommonCrawl) with Engineering/IT.

Off-Site & Authority
- Build a white-hat digital PR/backlink program with home & design publications, blogs, and partner sites.
- Strengthen E-E-A-T signals (author bios, brand story, policies, reviews/UGC, press mentions).

Analytics & Experimentation
- Run experiments; measure impact in GA4 & GSC with dashboards (Looker Studio).
- Prepare periodic reports and present insights and next steps to stakeholders.

90-Day Success
- Baseline audits completed (technical, content, entities, AI answer presence) and a prioritized 6-month roadmap delivered.
- Quick wins shipped: schema fixes, internal linking improvements, attribute completeness for top SKUs/collections, and one entity-driven content hub launched.
- GEO scorecard established (priority queries, inclusion/citation rate, answer snippets captured, supporting pages).

6–12 Month Outcomes
- Material growth in non-brand organic revenue and category rankings.
- Sustained inclusion/citation in AI answers for target queries across core categories.
- Repeatable GEO playbook embedded in content and merchandising workflows.

Job Requirements
- Proven SEO chops in eCommerce (preferably Shopify) with measurable revenue impact.
- Hands-on GEO experience (or a strong POV) optimizing for AI Overviews and answer engines: structuring content for direct answers, citations, and entity confidence.
- Proficiency with Ahrefs/Similar, Google Search Console, GA4, and Shopify; comfort with HTML/Liquid basics and JSON-LD.
- Ability to plan and execute on-brand digital PR/backlink programs.
- Strong analytical skills; can turn data into decisions and roadmaps.
- Excellent English writing/editing skills (you can own outreach and on-site copy).
- High ownership, bias to action, and communication via email, Slack, Zoom, and phone.
- Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience).
- Belief in our company mission and embodiment of our values.

Nice to have
- Experience with product feeds (Google Merchant Center), reviews/UGC integration, and PDP/PLP template optimization.
- Familiarity with log-file analysis, bot identification, and AI crawler management.
- Comfort building dashboards in Looker Studio or similar.

ABOUT HIEND ACCENTS
HiEnd Accents designs and creates everyday luxury bedding, linens, sheeting, decorative pillows, and other home textiles. Founded in 2005 and based in Dallas, Texas, our mission is to elevate customers' lives through beautifully designed home textiles that deliver quality and value.

As a family-owned business, we are committed to personal service and long-term relationships. Through our four brands, we offer unique designs across a spectrum of styles and price points, all united by exceptional value. Combined with our customer-first approach, prompt shipping, and low order minimums, we have become the preferred bedding supplier for thousands of top department stores, furniture retailers, interior designers, and boutique hotels nationwide. Recognized on the 2024 INC 5000 list as one of the fastest-growing businesses in the US, we continue to innovate and expand our reach in the home textiles market.

title: Funnel Builder & Content Strategist
description:
We are a US-based business with two active project streams — online brand and funnel marketing, and book and social media content. We are looking for one skilled person who can handle both. This starts as a project-based hire with strong potential for an ongoing retainer if the work is excellent.
Project 1 — Sales funnel build
First priority. Full funnel to be built inside GoHighLevel, ready for paid traffic.

7 Funnel pages (opt-in, sales, upsell, thank-you, etc.)
4 Digital products configured in GHL
10 Digital bonuses created and attached
22 Emails — buyer sequence (15) + abandoned cart (7)
10–15 Ad creatives ready for launch
1 Full GHL integration — live and ready for traffic

Low-ticket entry offer ($47–$97) with a high-ticket upsell. Target audience: US entrepreneurs and business owners. Timeline: fully live within 13–15 days of kickoff.

Project 2 — Book & social media support
Ongoing support for an author and content creator targeting a US audience.

Social media content creation and scheduling (Instagram, Facebook, possibly YouTube)
Book marketing support — landing pages, lead magnets, promotional content

Graphics and visual content for posts and promotions
Consistent brand voice across all platforms
Skills & tools required
GoHighLevel (GHL) — fluent, not just familiar
Sales funnel strategy and conversion copywriting for US audiences

Email automation — nurture sequences, buyer flows, abandoned cart
Social media content creation — writing, graphics, scheduling
Ad creative design — Facebook and Instagram formats
Strong written English — your copy must sound native to a US reader
Self-directed — you can manage your own deadlines without daily supervision

We read every application carefully. Answer every question. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

How we work — requirements before you apply
Screen tracking
We use the onlinejobs.ph built-in time tracker with screen recording. This is mandatory and non-negotiable.
Payment via onlinejobs.ph
All payment is processed through the onlinejobs.ph system. We do not pay outside the platform.

Daily communication

We expect a check-in every working day — a brief update on progress, blockers, or questions. No ghosting.
Response time
All messages must be answered within 24 hours. During active project phases, same-day responses are expected.
We are respectful, communicative clients. We give clear feedback and make fast decisions. We expect the same in return. This is a partnership, not a transaction.

English requirement
C2 level English is required — both written and spoken. The copy, emails, and content you produce will be read by US audiences. It must be fluent, natural, and persuasive. If your English is below C2, please do not apply. We will assess this during the interview.

In your application, write your answers to the questions below in your own words — do not use a template or ai (We know the patterns of ai and can tell. -This doesn't mean we won't allow you to work with ai for the job. It means we need to know you have good English comprehension and communication skills.). Your writing is part of the evaluation.


Question 1 — Work samples
Share 2–3 live links to funnels or landing pages you personally built. For each one: what does it sell, who is the target audience, and what was your specific role?

Applications without live links are automatically declined.

Question 2 — GoHighLevel experience
How long have you worked in GoHighLevel and what have you specifically built inside it — funnels, automations, email sequences, pipelines? Be specific.

GHL fluency is required. Vague answers tell us you are not the right fit.

Question 3 — Copywriting
Who writes the copy for your funnel pages and email sequences — you or the client? If you write copy, share one example of a sales page or email sequence you wrote.

Copy is what converts. We need to know this upfront.

Question 4 — Social media & content work
Share 2–3 examples of social media content or book marketing work you have done. What platforms, what type of content, and what were the results if you know them?

This role covers both funnel building and content. We need to see both sides of your skill set.

Question 5 — Availability
Can you start within the next 7 days? What is your realistic timeline to complete the funnel project? Do you have other active clients that could affect your availability?

We move fast and need someone who can match that pace.

Question 6 — Your fees
What is your fixed fee for the funnel project (Project 1)? And what would you charge per month for ongoing content and marketing support (Project 2)? Give us specific numbers and what is included.

No ranges. One number for each. Transparency upfront is a green flag for us.

No live work samples = no interview. We are serious buyers looking for a long-term partner. Prove your work first, then let's talk.

Qualifications

title: Fund Accountant
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented Fund Accountant to support financial operations for investment vehicles within our asset management organization. This role will focus on fund reporting, close activities, audit and tax coordination, and oversight of key deliverables prepared with external service providers. The ideal candidate brings strong investment accounting knowledge, sound analytical ability, and a disciplined approach to meeting recurring reporting deadlines.

Responsibilities:
• Produce and evaluate quarterly fund financial statements to ensure accuracy, completeness, and alignment with applicable accounting and reporting standards.
• Lead monthly close coordination by working closely with the fund administrator and internal investment accounting partners to keep deliverables on schedule.
• Examine supporting schedules and investor-related calculations, including fee allocations, distribution models, and statement packages, for quality and consistency.
• Oversee recurring and ad hoc investor reporting requests by partnering with external administrators and internal relationship teams to deliver timely supplemental information.
• Support the completion of annual audits across multiple investment entities by organizing documentation, addressing requests, and facilitating communication with auditors.
• Partner with external tax advisors on data gathering, analysis, filings, registrations, and review of fund tax return materials.
• Act as a key contact for compliance matters related to investment structures, coordinating with asset managers and outside tax professionals as needed.
• Prepare and maintain quarterly benchmark reporting, variance analysis, and other fund-level performance support materials.
• Review quarterly debt compliance reporting and contribute accounting support for acquisitions, transactions, and other investment activity.
• 3+ years of experience in fund accounting, investment accounting, or a closely related finance role.
• Background working with fund administrators, investment portfolios, and period-end close processes.
• Knowledge of financial statement preparation, external reporting, and annual reporting requirements for investment vehicles.
• Experience reviewing calculations and reporting tied to management fees, investor allocations, or similar fund activity.
• Ability to coordinate effectively with auditors, tax advisors, and cross-functional internal stakeholders.
• Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with close attention to detail.
• Proficiency in managing multiple deadlines and maintaining accuracy in a fast-paced reporting environment.

title: Advanced Python Developer
description:
Job Title: Python Developer - Edge Device Systems, Reporting & Dashboards

Job Summary: We are seeking a talented Python Developer to join our team as an expert in building systems for edge devices, reporting, and dashboards. As a Python Developer, you will be responsible for developing robust and scalable software solutions that enable efficient data collection, processing, and visualization from edge devices. Your expertise will contribute to building reliable systems that provide real-time insights and actionable intelligence. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, including hardware engineers, data scientists, and product managers, to deliver cutting-edge solutions in the rapidly evolving field of edge computing.

Responsibilities:

Design and develop Python-based software systems for edge devices, focusing on data collection, processing, and analysis.

Build scalable and efficient data pipelines that collect and transmit data from edge devices to centralized repositories.

Develop data processing algorithms to clean, transform, and enrich data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality and integrity.

Design and implement real-time monitoring and alerting mechanisms to ensure the timely detection of anomalies and issues in edge device data.

Collaborate with hardware engineers to integrate software components with edge device firmware and hardware interfaces.

Create reporting and dashboard solutions that visualize edge device data in a clear and actionable manner, using Python-based frameworks and libraries.

Work closely with data scientists to incorporate machine learning and AI algorithms into edge device systems, enabling advanced analytics and predictive capabilities.

Optimize performance and efficiency of edge device systems, considering resource-constrained environments and limited network connectivity.

Collaborate with product managers and stakeholders to gather requirements, provide technical guidance, and deliver high-quality software solutions.

Stay updated with the latest advancements in edge computing, IoT technologies, and data visualization techniques, and apply them to enhance edge device systems.

Conduct thorough testing, debugging, and performance tuning of Python-based systems to ensure reliability, scalability, and security.

Create and maintain technical documentation, including system design, API documentation, and user guides.

Requirements:

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.

Proven experience as a Python Developer, with a focus on building systems for edge devices, reporting, and dashboards.

Strong proficiency in Python programming and knowledge of relevant frameworks and libraries (e.g., Flask, Django, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib).

Experience in designing and developing data collection and processing pipelines, preferably in resource-constrained environments.

Familiarity with edge computing concepts and technologies, including edge device architectures, protocols, and connectivity options.

Solid understanding of data visualization techniques and experience with visualization libraries (e.g., Plotly, Dash, Bokeh).

Knowledge of database systems (SQL and/or NoSQL) and proficiency in writing efficient database queries and data manipulation operations.

Experience with integrating edge devices with cloud-based platforms and services (e.g., AWS IoT, Azure IoT) is desirable.

Familiarity with machine learning and AI concepts, and the ability to collaborate effectively with data scientists.

Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work in a fast-paced, agile development environment.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams.

Knowledge of version control systems, automated testing frameworks, and DevOps practices is a plus.

As a Python Developer specializing in edge device systems, reporting, and dashboards, you will play a crucial role in delivering intelligent solutions that leverage data from the edge. If you are passionate about working on cutting-edge technologies and enjoy building scalable and reliable systems, we look forward to hearing from you.

title: Boot Systems Developer
description:
Job Description: x86 System Boot Loader Designer

Position Overview: We are seeking a talented and experienced x86/ARM System Boot Loader Designer to join our team. As a Boot Loader Designer, you will play a critical role in developing and designing the software responsible for initializing x86/ARM-based computer systems during the boot process. Your expertise in low-level programming, firmware development, system architecture, and familiarity with Linux Kernel, Linux containers, and FlexBuild tool will contribute to ensuring reliable and efficient booting operations.

Responsibilities:

Develop and design the boot loader software for x86/ARM-based computer systems, including BIOS and UEFI firmware.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including hardware engineers, firmware developers, and system architects, to define boot requirements and specifications.

Conduct thorough research on system booting technologies and stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and advancements.

Implement low-level programming and optimization techniques to enhance boot performance and reduce boot time.

Customize boot loaders to support specific hardware configurations and system requirements.

Debug and troubleshoot boot-related issues, both independently and in collaboration with the firmware development team.

Conduct thorough testing and validation of the boot loader software to ensure its reliability and compatibility with target systems.

Document design specifications, architectural diagrams, and code structures for future reference and knowledge sharing.

Collaborate with the software engineering team to integrate the boot loader with the operating system and other software components.

Provide technical support and guidance to internal teams and external stakeholders regarding boot loader-related queries and issues.

Qualifications:

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field.

Strong understanding of x86 architecture and system booting processes.

Proficiency in low-level programming languages, such as Assembly and C/C++.

Experience with BIOS and UEFI firmware development, including familiarity with related standards and protocols.

In-depth knowledge of boot loader technologies, including boot stages, boot protocols, and boot loader customization.

Familiarity with system-level debugging tools, such as JTAG, GDB, and kernel debuggers.

Experience with firmware development tools, such as Intel System Studio, UEFI Development Kit (UDK), or similar.

Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to debug complex boot-related issues.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work effectively within cross-functional teams.

Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and meet project deadlines.

Preferred Qualifications:

Prior experience in designing boot loaders for embedded systems or real-time operating systems.

Knowledge of security features and considerations related to booting and firmware development.

Familiarity with virtualization technologies, such as Intel VT-x or AMD-V.

Experience with Linux kernel development, Linux containers, and knowledge of containerization technologies like Docker or Kubernetes.

Familiarity with the FlexBuild tool for building Linux systems or similar build automation tools.

Join our team and contribute to the development of cutting-edge x86-based computer systems. As an x86/ARM System Boot Loader Designer, you will have the opportunity to work on complex firmware development projects and play a vital role in shaping the booting experience of our products, leveraging your knowledge and experience with Linux Kernel, Linux containers, and FlexBuild tool.

title: Work Process Automation Specialist - Gemini / ChatGPT
description:
About the Role
We are looking for a forward-thinking Workflow Automation Specialist based in the Philippines to join our international team. Your primary mission is to eliminate repetitive tasks, connect our software ecosystem, and embed AI directly into our daily operations.

In this role, you won’t just be building standard "if-this-then-that" recipes. You will design intelligent workflows that leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) like Google Gemini or OpenAI to parse data, categorize customer inquiries, and make smart routing decisions autonomously.

What You’ll Do
Design & Build: Develop, test, and deploy end-to-end automated workflows connecting cloud applications (e.g., CRMs, project management tools, communication platforms) using  ----------  or Zapier.

AI Integration: Architect workflows that use Gemini API or other LLMs for intelligent tasks like automated email drafting, populate order inputs, and product listing template.

title: Business Development Representative
description:
Company Overview

The Compliance Services Organization didn’t exist. We built it. 

MP1 Solutions pioneered the Compliance Services Organization (CSO) — a new category of operating partner built to replace the fragmented vendor model that has defined compliance programs for decades. Built on the foundation of MedPro Disposal, the nation’s largest privately-held medical waste company serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states over the past 15 years, MP1 Solutions extends that operational infrastructure, vendor network, and customer trust into a category we’re actively building — and leading. 

What we do: We operate as the single compliance partner for non-acute healthcare facilities — consolidating every obligation into one coordinated program, assigning one dedicated account manager as the point of accountability, and actively managing the underlying vendor ecosystem through continuous evaluation, negotiation, and optimization on the practice’s behalf. 

Why it matters: Regulated facilities across the country are managing compliance the same fragmented way they always have. We’re changing that — and we’re looking for people who want to help build what comes next. 

Our technology: We’re actively building the AI infrastructure that sits behind this model — automating compliance tracking, surfacing risks before they become problems, and creating a system that gets smarter with every client we add. 

Our impact: Our clients never have to worry about something falling through the cracks — obligations are coordinated, tracked, and documented in one place. We audit costs, eliminate overcharges, and deliver proof of compliance that always keeps practices inspection ready. 

Position Overview:

The  ---------- nt a client signs with MedPro Disposal or MP1 Solutions is the  ---------- nt their experience with us truly begins — and the MP1 BDR owns introducing our additional ancillaries with our existing client base. title: Head of Contact Center Operations - Remote
description:
Company Overview

The Compliance Services Organization didn't exist. We built it.

MP1 Solutions pioneered the Compliance Services Organization (CSO) — a new category of operating partner built to replace the fragmented vendor model that has defined compliance programs for decades. Built on the foundation of MedPro Disposal, the nation's largest privately-held medical waste company serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states over the past 15 years, MP1 Solutions extends that operational infrastructure, vendor network, and customer trust into a category we're actively building — and leading. We're doing it with a technology-forward approach, actively developing the AI infrastructure that will make this model smarter, faster, and more scalable as we grow.

What we do: We operate as the single compliance partner for non-acute healthcare facilities — consolidating every obligation into one coordinated program, assigning one dedicated account manager as the point of accountability, and actively managing the underlying vendor ecosystem through continuous evaluation, negotiation, and optimization on the practice's behalf.

Why it matters: Regulated facilities across the country are managing compliance the same fragmented way they always have. We're changing that — and we're looking for people who want to help build what comes next.

Our technology: We're actively building the AI infrastructure that sits behind this model — automating compliance tracking, surfacing risks before they become problems, and creating a system that gets smarter with every client we add. If you want to build at the intersection of compliance and technology, you're in the right place.

Our impact: Our clients never have to worry about something falling through the cracks — obligations are coordinated, tracked, and documented in one place. We audit costs, eliminate overcharges, and deliver proof of compliance that always keeps practices inspection ready. Every client has one dedicated account manager who owns their program end to end. And unlike a traditional vendor relationship, our model is designed to get better over time — continuously re-evaluated, optimized, and improved with every client we serve.

Position Overview:
The Head of Contact Center Operations is the senior execution leader of MedPro and MP1's contact center — the person who makes the operation run, improves it continuously, and manages the managers who manage the floor. You report to the VP of Operations and translate their strategic direction into daily operational reality.

This role sits at the intersection of operator and thought partner. You have done this before: run high-volume, multi-channel service environments, built capacity plans between labor and systems, designed operating models from the ground up, and led teams through system migrations without losing service continuity. You are not here to hold the org steady — you are here to elevate it.

Where the VP of Operations sets the vision, you own delivery. When the VP of Operations needs to present performance to the COO, the data, the narrative, and the operational confidence behind it come from you. When the organization needs its service processes redesigned, its CRM environment assessed, or its team structure rebuilt — that work belongs to you.

Key Responsibilities:
Multi-Layer Team Leadership & People Development
• Lead, coach, and develop Operations Managers and Team Leads — you manage the managers, and they manage the floor. Build leaders who build others.
• Establish clear performance expectations, coaching cadences, and accountability frameworks across all leadership levels — weekly 1:1s, call calibrations, structured performance reviews, and real-time feedback loops.
• Drive workforce planning and capacity modeling: design the right staffing structure between labor, channels, and volume patterns — not just fill headcount, but engineer capacity.
• Build succession plans and internal development pathways that reduce external hiring dependency and create a promotion pipeline from within.
• Own recruitment strategy for the operations team in partnership with HR — attract, hire, and retain the caliber of leader and frontline talent that the CSO model demands.
• Foster a performance culture aligned to MedPro's values: Do the Right Thing, Figure It Out, Win as a Team, and Accountability & Ownership.

Operating Model Design & Service Delivery
• Assess the current state of the contact center operation — people, process, structure, channels, and systems — and translate findings into a clear-eyed redesign plan with sequenced priorities.
• Design and implement the operating model: how the team is structured, how work flows through channels, how performance is governed, and how the organization scales as the client base grows.
• Own day-to-day operational performance across all service channels — inbound and outbound voice, email, chat, and SMS — with direct accountability for SLA attainment, handle time, first-contact resolution, and quality scores.
• Build and maintain the full SOP and playbook library: every process documented, auditable, and owned. If it is not written down, it does not exist.
• Own the quality assurance framework — define rubrics, conduct or oversee audits, deliver structured coaching feedback to leads, and use quality data to drive continuous improvement.
• Manage real-time workload distribution across channels — monitor queues, adjust staffing deployment as volume shifts, and act on performance signals without waiting to be asked.

System Assessment & Service Technology Migration
• Conduct a thorough assessment of the current CRM and service technology environment — understanding what exists, what is working, what is limiting, and what migration path forward best serves the operation.
• Lead the end-to-end service system migration: requirements gathering, vendor evaluation, workflow configuration, data mapping, agent training, parallel testing, and go-live — while maintaining service continuity throughout.
• Design and configure case management workflows, escalation paths, SLA tracking, and omni-channel routing logic in the new system, aligned to MedPro's service model and client base.
• Partner with the internal technology team to ensure all system integrations — IVR handoffs, client account data, ticketing — flow cleanly from day one.
• Build post-migration operating model documentation: how cases are created, routed, resolved, escalated, and reported — so no process lives in someone's head.
• Drive full agent and Team Lead adoption: training materials, live coaching sessions, compliance monitoring, and performance metrics in the new system until it is the single active system of record.
• Design, build, and maintain IVR call flows that intelligently route clients, reduce handle time, and improve containment — build these, not just manage ones that already exist.

Performance Management, Data & Reporting
• Own the KPI framework — define the right metrics, build the dashboards, and drive accountability systems that make performance visible and improvable at every level of the team.
• Deliver structured weekly and monthly performance reports to the VP of Operations — covering channel SLA attainment, CSAT trends, quality scores, case volume, and system migration status.
• Translate floor-level data into operational decisions and clear narrative: what is driving variance, what specific action will improve it, and what the risk is if it is not addressed.
• Communicate proactively to the VP of Operations about service risks, capacity issues, and migration blockers — full transparency, solution-first framing, no surprises that should have been visible earlier.
• Identify and scope AI-assist and automation opportunities (IVR containment, agent-assist prompts, chatbot deflection) — bring the VP of Operations a prioritized business case, not just ideas.

Client Experience & Regulatory Compliance
• Own the client experience at the operational level — serving as the escalation point for complex service issues and ensuring every client interaction reflects MedPro and MP1's positioning as a true compliance partner.
• Map and continuously improve the client journey across all contact touchpoints — identifying friction, closing gaps, and driving measurable improvements in client satisfaction scores.
• Ensure operational adherence to all applicable data security, privacy, and healthcare regulatory requirements including HIPAA and related standards.
• Partner with Sales and Client Success to align operational delivery with client growth, renewal, and expansion strategies.

Qualifications:
Required
• 10–15 years of total experience in contact center operations, service delivery, or BPO leadership — with significant, demonstrable accountability for team performance, service transformation, and operational change.
• Minimum 5 years in a senior people-management role with direct accountability for multi-layer teams (managers managing managers), department-level KPIs, and operational planning. Candidates who have only managed frontline teams — and not managed managers — will not be a fit.
• Proven experience operating in high-volume, multi-channel service environments with large numbers of customer interactions across voice, email, chat, and digital touchpoints. This is an execution role — we need someone who has run the floor at volume, not just designed programs from above it.
• Demonstrated experience leading a service system migration in a live contact center — transitioning a team from a legacy CRM to a modern platform while maintaining service continuity. Salesforce Service Cloud experience is strongly preferred.
• Hands-on capacity planning expertise: ability to model the right labor structure against channel mix, volume forecasts, and SLA requirements — and build the operating model that delivers it.
• Deep IVR design and multi-channel routing experience — you have built IVR flows from scratch, configured routing logic, and measured containment impact.
• Strong analytical capability — builds and interprets dashboards, identifies performance drivers, and communicates findings clearly to senior leadership.
• Exceptional written and verbal English communication skills for daily interaction with U.S.-based leadership.
• Background in healthcare BPO, compliance services, regulated service industries, or U.S.-facing contact center operations strongly preferred.

Nice-to-Have
• Salesforce Service Cloud proficiency — case management, workflow rules, SLA configuration, omni-channel routing, and reporting.
• Experience with AI-assist tools in contact center settings: agent-assist prompts, chatbot deflection, predictive routing.
• Workforce management platform experience (NICE IEX, Verint, Calabrio, or similar).
• HIPAA familiarity and/or exposure to U.S. healthcare regulatory compliance operations.
• Track record of building or rebuilding operations within a high-growth or category-defining company.
• Six Sigma, COPC, or equivalent contact center quality certification.

Compensation & Benefits:
• Salary Range: $18-$24/hr 
• Permanent work-from-home
• 40 hours per week, aligned with U.S. business hours (CST, graveyard shift).
• Fixed weekends off (Saturday & Sunday)
• Work Anniversary Bonus
• No work with pay on US Holidays
• Paid Leaves after 6 months of employment!

Basic Technical Requirements:
• Your own laptop/desktop PC with the following minimum specs: i5 5th gen and above/AMD equivalent; 8GB RAM; Mozilla or Chrome installed; Windows 10 upwards.
• A consistent and stable internet connection of at least 20Mbps
• With back-up computer, internet connection and power supply
• Noise-canceling headset/headphones
• A quiet and dedicated workspace at home

Application Process:
1. Your most updated resume.

2. Submit to all of these emails:

 ---------- 
 ---------- 

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement
MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions are equal opportunity employers. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Meta Ads & Lead Generation Specialist (Senior Care Industry)
description:
About Us

The TurnKey Experience is a fast-growing company that provides done-for-you marketing systems, virtual assistants, and automation solutions for home care and franchise-based businesses.

We Are Not Looking for a Social Media Manager.

We are looking for a lead-generation specialist who happens to use Meta Ads.

Most social media managers focus on likes, comments, followers, and engagement.

We don't care about any of that.

We care about one thing:

Generating qualified senior care leads that turn into in-home care assessments and paying clients.

If you're the type of marketer who gets excited by:

Cost per lead
Lead quality
Conversion rates
Audience testing
Funnel optimization
Landing page performance
Retargeting campaigns
A/B testing creatives

...keep reading.

About Us

We are a rapidly growing senior home care agency serving families throughout Palm Beach County, Florida.

We help seniors remain safe, independent, and comfortable in their own homes by providing compassionate in-home care services.

Our ideal clients are:

Adult children searching for care for aging parents
Spouses caring for loved ones
Families navigating hospital discharges
Families dealing with dementia or cognitive decline
Veterans seeking care benefits

The person making the decision is usually not the senior.

It's their son, daughter, spouse, or family caregiver. Successful campaigns must understand that. Research consistently shows that adult children are often the true decision-makers in home care purchasing decisions.

What We Need You To Do
Meta Advertising
Build and manage Facebook and Instagram ad campaigns
Create and test new audiences
Create high-converting lead generation funnels
Build retargeting campaigns
Develop lookalike audiences
Optimize campaigns for qualified leads, not cheap leads
Continuously improve cost per lead and cost per appointment
Creative Development
Write compelling ad copy
Develop new hooks and offers
Create image and video concepts
Test multiple ad angles simultaneously
Identify winning creatives and scale them
Funnel Optimization
Improve lead quality
Improve landing page conversion rates
Improve lead form completion rates
Track every step of the funnel
Identify and eliminate bottlenecks
Analytics & Reporting
Weekly performance reporting
Cost per lead analysis
Audience performance analysis
Creative performance analysis
Recommendations for scaling
The Ideal Candidate Has
REQUIRED

? 3+ years managing Meta Ads

? Proven experience generating leads

? Strong copywriting skills

? Experience creating and testing audiences

? Ability to build conversion-focused campaigns

? Experience with lead forms and landing pages

? Strong understanding of Meta Ads Manager

? Excellent English communication skills

HUGE BONUS

? Home Care Marketing Experience

? Senior Living Marketing Experience

? Healthcare Marketing Experience

? Local Service Business Marketing Experience

? Experience generating leads for high-ticket services

? Experience with CRM integrations

? Experience using AI tools for ad creation

What Success Looks Like

Within your first 90 days:

Consistent flow of qualified leads
Reduced cost per lead
Higher contact rates
More scheduled assessments
Improved audience targeting
Clear reporting and optimization process
To Apply

Please answer ALL of the following:

1. What industries have you generated leads for?
2. What was your best-performing Meta Ads campaign?

Include:

Industry
Monthly budget
Cost per lead
Number of leads generated
3. Have you generated leads for:
Home Care Agencies?
Senior Living Communities?
Healthcare Companies?
Local Service Businesses?
4. Explain your process for identifying and testing new audiences.
5. Explain your process for improving lead quality.
6. What is the biggest mistake most businesses make with Meta Ads?
7. What would your first 30 days look like if hired?
8. Provide screenshots or case studies of campaigns you have managed.
9. What is your expected monthly compensation?
10. Why are you the best candidate for this role?
Important

We are not looking for someone to "post on social media."

We are looking for someone who can build a predictable lead-generation machine that produces qualified senior care prospects every month.

If you can consistently generate high-quality leads for a home care agency and prove your results with data, we want to speak with you.

Bonus consideration will be given to applicants who have generated leads for home care, healthcare, senior living, or other local service businesses.

Tell me about a campaign where you generated at least 25 qualified leads per month. What was the industry, cost per lead, and how did you achieve those results

title: Virtual Assistant – Sales & Lead Generation
description:
Volume 11 Marketing Inc. designs and builds custom trade show exhibits, museum displays, branded environments, and corporate interiors for clients across the country.
We are looking for a motivated, organized, and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to help us identify, research, and connect with potential clients. This role will support our sales and business development efforts by building targeted prospect lists, conducting outreach, following up with leads, and helping schedule qualified sales conversations.
This is not a basic admin role. We are looking for someone who is comfortable with research, lead generation, email outreach, LinkedIn, CRM/database management, and professional communication.

Key Responsibilities
Lead Research & Prospecting
• Research and identify potential clients in target industries, including companies exhibiting at major trade shows.
• Identify decision-makers such as marketing directors, trade show managers, event managers, brand managers, and executives.
• Build and maintain accurate prospect lists with company names, contact names, titles, emails, phone numbers, websites, LinkedIn profiles, and relevant notes. (some lead lists also provided)
• Research trade show exhibitor lists, event calendars, industry directories, LinkedIn, company websites, and other sources to find qualified prospects.
• Track upcoming trade shows and identify companies that may need exhibit design, fabrication, storage, or show services support.

Outreach & Follow-Up
• Draft and send professional introductory emails to prospects.
• Conduct organized follow-up email sequences to nurture leads and confirm interest.
• Assist with LinkedIn outreach, connection requests, and follow-up messaging.
• Make outbound calls when appropriate to qualify prospects and help schedule appointments. (when/if necessary)
• Respond to basic website inquiries or route them to the appropriate tea ---------- mber.
• Schedule meetings and calls between qualified prospects and the Volume 11 sales team.

CRM & Reporting
• Keep our CRM, spreadsheets, or prospect databases updated with all outreach activity, notes, contact information, and next steps.
• Track email outreach, calls, replies, meetings booked, and lead status.
• Provide a clear weekly report showing:
o prospects researched
o emails sent
o follow-ups completed
o calls made
o replies received
o meetings booked
o recommended next steps

Light Marketing Support
• Assist with basic LinkedIn activity and prospect engagement.
• Help organize and update marketing lists.
• Support simple email campaigns or outreach projects.
• Help review competitor and industry activity in the trade show/exhibit space.

Qualifications
• Proven experience in lead generation, sales support, appointment setting, or virtual assistance.
• Strong online research skills and ability to find accurate decision-maker contact information.
• Excellent written English and ability to write clear, professional outreach messages.
• Comfortable making professional outbound calls when needed.
• Experience using spreadsheets, CRMs, email tools, LinkedIn, and online research platforms.
• Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage follow-ups without constant reminders.
• Self-motivated and comfortable working independently.
• Professional, persistent, and respectful communication style.
• Experience with B2B sales, trade shows, marketing agencies, events, construction, design, or manufacturing is a plus.

Ideal Candidate
The ideal person is proactive and understands that successful lead generation requires consistency, accuracy, and follow-through.
You should be comfortable saying:
• “I found 50 companies exhibiting at this upcoming show.”
• “Here are the likely decision-makers.”
• “These 10 prospects look especially strong.”
• “These companies replied and need follow-up.”
• “This lead seems qualified and should be scheduled with sales.”

We want someone who can help create real sales opportunities, not just complete basic tasks.
Required Application Video
To apply, please send a short video, no longer than two minutes, introducing yourself.
In the video, please briefly answer:
1. What experience do you have with lead generation or sales support?
2. What tools have you used for prospect research, email outreach, LinkedIn, or CRM management?
3. Are you comfortable making outbound calls? (though likely not necessary)
4. Why do you think you would be a good fit for this role?

Applications without a video will not be reviewed.

title: Sales Admin & Data Tracking VA (High-Ticket Coaching)
description:
About Us

We are a fast-growing U.S.-based high-ticket coaching company scaling beyond 7-figures. We generate leads through paid ads, DMs, and webinars. We have a full team of setters and closers, but we need a Data Hawk to ensure our sales and marketing numbers are 100?curate and easy to read.
-------------------

The Role

We don't just need someone to "input data." We need someone to own the integrity of our tracking. You will be the bridge between our sales team (closers/setters) and our marketing data. Your goal is to ensure that every dollar spent on ads and every DM sent is accounted for in a clean, easy-to-read Google Sheet dashboard.
-------------------

Responsibilities

- Daily Data Management: Reconcile CRM data (GoHighLevel/Close) with our Master Tracking Sheet.

- Funnel Tracking: Track performance across three main pillars: Call Funnel, DM Ads/Organic, and Paid Webinars.

- Sales Accountability: Audit our setters and closers daily to ensure they are logging lead sources, call outcomes, and "cash collected" correctly.

- Marketing Attribution: Connect the dots between a lead clicking an ad and that lead showing up on a sales call.

- Sheet Maintenance: Maintain and improve our Google Sheets/Airtable dashboards so they remain "the source of truth."
-------------------

Requirements

- Advanced Google Sheets/Excel Skills: You must be an expert with formulas, pivot tables, and data visualization.

- CRM Experience: High proficiency in GoHighLevel, Close, or similar sales CRMs.

- Attention to Detail: You are the type of person who notices if a $1.00 discrepancy exists between the payment processor and the tracking sheet.

- High-Ticket Knowledge: You understand the "Setter/Closer" model and how a coaching funnel works.

- Proactive Communicator: You aren’t afraid to ping a closer if they forgot to update a lead status.

- Tech Savvy: Familiarity with Zapier/n8n and ManyChat is a major plus.
--------------------------

Compensation & Benefits

Salary: $700 – $1,200 USD/month (Full-time, 40 hours/week). Negotiable based on experience.

Schedule: Standard U.S. Business Hours (overlap required).

Growth: Work directly with a founder who has scaled multiple 8-figure companies.

Stability: Long-term role with a stable, growing team


How to Apply:

We are looking for someone who is highly organized and obsessed with data accuracy.

Please fill out this TypeForm to apply:  ---------- 

Note: You will be asked to provide a brief Loom video introducing yourself and explaining your experience managing sales data for coaching or agency businesses.
