title: Billing & Accounts Receivable Specialist
description:
About the role
We are a US-based equipment manufacturer seeking a detail-oriented Billing & Accounts Receivable Specialist to manage the day-to-day accounts receivable and invoicing functions of the company. You will handle customer invoicing, payment application, refunds, and reconciliation between our Fishbowl inventory system and QuickBooks, working closely with our production, sales, and customer service teams as well as directly with customers. You will also lead our weekly open orders meeting and support several ongoing special projects. This is a long-term role for someone who is highly organized, accurate, and a clear communicator.
Key responsibilities
Customer invoicing

Review open tickets and confirm accuracy against purchase orders and company policies
Update customer records in Fishbowl as needed prior to invoicing
Collect prepayments from designated customers before orders are released
Enter invoices into the Fishbowl inventory system
Upload PDF copies of invoices to the associated JIRA ticket and update ticket status to alert the production team

Invoice verification and reconciliation

Confirm the nightly Fishbowl-to-QuickBooks export has run successfully, and request a manual push from technical support when needed
Verify pricing and all invoice details for accuracy
Ensure final invoices are sent to the customer, or notify the appropriate internal recipients if not
Apply prepayments in QuickBooks
Collect remaining payments from prepaid and COD customers, and request payment from customers on net terms
Enter invoices for secondary companies into the appropriate QuickBooks or QuickBooks Online file
Perform currency exchange calculations when required
Correct invoices when errors are identified
Upload PDF copies of equipment invoices to JIRA tickets and update status to "Done" once verified

Accounts receivable

Review daily emails from the accounting team and apply customer payments in QuickBooks using remittance details from wires and correspondence
File customer deposit backup documentation when provided
Contact customers for remittance details when needed
Alert the production team when prepayments on equipment have been received
Monitor unpaid invoices and send payment reminders and statements as appropriate
Notify customers of payment holds and add notes in Fishbowl so new orders are not shipped
Respond to customer inquiries about invoices and payments, including sending copies and revising invoices that contain errors

Refunds processing

Review and download refund submissions from the customer refund portal
Format the refund spreadsheet and remove duplicate submissions, keeping the most accurate information
Communicate with customers when additional information is needed to process a refund
Research and issue refunds through our payment platforms (including Stripe and Nayax)
Create and upload CSV files to issue refund eChecks
Respond to customer refund inquiries by phone and email, and explain the refund process

Weekly open orders meeting

Prepare by reviewing notes and building a spreadsheet of all open orders that have not yet shipped
Track issues as they arise, with notes on actions taken, next steps, and expected resolution dates
Host the virtual meeting, sharing a color-coded, organized spreadsheet and walking the team through each row

Advance exchanges

Confirm exchanged products have been received back at our warehouse or the appropriate vendor
Create credit memos to fully cover in-warranty exchanges, and partial credit memos for out-of-warranty exchanges with the balance collected afterward

Email customers to request the return of overdue products

Communication

Review and respond to email, Teams chat, and JIRA-tagged requests from customers and coworkers
Answer the accounting phone line and accept customer payments by phone

Special projects

Maintain a free media tracking spreadsheet for a designated customer, track related payments, communicate with the customer, and create and follow up on JIRA tickets

Must-have qualifications

2 or more years of accounts receivable, invoicing, or general accounting experience
Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field, or equivalent work experience
Working knowledge of both QuickBooks Desktop and QuickBooks Online (this role uses both)
Strong written and verbal English, since the role includes customer phone contact and hosting a weekly team meeting
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including formulas, sorting, filtering, and basic formatting
High accuracy and strong attention to detail when working with financial data
Reliable computer, stable high-speed internet, a headset for calls, and a quiet work environment

Nice to have

Experience with Fishbowl inventory software (strongly preferred; we will train the right person)
Familiarity with JIRA or a similar ticketing system
Experience processing refunds through online payment platforms such as Stripe
Experience with multi-currency invoicing and currency exchange calculations
Experience supporting US-based businesses or B2B customers

Schedule and availability
This is a full-time role, approximately 40 hours per week. We need at least 4 hours of daily overlap with US Eastern Time, so this is an evening or overnight shift in the Philippines. Please only apply if you can reliably commit to these hours.
Confidentiality
This role handles confidential financial and customer information and requires discretion at all times. The successful candidate will sign a non-disclosure agreement, and system access will be limited to what the role requires.

title: Remote Property Manager
description:
Job Overview
We are seeking a highly responsive, detail-oriented Property Manager to oversee day-to-day operations of residential properties across the United States.
This role requires a strong ownership mindset, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities across tenant relations, maintenance coordination, leasing, compliance, and financial tracking.
This is a hands-on role where responsiveness is critical—you will be expected to actively monitor tasks, follow up consistently, and support both tenants and ownership in real time.

Core Responsibilities
1. Tenant Relations & Communication
Serve as the primary point of contact for tenants
Respond to tenant inquiries via phone, text, and email promptly
Handle escalations, disputes, and lease violations professionally
Send notices (entry, rent increases, lease renewals, violations)
Coordinate move-ins and move-outs
Maintain strong relationships with tenants and caseworkers

2. Leasing & Lead Management
Manage all incoming leads and inquiries
Schedule and conduct property showings
Convert leads into qualified applicants using effective sales techniques
Screen tenants and provide daily updates on new applications
Prepare lease documents and manage onboarding process
Track lease renewals and proactively follow up (90-day / 60-day notices)


3. Maintenance & Vendor Coordination
Manage maintenance requests and monitor portals daily
Assign and reassign tasks to ensure timely completion
Follow up with vendors and confirm scheduling
Verify completed work with tenants after closure
Coordinate move-in ready turns and repairs
Track open maintenance requests and claims
Schedule preventative maintenance (HVAC, seasonal, etc.)
Manage vendor relationships and accountability

4. Compliance, Inspections & Legal
Coordinate housing authority inspections (HAP compliance)
Ensure properties meet required standards
Schedule and track routine and certification inspections
Serve legal notices (7-day notices, demands, etc.) when required
Prepare documentation for court and eviction processes
Monitor lease compliance and enforce terms

5. Financial Management & Reporting
Track rent collection and monitor delinquencies
Apply and enforce late fees (after 5 days)
Manage and monitor payment plans with tenants
Review and explain P&L statements and owner reports
Reconcile expenses and assign costs to correct properties
Review credit card transactions and categorize expenses
Monitor utilities (water, gas, electric) and provide monthly summaries
Handle mortgage, HOA, and insurance tracking
Reconcile payments with county and vendor portals

6. Owner Communication & Reporting
Maintain strong relationships with property owners
Provide regular updates on property performance
Share monthly reports, maintenance updates, and financial summaries
Obtain approvals for repairs and major expenses
Deliver proactive recommendations to improve performance

7. Administrative & Operational Tasks
Maintain accurate records in CRM/property management systems
Upload invoices and manage documentation
Track lease data, tenant records, and compliance requirements
Monitor insurance policies and renewals
Ensure all utilities and accounts are properly set up
Maintain inventory tracking (filters, maintenance items, etc.)

8. Emergency & After-Hours Support
Act as point of contact for urgent maintenance or safety issues
Coordinate emergency vendor response when needed

Monthly / Recurring Responsibilities
Lease renewal tracking and follow-ups
Delinquency outreach and collection updates
Owner check-ins and reporting
Property condition and maintenance reports
Preventative maintenance planning
Eviction tracking and execution support
Utility and expense reconciliation
Claims tracking (e.g., Nicor Home Solutions)

Qualifications
Prior experience in property management, leasing, or real estate operations
Experience with US-based properties strongly preferred
Strong communication skills (clear, neutral English—written and spoken)
Comfortable handling phone calls with tenants, vendors, and owners
Highly organized with strong attention to detail
Ability to multitask and work independently
Strong problem-solving and follow-up skills
Experience with property management software preferred
Familiarity with Zillow, HotPads, and leasing platforms

Preferred Experience
Section 8 / Housing Authority experience
Vendor coordination and maintenance dispatch
Financial tracking (P&L, expense categorization)
Tenant screening and leasing conversions
Customer service or call-based roles

Required Mindset
Strong ownership mentality (takes responsibility, follows through)
Highly responsive and proactive
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment
Ability to push tasks forward without constant direction

Application Requirements
Please include the following in your application:
Years of property management experience
Number of units currently or previously managed
Types of properties managed (single-family, multifamily, etc.)
Software used (Doorloop)
Your role (owner, manager, assistant, etc.)
Markets you’ve managed properties in
What parts of your process are automated

Key Traits for Success
Strong communication + follow-up
Detail-oriented and organized
Sales mindset (for leasing conversions)
Accountability and reliability

title: Lead C# Developer
description:

title: Email Marketing Manager (ECommerce) - Full Time, Remote
description:
We are looking for a strategic and performance-driven Email & SMS Marketing Manager to lead the charge in growing and optimizing our owned marketing channels. This role is key to delivering high-converting campaigns that drive customer engagement, retention, and revenue across our skincare and hair care product lines. You’ll turn customer insights into action, bringing value at every touchpoint while aligning messaging with product and brand goals.


Primary Duties and Responsibilities:
Own the strategy, execution, and optimization of all email and SMS campaigns for multiple product lines (e.g., hair thinning shampoo, supplements, anti-aging SPF, and skin supplements).
Segment audiences and personalize messaging to maximize relevance, engagement, and conversion.
Develop and manage high-performing lifecycle flows including welcome, cart abandonment, post-purchase, and win-back sequences.
A/B test subject lines, content, offers, and creative assets to continuously improve key performance metrics (CTR, open rate, CVR, revenue per send).
Monitor and report on campaign performance; adjust strategy based on insights and trends.
Build surveys and gather customer feedback to inform future content and product ideas.
Collaborate with creative, product, and customer experience teams to ensure consistent and on-brand messaging.
Maintain a strong understanding of compliance and best practices in email/SMS marketing (e.g., CAN-SPAM, TCPA).
Suggest and implement new technologies, tools, and tactics to stay ahead of the curve.


Qualifications:
3+ years of experience in email and SMS marketing, ideally in eCommerce or beauty/wellness.
Expert in Klaviyo or similar marketing automation platforms.
Strong writing skills and a keen eye for visual branding and user experience.
Solid understanding of customer behavior, segmentation, and marketing psychology.
Results-oriented with a deep passion for data, testing, and optimization.
Familiarity with tools like Rebuy or product recommendation engines is a plus.
Proactive, curious, and comfortable working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.
Passion for skincare, wellness, and helping people feel their best.


What You’ll Help Shape:

title: Operations, Workflow, and Systems Coordinator
description:
We are a boutique, luxury inn located in the heart of Pensacola’s historic district, offering an elevated blend of Southern hospitality, curated experiences, and thoughtful, design-forward comfort. Rooted in family legacy and a passion for refined guest care, we have quickly become a haven for travelers seeking warmth, beauty, and a truly personal touch. From chef-crafted breakfasts to intimate seasonal events and immersive cultural experiences, every detail is intentionally crafted to inspire connection, relaxation, and a sense of home. As we continue to grow, we’re building a team of individuals who care deeply about hospitality, excellence, and creating  ---------- nts that feel unforgettable.

The Operations, Workflow & Systems Coordinator is the architect behind our daily workflow, communication rhythm, guest experience systems, and marketing organization. This is not an administrative assistant role; it is a creator role.

The person in this position will design the internal backbone of a boutique, luxury bed and breakfast where hospitality, culinary excellence, and refined guest experiences come together. They will build processes, create templates, implement systems, and maintain rhythm across all departments so the Owner can remain hands-off while operations run smoothly and cohesively.

This role requires someone who thinks like a systems builder, organizes like a project manager, communicates like a hospitality professional, and operates with a deep sense of ownership.

Core Responsibilities
1. Systems Building & Workflow Creation
You will build the operational infrastructure, including:
- Checklists for Guest Experience, Housekeeping, Culinary support, and Events
- SOPs for check-ins, check-outs, room readiness, and breakfast coordination
- Templates for guest communication, team updates, and internal documentation
- Workflows that streamline coordination between leads and departments
- A central digital hub where all documents, schedules, and SOPs live
- Improved systems for consistency, quality, and team alignment
Your role is to create, not just maintain.

2. Operational Coordination & Calendar Management
Build and maintain the master scheduling system for:
- Check-ins and check-outs
- Housekeeping and room turnovers
- Breakfast staffing
- Events and workshops
- Tea ---------- etings
Ensure all tea ---------- mbers always have correct, up-to-date schedules. Answer guest calls and ensure all requests are answered.

Anticipate operational needs before they arise

You own the coordination that keeps the business synchronized.

3. Communication & Documentation Development
- Create and automate standardized guest communication flows (pre-arrival, stay updates, checkout)
- Maintain the communication library in Celestine’s brand voice
- Draft agendas for weekly meetings and document action items
- Write internal updates for the team
- Produce templates for event communication, vendor coordination, and internal notes
- Create social media and newsletter content
- You help maintain a smooth, polished communication experience across the business and communicate directly with customers.

4. Data Tracking, Reporting & Accountability Support
- Build the operations dashboard (weekly and monthly)
- Track occupancy, revenue, guest feedback trends, and team performance metrics
- Monitor progress toward monthly and quarterly goals
- Identify gaps and propose improvements
- Support light accountability by circulating reminders, deadlines, and summaries
- You create visibility into the health of the business.

5. Marketing Organization & Content Workflow Design
- Build the social media content calendar system
- Organize photos and videos into labeled content libraries
- Set up workflows for content creation, approvals, and posting
- Coordinate with the Owner, Culinary Lead, and Events Lead and Marketing Lead to gather content
- Track content performance monthly
- You will organize the marketing engine.

6. Event & Experience Support
- Build event playbooks for brunches, tasting dinners, workshops, and seasonal experiences
- Create event coordination templates
- Track guest registrations, vendor communication, and timelines
- Capture feedback and update systems for future events
- You help standardize the event experience so each one runs flawlessly.
- Develop proposals in response to private event inquiries.

title: AI-First Full-Stack Developer (Next.js)
description:
# AI-First Full-Stack Developer (Next.js) — Moredemand (Remote)

## About Moredemand

Moredemand is an AI-powered SaaS platform for paid advertising analytics, funnel building, and demand generation. We serve agency owners and in-house performance marketers who need to connect ad spend to revenue outcomes without the guesswork.

The platform is in active build across multiple workstreams — ad account analytics, CRM and lead management, page and funnel builder, conversion mapping, and an AI assistant layer (MODA) that ties it all together. This is a ground-floor opportunity to shape a product that sits at the intersection of advertising data, automation, and applied AI.

## The Role

We're hiring a full-stack developer whose default mode is building with AI — not just using it as a novelty feature, but treating it as core infrastructure. You'll be working across the Moredemand platform in Next.js, shipping features end-to-end from spec to production.

This is a hands-on building role. You'll receive detailed specs and PRDs with user stories, business logic, and acceptance criteria (written in Gherkin). You won't be guessing what to build — but you will need to make smart technical decisions about how to build it, and move fast doing so.

You'll be working alongside an existing small dev team and reporting directly to the founder.

## What You'll Be Doing

- Building and shipping production features across the Moredemand platform in Next.js (App Router, Server Components, Server Actions)
- Integrating AI/LLM capabilities throughout the product — not bolted-on chat widgets, but intelligent features woven into the user experience (triage, analysis, recommendations, natural language querying, content generation)
- Working with the Google Ads, Meta Ads, and analytics APIs to ingest, normalise, and surface advertising data
- Building CRM-style interfaces — database views, saved filters, inline actions, dynamic tables, drag-and-drop workflows
- Developing the page/funnel builder module with overlay and popup elements
- Implementing a consent management layer and data-triggered qualification rules
- Writing clean, testable code against Gherkin acceptance criteria provided in each spec
- Using Claude Code, Cursor, or equivalent AI-assisted development tooling as a core part of your workflow — not occasionally, but as standard practice
- Shipping iteratively with a bias toward working software over perfect abstractions

## What We're Looking For

- Strong production experience with Next.js 14+ (App Router, not Pages Router) — this is non-negotiable
- Proven ability to build with LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, or similar) — prompt engineering, structured outputs, tool use, streaming responses, RAG patterns
- Comfortable across the full stack: React/TypeScript on the front end, Node.js/serverless on the back end, PostgreSQL or equivalent relational database
- Experience integrating third-party APIs — particularly ad platforms, CRMs, or data-heavy SaaS integrations
- Ability to work from a detailed spec and translate user stories and business logic into shipped features without requiring architectural hand-holding
- Familiarity with AI-assisted development workflows (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, or similar) and the discipline to review and own what the tools produce
- Test-driven mindset — you understand Gherkin acceptance criteria and write code that satisfies them
- Clean component architecture, sensible state management, and an instinct for UI that feels fast and responsive
- Self-directed and reliable in a remote, async-first environment — you ship, you communicate, you don't disappear

## Tech Environment

- Next.js (App Router), React, TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- PostgreSQL
- Anthropic Claude API (primary LLM layer)
- Google Ads API, Meta Marketing API
- Vercel deployment
- Claude Code / AI-assisted development as standard workflow
- Spec-first methodology — block-based architecture, PRDs with Gherkin acceptance criteria, no code or DB schema in specs

## Bonus Points

- Experience building SaaS platforms for marketers or agencies
- Familiarity with advertising data models (campaigns, ad groups, keywords, conversion actions, attribution)
- Experience with real-time dashboards, charting libraries (Recharts, D3), or data visualisation
- Background in building form/page builders or drag-and-drop interfaces
- Understanding of FCA-regulated or compliance-sensitive product environments
- Contributions to open-source or a portfolio of shipped SaaS products

## How to Apply

Send the following:

1. A short Loom (under 3 minutes) walking through a Next.js project you built — show the code, explain the decisions, highlight any AI/LLM integration
2. A brief description of how you currently use AI tooling in your development workflow — what tools, how often, and what you trust them with vs. what you don't
3. One example of a feature you shipped from spec to production, and what the spec looked like vs. what you delivered
4. Your availability (hours per week or full-time) and rate (hourly, daily, or monthly)

No agencies. No CVs padded with frameworks you used once on a tutorial. Show the work.

title: AI Developer — Agents, Automation & SaaS Tools
description:
Solomon X is an AI consulting and agent development company serving mid-market businesses in the UAE and South Africa. We build and deploy AI agents that absorb the repetitive, expensive work inside businesses — quoting, operations, customer care, growth, and orchestration — so the people inside those businesses can focus on what only humans can do.

We're looking for a skilled AI developer to join as our core builder. You'll work directly with the founder to design, build, and maintain the tools we sell to clients.

What you'll build:

- AI agents using the Claude API (agentic workflows, tool use, structured outputs, RAG pipelines)
- Automation workflows in n8n connecting APIs, webhooks, databases, and messaging channels (WhatsApp, email, SMS)
- Backend systems on Supabase (Postgres, auth, storage, edge functions)
- Frontend interfaces in Next.js for client dashboards and agent management
- Multi-tenant SaaS architecture — one engine, many clients, configuration-driven (not custom-coded per client)
- Prompt engineering and agent logic for verticals like panel beaters, electrical contractors, professional services

What we expect:

- You can take a brief and ship working code, not just ideas
- You build for reuse — every workflow and agent is a template, not a one-off
- You log everything by default — structured outputs, audit trails, traceable decisions
- You communicate clearly and flag blockers early
- You're comfortable with ambiguity in a fast-moving early-stage build

Tech stack (what you need to know):

- Claude API / Anthropic SDK — non-negotiable
- n8n (self-hosted workflow automation) — non-negotiable
- Supabase (Postgres, auth, storage) — non-negotiable
- Next.js / React — required
- REST APIs, webhooks, JSON — required
- Python or JavaScript/TypeScript — at least one fluently
- WhatsApp Business API (360dialog or Twilio) — a strong plus
- Vector databases / RAG architecture — a strong plus

This role is right for you if:
- You've built AI agents or automation workflows that real users actually use
- You enjoy the problem-solving side of building, not just executing tickets
- You want to grow with a business that's serious about quality, not just speed
- You're reliable, honest, and you do what you say you'll do

This role is not for you if:

- You need heavy hand-holding or detailed specs for everything
- You've only worked with no-code tools and haven't touched real APIs
- You treat every project as a brand new snowflake instead of looking for reusable patterns

Hours: Starting at 20 hours/week, scaling to full-time as the business grows. Flexible hours — results matter more than when you work.

To apply: Send a short note explaining one AI agent or automation workflow you've built, what problem it solved, and what tools you used. No note = no review.

title: Investment Website Keyword Research & Stock Teaser Discovery Assistant
description:
I run a website that uncovers the real stocks and cryptocurrencies being promoted in investment newsletters and marketing presentations.

We're looking for a research assistant who can help identify new stock teaser opportunities before they become widely covered.

This is not traditional financial analysis. You will not be picking stocks or making investment recommendations.

Instead, your job will be to find investment promotions, newsletter advertisements, teaser campaigns, and emerging keyword opportunities that investors are searching for online.

Responsibilities
- Sign up for investment newsletters and promotional email lists
- Monitor incoming emails for new stock and crypto teaser campaigns
- Identify newsletter promotions that may generate significant search traffic
- Record teaser details in a spreadsheet or database
- Research well-known newsletter publishers and analysts
- Search Google, YouTube, and financial websites for newly released teaser promotions
- Identify phrases and keywords that investors are likely to search after watching a teaser presentation
- Flag promising opportunities for review by our editorial team

Ideal Candidate:

You do NOT need to be a professional investor.

However, you should have:

- Basic familiarity with the stock market, investing, or cryptocurrencies
- Strong internet research skills
- Good attention to detail
- Ability to recognize patterns and trends
- Basic understanding of keyword research and search intent
- Strong written English
- Ability to work independently after training

***Training Provided***

We will provide training on:

- How newsletter teaser promotions work
- How to identify worthwhile opportunities
- How to evaluate keyword potential
- How we prioritize research opportunities
- The specific process we use to track and organize findings

What Success Looks Like:

A successful candidate will consistently find:

- New teaser promotions before competitors cover them
- High-interest stock and crypto promotions
- Keywords that real investors are actively searching for
- Opportunities that can be turned into future articles

Application Instructions:

To apply, please answer the following:

- Do you have any experience with investing, stocks, or cryptocurrencies?
- Have you ever done keyword research or SEO-related work?
- Who are two investment newsletter publishers or financial personalities you are familiar with?
- Why do you think you'd be a good fit for this role?
- Include the phrase "Hidden Stock Pick" somewhere in your application so we know you read the entire job posting.

Final candidates will be asked to complete a short paid research assignment. You will be given a sample teaser campaign and asked to identify potential search keywords and explain why you believe the opportunity is worth pursuing.

**CRM, EDM & Digital Systems**

- Create, schedule and assist with EDMs and smart email campaigns
- Upload project information, documents, images and assets into relevant systems
- Coordinate listing updates across websites, publisher portals and digital platforms
- Assist with app and portal content updates

**Canva, Design & Brand Support**
- Maintain and update Canva design templates
- Make basic design changes to existing templates, social posts, flyers and campaign assets
- Ensure marketing materials follow NPA brand templates, colours, fonts and standards
- Organise and maintain brand assets, logos, templates, images and design files

**Supplier & Team Coordination**
- Liaise with designers, printers, signage contractors, media suppliers and external providers
- Coordinate supplier booking requests, artwork requirements and production timelines
- Follow up suppliers to ensure deadlines are met
- Coordinate approvals, amendments and revisions between internal staff and suppliers

***Required Skills & Experience***
You must have:
- A definite background in marketing — this role requires someone who understands marketing principles and can execute tasks independently
- Experience coordinating marketing campaigns, projects or workflows
- Strong Asana experience (or a similar project management tool)
- Strong Canva experience — you must be able to create and edit marketing assets
- Experience creating or assisting with EDMs and email campaigns
- AI proficiency — you actively use tools like ChatGPT or Claude in your work
- High attention to detail — errors in marketing materials are not acceptable
- Strong written English
- The ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines simultaneously

Highly regarded but not entirely required:
- Adobe Suite experience (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign)
- Claude Code experience
- GoHighLevel (GHL) experience
- Experience working with Australian clients or businesses
- Experience with property, real estate or project marketing
- AI tool proficiency (ChatGPT or similar)

***If you do not have a marketing background or cannot demonstrate experience with Asana and Canva, and AI tools, this role is not the right fit.

***Work Details***
- Full-time, 40 hours per week
- Australian business hours
- Remote contractor role
- Long-term opportunity
- Salary: AUD $1,600 – $1,800 per month, based on experience
- 13th month bonus

**Work Environment Requirements**
You must have:
- Reliable high-speed internet
- Backup internet connection
- Backup power supply or an alternative workspace option
- Quiet and professional workspace
- Reliable computer/laptop suitable for design and marketing tools

**Growth Opportunity**
This role has strong long-term potential. The successful candidate may grow into a senior marketing coordination or campaign management role as the business expands.
We value people who are reliable, organised, take initiative and take pride in the quality of their work.

***How to Apply***

To be considered for this role, all applicants MUST complete our Google Form application process.

Applications submitted directly through OnlineJobs.ph without completing the Google Form will not be reviewed.

The Google Form application includes:
- Marketing experience and background questions
- Tools and software assessment (Asana, Canva, GHL, etc.)
- Marketing knowledge and execution questions
- A practical EDM task — you will be given a brief and asked to write an on-brand NPA email
- Coordination and workflow questions
- Work setup questions
- Resume upload
- Mandatory 2–4 minute video submission

**Mandatory Video Submission
All applicants are required to submit a 2–4 minute video.

Your video MUST cover all 3 of the following — missing any one will result in your application not being considered:

1. Personal introduction — who you are, your background and why you are suited to this role
2. Marketing experience — walk us through your experience, the tools you use and campaigns or projects you have worked on
3. Work sample showcase — show examples of marketing work you have created on screen (EDMs, Canva designs, social posts, campaign assets). If you do not have samples available, describe a specific project in detail

Videos that do not cover all 3 components will not be shortlisted.

**Attention to Detail**

To confirm you have carefully read this job description, you will be asked in the application form to type the phrase:

Marketing Assistant Ready

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Please complete the Google Form application below:

title: Guest Support Specialist for Airbnb / Short-Term Rental Company
description:
We are Sophari — a premium short-term rental company operating furnished apartments across downtown Bellevue, WA (near Seattle, USA). We serve corporate professionals, relocating tech employees, and extended-stay travelers, with guests booking through platforms like Airbnb,  ---------- , and other online travel agencies.

We are a small, close-knit remote team where everyone does the core work. No one is above answering a guest message, logging a note, or following up on a reservation detail. If you like being hands-on and part of a high-ownership team, you will fit in well.

We are looking for a reliable, experienced Guest Support Specialist based in the Philippines to join us full-time, handle guest communications, resolve issues across our platforms, and support day-to-day guest operations.

About the Role
Full-time, remote role for candidates based in the Philippines.

Core schedule:

Weekdays (Monday–Friday): 3:00pm–11:00pm Pacific Time

Weekends (Saturday–Sunday): 10:00am–5:00pm Pacific Time

Weekend coverage on both days is required. If you are able to consistently work full weekend coverage, we can offer up to $8/hour.

You will work across several tools, including a property management system such as Guesty or Jurny, plus Airtable and Breezeway for tracking, coordination, and operations workflows.

This role is focused on reliable shift coverage, strong guest communication, and accurate execution of process.

What You Will Do
Monitor and respond to guest messages across booking channels such as Airbnb,  ---------- , and direct reservations.

Handle common guest issues such as late check-ins, lockouts, extension requests, parking questions, Wi-Fi issues, and general stay support.

Log guest interactions, notes, and follow-ups accurately and in real time.

Coordinate with operations and cleaning teams on same-day issues, unit readiness, and turnover flags.

Work inside our systems and workflows, including a PMS such as Guesty or Jurny, Airtable, and Breezeway.

Identify and flag upsell opportunities such as early check-in, late checkout, parking, and extra cleaning, and send payment instructions when appropriate.

Escalate discount requests, pricing exceptions, and unusual situations according to our internal process.

What We Are Looking For
At least 2 years of experience in short-term rental operations, property management, hotel front desk, or hospitality guest support.

Strong written English and professional guest communication.

Experience with Airbnb,  ---------- , Agoda, Expedia, or similar OTA platforms.

Organized and reliable; you log notes in real time, show up on schedule, and follow through on tasks.

Comfortable working independently from home while coordinating with a small remote team.

Calm, practical, and solution-focused when guests are frustrated or situations are unclear.

Comfortable learning and using systems such as a PMS (Guesty, Jurny, or similar), Airtable, and Breezeway.

What This Is Not
This is not a sales or account management role; it is a guest support and operations role.

This is not a high-autonomy pricing role; there are clear escalation paths for discounts and exceptions.

This is not a fit for someone juggling too many demanding clients at once; we need dependable coverage during scheduled hours.

Compensation and Growth
$5–$7/hour to start, depending on experience and fit.

Up to $8/hour for candidates who can consistently work full coverage on both weekend days.

Full-time hours based on the schedule above.

Paid onboarding and training period.

90-day review with the opportunity to revisit pay and responsibilities.

Room to grow as the company adds properties and expands operations.

How to Apply
Please answer all of the following in your application:

Describe your short-term rental, hotel, or hospitality guest support experience. Include number of properties or rooms supported, platforms used, and how long you did the work.

Confirm your availability for this schedule:

Weekdays: 3:00pm–11:00pm Pacific Time

Weekends: 10:00am–5:00pm Pacific Time

Confirm whether you can work both Saturday and Sunday on a regular basis.

Share your experience using any of the following: Guesty, Jurny, Airtable, Breezeway, or similar tools.

Share one example of a difficult guest situation you handled, what you did, and the outcome.

We take this hire seriously. Clear, direct, and specific applications stand out much more than long generic ones.

title: Assistant Property Manager (Yardi)
description:
******TO BE CONSIDERED AS AN APPLICANT, PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR MOST UP-TO-DATE RESUME AND A VIDEO INTRODUCTION TAILORED TO THIS JOB POSTING!****** 

Assistant Property Manager

Job Type: Full-Time
Work Hours: Monday to Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM Mountain Time, with a 30-min break from 11:30 AM to 12:00 PM
Salary: $1,000 per month

Job Overview: 
We are seeking a highly organized and proactive Assistant Property Manager to provide essential support for day-to-day business operations in a fast-paced property management environment. The ideal candidate will have commercial property management experience and proven hands-on experience using Yardi for day-to-day property management operations. This role serves as a key point of contact for internal teams, tenants, vendors, and external stakeholders, ensuring smooth administrative operations, effective communication, and exceptional customer service. The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing a wide range of administrative tasks, including scheduling, database maintenance, document processing, maintenance coordination, reporting, and general office support. This position requires strong multitasking abilities, attention to detail, excellent communication skills, and the ability to manage projects independently with minimal supervision while maintaining a high standard of professionalism, safety awareness, and customer satisfaction.

Key Responsibilities:
* Serve as the primary point of contact for incoming calls, emails, online inquiries, and general requests, ensuring prompt and professional assistance.
* Answer telephone calls, log detailed messages, and direct inquiries to the appropriate tea ---------- mbers
* Communicate effectively with internal teams, tenants, vendors, and external stakeholders
* Deliver exceptional customer service and maintain strong professional relationships
* Manage calendars, create reminders, and schedule tasks using Microsoft Outlook
* Maintain and update contact information and records across multiple databases
* Prepare, format, and distribute correspondence via email, mail, or other communication channels
* Collect, organize, and process documentation related to contracts and administrative records
* Maintain accurate digital filing systems and organize electronic records to ensure efficient document management and accessibility
* Manage and update the Yardi database, including tracking maintenance work orders and related records
* Coordinate maintenance scheduling and support staff workflow management
* Log, monitor, and report building issues and maintenance requests to the facilities team
* Coordinate virtual meeting schedules and communicate operational scheduling needs with relevant teams
* Manage tenant insurance records, database updates, and compliance tracking
* Assist with ad hoc administrative projects and special assignments from leadership/management
* Support day-to-day administrative and operational functions to ensure smooth business operations in a remote setting

Required Skills & Qualifications:
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to multitask effectively in a fast-paced environment
* Ability to manage projects independently with minimal supervision and follow through on assigned tasks
* Excellent customer service, interpersonal, and communication skills, both written and verbal
* High attention to detail with strong time management and problem-solving abilities
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, including:
* Microsoft Word – creating professional correspondence, agendas, notes, and maintaining call logs
* Microsoft Excel – creating reports, tracking data, maintaining logs, and managing spreadsheets
* Microsoft Outlook – managing email communication, calendars, contacts, reminders, and task scheduling
* Proficiency in Adobe Acrobat for document review, editing, and file management
* Experience maintaining accurate records and working with multiple databases
* Ability to handle confidential information with professionalism and discretion
* Experience supporting commercial property management operations (required)
* Hands-on experience using Yardi for property management operations, tenant records, and maintenance/work order tracking (required)


Note: An NBI or police clearance will be required upon acceptance of the job offer.

title: TRUST ELITE CLEANING SOLUTIONS VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — CLEANING BUSINESS OPERATIONS Full Time | Pacific Standard Time | Long
description:
TRUST ELITE CLEANING SOLUTIONS
VIRTUAL ASSISTANT — CLEANING BUSINESS OPERATIONS
Full Time | Pacific Standard Time | Long-Term Position

???????????????????????????????????????????????

WHO WE ARE

Trust Elite Cleaning Solutions is a professional cleaning company
serving Los Angeles and the San Bernardino / Inland Empire markets
in Southern California. We handle residential and commercial
accounts. We use Jobber for all operations, Slack as our command
center, and QUO for phones. Everything runs through systems —
your job is to keep those systems moving every single day.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We are looking for a mature, experienced, dependable professional
with a minimum of 5 years of real work experience — ideally in
the cleaning industry, home services, or field service operations.

We need someone who has been through real workplace challenges
and knows how to handle them without hand-holding. Maturity,
professional stability, and reliability are more important to us
than anything else. If you are just starting out in your career
this is not the right position for you.

We have a small tight-knit team and are looking for someone who
will blend well with our existing team culture and take genuine
ownership of their role.

This is a long-term position with real growth opportunity.
If you show up, do the work correctly, and grow with us —
you will move up in this company. We promote from within.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

REQUIREMENTS — NON-NEGOTIABLE

You MUST meet all of the following or do not apply:

  • Minimum 5 years of professional work experience
  • Currently working or has worked in Pacific Standard Time (PST)
    business hours — 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST — for at least 1 year
  • Previous experience working as a VA for a cleaning company,
    home services company, or field service business
  • Knows how to use Jobber or a similar field service CRM
    such as Housecall Pro or ServiceTitan
  • Fluent in English — written and spoken — professional level
  • Reliable high-speed internet connection
  • Quiet, professional workspace at home
  • Stable work history — no job hopping
  • Available to start within 2 weeks

???????????????????????????????????????????????

TOOLS YOU MUST KNOW OR LEARN IMMEDIATELY

  • Jobber — quoting, booking, dispatching, invoicing
  • Slack — primary communication tool, you will live here
  • QUO / OpenPhone — answering calls and texts, managing inbox
  • Google LSA — responding to leads, managing the lead inbox
  • Microsoft 365 — email, SharePoint document management
  • PandaDoc — sending contracts and onboarding documents
  • Basic Zapier understanding is a plus

???????????????????????????????????????????????

YOUR DAILY RESPONSIBILITIES

Every single day you are responsible for:

LEAD MANAGEMENT
  • Monitor Google LSA inbox starting at 8:00 AM PST sharp
  • Respond to every new lead within 15 minutes of it coming in
  • Use the company pricing tool to generate accurate quotes
    every time — never guess a price
  • Follow up on all unanswered quotes within 24 hours
  • Log every lead in Slack #leads with lead ID and dollar amount

JOBBER OPERATIONS
  • Create new client profiles for every approved lead
  • Build and send quotes in Jobber using the correct templates
  • Convert approved quotes to jobs and schedule them correctly
  • Assign cleaners to jobs and confirm their availability first
  • Add complete job instructions including entry codes, parking,
    scope of work, and any special instructions
  • Monitor job status throughout the day — check-ins, completions
  • Follow up on any job that has not been marked complete on time

CLIENT COMMUNICATION
  • Respond to all client texts and calls through QUO professionally
  • Handle rescheduling, cancellations, and schedule changes
  • Escalate any client complaints to ownership immediately
  • Never discuss pricing changes or make promises without approval

SLACK ACCOUNTABILITY
  • Post your daily check-in by 8:00 AM PST every workday
  • Confirm every completed task in the assigned Slack channels
  • Respond to all Slack messages within 15 minutes during work hours
  • Post any issues, problems, or questions in the correct channel
    immediately — do not wait

CLEANER COORDINATION
  • Send job assignments to cleaners through Jobber
  • Follow up if a cleaner has not checked in within 15 minutes
    of their scheduled start time
  • Notify ownership immediately of any no-shows or problems
  • Send onboarding documents through PandaDoc to new cleaners
    and follow up until all documents are returned signed

???????????????????????????????????????????????

WHAT IS NOT ACCEPTABLE — READ THIS CAREFULLY

The following will result in immediate termination:

  • Not responding to leads within 15 minutes during work hours
  • Sending a quote with a price that was not generated from
    the company pricing tool
  • Missing the 8:00 AM PST daily check-in without prior notice
  • Not answering Slack messages during work hours
  • Making promises to clients without ownership approval
  • Logging off early without permission
  • Not logging leads, quotes, or completed tasks in Slack
  • Blaming cleaners or clients for errors that were your
    responsibility
  • Dishonesty of any kind — about tasks completed, client
    interactions, or cleaner performance

???????????????????????????????????????????????

HOURS AND SCHEDULE

  • Monday through Friday — 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM PST required
  • Saturday — 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM PST required
  • Sunday — occasional coverage may be needed
  • You are expected to be at your desk and available during
    all scheduled hours — not semi-available

This is a Pacific Standard Time role. If you cannot commit to
PST hours do not apply.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

PAY RATE

Starting rate: $4.50 to $6.00 USD per hour depending on
experience and Jobber knowledge.

Pay is weekly via Wise or direct transfer.

Performance reviews at 30 days and 90 days.
Raise available at 90 days for top performers.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

GROWTH OPPORTUNITY

We are building this company for the long term. If you are
the right person — reliable, sharp, experienced, and genuinely
good at this — there is room to grow into a senior operations
role. We promote from within. The person who earns it gets more
responsibility, more pay, and a real career path with this company.

We are not looking for someone to do the bare minimum.
We are looking for someone who takes ownership of their role
and treats this business like it matters — because it does.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

HOW TO APPLY

To be considered you must include ALL of the following
in your application or it will not be reviewed:

  1. Your full name
  2. How many years of total professional work experience you have
  3. Confirm you are available 8 AM to 5 PM PST Monday through Saturday
  4. Describe your experience working for a cleaning company or
     home services business — be specific about what you did daily
  5. List every CRM or field service tool you have used
     such as Jobber, Housecall Pro, or ServiceTitan
  6. Tell us what PST time zone hours you have worked previously
     and for how long
  7. Write this exact sentence at the top of your application
     so we know you read the entire post:
     "I have read all requirements and I meet every one of them."

Applications that do not include all 7 items will not be reviewed.

???????????????????????????????????????????????

title: Graphic Designer - Part Time (Remote)
description:
The Compliance Services Organization didn’t exist. We built it.

MP1 Solutions pioneered the Compliance Services Organization (CSO) — a new category of operating partner built to replace the fragmented vendor model that has defined compliance programs for decades. Built on the foundation of MedPro Disposal, the nation’s largest privately-held medical waste company serving 35,000+ providers across 48 states over the past 15 years, MP1 Solutions extends that operational infrastructure, vendor network, and customer trust into a category we’re actively building — and leading.

What we do:
We operate as the single compliance partner for non-acute healthcare facilities — consolidating every obligation into one coordinated program, assigning one dedicated account manager as the point of accountability, and actively managing the underlying vendor ecosystem through continuous evaluation, negotiation, and optimization on the practice’s behalf.

Why it matters:
Regulated facilities across the country are managing compliance the same fragmented way they always have. We’re changing that — and we’re looking for people who want to help build what comes next.

Our technology:
We’re actively building the AI infrastructure that sits behind this model — automating compliance tracking, surfacing risks before they become problems, and creating a system that gets smarter with every client we add. If you want to build at the intersection of compliance and technology, you’re in the right place.

Our impact:
Our clients never have to worry about something falling through the cracks — obligations are coordinated, tracked, and documented in one place. We audit costs, eliminate overcharges, and deliver proof of compliance that always keeps practices inspection ready.

Position Overview:
MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions compete in a market where first impressions are made visually — across paid ads, landing pages, sales collateral, and brand touchpoints that span two distinct but related brands. The Graphic Designer owns the visual execution layer for both, producing digital and print assets that are on-brand, conversion-focused, and built to perform in a high-volume marketing environment.

This is not a passive production role. The right person brings creative instincts and design craft to every asset, understands how design decisions affect conversion and brand perception, and operates with enough independence to move fast without constant direction. Quality, consistency, and speed across two brands is the standard.

Key Responsibilities:
Digital Design:
• Design high-converting paid ad creative for Google Display, Meta (Facebook/Instagram), and Reddit across both MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions — including static, carousel, and banner formats in all required platform dimensions.
• Create landing page visual assets, hero images, icons, and supporting graphics that align with campaign objectives and brand standards.
• Design social media graphics, email headers, and digital content assets for ongoing marketing programs across both brands.
• Produce visual assets for web properties including comparison sites, client-facing tools, and web forms — in close coordination with the Web Developer.
• Iterate quickly on creative variations for A/B testing — resizing, copy swaps, color variants, and format adaptations without sacrificing design quality.

Print & Brand Collateral:
• Design and produce print-ready sales collateral including one-pagers, brochures, sell sheets, and case study layouts for both brands.
• Create branded presentation templates, pitch decks, and internal documents that maintain visual consistency across the organization.
• Develop and maintain brand style elements — iconography, typography applications, color usage, and layout templates — that ensure consistency across all market-facing surfaces.
• Support campaign launches with print materials, event graphics, and partner co-branded assets as needed.

Brand Consistency & Creative Operations:
• Maintain and enforce visual brand standards across both MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions — ensuring every asset, across every channel, looks like it belongs to the same professional organization.
• Organize and maintain a shared asset library of approved brand elements, templates, and production files that the broader team can access and use.
• Collaborate with the Paid Media Manager, SEO & AEO Specialist, and Web Developer to ensure design assets are correctly sized, formatted, and optimized for their intended platform and purpose.
• Manage multiple concurrent design requests through Asana, prioritizing effectively and communicating proactively when timelines are at risk.

Qualifications:
Required:
• 3+ years of graphic design experience with a strong portfolio demonstrating both digital and print work — portfolio submission is required and will be the primary evaluation criteria.
• Expert-level proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite — specifically Photoshop, Illustrator, and InDesign. This is non-negotiable; candidates must demonstrate mastery, not familiarity.
• Proven experience designing paid digital ad creative across Google Display, Meta, and similar platforms — with an understanding of what makes ad creative perform, not just look good.
• Solid print design experience: bleed setup, CMYK color management, print-ready file preparation, and working with print production specifications.
• Strong typography skills and a sharp eye for layout, hierarchy, and visual balance across both digital and print formats.
• Experience maintaining brand consistency across multiple brands or product lines simultaneously.
• Asana or equivalent project management tool experience — able to self-manage a queue of concurrent design requests without micromanagement.
• High proficiency with AI design tools (Adobe Firefly, Midjourney, or equivalent) for concept generation, image enhancement, and accelerating production workflows. We expect AI to be a standard part of how this person works.
• Excellent written English for creative briefs, revision requests, and cross-team communication.
• Must be available to overlap with U.S. Central Standard Time (CST) business hours for collaboration and feedback cycles.

Nice-to-Have:
• Experience with Figma or similar tools for web-adjacent design work and handoff to developers.
• Motion graphics or basic video editing capability (After Effects, Premiere, or Canva Pro) for social and ad content.
• Experience designing for healthcare, B2B SaaS, or compliance-driven brands.
• Canva Pro proficiency for rapid template creation and lightweight asset production.
• Experience working with U.S.-based marketing teams remotely, with strong cross-cultural communication skills.

Compensation & Benefits:
• Competitive hourly rate of $5–$8/hour USD, based on experience and portfolio strength
• Part-time role (20 hours/week) with flexible scheduling and weekly pay
• 100% remote work — work from anywhere in the Philippines
• Long-term engagement with growth potential within a U.S.-based organization
• Collaborative and performance-driven culture focused on professional development and results
• Exposure to U.S. marketing operations, brand strategy, and multi-brand creative environments
• Opportunity to grow your portfolio working across paid media, print, and digital channels

Technical Requirements:
• Personal laptop or desktop capable of running Adobe Creative Suite without lag
• Stable primary internet connection (at least 25–30 Mbps) with a backup internet source
• Quiet, dedicated work environment

Application Process:
To apply, please submit the following:

1. Your most updated resume; 
2. Send it to these email address:  ----------  and  ---------- 
3. Portfolio link or attached samples showcasing digital ad creative, print collateral, and brand work
4. Brief introduction about your experience and why you’re a fit for this role

Portfolio submissions without a portfolio will not be reviewed. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

MedPro Disposal and MP1 Solutions are equal opportunity employers. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
