title: Virtual Assistant — Med Spa (GHL Required, Phone-Based)
description:
Luxury Arizona med spa is hiring a full-time, long-term Virtual Assistant.

We're 112 West Medical Aesthetics & Wellness — a fast-growing, military family-owned luxury med spa in Queen Creek, Arizona. We're looking for one sharp, reliable VA to become a permanent part of our team.

You'll be the friendly voice clients hear when they call, the person managing our leads in GoHighLevel, and the creative behind our Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok presence. This isn't a task-rabbit gig — we want someone who grows with us.

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Answer inbound phone calls from clients (US clients, in clear English)
Book, reschedule, and confirm appointments in Boulevard (we'll train you)
Manage leads and pipelines in GoHighLevel (GHL)
Post content to Instagram, Facebook, and TikTok
Engage with our followers — reply to DMs and comments
Run simple automations through Zapier
Help us stay organized, follow up with leads, and close more bookings

REQUIRED

GoHighLevel (GHL) proficiency — non-negotiable. You must already use GHL daily and be comfortable building workflows, managing pipelines, and setting up campaigns. We will ask you to describe what you've built.
Excellent spoken and written English (US clients will hear you on the phone)
1+ year customer service or VA experience
Reliable internet (50+ Mbps preferred) with backup power or backup internet
Comfortable on the phone with US clients in a professional setting
Quiet workspace for calls

STRONGLY PREFERRED (big bonus)

Med spa, aesthetics clinic, dermatology, or beauty industry experience
Familiarity with Botox, fillers, lasers, hormone therapy, weight loss programs, or skincare
Boulevard scheduling software experience
Zapier automation experience
Canva graphic design skills

WHAT WE OFFER

$5–$8 USD/hour starting (depending on experience), paid weekly
2-week paid trial ? permanent full-time hire if it's a fit
Long-term role with a small, growing business — you'll be part of the team, not a contractor we forget about
Direct communication with the owner (no middlemen)
Training on Boulevard and our internal systems
Potential to grow into a senior role as we scale

HOW TO APPLY

Apply at our hiring page:  ---------- 

The application takes about 10 minutes and includes a short voice recording so we can hear how you sound on the phone. Find a quiet spot before you start.

Please don't send applications via OnlineJobs.ph chat or email — they will not be reviewed. Only applicants who complete the form will be considered.

We review every submission personally. If you're a strong fit, you'll hear from us within 5 business days.

ABOUT US

112 West Medical Aesthetics & Wellness is a luxury med spa in Queen Creek, Arizona. We're owned and operated by a military family. We offer Botox, fillers, lasers, microneedling, hormone optimization, weight loss programs, and IV therapy in a state-of-the-art facility. We're not the biggest med spa — yet — but we treat every client and every tea ---------- mber like family. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we want to hear from you.

title: Account Manager — Payment Service Provider (PSP)
description:
Location: Remote
Working Hours: 8:00am–5:00pm GMT
Role Type: Full-time

We are looking for an Account Manager to join our Payment Service Provider team. The role is focused on merchant onboarding, day-to-day account management, and coordination between merchants, technical teams, and support.

Responsibilities

* Onboard new merchants, including collecting KYC/onboarding documents and issuing merchant agreements.
* Act as the main day-to-day contact for merchants.
* Assist merchants during technical integration and coordinate with 2nd Level Support where required.
* Monitor merchant communication channels, including Telegram and Slack.
* Follow up on open merchant requests, integration issues, and operational questions.
* Ensure merchants receive timely updates and that internal teams are aligned on next steps.
* Support internal reporting and keep merchant records up to date.

Requirements

* Previous experience in payments, PSPs, fintech, merchant services, or account management.
* Strong communication skills and ability to manage multiple merchants/chats at the same time.
* Good understanding of merchant onboarding, payment flows, and basic technical integrations.
* Comfortable working with Slack, Telegram, email, and support/ticketing tools.
* Organised, proactive, and able to follow up without being chased.
* Fluent written and spoken English.

Nice to have

* Experience with card acquiring, APMs, chargebacks, settlements, MIDs, or payment gateways.
* Experience working with technical teams or API-based integrations.
* Experience in high-volume or international merchant environments.

How to apply
Please send your CV and a short summary of your relevant experience, especially any work with PSPs, payment gateways, merchant onboarding, or technical integrations.

title: Marketing Operations Analyst
description:
POSITION SUMMARY
The Marketing Operations Analyst will bring experience and a passion for data, leveraging digital technologies to port, extract, and report on data from company initiatives and marketing channels. This role requires critical thinking, complex problem-solving, and the ability to transform insights and trends into actionable strategies. The ideal candidate will have an understanding of digital marketing campaigns, the integration of CRM tools across a marketing tech stack, managing digital connections through Zapier, and developing insightful reports. They will work closely with the Senior Marketing Operations Manager to accomplish daily marketing operations tasks and assist in optimizing daily marketing operations.

This position is 100% remote.

ABOUT CARDONE VENTURES
Our mission is to help business owners achieve their personal, professional, and goals through the growth of their businesses. We work in dozens of verticals and provide strategic business guidance through courses, live events, partnerships, and investments. Our core values are the backbone of our business and guide our hiring process: we are inspirational, accountable, transparent, disciplined, aligned, and results-oriented. This company operates nationally and is growing by the day.

What is it like to work here? Check this:
 ---------- /watch?v=PIIv7sYj1Ns

This role will take on the following tasks;
- Key Report maintenance and automation
- Daily reporting automation
- Marketing Workflow management
- Execution of all marketing funnel system integrations
- Marketing funnel audits and tracking

OBJECTIVES
- Integrate digital platforms across the tech stack to ensure seamless data flow and integration.
- Manage CRM tools and digital connections to ensure optimal performance and data accuracy.
- Assist in various marketing operations tasks, including updating marketing meeting links, creating Zoom links, Shipbox tracking and backend management, Kajabi course creation, Select Your Rep pages, QR code creation, and handling marketing requests.
- Assist in automation, such as lead form workflows, Typeform creation and integration, funnel integrations and audits, and general workflows.

COMPETENCIES
- Proficiency in extracting, porting, and reporting on data from various data sources and marketing channels.
- Ability to analyze data trends and generate actionable insights.
- Expertise in data reporting, KPI tracking, and developing comprehensive reports that drive marketing and business growth.
- Skilled in integrating CRM tools and managing digital connections through Zapier.
- Expertise in leveraging digital technologies to enhance data flow, data integrations, and reporting optimizations..
- Ability to learn new technology quickly and apply key benefits to the current tech stack.
- Proficient in operating within and pulling reports from HubSpot, ClickFunnels, Kajabi, and Shopify.
- Strong critical thinking skills to analyze complex problems and develop innovative solutions.
- Capability to transform insights and trends into actionable strategies.
- Proven ability to manage projects from initiation to completion with consistent daily communication and updates.
- Proficient in tracking and campaign management through tools like Asana.

PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS
Prolonged periods sitting at a desk and working on a computer

COMMITMENT TO DIVERSITY
As an equal opportunity employer committed to meeting the needs of a multigenerational and multicultural workforce 10X Global Services recognizes that a diverse staff, reflective of our community, is an integral and welcome part of a successful and ethical business. We hire local talent at all levels regardless of race, color, religion, age, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, or disability, and actively foster inclusion in all forms both within our company and across interactions with clients, candidates, and partners.

If this position caught your eye, send us your resume! For best consideration, please complete the online application form:
 ---------- /forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfL4IymosQ_a387yakr5NaXWOoahXXKN-84cVffi5WrZWE1uw/viewform

title: Operations Manager - Luxury Bed and Breakfast
description:
We are a boutique, luxury bed and breakfast that offers an elevated guest experience with curated experiences and thoughtful, design-forward comfort. Rooted in family legacy and a passion for refined guest care, we have become a haven for travelers seeking warmth, beauty, and a truly personal touch. From chef-crafted breakfasts to intimate seasonal events and immersive cultural experiences, every detail is intentionally crafted to inspire connection, relaxation, and a sense of home. As we continue to grow, we’re building a team of individuals who care deeply about hospitality, excellence, and creating  ---------- nts that feel unforgettable.

The Operations Manager will help develop, refine, and implement daily workflows, communication rhythm, guest experience systems, and marketing organization. This is not an administrative assistant role; it is a creator role.

The person in this position must be curious and proactive. While they are not onsite, they must be in-sync with onsite staff to build processes, create templates, implement systems, and maintain the rhythm across all tea ---------- mbers. This role requires someone who thinks like a systems builder, organizes like a project manager, communicates like a hospitality professional, and operates with a deep sense of ownership.

Core Responsibilities

1. Systems Building & Workflow Creation
You will build the operational infrastructure, including:
- Automated workflows that streamline coordination between tea ---------- mbers 
- Checklists for Guest Experience, Housekeeping, Culinary support, and Events
- SOPs for check-ins, check-outs, room readiness, and breakfast coordination
- Templates for guest communication, team updates, and internal documentation
- A central digital hub where all documents, schedules, and SOPs live
- Improved systems for consistency, quality, and team alignment

2. Operational Coordination & Calendar Management
Build and maintain the master scheduling system for:
- Check-ins and check-outs
- Housekeeping and room turnovers
- Breakfast staffing
- Events and workshops
- Tea ---------- etings
Ensure all tea ---------- mbers always have correct, up-to-date schedules. Answer guest calls and ensure all requests are answered.

3. Communication & Documentation Development
- Create and automate standardized guest communication flows (pre-arrival, stay updates, checkout)
- Respond to guest reviews
- Draft agendas for weekly meetings, document, and track action items
- Write weekly internal updates for the team
- Coordinate directly with the onsite team to understand and surface opportunities for improvement
- Produce templates for event communication, vendor coordination, and internal notes
- You help maintain a smooth, polished communication experience across the business and communicate directly with customers.

4. Data Tracking, Reporting & Accountability Support
- Build the operations dashboard (weekly and monthly)
- Track occupancy, revenue, guest feedback trends, and team performance metrics
- Monitor progress toward monthly and quarterly goals
- Identify gaps and propose improvements
- Support light accountability by circulating reminders, deadlines, and summaries
- You create visibility into the health of the business.

title: ADVICE ASSOCIATE  (URGENT - FINANCIAL ADVICE )
description:
ADVICE ASSOCIATE - FINANCIAL ADVICE INDUSTRY (APPLY IF WITH EXPERIENCE)
Location: Remote
Type: Full-time
Salary: AUD$10-$15ph

ABOUT LIFE UNSHACKLED
LifeUnshackled is a platform built to help people reclaim the life they once dreamed of, clients and team alike. We exist because “retire comfortably” is not the goal, freedom is. We bring bold financial strategy and strong follow-through so clients can build real wealth and live life on their terms. You will be joining a growth-minded team that cares about ambition, high standards, and doing work that genuinely helps people unshackle their lives.

ABOUT THE ROLE
The Associate is the operational and relational lead for their assigned clients, acting as the Chief Operating Officer of each client relationship. This role combines precision in data and document management, project management discipline, people and communication skills, hands-on platform execution, and a delegation mindset. Each Associate is responsible for ensuring timely, compliant, and high-quality delivery of financial advice implementation and client outcomes, in coordination with the broader LifeUnshackled team.

FOUR KEY AREAS OF RESPONSIBILITIES
1. The Information Steward
- Custodian of all essential client information and access rights.
- Curate and maintain a “Client Support Pack” with critical data and supporting documents for rapid use in preparing application forms. This includes identity documents (individuals and entities), FATCA, ABNs, ACNs, TFNs, ESAs, bank accounts, wholesale certificates, etc.
- Collect and store tax-related and performance-related documentation (e.g. holding, distribution, loan, rental, annual, market value, tax statements).
- Maintain and enforce the standard client folder structure, file naming conventions, and financial year categorisation so that files are audit-ready at all times.
- Own the client Fact Find end-to-end — keeping it current after every meeting, statement, or client communication, and ensuring it underpins all advice documents as an auditable chain of evidence.
- Monitor and update the real-time client balance sheet, sourcing data from various platforms and reconciling across systems quarterly.
- Ensure adviser access and login credentials are in place and securely managed across all relevant platforms.
- Collaborate with the PSM to standardise and streamline information management.
- Mindset: “Would I be confident this is clean and client-ready at any time?”

2. The Delivery Project Manager
- Leader of accurate client service implementation and delivery timelines.
- Ensure investment transactions are implemented same-day and insurance applications are begun within 48 hours of approval.
- Turn meeting actions and strategic goals into clearly written, trackable tasks — nothing falls through the cracks.
- Own timelines and deadlines — chasing overdue tasks, flagging bottlenecks, and ensuring every task reaches a clear resolution.
- Delegate technical or administrative tasks appropriately while owning the outcome.
- Coordinate closely with paraplanners, the Practice Support Manager, the Principal Adviser, and external professionals.
- Ensure documents and forms are completed correctly, on time, and sent to the right parties.
- Mindset: “What’s needed to move this forward without needing Principal involvement?”

3. The Relationship Anchor
- Point of continuity, context, and connection for each client.
- Understand the needs and preferences of your clients and orchestrate effective service delivery accordingly.
- Be the primary internal advocate and coordinator for your clients.
- Track developments in the client’s personal and financial life.
- Stay across all meeting notes and ensure task systems reflect meetings.
- Notice changes in goals, mindset, or relationships and raise them with the Principal.
- Support the Principal by ensuring they are well-informed before every meeting.
- Ensure the client never has to repeat themselves or chase us for updates.
- Mindset: “What’s shifted for the client that we haven’t yet actioned?”

4. The Compliant Storyteller
- Ensuring each client file tells a compelling and compliant story.
- Keep client files audit-ready with consistent, clear, and updated notes.
- Manage the full post-meeting workflow: consent and transcription, file note generation and review, follow-up emails, and updating the Fact Find with any new information.
- Ensure fact finds, SOAs, ROAs, and client communications are accurate and well-organised.
- Maintain clarity in the chronology and context of advice decisions.
- Highlight progress and transformation in the client’s journey, tying actions back to their goals.
- Ensure documentation not only meets compliance and privacy standards but tells the narrative of value delivered.
- Mindset: “Would this file make sense to an outsider, showing a clear and compliant story of impact?”

PRIORITISATION ORDER
- From a business perspective, your tasks should be prioritised in the following order:
- Cash / investment transacting, including opening of associated accounts and completion of any KYC / onboarding requirements with third parties where this would delay a cash / investment transaction.
- Insurance applications and getting policies into force.
- Preparing meeting summaries and post-meeting emails for your clients, and any sub-10-minute tasks that are directly requested of you by the Principal Adviser.
- Responding to requests made of us by other professionals (we expect fast responses from them and should return the favour).
- Other work pertaining to clients who are in their first 6 weeks with us (including onboarding).
- Other work pertaining to your broader client list, prioritised by the annual fee that they are paying us and any relationship considerations (including preparing their reports / maintaining their client file with current information / supporting loan applications / etc).
- Balance this priority list with approaching deadlines — as deadlines near, the work becomes a higher priority.
- Anything you can’t get to in a reasonable time, just flag it, and we can try to delegate to others or make a business decision to defer that task.

REPORTING & COLLABORATION
Reports to: Practice Support Manager (PSM) for delivery oversight; Principal Adviser for client outcomes.
Collaborates with: PSM, Paraplanner, Admin Assistant, Principal Adviser.

Indicators of Success
- 100% of clients have complete, current documentation and adviser access.
- Tasks are delivered on time with minimal Principal intervention.
- High service ratings and positive feedback from assigned clients.
- Accurate and timely handoff of technical and administrative work.
- Adviser’s administrative workload is reduced for each client in your portfolio.
- Investment transactions executed same-day; insurance applications commenced within 48 hours.
- Demonstrated proficiency across all required platforms.

Expected Behaviour
- Proactive, reliable, and accountable.
- Thoughtful and detail-oriented.
- Able to prioritise and escalate as needed.
- Collaborative, not siloed.
- Focused on both client satisfaction and internal efficiency.

APPLICATION PROCESS
To apply, please submit the following:
• A 2–5 minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are the best fit for this role
• Your updated CV and LinkedIn profile link
• Names and contact details of three references (2 professional + 1 personal)

Please apply and email all details to:  ---------- 
ONLY those who follow the instructions in how to apply will be accommodated.

title: Inbound SDR
description:
About Surface Labs
> Surface Labs is an applied AI lab building the Lead Operations Center for B2B marketing teams.

> Backed by Y Combinator, Garry Tan, Dharmesh Shah (co-founder of HubSpot), and the CEO of AngelList — our AI agents have helped drive $40M+ in inbound pipeline for customers and process 50K+ leads monthly.

> We unify lead capture, qualification, routing, follow-ups, and nurture — so marketers spend 80% less time on manual ops and more time converting leads into revenue. In the last 3 months, we just grew from 5 ? 10 people across Engineering, Design, and are now bringing on an SDR to scale our outbound motion.

The Role
> You'll work directly with the CEO to run outbound experiments and open doors at the right companies. High ownership, real GTM exposure, quota-carrying role. If you’re killing it, you will be on track for Founding AE role.

What You’ll Do
> Run outbound across cold calls, LinkedIn, email, and in-person (SF events + occasional travel)
> Own the full pipeline: targeting ? messaging ? scheduling ? follow-up
> Maintain CRM hygiene and hit monthly meeting quotas

You’re a Fit If You…
> 1–3 years in sales, GTM, or customer-facing roles (or strong hustle background)
> You've done the unglamorous work: built prospect lists from scratch, sat down and ground out cold calls and emails for a full day, and done it again the next day
> Comfortable with inbound and rejection
> Used tools like Clay, Sales Navigator, or Linkedin
> Someone who’s relentless, high-energy, and always looking to keep growing

Nice To Have's:
> Experience pitching B2B SaaS or GTM tech tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, marketing automation, etc.)
> Technical interest or experience (vibe coding, Claude Code, etc)
> Willing to find ways to automate your job using AI automations/ code

title: Virtual Assistant (LinkedIn Lead Management & Client Admin)
description:
Cheeky Learning — Virtual Assistant (LinkedIn Lead Management & Client Admin)
Part-time | 15 hours/week | Remote | Ongoing

Cheeky Learning is a boutique instructional design consultancy helping schools, government agencies, and coaches build professional learning programs that actually work. We're growing, and the founder needs a sharp, proactive VA to take ownership of LinkedIn outreach, lead pipeline management, and client folder organisation.
This is not a "copy-paste tasks from a list" role. You'll be thinking, making judgements, and using AI tools to do your best work.

What you'll be doing

Managing and organising client folders so projects stay clean and easy to find
Reviewing LinkedIn connections and identifying who's worth pursuing as a potential client
Sending personalised connection requests and follow-up messages on behalf of the business
Qualifying inbound leads using an AI tool (training provided) and updating the pipeline accordingly
Following up with warm leads at the right intervals — not spammy, not forgotten
Keeping the pipeline document current, accurate, and useful

You're a great fit if you:

Write excellent English — clear, professional, and natural (not robotic)
Are comfortable using AI tools as part of your daily workflow
Have experience with LinkedIn outreach or lead management
Are highly organised and self-directed — you don't need hand-holding to get things done
Understand that small business communication needs to feel human, not templated
Can handle ambiguity and make sensible decisions without asking about every little thing


Nice to have (not essential)

Experience with CRM tools or pipeline documents
Background in education, consulting, or professional services
Previous VA experience supporting a solo operator or small business owner

Increased hours if it is the right fit

title: Design & Construct (D&C) Estimator + AU Client
description:
Job Brief  
We are seeking a detail-oriented Design & Construct (D&C) Estimator to support a well-established commercial electrical contracting business. The successful candidate will work closely with the Estimating & Design Team in Australia to assist in preparing accurate cost estimates, gathering project documentation, and supporting the end-to-end quoting process. This role is ideal for someone with strong analytical skills, proven experience in D&C electrical estimating and the ability to manage multiple deadlines in a fast-paced environment. 

Must-Haves (Essential) 
Minimum 3+ years’ experience in electrical D&C estimating on commercial projects  
Proficiency in Groundplan for take-offs and measurements 
Strong communication skills and confidence using Microsoft Teams (meetings, screen sharing, and collaboration in English) 

Key Responsibilities 
Review and interpret project briefs, PPRs (Project Progress Reports), drawings, and specifications to understand design intent and scope  
Perform quantity take-offs and measurements using Groundplan. 
Request, compare and collate supplier and subcontractor quotations as required 
Assist in preparing bill of quantities and cost estimates for commercial electrical D&C projects 
Liaise with the Estimating & Design Team in Australia to ensure accuracy and completeness of all estimates and drawings. 
Actively participate in design coordination meetings, contributing to the production of accurate and well-documented Groundplan drawings aligned with design briefs, client requests, and best practices 
Support preparation of final tender documentation and submission packs 
Provide administrative, document control, and quality-checking support during the pre-construction phase 

Requirements 
3+ years’ experience in electrical contracting estimating with a focus on Design & Construct delivery. 
Strong understanding of Australian Standards (AS:3000) & construction project processes & documentation. 
Proficient with Groundplan and Microsoft Excel (Advanced) 
Excellent attention to detail, accuracy, and analytical skills 
Strong written and verbal English communication  
Strong organisational and time-management skills 

Desirable 
Experience using SimPro (highly desirable)  
Previous experience working on Australian construction or commercial projects 
Familiarity with Australian building codes, commercial tendering processes, and D&C documentation standards  
Experience supporting remote, Australia-based teams. 
Qualification or certification in Electrical, Construction Management, Quantity Surveying or Engineering 

Software Proficiency 
Groundplan – Essential for take-offs and drawing interpretation  
Microsoft 365 Suite – Teams (Essential), OneDrive, Planner, Excel (Advanced), Word (Advanced) 
Estimating/take-off tools such as Groundplan or similar 
OneDrive (Teams, Planner, etc) 
Project or Job Management Systems – SimPRO (advantageous)  

Education 

Degree or diploma in Construction Management, Electrical Trade or Engineering, Quantity Surveying, or related field preferred. 

Time Zone: Australian Business Hours 
Applicant Location: Must be located in the Philippines

title: AU Mortgage Loan Processor
description:
Loan Processing & Broker Support Executive
Remote / Work from Home | AEST Hours |Philippines-based

To Apply, please fill out this form:  ---------- 

Who We're Looking For:
We're building a dedicated team of loan processing and broker support professionals to work directly with Australian mortgage brokers. If you have hands-on experience in AU mortgage processing and know your way around Connective Mercury and ApplyOnline, we want to hear from you.

What You'll Be Doing Every Day:
Managing loan files end-to-end — from initial client follow-up through to post-settlement
Data entry in Mercury CRM and ApplyOnline (NextGen)
Ordering and tracking property valuations and pricing discount requests via lender portals
Preparing compliance documents, credit diary notes, and formal approval letters
Running serviceability calculators and preparing funds-to-complete tables
Ordering and interpreting Equifax credit reports
Coordinating discharge forms, solicitor letters, and settlement confirmation emails
Managing client appointments and follow-up communications
Post-settlement tasks and client retention activities

You Must Have experience with any of the following:
Connective Mercury CRM — hands-on experience, not just awareness
ApplyOnline / NextGen — confident with lodgement and tracking
Lender portal experience — valuation ordering, pricing requests, discharge forms
AU mortgage process knowledge — you understand the full journey from pre-submission to settlement
Income assessment — PAYG (payslips) and self-employed / company / trust structures
AEST business hours availability — full-time, Monday to Friday
Excellent written and spoken English — you'll communicate directly with brokers and clients

Minimum Requirements:
2–3 years of experience in Australian mortgage loan processing or broker support
Solid understanding of residential lending, lender policies, and NCCP compliance
Comfortable handling multiple loan files simultaneously with strong attention to detail
Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
Fast internet connection and a professional home office setup

Nice to Have:
Cert IV in Finance & Mortgage Broking
Experience with Quickli, Simpology, Salestrekker, MyCRM, or Podium
Exposure to construction loans, SMSF lending, or trust/company income structures

Education:
A bachelor's degree in Finance, Banking, Accounting, or a related field is preferred — but not required. We value relevant AU mortgage experience above all else. Non-graduates with 2+ years of hands-on broker support experience are strongly encouraged to apply.

What We Offer:
Competitive salary (based on experience)
HMO coverage
Leave credits
13th Month Pay

title: AU Asset Finance
description:
Asset Finance Support
Remote / Work from Home |AEST Hours | Supporting Australian Asset Finance Brokers

Who We're Looking For
We're building a dedicated team of asset finance support professionals to work directly with Australian asset finance brokers. If you have hands-on experience processing equipment, vehicle, or commercial asset finance applications and understand the end-to-end broker workflow, we want to hear from you.

To Apply, please submit this form:   ---------- 

What You'll Be Doing Every Day

Managing asset finance files end-to-end — from initial client enquiry through to settlement and post-settlement
Data entry and file management in broker CRM platforms (Mercury, Salesforce, or equivalent)
Preparing and submitting finance applications to lenders via broker portals
Ordering and tracking asset valuations and credit assessments
Requesting and tracking pricing and rate concessions from lenders
Running asset finance calculators — balloon payments, residual values, comparison rates, repayment schedules
Preparing compliance documents, credit diary notes, settlement letters, and payout figures
Ordering and interpreting commercial credit reports (e.g. Equifax, CreditorWatch, Veda)
Coordinating with lenders, vendors, dealers, and clients throughout the approval and settlement process
Managing discharge and payout requests, PPSR checks, and ASIC searches
Preparing funds-to-complete confirmations and settlement instructions
Post-settlement tasks — invoice reconciliation, asset registration follow-up, client retention


You Must Have
2–3 years of experience in Australian asset finance — broker-side, not lender operations
Asset finance product knowledge — equipment finance, chattel mortgage, novated lease, hire purchase, commercial hire purchase, finance lease, operating lease
 Lender portal experience — submitting and tracking applications with major AU asset finance lenders (e.g. Westpac, NAB, ANZ, Macquarie, Pepper Money, Angle Finance, Grow Finance, Shift, ScotPac, Liberty, Fifo Capital)
Income assessment — PAYG, self-employed, company, and trust financials for asset finance purposes
CRM proficiency — Mercury, Salesforce, or comparable broker CRM
Understanding of GST, ABN, and tax implications relevant to asset finance structures
AEST business hours — full-time, Monday to Friday
Excellent written and spoken English — direct communication with brokers, clients, and lenders

Minimum Requirements

Strong understanding of chattel mortgage and finance lease structures
Comfortable handling multiple files simultaneously with strict attention to detail and deadlines
Familiar with PPSR (Personal Property Securities Register) searches and registrations
Understanding of responsible lending obligations and privacy requirements under Australian law
Proficient in Microsoft Office — Word, Excel, Outlook
Fast internet connection and professional home office setup


Nice to Have

Experience with novated leasing or fleet finance
Exposure to construction equipment, yellow goods, or specialised asset classes
Familiarity with commercial property-backed asset finance
Experience using ApplyOnline, Loanapp, or Simpology for commercial submissions
Cert IV in Finance & Mortgage Broking or equivalent


Education
A bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related field is preferred but not required. We value hands-on Australian asset finance experience above qualifications. Candidates without a degree but with 2+ years of direct broker support experience are strongly encouraged to apply.

What We Offer

Competitive base salary based on experience
HMO coverage
Leave credits
13th Month Pay
Career growth — we're building a 50+ person capability centre within 12 months


About the Role
This is a direct broker support role — you work for the broker, not a lender or bank. You are the engine behind the broker's business, managing their asset finance pipeline, keeping deals moving, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks between application and settlement.
You'll be part of Pierian Australia's dedicated back-office capability centre, supporting Australian asset finance brokers across commercial equipment, vehicles, technology, and other asset classes.

title: Project Coordinator
description:
#### ---- ATTENTION ---- ####

ATTENTION - ALL APPLICATIONS MUST GO THROUGH THIS LINK (Log into onlinejobs to reveal application link):  ----------

ATTENTION - ALL APPLICATIONS MUST GO THROUGH THIS LINK (Log into onlinejobs to reveal application link):  ----------

As a Project Coordinator at Ethnolink, you'll play a vital role in our multicultural communications
industry, managing translation projects from inception to completion, ensuring timely delivery,
adherence to budget, and the highest quality standards. Your daily activities will involve intricate
collaboration with clients, translators, and our internal Production team to facilitate seamless
translation projects.

You will leverage your meticulous coordination skills to manage project resources, maintain
excellent client relationships, and ensure our operational and financial systems are up-to-date and
accurate. This entry level role provides an exciting opportunity to make a significant difference by
delivering culturally appropriate resources to diverse communities in Australia.


Key Responsibilities
- End to end project coordination of translation projects, including managing project scope,
deadlines, resource assignments and project budgets.
- Manage most project types reasonably autonomously.
- Coordinate with translators and other vendors ensuring that they have all the necessary
information and resources to complete assignments.
- Continually monitor project progress, making adjustments as necessary to ensure project
completion within timelines and budgets.
- Ensure compliance with industry standards - ISO-17100 and ISO-18587.
- Support the quality management process, including proofreading documents and reviewing
video synchronization.
- Use appropriate tools and systems to analyse files and prepare them for translation, and to
convert and publish translated files.
- Participate in daily, weekly and monthly meetings with the Production team, to ensure that
projects are being effectively managed.
- Develop and maintain business relationships with assigned clients and ensure that their
experience with Ethnolink is world-class.
- Input data accurately and in a timely fashion into our operational & financial systems.
- Escalate any issues or roadblocks in a timely manner to the relevant stakeholders or
management.
- Provide an exceptional level of customer service by being prompt, professional, and
courteous in all client interactions.
- Respond to online chat enquiries, phone calls, and emails in a timely and helpful manner,
ensuring that all client requests are acknowledged and actioned efficiently.
- Proactively communicate with clients to provide updates, confirm requirements, and clarify
queries to ensure accuracy and satisfaction.
- Work regular weekend and evening (AU-time) hours.

Measurables:
- Achieve an average of 65% Gross Profit Margin (GPM) across assigned projects by
managing project costs effectively.
- Contribute to the team’s goal of an 80+ Customer Net Promoter Score (NPS) by providing a
world-class client experience.
- Achieve an average of 95% timely delivery of projects by managing project schedules and
resources effectively.

Desired Skills & Qualifications
- At least 1–2 years of experience working in the translation and localisation industry
- Operations or project coordination experience
- Strong communication skills and excellent grasp of the English language, both written and
spoken
- Excellent phone, email and instant messaging communication skills, with a focus on
providing prompt, professional and friendly client service
- High responsiveness to client enquiries across online chat, phone, and email channels
- Excellent time management skills and the ability to work autonomously as well as
collaboratively within a small team
- Exceptional interpersonal skills, capable of working effectively with different personalities
and stakeholders
- Attention to detail and a well-organised, detail-oriented approach to managing tasks and
projects
- An understanding, appreciation and passion for cultural diversity
- Familiarity with current technologies such as desktop sharing, cloud services and VoIP
- Knowledge of online calendars and scheduling tools (e.g. Google Calendar)
- High proficiency with Google Suite applications and Microsoft Office
- An ability to learn and grow quickly
- Alignment with Ethnolink’s core values

title: HIRING: Experienced Service Coordinator (Security Industry | Remote | PST Hours)
description:
We are looking for an experienced and highly skilled Service Coordinator to join our remote team! This is a leadership role managing guard schedules, client relationships, and operational processes for a security services company based in California, USA.

About the Role
This is a remote, full-time position requiring 24/7 availability for urgent issues, as is standard in the security industry. You’ll work closely with guards, clients, and internal teams to ensure seamless operations, high customer satisfaction, and efficient service delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Guard & Client Management

- Oversee guard performance, attendance, and shift replacements.
- Act as HR contact for assigned guards — handle disputes, complaints, and corrective actions.
- Serve as primary liaison between guards and clients.
- Ensure services meet contract scope and client expectations.
- Manage contract renewals and client retention efforts.
- Conduct regular quality assurance checks and address performance concerns.

Scheduling & Workforce Management (Humanity Software)

- Audit and maintain accurate employee profiles.
- Handle last-minute shift coverage and minimize service gaps.

I ----------  Management & Compliance (SilverTrack)

Automation & Process Efficiency

Reporting & Analytics

- Generate reports on guard performance, i ---------- , client satisfaction, and retention.
- Analyze operational data and recommend process improvements.

Collaboration & Support

- Work closely with an assigned assistant for admin tasks.
- Attend virtual meetings and client presentations.
- Be familiar with California, especially Southern California, for efficient guard deployment.

5+ years in customer service and operations management.
3+ years in client management or service coordination (security industry preferred).
3+ years experience with QBO for billing and collections.
Experience working with U.S.-based companies (California experience preferred).

I ----------  Management: SilverTrack (or similar)

title: Transaction Coordinator (Real Estate Platform)
description:
Transaction Coordinator Position – NexPath Real Estate Platform

NexPath Real Estate Platform is looking for a highly experienced, dependable Transaction Coordinator to help manage real estate transactions from contract to close.

We are looking for someone who is organized, detail-oriented, responsive, and confident handling a high volume of files. This position is for someone who already understands real estate transaction coordination and does not need a lot of training.

The ideal candidate is someone who is great at time blocking, communication, deadlines, follow-up, and keeping multiple transactions moving smoothly at the same time.

Requirements:

* Minimum of 5+ years of Transaction Coordinator experience
* Must understand real estate terminology and the contract-to-close process
* Experience with Dotloop is preferred
* Experience with AFrame is strongly preferred
* Experience with Alabama, Florida, Mississippi, Louisiana, and/or Georgia transactions is a big plus
* Must be able to manage a high transaction volume
* Must be comfortable handling 15–20+ transactions at one time
* Experience managing 25–30 transactions per month is a major plus
* Must be dependable, responsive, organized, and good with deadlines
* Must be able to communicate professionally with agents, clients, lenders, title companies, and other parties involved in the transaction

When applying, please send the following:

1. A short summary of your Transaction Coordinator experience
2. The states you have worked in
3. The platforms you have used, including Dotloop, AFrame, Open to Close, or any others
4. How many transactions you have managed at one time
5. How many transactions you feel confident handling per month
6. A photo of your desk/workspace
7. A screenshot of your internet speed


We are looking for someone who can help create a smooth and professional contract-to-close experience for our agents and clients. This is a great opportunity for someone who is experienced, reliable, and ready to be part of a growing real estate platform.

Group Interview:

We will be hosting a group interview on Friday at 5:00 PM Central Time. Please make sure you are available at that time before applying.

title: Experienced DMCA & Brand Protection Specialist for Shopify Store Takedowns
description:
Experienced DMCA & Brand Protection Specialist for Shopify Store Takedowns

We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented DMCA Specialist to help protect our brand from copycat Shopify stores and intellectual property infringement.

Your main responsibility will be identifying stores that copy our products, branding, images, or content, and submitting DMCA takedown requests to Shopify and other relevant platforms.

Responsibilities:
- Find Shopify stores that infringe on our brand or intellectual property.
- Gather evidence of copyright and trademark violations.
- Prepare and submit DMCA takedown notices.
- Follow up on submitted complaints until resolution.
- Maintain records of all reports and outcomes.
- Monitor for new infringements on an ongoing basis.

Requirements:
- Experience with DMCA takedown requests.
- Understanding of copyright, trademarks, and intellectual property protection.
- Strong attention to detail.
- Good written English.
- Ability to work independently and provide regular updates.
- Experience with Shopify is a plus.

Nice to Have:
- Previous experience working with e-commerce brands.
- Experience reporting violations to Shopify, Meta, Google, TikTok, or marketplaces.
- Knowledge of online brand protection tools.

What We Need From You:
Please include:
1. A brief summary of your experience with DMCA takedowns.
2. Examples of successful takedown cases you have handled.
3. Your hourly rate.
4. The word "PROTECT" at the beginning of your application so we know you read the entire job post.

Position Type:
- Part-time to start (with potential for ongoing work).
- Remote.

We are looking for someone who can take ownership of this process and help us actively protect our brand online.

title: Real Estate Capital Raising & Investor Outreach Associate
description:
We are seeking a highly motivated Real Estate Capital Raising & Investor Outreach Associate to support investor, lender, and capital partner origination for an active real estate platform with a growing pipeline of acquisition, refinance, sale-leaseback, healthcare real estate, supportive housing, and value-add opportunities. US experience preferred 
This is a performance-oriented role suited for a strong communicator with experience in real estate, capital markets, investor relations, private lending, commercial brokerage, debt/equity placement, or high-ticket financial sales. The ideal candidate is confident identifying capital sources, opening new relationships, managing follow-up, organizing investor conversations, and helping move prospects from initial interest to committed capital.

Key Responsibilities

Investor and Capital Partner Outreach
• Identify and contact high-net-worth investors, family offices, private lenders, debt funds, equity groups, commercial real estate investors, brokers, and strategic capital partners.
• Conduct outbound outreach through calls, emails, LinkedIn, and other approved communication channels.
• Qualify prospects based on investment appetite, capital capacity, preferred deal structure, and timing.
Investor Pipeline Management
• Build, organize, and maintain a structured pipeline of investor and capital partner prospects.
• Track calls, follow-ups, active discussions, objections, next steps, and committed capital status.
• Ensure all prospect activity is accurately recorded and updated in the CRM.
Deal Positioning and Investor Communication
• Help present real estate opportunities using teasers, pitch decks, financial summaries, property narratives, investor packages, and track record materials.
• Communicate key deal points clearly, including opportunity type, capital need, projected returns, collateral, structure, and investment rationale.
• Match prospects with the most relevant opportunities, including equity, bridge debt, refinance, joint venture, sale-leaseback, acquisition financing, or preferred equity structures.
?
Follow-Up and Relationship Development
• Manage consistent follow-up after investor conversations.
• Send requested materials, answer initial questions, schedule follow-up meetings, and move qualified prospects to the next stage.
• Escalate serious prospects to company principals or senior tea ---------- mbers when appropriate.
Market Feedback and Capital Strategy Support
• Report investor objections, return expectations, pricing feedback, lender concerns, collateral preferences, and deal-structure suggestions.
• Help the team understand market appetite for different opportunities and capital structures.
• Support improvements to investor messaging, deal positioning, and outreach strategy based on market response.
Closing and Diligence Support
• Coordinate diligence requests, investor calls, document sharing, and follow-up communication.
• Support the handoff process to legal, finance, or transaction teams once a prospect becomes serious.
• Help ensure a smooth transition from investor interest to commitment and closing support.
Ideal Candidate Profile
Experience
• 2–7+ years of experience in one or more of the following areas:
o Real estate capital raising
o Investor relations
o Debt or equity placement
o Commercial brokerage
o Private lending
o Investment sales
o Real estate finance
o High-ticket financial sales
Network
• Existing relationships with investors, lenders, brokers, family offices, high-net-worth individuals, or real estate capital sources are strongly preferred.
• Ability to build new relationships quickly and professionally.
Communication Skills
• Confident on calls and comfortable speaking with investors, lenders, brokers, and capital partners.
• Polished written communication skills.
• Able to explain real estate investment opportunities clearly and professionally.
Sales and Follow-Up Discipline
• Strong outbound mentality with the ability to make calls, send emails, manage rejection, and follow up consistently.
• Comfortable working in a performance-driven environment.
• Persistent, organized, and proactive in moving conversations forward.
Real Estate and Capital Markets Knowledge
• Understanding of basic real estate and finance concepts, including:
o Equity
o Debt
o LTV
o DSCR
o NOI
o Refinance
o Acquisition financing
o Joint ventures
o Collateral
o Investor returns
Organization and CRM Management
• Strong ability to maintain clean records, clear notes, structured pipelines, and next-step discipline.
• Detail-oriented and reliable in tracking prospect communication and deal progress.

This role is best suited for candidates who are comfortable with a performance-based compensation model and motivated by results, relationship-building, and successful capital placement

Compensation Structure
Base: $0–$2,500/month draw or stipend
Commission: 1%–3% of capital successfully raised

title: Marketing Growth Lead - experienced.
description:
MARKETING GROWTH LEAD


About the Role

We are looking for a Growth Marketing Lead who can own our entire growth engine — from getting discovered online to keeping users engaged and coming back. This is a high-ownership role suited to someone who thinks in funnels, moves fast, and is as comfortable analysing data as they are writing compelling copy.

You will be the primary driver of organic acquisition, app store presence, CRM flows, and community engagement. You will work directly with the founding team and have significant influence over how the brand grows.



Primary Responsibilities

Search & Discoverability (SEO, GEO, ASO)

Own end-to-end SEO strategy — technical audits, on-page optimisation, content strategy, and link building

Drive Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) — structure content and build authority so the product surfaces in AI-generated answers on ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, and similar platforms

Manage App Store Optimisation (ASO) across iOS App Store and Google Play — keyword research, A/B testing creatives, monitoring ratings and reviews, improving conversion rates on store listings

Track and report on organic search performance using Google Search Console, Ahrefs, or equivalent

CRM & Email Marketing

Own the CRM platform (HubSpot / Klaviyo / Brevo or equivalent) — build and maintain user segments, lifecycle stages, and automation flows

Design and execute email campaigns including onboarding sequences, re-engagement flows, newsletters, and transactional messages

Monitor deliverability, open rates, CTR, and conversion — continuously test and improve

Social Media & Community Engagement

Monitor and respond to comments, DMs, and mentions across all social platforms — maintain a consistent, on-brand voice

Draft replies and engage authentically with users, partners, and the broader community



Secondary Responsibilities

Plan, schedule, and publish organic social media content across Instagram, LinkedIn, X/Twitter, TikTok, or relevant platforms

Build and maintain a content calendar aligned with product launches, campaigns, and seasonal  ---------- nts

Produce or brief landing page copy with CRO principles in mind

Coordinate with external freelancers or agencies where needed (designers, content writers, link builders)

Set up and maintain marketing dashboards ( USE SUITABLE TOOLS )

Support influencer and creator outreach when relevant to growth campaigns

Set up all suitable and appropriate KPIs and monitor all marketing Deliverables.



REQUIREMENTS

Must-Have

3+ years of hands-on growth or digital marketing experience, ideally at a startup or product-led company

title: GoHighLevel Funnel & Meta Ads Setup Virtual Assistant
description:

title: Senior Automation & Web Developer — Zapier / Airtable / APIs / WordPress / GTM / AI
description:
About Us

We're a fast-growing Australian business finance company. We're a lean team that competes through automation — our tech stack does the work of 100 people, and we're hiring one senior person to own, extend, and improve it full-time. (Company details shared with shortlisted candidates at interview stage.)

This is NOT a VA role and NOT a junior dev role. You'll be the technical builder for the business, working directly with the owner (who built the current systems and knows them deeply — you won't be guessing alone).

Our Stack (you'll work across most of this)

-Zapier (advanced: code steps, webhooks, paths, error handling — not just simple Zaps)
-Airtable (automations, scripting, complex base design, API)
-Pipedrive CRM (API, webhooks, workflow automation)
-ActiveCampaign (automations, API)
-Python (Flask services deployed on Google Cloud Run)
-REST APIs generally — reading API docs and building reliable integrations is the core of this job
-LLM integrations (Claude / OpenAI APIs — building automations that use AI models via API)
-WordPress — custom code (PHP/HTML/CSS/JS), performance optimisation (Core Web Vitals), and ongoing site management. Not just themes and plugins — you must be able to work in the code.
-Google Tag Manager & tracking — GTM container setup, dataLayer coding, UTM parameter architecture, conversion tracking (including server-side / Conversions API)
-Cloudflare, DNS, domains & hosting — DNS records, security settings, SSL, domain management (GoDaddy etc.), hosting environments
-Softr (client portal on top of Airtable)
-Plus: SMS APIs (Twilio/Sinch), Meta Conversions API, and AI voice APIs

You don't need experience with every tool — but you MUST be strong on Zapier + Airtable + APIs + Python + WordPress (real coding, not just plugins), and able to learn the rest fast from documentation.

What You'll Do

-Build new automations and improve existing ones (lead routing, CRM workflows, client notifications, and LLM-powered automation systems)
-Own our WordPress website end-to-end: performance (Core Web Vitals), custom code changes, design polish, and reliability
-Own our tracking infrastructure: GTM, dataLayer, UTM architecture, and conversion tracking accuracy across Meta and Google
-Manage the infrastructure layer: Cloudflare, DNS, domains, hosting, SSL, and security settings
-Innovate with AI. We use LLMs heavily across our systems and expect you to stay current with new models, tools, and techniques — and to proactively propose where AI can improve or replace existing processes. "I saw this new capability and built a prototype" is the energy we pay bonuses for.
-Debug production issues by finding root causes — not patching symptoms
-Integrate third-party APIs by reading official documentation carefully and verifying exact parameter specs (this is a hard requirement for us — guessing at API parameters is how systems break)
-Write clear documentation: every automation you ship comes with a written spec and runbook. No exceptions.
-Proactively identify problems and improvements before being asked — we pay bonuses specifically for this

Compensation

-Base: USD $2,500–3,000/month depending on experience
-Monthly performance bonus: USD $300–800 based on shipped systems that run reliably, documentation quality, and -proactive problem-finding. Opportunity to go even higher, based on performance.
-Annual base increases for strong performers
-Long-term, stable, full-time role — we are building for years, not months

Hours Of Work

Full-time, Monday–Friday, working - 9am - 6pm SYDNEY TIME.

Who This Role Suits

-5+ years building automations, integrations, and websites professionally — you're a senior generalist who's comfortable owning the entire technical surface of a business, not a specialist in one tool
-You're a problem solver and have a high degree of attention to detail
-You chase root causes, not symptoms — "I found why it breaks" instead of "I added a workaround"
-You read API documentation for fun (or at least without pain)
-You're genuinely excited by AI — you already use LLMs in your own workflow, follow new releases, and have built things with -AI APIs because you wanted to, not just because a client asked
-You test your own work before calling it done
-You communicate clearly and proactively — if something is blocked or broken, we hear it from you first
-You want one stable long-term client who pays well and pays bonuses, not five gigs

Who This Role Does NOT Suit

-Anyone whose Zapier experience is limited to simple two-step Zaps
-Anyone whose WordPress experience is page builders only (Elementor/Divi) — if you can't work directly in PHP/HTML/CSS/JS and diagnose performance issues in the code, this isn't your role
-Anyone who hardcodes credentials or guesses at API parameters instead of checking docs
-Anyone juggling multiple clients (we require exclusivity during work hours)
-Agencies, teams, or anyone planning to subcontract any part of this work. We are hiring you, one individual — the person we interview is the person who does the work, always. Given the financial data our systems handle, subcontracting is treated as a serious breach and grounds for immediate termination.
-Anyone uncomfortable with logged/audited system access — we run strict security practices (scoped credentials, audit trails). Honest builders find this boring; that's the point.

Security Note (read this — it matters)

We work with financial data, so we follow least-privilege access practices: you'll use your own named accounts and scoped API keys, production credentials are managed by the owner, and system activity is logged. This is standard, professional, and protects you as much as us. If that sounds reasonable, we'll get along great.

SKILL REQUIREMENTS 

Required:

-Zapier (Expert)
-Airtable (Expert)
-AI Tool Use — Claude Code / ChatGPT (Expert)
-API Integration / REST APIs (Expert)
-WordPress — custom code & performance (Advanced)
-Google Tag Manager / UTM / conversion tracking (Advanced)
-Python (Advanced)
-HTML / CSS / JavaScript (Advanced)
-Webhooks (Advanced)
-Cloudflare / DNS / domain & hosting management (Intermediate–Advanced)

Strongly preferred:

-Pipedrive or similar CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce)
-ActiveCampaign or similar (Mailchimp, Klaviyo)
-Google Cloud Platform (Cloud Run, Cloud Functions)
-Flask or similar Python web frameworks
-Meta Conversions API / server-side tracking

Nice to have:

-Softr / no-code app builders
-Twilio / Sinch / Kixie, SMS APIs
-SQL


HOW TO APPLY 

To apply, send a message that includes ALL of the following — applications missing any item will not be read:

1. Start your message with the word "PINEAPPLE" so we know you read the full post.

2. Your 2–3 best automation/integration projects — for each: what it did, which tools/APIs were involved, and one problem you hit and how you solved it. Specific detail matters more than polish. Include links wherever possible — live websites you've built or optimised, GitHub, portfolio, or Loom walkthroughs. Claims we can verify are worth far more than claims we can't.

3. Answer this question: A Zapier webhook step is receiving data from a third-party API, but one field arrives blank for about 4% of records even though the source system shows the data exists. Describe, step by step, how you would diagnose this. (There is no single right answer — we're looking at how you think.)

4. Answer this question: A WordPress landing page scores poorly on Core Web Vitals (high CLS, slow LCP) and its Meta conversion tracking is undercounting leads. Briefly: what are the first things you'd check for each problem?

5. Your expected monthly salary in USD.

6. Confirm you can work 9:00am–6:00pm Sydney time (including the earlier Manila start during Australian daylight saving) full-time and exclusively.

7. A short video introduction (3–5 minutes, camera on) — Loom or similar. Briefly: who you are, what you've built that you're proudest of, and what specifically about this role appeals to you. No script needed, no editing, no polish — we just want to meet the actual person we'd be talking to every morning.

If you're selected, we'll contact you back to arrange a quick interview to see if we're a good fit to work together.

title: Senior PHP Developer For Gaming Website & Technical Operations Specialist
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled PHP Developer to help maintain, improve, and scale an established web platform with a large user database and active production environment.

This is not a junior role.

We are looking for someone who is comfortable working inside a mature codebase, understanding existing architecture, making improvements without introducing regressions, and helping establish proper development workflows and operational processes.

The ideal candidate is equally comfortable writing code, managing databases, configuring environments, troubleshooting production issues, and working independently to solve technical challenges.

What You'll Be Working On

- Maintaining and improving an existing PHP web application
- Enhancing performance, stability, and scalability
- Fixing bugs and implementing new features
- Modernizing legacy systems where appropriate
- Managing development, staging, and production environments
- Assisting with third-party integrations and platform setup
- Working with large databases and user data
- Improving documentation and deployment processes

Core Responsibilities

PHP Development

- Develop and maintain PHP applications
- Debug and resolve production issues
- Improve existing functionality without disrupting live users
- Review code for security, performance, and maintainability
- Refactor legacy code where appropriate
- Implement new features and enhancements

Git & Development Workflow

- Establish and maintain proper Git workflows
- Setup and manage Development, Staging, and Production branches
- Create deployment procedures and release processes
- Perform code reviews and quality assurance testing
- Ensure safe deployment practices

Database Management

- Work with large MySQL / MariaDB databases
- Manage database imports, exports, backups, and migrations
- Optimize database performance
- Troubleshoot data-related issues
- Work comfortably within phpMyAdmin and direct SQL environments
- Maintain data integrity and security

Infrastructure & Operations

- Configure and manage development environments
- Assist with Linux server administration
- Troubleshoot hosting and deployment issues
- Monitor application performance
- Manage SSL certificates, DNS, email systems, and web hosting configurations
- Work with Apache, Nginx, or similar web servers

Integrations & Platform Setup

- Configure OAuth integrations (Google, Microsoft,  ---------- , etc.)
- Setup APIs and third-party services
- Troubleshoot authentication systems
- Configure webhooks and external integrations
- Work with Stripe, payment gateways, email providers, analytics platforms, and SaaS tools

Technical Project Management

- Document technical processes
- Create implementation plans
- Identify technical debt and propose improvements
- Work independently with minimal supervision
- Recommend best practices and system improvements

Required Skills

- Advanced PHP experience
- MySQL / MariaDB experience
- GitHub experience
- Experience using professional IDEs (PhpStorm preferred)
- Strong debugging and troubleshooting abilities
- Understanding of software architecture and application design
- Experience working on existing production applications
- Strong understanding of web security best practices
- Experience with Linux hosting environments
- Experience with APIs and third-party integrations
- Ability to work independently and make sound technical decisions

Preferred Qualifications

- Laravel experience
- Composer experience
- Apache and Nginx administration
- OAuth implementation experience
- Stripe integration experience
- Experience managing large user databases
- Experience with CI/CD pipelines
- JavaScript experience
- HTML and CSS proficiency
- Docker experience
- Redis experience
- Cloud hosting experience (AWS, DigitalOcean, OVH, Vultr, etc.)

What We're Looking For

We are not looking for a developer who only wants to build new features.

We are looking for someone who can take ownership of a platform, understand existing systems, identify risks before changes are deployed, and make thoughtful improvements without breaking production.

The ideal candidate is comfortable jumping between development, database administration, server management, integrations, troubleshooting, and technical planning.

You should be the type of person who can:

- Safely upgrade an existing platform
- Setup proper development workflows
- Manage large databases
- Configure OAuth providers
- Debug difficult production issues
- Work independently without constant oversight
- Think critically about long-term scalability and maintainability

Screening Questions

- How many years of PHP experience do you have?
- Describe the largest PHP application you have worked on.
- What IDE do you use and why?
- Explain your preferred Git workflow for Development, Staging, and Production environments.
- Have you worked with databases containing more than 100,000 users? Please describe your experience.
- Describe a situation where you inherited a legacy PHP application. How did you approach understanding and improving it?
- Have you implemented OAuth integrations before? Which providers?
- How comfortable are you working directly with MySQL, MariaDB, SQL queries, backups, imports, exports, and phpMyAdmin?
- Describe a production outage or major issue you resolved.
- Please provide links to your GitHub profile, portfolio, LinkedIn, or examples of previous work.

Important

To confirm you have read this entire job posting, begin your application with the phrase:

"Legacy Systems First"

Applications that do not include this phrase may not be considered.

title: Senior Accountant
description:
Senior Accountant — Full-Time Remote
US Fintech Client • Big 4 / MNC Background Preferred

WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU
- PHP 80,000–120,000/month, commensurate with experience. Plus two bonuses each year— mid-year and year-end.
- 100% remote, forever. Work from Davao, Cebu, Baguio, the beach, the mountains —wherever you do your best work.
- No staffing tetris. You're dedicated full-time to ONE US client. No juggling five accounts, no context-switching. Real ownership, real career growth.
- 24 days of paid time off. Not to escape your job — to live your life on your terms.
- Direct mentorship from Jane Eagle (outsourced CFO, former Big 4) and real exposure to US executive teams. You'll learn at the level you want to be at, not just at the level your current role requires.
- PHP 5,000 monthly health stipend — apply it toward the HMO or insurance that fits your life.
- High bar, with us in your corner. Our client has high standards and moves fast — but you're working with us, not for them. We hire people who take pride in great work, and we make sure you have the support, structure, and advocacy to do it well.

ABOUT ME
Mom, surfer, party-lover, and teller of jokes. I help the world's biggest companies — and scrappy startups — make sense of their money as a fractional CFO (Big 4 alum, Berkeley & NYU MBA). Through Jane Eagle Consulting, I hire from a narrow pool — CPAs from top PH universities with Big 4 or large MNC experience — and place them with growing US clients. For this role, I'm the fractional CFO, so when I hire you, I'm hiring you onto a team I'm already on. If you've got high standards, want to learn from someone who's been in the seat, and still want a real life outside of work — let's talk.

ABOUT THE ROLE
We're hiring a Senior Accountant for our US-based fintech client (B2B payments technology). You'll report directly to the company's Controller and own the day-to-day accounting — AR, AP, and month-end close — for a growing, well-funded company.

This is a hands-on Senior Accountant seat, not a leadership role. You'll be in the books daily and partnering closely with the Controller, Customer Success, and engineering teams. There is real growth potential as the business scales.

The client moves fast and sets a high bar — you'll be doing real, meaningful work. The benefit of working with Jane Eagle Consulting: you have us in your corner. We mentor, advocate for you, and make sure you have what you need to succeed.

WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING
Accounts Receivable & Collections
• Generate and review monthly invoices in QuickBooks Online (QBO)
• Identify and flag discrepancies; perform month-over-month variance analysis
• Verify subscription start dates for newly onboarded merchants
• Follow up with merchants on outstanding invoices and manage collections
• Partner with Customer Success on renewals validation
• Support ad hoc reporting on revenue and collections

Accounts Payable & Vendor Management
• Review incoming vendor invoices and categorize to appropriate expense accounts
• Manage approval workflows in Ramp
• Flag and escalate material invoice amounts for review

General Bookkeeping & Month-End Close
• Maintain and execute the month-end checklist (accruals, prepaids, fixed assets)
• Record and reconcile bonus accruals, commission accruals, and payroll entries
• Perform monthly bank reconciliations
• Manage fixed asset, depreciation, and amortization schedules
• Manage the company's virtual mailbox (Earth Class Mail)

WHO YOU NEED TO BE
• Certified Public Accountant (CPA), Philippines
• Bachelor's degree in Accountancy from a top Philippine university (Ateneo, UP, La Salle, UST, or equivalent)
• 4-8 years of accounting experience
• Big 4 audit experience (SGV/EY, Isla Lipana/PwC, KPMG, Deloitte) OR senior accounting experience at a large US/multinational corporation
• Hands-on experience with QuickBooks Online (QBO) — non-negotiable for this role
• Strong Excel skills (pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, financial modeling)
• Solid understanding of US GAAP
• Direct experience with full-cycle month-end close, journal entries, and reconciliations
• Excellent written and spoken English
• Available during Pacific or Eastern Standard Time hours

NICE TO HAVE
• Experience with subscription/SaaS revenue, recurring billing, or partner revenue share models
• Familiarity with Ramp, Salesforce/HubSpot, or Earth Class Mail
• Comfort working with AI tools (e.g., Claude) for research and workflow automation

HOW TO APPLY
We want to get to know you — not just your resume. Send us:
1. Your updated resume (PDF preferred)
2. A short written note OR a short video — whichever you prefer — telling us about your professional background (especially your Big 4 or MNC experience and your most recent hands-on QuickBooks Online work), why you want this role specifically, and one thing most people would be surprised to learn about you

Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Client Success & Operations Coordinator (Remote)
description:
RECKLESS ROYALTIES LLC
Client Success & Operations Coordinator (Remote)

Position Overview:
Reckless Royalties LLC is seeking a highly organized and professional Client Success & Operations Coordinator to manage client communication, consultation scheduling, onboarding, follow-ups, and day-to-day customer interactions.

This role is ideal for someone who is proactive, dependable, personable, and confident communicating with clients. The ideal candidate must be comfortable handling conversations, maintaining organization, and helping move leads through the onboarding process smoothly.

This is not a graphic design or social media management role. The primary responsibility is communication, organization, and client management.

Responsibilities:

* Respond to inquiries from leads and potential clients
* Schedule and confirm consultation calls
* Conduct client onboarding and onboarding conversations with new clients
* Follow up with leads consistently
* Maintain organized client records and communication logs
* Send reminders, updates, and follow-up messages
* Assist with ManyChat flows and communication automation
* Help improve customer experience and client retention
* Communicate weekly updates and reports to the owner
* Identify bottlenecks or missed follow-ups in the system

Requirements:

* Strong written and verbal English communication
* Professional yet personable communication style
* Reliable internet connection and availability
* Ability to work independently and stay organized
* Experience with customer service, sales, or appointment setting preferred
* Familiarity with CRMs, Google Workspace, or scheduling systems is a plus
* Ability to follow systems and SOPs

Ideal Traits:

* Detail-oriented
* Self-motivated
* Emotionally intelligent
* Consistent communicator
* Problem solver
* Comfortable speaking with different personalities

Compensation:

* Remote position
* Part-time to start with potential for long-term growth
* Performance-based opportunities available

About the Company:
Reckless Royalties LLC is a growing credit repair/funding brand focused on helping people elevate financially, emotionally, and personally despite difficult environments or backgrounds. The company values authenticity, consistency, growth, and impact.
When applying answer these short questions 
1. Tell me about a time you had to follow up with someone multiple times.
2. How do you stay organized with multiple clients?
3. How would you handle an upset customer?
4. How fast do you typically respond to messages?
5. Record a short voice introduction.
This Role will be trial based pay and will move to part time unless discussed otherwise.

title: Inbox Manager
description:
Are you the person who keeps the inbox from becoming chaos?

Do you notice the difference between a real prospect reply, an out-of-office, a referral, a bounce, and someone politely saying "not now"? Do you like clear rules, clean labels, organized CRM records, and fast follow-up?

If you are careful, responsive, and genuinely enjoy making sure every message gets handled the right way, this may be a strong fit.

Why this role exists

We run outbound campaigns for B2B service companies. When prospects reply, those replies need to be read, categorized, answered, logged, and followed up quickly. If that workflow gets sloppy, good leads get missed and client reporting becomes unreliable.

We are hiring an Inbox Manager to own that daily response workflow. You will not be expected to design strategy or close deals. Your job is to keep the response engine clean, fast, and accurate.

This role is opening because one of our current tea ---------- mbers is changing schedules, and we need dedicated coverage for inbox work.

Why work with us

- Fully remote contractor role with clear SOPs and a defined workflow
- Direct support from leadership during training
- Long-term opportunity for someone who is reliable and precise
- Work that rewards consistency, judgment, and careful communication
- Clear expectations and measurable performance standards
- A team that cares about clean systems and fast execution

What you'll be doing

Daily Inbox Triage
- Monitor campaign inboxes during the agreed coverage window.
- Separate warmup emails, bounces, out-of-office replies, unsubscribe requests, neutral replies, positive replies, referrals, bid requests, and call-booking opportunities.
- Apply the correct labels/dispositions so reporting stays accurate.
- Escalate hot leads, bid requests, and unclear cases quickly.

Replies and Follow-Up
- Use approved response templates and edit them so they sound natural and address the prospect's actual message.
- Reply within the required SLA during coverage hours.
- Schedule send-later replies when a prospect is outside their business hours.
- Create follow-up tasks when a reply needs future action.
- Make sure no real lead is left without a next step.

CRM Hygiene
- Add or update contacts in OutreachSphere Direct CRM when the SOP requires it.
- Include all relevant contacts on a thread, including CC'd referrals when appropriate.
- Keep tags, contact details, notes, and next steps accurate.
- Process unsubscribes, undeliverables, and do-not-contact requests same business day.

You'll crush this role if you...

- Are fast, careful, and responsive during your working hours
- Write clear professional English without sounding robotic
- Naturally organize information and follow checklists
- Can make good judgment calls when a reply is ambiguous
- Are comfortable using templates while still personalizing the final message
- Have experience with inbox management, CRM updates, sales support, customer support, or lead follow-up
- Are honest when you are unsure and escalate instead of guessing
- Like repetitive operational work and find satisfaction in getting it right every time
- Can learn tools like Missive, Smartlead, GoHighLevel, or similar CRMs

This role is not for you if...

- You want a creative strategy role
- You get bored by repetitive queue work
- You are slow to respond during your working hours
- You dislike detailed labeling, CRM updates, or process documentation
- You improvise instead of following approved templates and rules
- You need someone checking on you constantly to stay on task
- Written English is difficult for you

HOW TO APPLY

Submit your application here:  ---------- 

The application takes about 10 minutes and includes a short personalized video. Applications without a video submission will not be reviewed.

Strong candidates will hear from us within 24 hours.

title: Virtual Operations & Scheduling Coordinator (Part-Time, Australian Hours)
description:
The Opportunity:**

This is a pivotal part-time role for an "Operations & Scheduling Coordinator" who will be instrumental in ensuring our operations run smoothly and efficiently. You will manage crucial administrative tasks, coordinate scheduling, and maintain our business systems, directly contributing to our service delivery excellence. If you are operations-minded, technically savvy, and thrive in an autonomous role where you can make a real impact, we encourage you to apply!

---

**Your Responsibilities:**

As our Virtual Operations & Scheduling Coordinator, you will be instrumental in:

*   Preparing and managing client quotes accurately, including detailed technical and job-specific information.
*   Strategically scheduling jobs to minimise technician travel time, utilizing your knowledge (or ability to quickly learn) of our service-area geography.
*   Maintaining and updating our core business systems (GoHighLevel, ServiceMATE, Xero) to ensure data integrity and operational efficiency.
*   Accurately recording referral sources for every new client within our service software.
*   Answering and directing incoming customer calls professionally via the C3X phone system.
*   Ensuring daily operations run smoothly and effectively with minimal supervision.

---

**What We're Looking For (Requirements):**

We're searching for someone who possesses:

*   An operations-minded approach, coupled with strong technical aptitude and exceptional attention to detail.
*   A background in engineering, project management, or trades coordination is a strong advantage.
*   The ability to quickly learn and apply service-area geography for highly efficient scheduling.
*   Excellent verbal and written English communication skills.
*   Comfort working autonomously and adapting to a flexible management style.
*   Availability to work part-time hours aligned with Australian business hours (with some flexibility on the exact start time).
*   Familiarity with GoHighLevel, ServiceMATE, Xero, or similar business management tools is a significant advantage (comprehensive training will be provided for all systems).

---

**Tools You'll Be Using:**

You will be working with industry-standard software that keeps our business running:

GoHighLevel:** For CRM and marketing automation.
ServiceMATE:** For service management and job scheduling.
Xero:** For accounting and invoicing.
C3X Phone System:** For managing customer communications.
